Testable assert: assert .env is in .gitignore; run git ls-files | xargs grep -l "password\s*=\s*\S" and assert empty output (excluding test fixtures with obviously fake values).

docs.title: US-D1 — C4 Architecture Diagrams
docs.text:
Feature ID: D1 (docs artefact) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want C4 context and container diagrams in docs/architecture/ describing the v0.1 system boundary, so that every new engineer and agent has an accurate visual overview of what exists, and the I8 docs-as-DoD gate is satisfied.

Invariants touched: I8 (D1 is a required M1 artefact; must be reviewed and approved before sign-off)

docs.title: AC-D1-1 — context.mmd is valid Mermaid C4Context
docs.text:
Given docs/architecture/context.mmd has been authored by the tech-writer, When it is passed to a Mermaid CLI renderer (mmdc -i context.mmd -o /dev/null), Then the command exits 0 (no parse errors), and the file begins with C4Context or uses valid C4Context Mermaid syntax.

Testable assert: subprocess.run(["mmdc", "-i", "docs/architecture/context.mmd", "-o", "/dev/null"]).returncode == 0

docs.title: AC-D1-2 — container.mmd is valid Mermaid C4Container showing all v0.1 containers
docs.text:
Given docs/architecture/container.mmd has been authored by the tech-writer, When it is passed to a Mermaid CLI renderer, Then the command exits 0, and the file content includes labels or identifiers for at minimum: Synapse FastAPI backend, Postgres 16, Qdrant, bge-m3 (embedding service), and the vault filesystem.

Testable assert: parse file content; assert each of the five identifiers appears as a string somewhere in the file.

docs.title: AC-D1-3 — both diagrams reviewed and approved by solution-architect (gate)
docs.text: Given context.mmd and container.mmd are present and pass AC-D1-1 and AC-D1-2, When the solution-architect reviews them, Then the architect records approval in the sign-off register in docs/sprints/v0.1-scope.md before M1 sign-off. This is a mandatory human gate; it is binary (approved / not approved).

docs.title: US-D2 — ER Diagram
docs.text:
Feature ID: D2 (docs artefact) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want docs/er/schema.mmd to be generated by make er from the live SQLAlchemy models, so that the ER diagram always reflects the actual Postgres schema and never drifts out of sync.

Invariants touched: I8 (D2 must match live schema; make er is the only valid generation path)

docs.title: AC-D2-1 — schema.mmd is generated by make er, not hand-written
docs.text:
Given docs/er/schema.mmd may or may not exist, When make er is run after deleting the file, Then the file is (re-)created by the make target. If the file was hand-deleted and make er did not recreate it, the test fails.

Testable assert: Path("docs/er/schema.mmd").unlink(missing_ok=True); subprocess.run(["make", "er"]); assert Path("docs/er/schema.mmd").exists()

docs.title: AC-D2-2 — ER diagram matches live Postgres schema
docs.text:
Given Alembic migrations have been applied to a Postgres instance, When make er is run and then the generated schema.mmd is compared against the live schema (queried via information_schema), Then every table and column name present in the Postgres schema appears in the Mermaid ER diagram, and no table or column in the diagram is absent from Postgres.

Testable assert: parse schema.mmd for entity and attribute names; query Postgres for all tables and columns; assert both sets match exactly (no extras, no omissions).

docs.title: AC-D2-3 — reviewed and approved by solution-architect (gate)
docs.text: Given docs/er/schema.mmd passes AC-D2-1 and AC-D2-2, When the solution-architect reviews it, Then approval is recorded in the sign-off register before M1 sign-off. Binary gate.

docs.title: US-D4 — OpenAPI Reference
docs.text:
Feature ID: D4 (docs artefact) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want docs/api/openapi.json to be auto-generated from the FastAPI application and committed to the repository, so that API consumers and future agents have an always-current reference for the four v0.1 endpoints.

Invariants touched: I8 (D4 is a required M1 artefact; must be generated, not hand-written)

docs.title: AC-D4-1 — docs/api/openapi.json is valid OpenAPI 3.1
docs.text:
Given make openapi has been run, When openapi-spec-validator docs/api/openapi.json is executed, Then the validator exits 0 (no schema violations) and the JSON root contains "openapi": "3.1.0".

Testable assert: run validator; assert exit code 0; parse JSON; assert data["openapi"].startswith("3.1").

docs.title: AC-D4-2 — file generated by make openapi, not hand-written
docs.text:
Given docs/api/openapi.json may or may not exist, When the file is deleted and make openapi is run, Then the file is (re-)created by the make target. The file's content must match what FastAPI serves at /openapi.json (byte-for-byte or semantically equivalent JSON).

Testable assert: delete file; run make openapi; assert file exists; compare content with GET /openapi.json response.

docs.title: AC-D4-3 — all four v0.1 endpoints documented with request/response schemas
docs.text:
Given docs/api/openapi.json has been generated, When its paths object is inspected, Then all four paths are present: /status (GET), /pages (GET), /pages/{id} (GET), /ingest/trigger (POST), each with at minimum one response schema defined.

Testable assert: 

spec = json.loads(Path("docs/api/openapi.json").read_text())
assert "/status" in spec["paths"]
assert "/pages" in spec["paths"]
assert "/pages/{id}" in spec["paths"]
assert "/ingest/trigger" in spec["paths"]
for path, ops in spec["paths"].items():
    for method, op in ops.items():
        assert "responses" in op

docs.title: Ambiguities and invariant risks flagged for solution-architect
docs.text: The following issues were identified during AC authoring and must be resolved before engineers begin implementation.

docs.title: AQ-1 — bge-m3 embedding dimension (blocks AC-QD-1)
docs.text:
BACKLOG.md AC-QD-1 states "bge-m3 = 1024 dims — verify against running instance." The value is not confirmed in the specification; it is noted as needing verification. If the running bge-m3 instance returns a different dimension (768 is another common bge-m3 variant), the collection creation call and all downstream embedding tests will fail.

Required action: architect or devops-engineer must query the running bge-m3 endpoint before the Qdrant collection schema is committed, and record the confirmed dimension in an env var (EMBEDDING_DIM) rather than hardcoding 1024.

docs.title: AQ-2 — I1 compliance: how "unchanged file" is determined (blocks AC-WATCH-2, AC-K4-3)
docs.text:
BACKLOG.md states the watcher uses "mtime/hash" to detect unchanged files. The AC as written assumes both mtime AND content hash are checked. However, the implementation could use mtime-only (cheaper but less safe on copy operations that preserve mtime) or hash-only (safe but slower). The mechanism must be decided and locked before the watcher is coded, because the test in AC-WATCH-2 must know which condition to simulate.

Required action: architect to decide and document the skip policy (mtime-only / hash-only / mtime-then-hash-on-mismatch). Recommend mtime-then-hash as the default: skip if mtime unchanged; recompute hash only if mtime changed.

docs.title: AQ-3 — AC-WATCH-5 startup behaviour: empty vault vs. pre-populated vault
docs.text:
AC-WATCH-5 requires that service startup does NOT trigger DB writes for pre-existing files. BACKLOG.md and the scope lock are clear that no startup rescan should happen. However, it is unclear whether the watcher should silently ignore pre-existing files or log a startup warning that files exist but were not scanned. The test harness needs to know which behaviour to assert.

Required action: architect to confirm: on startup with pre-existing files, should the service log a warning (and what level/format) or be completely silent? This affects what the test asserts beyond "no DB write."

docs.title: AQ-4 — vault_state row initialisation: one row or per-vault?
docs.text:
AC-F16dv-1 defines vault_state with columns id, vault_id, data_version, updated_at, implying potentially multiple vaults. In v0.1 there is exactly one vault. It is ambiguous whether the service creates this row on startup (if absent) or only on first ingest. AC-F16dv-4 tests that version is not reset on restart, which requires the row to survive restarts — implying it must be created on startup, not first ingest.

Required action: architect to confirm: (a) vault_state is seeded on service startup if no row exists (with data_version = 0); (b) one row per vault (vault_id = configurable); (c) restart preserves the existing row. Confirm all three so engineers code it correctly and AC-F16dv-4 is unambiguous.

docs.title: AQ-5 — AC-K7-3 is a manual human gate, not automatable (coverage gap)
docs.text: AC-K7-3 ("Opening vault/wiki/ in Obsidian shows no errors") cannot be expressed as a pytest assertion. It is recorded here as a mandatory human verification step (human gate in EC-1). QA-test-engineer should note this as a non-automatable criterion and ensure it appears explicitly in the sign-off register. No pytest test ID will cover it; QA should mark it as MANUAL in the traceability matrix.

docs.title: AQ-6 — POST /ingest/trigger: synchronous in v0.1 but async later (AC-REST-4 scope)
docs.text:
AC-REST-4 states the endpoint "triggers ingest pipeline synchronously in v0.1." This means in v0.1 the endpoint blocks until ingest is complete and returns 202 only after the Postgres row and Qdrant point exist. This is intentionally not the production pattern (async in later sprints). Engineers must not design the endpoint in a way that makes moving to async harder. The response schema must include a task_id field even in v0.1 (stubbed as null or a synchronous token) so the contract can be extended in v0.2 without a breaking change.

Required action: architect to confirm the v0.1 response schema for POST /ingest/trigger (recommend: {"task_id": null, "status": "completed", "page_id": "<uuid>"}) so that v0.2 can swap task_id for a real async ID without breaking callers.

docs.title: AQ-7 — AC-PG-4 (no raw SQL) clashes with Alembic migration files
docs.text:
AC-PG-4 bans raw SQL strings from backend/app/ but migration files (Alembic) necessarily contain SQL. The grep-based test must exclude the alembic/versions/ path to avoid false positives.

Required action: engineers note that the AC-PG-4 test scope is backend/app/ excluding alembic/ subdirectory. Confirm with architect whether the Alembic directory lives inside backend/app/ or at backend/alembic/ (the latter is the conventional layout and would make the exclusion implicit).

docs.title: Sprint 2 — v0.2 (M2) — Architect PR Review + F17 Invariant + Diagram Sign-off
docs.text:
Reviewer: solution-architect Date: 2026-06-28 Milestone: M2 — "Agentic loop closed, 3 providers" Gate: EC-M2-15 (architect sign-off on D3 + D7 + D1u); the I6/I7 invariant gate Scope: implementation review of F17 against docs/sprints/v0.2-architecture.md + ADR-0007..0011, and the 9 invariants in CLAUDE.md §3. Code was NOT modified. Authority: this verdict gates the merge of sprint/v0.2.

This review verifies the IMPLEMENTATION against the design I signed off in v0.2-architecture.md §9. It is a per-item APPROVE/BLOCK gate. Verdict at §10.

docs.title: 1. I6 — Pluggable inference (capability-aware routing, no hardcode) — APPROVE
docs.text:
Check Evidence Result Routing decides on capabilities().supports_agentic_loop ONLY orchestrator.run_ingest_pipeline() reads caps = provider.capabilities() then if caps.supports_agentic_loop: (orchestrator.py:313/322). No other signal gates the branch. PASS No isinstance / type() / class-name / provider_type == in the routing path grep of orchestrator.py + loop.py: the only isinstance hits are unrelated (page.type PageType check in the validator, _sources_raw list coercion). No type(, __class__, provider_type ==, or .name == branch anywhere in routing. PASS resolve_provider has NO hardcoded default backend, raises on unknown/missing type provider/__init__.py:68 — None row → ValueError ("no hardcoded default"); unknown provider_type → ValueError listing valid types. _REGISTRY is a config-value→class dispatch (the row's provider_type), explicitly NOT a routing decision. PASS Resolution order operation > vault > global provider_config_service.resolve_provider_config() queries scope='operation'+vault+op, then scope='vault'+vault, then scope='global', each with is_fallback IS FALSE. ConfigNotFoundError on no match — never a silent default (I6). PASS No model id / API key / base_url literal outside provider_config rows + env grep across app/: the only claude-* strings are in column/field doc comments (models.py:264, main.py:207 descriptions) and the Alembic seed default (os.environ.get("DEFAULT_MODEL_ID", "claude-sonnet-4-6")). The only base_url literal is https://api.anthropic.com, confined to provider/api.py and env-overridable (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL). No key literals; all keys via os.environ inside api.py/cli.py only. PASS chat() is a locked stub (NotImplementedError) on all 3 backends ollama.py:90, api.py:120, cli.py:97 all raise NotImplementedError(... v0.4 (F6)) with no network call. Signature locked per ABC. PASS Provider selectable per operation/vault _resolve_ingest_provider_config() resolves operation='ingest'; same ConfigResolver serves chat/lint scopes. Per-vault + per-operation rows honoured. PASS Backends are config, not new classes OpenAI-compatible path is base_url-driven inside the single ApiProvider (api.py:83 _openai_compatible = bool(config.base_url)). No subclass-per-vendor. PASS provider_name/model_id audit-only, never routed on Stored on ingest_runs from caps.name/config; never read back into a routing decision. PASS

I6 VERDICT: APPROVE. Routing is single-signal capability-based with machine-checkable guardrails. No backend is hardcoded; no provider literal escapes provider/ + env + seeded DB rows. The CLI delegated vs orchestrated separation is correct (delegate_ingest on the agentic path; analyze()/generate() raise RuntimeError on CliAgentProvider to make accidental orchestrated use loud — exceeds the design ask).

docs.title: 2. I7 — Bounded loops + cost logging — APPROVE
docs.text:
Check Evidence Result Orchestrated loop bounded by BOTH max_iter AND token_budget loop.run_orchestrated_loop(): for i in range(1, max_iter+1) (bound #1) with a pre-call if accumulator.total_tokens >= token_budget: break (bound #2). Both read from provider_config with the ADR-0009 defaults (3 / 60 000). PASS analyze() runs ONCE; only generate() retries (AQ-v0.2-1) analyze() is called before the loop (loop.py:132); only generate() is inside the loop with augmented context. Matches ADR-0007 §4. PASS Non-convergence stops at max_iter, no overrun Loop exits cleanly with converged=False, stop_reason="max_iter"; iterations capped at max_iter. AC-K2-5 honoured. PASS token_budget halts before an unaffordable call Pre-call check sets stop_reason="token_budget" and breaks before generate(). Correct (analyze already spent budget). PASS total_cost_usd written to ingest_runs every run run_ingest_pipeline() finalizes the accumulator and always INSERT ingest_runs{...total_cost_usd...} on both routes (orchestrated and delegated). Numeric(10,4). PASS Fallback bounded to exactly once _run_orchestrated() catches (TimeoutError, ConnectionError), resolves ONE is_fallback=True row, retries once; a second failure raises IngestError with no chain (from exc2, no recursion). PASS $1/run anomaly → WARNING + cost_anomaly flag cost_anomaly = total_cost_usd > COST_ANOMALY_THRESHOLD_USD (1.00); flag persisted on the row; inline logger.warning(...) emitted AFTER the row write (AQ-v0.2-8). PASS Usage normalization across backends Ollama→prompt_eval_count/eval_count, cost 0.0; Anthropic→usage.input/output_tokens × env price map; OpenAI→prompt/completion_tokens; CLI→SDK metadata, cost 0.0 by convention with tokens=0 + WARNING when absent. All push via _record_usage to the run-scoped UsageAccumulator (out-of-band, ADR-0007 §1). PASS CLI budget passed/abort CliAgentProvider carries config.token_budget (100k default via DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET_CLI); records exactly one ingest_runs row at the orchestrator. PASS

I7 VERDICT: APPROVE. Dual bounds enforced together; analyze-once retry-generate is exact; fallback is exactly-once with no chaining; cost is persisted and anomaly-flagged on every run.

Non-blocking note NB-1 (tracked, not a gate): the fallback except clause catches (TimeoutError, ConnectionError). ADR-0009 §4 phrases the trigger as "timeout / 503". An HTTP 503 surfaces from httpx as HTTPStatusError (via raise_for_status()), which is NOT a subclass of those two — so a literal 503 would currently surface as IngestError WITHOUT attempting the single fallback. This does not violate I7 (the loop and fallback remain bounded; no overrun, no chain) and the timeout path — the primary failure mode — is covered. Recommend a follow-up (v0.3 hardening) widening the catch to include httpx.HTTPStatusError for 5xx so the fallback engages on 503 as the ADR intends. Filed to backend-engineer; does not block M2.

docs.title: 3. §12 — Secrets — APPROVE
docs.text:
Check Evidence Result provider_config has NO api_key column models.py ProviderConfig + migration 0002 create_table("provider_config") — no api_key column. ER PROVIDER_CONFIG has none. REST ProviderConfigCreate/Response (main.py) accept/return none. PASS API keys read from env only ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/OPENAI_API_KEY via os.environ.get(...) inside api.py and cli.py only; missing key → ValueError referencing §12/ADR-0008. PASS No secrets in code grep for sk-ant/sk-…/api_key = "…" → none. Price map from PROVIDER_PRICE_MAP env (no price literals in code). PASS

§12 VERDICT: APPROVE. Keys are env-confined to provider/; nothing secret in DB, config, ER, OpenAPI, or source.

docs.title: 4. Data model / D2 (ER + migration) — APPROVE
docs.text:
Check Evidence Result 5 tables present models.py: pages, vault_state, provider_config, ingest_runs, links. ER schema.mmd renders all 5. PASS provider_config columns match spec (ADR-0008 §2 / arch §5) id, scope, operation(null), vault_id(null), provider_type, model_id, base_url(null), max_iter(def 3), token_budget(def 60000), is_fallback(def false), created_at, updated_at. No api_key. PASS ingest_runs columns match (I7) id, vault_id, page_id(FK pages, null), provider_name, provider_type, model_id, route, max_iter_used, total_tokens, total_cost_usd Numeric(10,4), converged, cost_anomaly, started_at, finished_at. PASS links columns match (K5) id, source_page_id(FK pages, not null), target_title, target_page_id(FK pages, nullable), alias(null), dangling(def false), created_at. Two indexes on source/target. PASS Migration 0002 matches models Column-by-column identical to models.py (types, nullability, server_defaults, FKs). One migration for all three tables (one schema-change event, I8). PASS Migration seeds from env, not hardcoded model ids Global + fallback rows seeded with model_id from os.environ.get("DEFAULT_MODEL_ID", ...) / DEFAULT_FALLBACK_MODEL_ID. The literal is a fallback DEFAULT only; runtime app code always reads the DB row (AC-F17-8). PASS ER reflects reality (zero drift) DOCS_STATUS reports make er zero drift; ER comments mirror model comments verbatim. PASS

DATA-MODEL VERDICT: APPROVE. Models, migration, and ER are mutually consistent and match the design.

Non-blocking note NB-2: the seed global/fallback rows pin provider_type='api'. This is a sensible default (API is the design's "default recommendation") and is user-editable via the /provider/config CRUD; it is NOT a hardcode-in-code (it is a seed row). Acceptable. If the operator runs offline-only they re-point the global row to local. No action required.

docs.title: 5. K5 / K3 + MCP — APPROVE
docs.text:
Check Evidence Result K5 links parser + dangling resolution wiki/links.py: regex \[\[([^\[\]]+?)\]\]; handles [[T]], [[T\|alias]], [[T#sec]], dedupes; persist_links() DELETEs prior rows for the page then re-inserts (incremental, I1), bulk-resolves titles, sets dangling=True when unresolved — warn-not-error, does not invalidate (AQ-v0.2-7). PASS K3 index.md updater (I5 valid frontmatter) wiki/index.py: writes type: index frontmatter block first (I5), groups live pages by type, excludes overview/index, idempotent (byte-stable for same DB state). PASS K3 is not a full rescan (I1) update_index() reads the pages table (live rows) and rewrites one catalogue file per write event — it queries DB state, never re-walks the vault filesystem. Per-write, not per-vault-scan. PASS MCP write_page reuses the shared write path mcp/server.py write_page → validates via the SAME validate_pages() (loop.py) → calls write_wiki_page() (orchestrator.py). No divergent write path; K5 parse + K3 index + Qdrant upsert + log all run identically (ADR-0010 §2). Returns structured {"error": ...} dicts, not exceptions. PASS search_wiki uses query_points (client ≥1.10) mcp/server.py:98 await qdrant.query_points(... query=vector ...) reading query_response.points; cosine score normalised to [0,1]. Legacy .search( removed (only referenced in a comment). Reuses bge-m3 + Qdrant (I9). PASS 4 tools registered search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages — all on the single mcp FastMCP instance; list_pages excludes soft-deleted; stdio entrypoint. PASS

K5/K3/MCP VERDICT: APPROVE. Single write path is honoured end-to-end; the orchestrated and delegated (MCP) paths both flow through write_wiki_page, so I1/I5 hold identically. The query_points migration is correct for qdrant-client ≥1.10.

Observation O-1 (informational, not a defect): the delegated path wires the MCP server via from app.mcp.server import mcp and CliAgentProvider.delegate_ingest raises if mcp_server is None — correctly refusing to let the agent fall back to raw filesystem writes that would bypass I1/I5. The live SDK↔MCP wiring is exercised only under the deferred live smoke (EC-M2-5). The seam is correct and safe-by-default; live verification stays a runtime/human gate.

docs.title: 6. Diagram sign-off (EC-M2-15 — resolves AC-D3 + D7 + D1u) — APPROVE
D1 — C4 (context / container / component)
docs.text:
Diagram Accuracy check Result context.mmd Line 1 C4Context. Shows Synapse, Emanuele, Ollama + Anthropic/OpenAI-compat + Qdrant + bge-m3 + Vault FS. Synapse description states capability-aware loop (I6) + bounded I7. Key via env (§12). No v0.3+ drawn: no graph/sigma, no SearXNG, no web UI. APPROVE container.mmd Line 1 C4Container. InferenceProvider layer container ("the ONLY inference surface (I6); no model id/key/endpoint leaks; chat() stubbed") + MCP server (FastMCP, stdio) + Orchestrator ("ROUTES on supports_agentic_loop (I6)", "bounded by max_iter+token_budget (I7)"). Postgres lists all 5 tables; "No secrets stored (§12)". Routing shown as capability-based. APPROVE component.mmd Line 1 C4Component. All 3 backends under the ABC; ConfigResolver (operation>vault>global), WikiPage validator, K5 parser, K3/F3 writer, MCP server (4 tools, write_page reuses primitives I1/I5). Orchestrator annotated "ROUTES on supports_agentic_loop (I6 — never isinstance/class)". Arrows: orch→prov(ABC)→3 backends; clip→mcp; no class/isinstance reference. APPROVE

Minor label drift (non-blocking): component.mmd cites file paths ingest/config.py, ingest/parser/wikilinks.py, ingest/catalogue.py for ConfigResolver/K5/K3, whereas the implementation lives at provider_config_service.py, wiki/links.py, wiki/index.py. The COMPONENTS and their responsibilities are correct; only the illustrative path labels are stale. This is a diagram-cosmetic note for tech-writer (does not affect topology accuracy or the gate). Filed as DIAG-NB-1.

docs.title: D3 — sequence diagrams
docs.text: Diagram Accuracy check Result ingest-routing.mmd Line 1 sequenceDiagram. Both branches: alt supports_agentic_loop == True (CLI — DELEGATED) / else == False (Local/API — ORCHESTRATED). Routing reads ProviderCapabilities{supports_agentic_loop, ...} only — no isinstance/type. ConfigResolver order shown. CLI path writes via MCP (validated, I5; persist + K5). ingest_runs INSERT + inline $1 anomaly at end, both branches. Matches run_ingest_pipeline. APPROVE ingest-loop.mmd Line 1 sequenceDiagram. analyze() ONCE before the loop (AQ-v0.2-1). Loop label iteration = 1..max_iter (stop when converged OR iteration==max_iter OR tokens>=budget) — dual bound (I7). Pre-call token_budget check shown. validate → whole-batch retry with augmented context. converged + non-converged paths. ingest_runs INSERT + anomaly at end. Matches run_orchestrated_loop. APPROVE

docs.title: D2 — ER
docs.text: 5 tables, no api_key, I7 columns (total_cost_usd/converged/cost_anomaly), K5 links (nullable target_page_id, dangling). Zero drift vs models.py. APPROVE.

docs.title: D7 — ADRs
docs.text:
ADR-0007 (ABC + analyze-once + validator + routing-via-capabilities), 0008 (provider_config + ingest_runs + links, secrets-via-env, operation column), 0009 (max_iter=3, budgets, Usage normalization, $1 anomaly inline, fallback-once), 0010 (stdio + shared write path), 0011 (locked schemas). All Accepted, indexed, and consistent with the code. APPROVE.

DIAGRAM SIGN-OFF: APPROVED. All D1/D2/D3/D7 artifacts are v0.2-accurate; no v0.3 graph/UI is drawn as present; routing is shown as capability-based; the loop shows the I7 dual bounds. Two cosmetic, non-blocking diagram notes (DIAG-NB-1 component path labels; the openapi.json version/description string already tracked by tech-writer/backend) are handed to tech-writer.

docs.title: 7. Invariant sweep (I1, I5, I9 + others)
docs.text: Invariant Result Evidence I1 incremental only PASS Source ingest keeps the mtime-then-hash gate; write_wiki_page upserts per page; K5 persist_links delete+reinsert per page; K3 update_index rewrites the catalogue from DB rows per write event. No vault rescan introduced. I5 Obsidian valid PASS Typed WikiFrontmatter enforces type/title/sources/lang at the schema boundary; writer serializes enum as string value; index.md/overview.md carry valid frontmatter. I6 PASS §1. I7 PASS §2. I8 docs-as-DoD PASS ADR-0007..0011 + C4 (3) + D3 (2) + ER + mcp-tools.json + openapi present; ER/openapi zero drift. I9 do not reinvent PASS OllamaProvider reuses running Ollama; search_wiki reuses Qdrant + bge-m3; FastMCP for the server; no new inference/search service. I2/I3/I4 N/A No graph/chat-UI/editor in v0.2; nothing introduced that breaks them.

docs.title: 8. Blocking defects
docs.text: None. No invariant is traded. The two notes (NB-1 fallback-on-503 catch width; NB-2 seed provider_type) and the diagram-cosmetic note (DIAG-NB-1) are non-blocking and tracked.

docs.title: 9. Sign-off conditions from v0.2-architecture §9 — verification
docs.text:
# Condition Result 1 Routing branch contains no isinstance/type/class-name test MET (§1) 2 No model id / key / endpoint outside provider/ MET (§1, §3) — literals confined to seed-default + env-overridable Anthropic base + doc comments 3 validate() and write_page share the ONE validator MET — both call loop.validate_pages (§5) 4 Non-converging loop stops at exactly max_iter=3 with converged=False MET (§2) — QA AC-K2-5 green 5 D2 regenerated by make er matches live schema MET — zero drift (§4, DOCS_STATUS)

All five merge conditions are satisfied.

docs.title: 10. ARCHITECT VERDICT
docs.text:
APPROVED.

The F17 implementation conforms to the design signed off in v0.2-architecture.md §9 and honours all nine invariants. I6 (single-signal capability routing, no hardcoded backend, no provider-literal leakage, env-only keys, locked chat() stubs) and I7 (dual-bounded loop, analyze-once retry-generate, exactly-once fallback, per-run cost persistence + anomaly flag) — the two gate invariants for M2 — are met with machine-checkable guardrails. Data model, migration, and ER are consistent and drift-free. K5/K3 and the MCP write path correctly reuse the single write_wiki_page seam, preserving I1/I5; query_points is correct for qdrant-client ≥1.10. All D1/D2/D3/D7 diagrams are v0.2-accurate and signed off (EC-M2-15 cleared).

This sign-off covers the design+implementation+docs gate. The remaining M2 items — EC-M2-5 (live 3-provider smoke on TrueNAS) and EC-M2-13/EC-M2-17 (open-in-Obsidian + human checkpoint) — are runtime/human gates outside the architect's scope and remain correctly deferred per the QA report.

Hand to orchestrator: M2 architect gate MET (pending the documented live-smoke + human checkpoint). Three non-blocking follow-ups handed off: - NB-1 → backend-engineer: widen fallback catch to include httpx.HTTPStatusError 5xx (503). - DIAG-NB-1 → tech-writer: refresh component.mmd path labels (config/links/index modules). - (already tracked) openapi.json info.version/description string → backend-engineer.

Signed: solution-architect · 2026-06-28

docs.title: Sprint 2 — v0.2 — Architecture & F17 Sign-off
docs.text:
Owner: solution-architect Milestone: M2 — "Agentic loop closed, 3 providers" Date: 2026-06-28 Status: SIGNED OFF — APPROVED to implement (see §9) Authority: this document is the binding design for v0.2. Engineers implement to it; PRs are reviewed against it. ADR-0007..0011 carry the rationale.

This is the design sign-off for F17 (InferenceProvider abstraction + capability-aware routing) and the bounded ingest loop. It resolves the 8 functional-analyst questions (AQ-v0.2-1..8), locks the contract schemas, specifies the data model and the routing/loop, defines the MCP tool contracts, and states invariant compliance. It does NOT contain provider implementation code (that is ai-agent-engineer's work).

docs.title: 1. AQ decision table (the 8 blocking questions — RESOLVED)
docs.text: AQ Question Decision Recorded in AQ-v0.2-1 analyze() re-run on retry? analyze ONCE per run; only generate() is retried with validation errors appended to retrieval_context. analyze.call_count==1, generate.call_count==max_iter on non-convergence. ADR-0007 §4 AQ-v0.2-2 ingest-run logging: structlog vs Postgres table? Postgres ingest_runs table (system of record for cost auditing per I7/§11) PLUS a structured log line for live tail. ADR-0008 §4 AQ-v0.2-3 exact Pydantic schemas for Analysis/WikiPage/Message/ProviderCapabilities? Locked in backend/app/ingest/schemas.py; Pydantic v2 for parsed DTOs, frozen dataclass for descriptors. Full field tables in §2 below. ADR-0011, §2 AQ-v0.2-4 per-backend token accounting? Uniform Usage{input_tokens, output_tokens, total_cost_usd}; each provider maps native fields. Anthropic usage.*; Ollama prompt_eval_count/eval_count; CLI cost=$0.00; Local cost=$0.00. ADR-0009 §2 AQ-v0.2-5 provider_config "operation" parameterisation? Add a separate nullable operation column (ingest|chat|lint); scope ∈ global|vault|operation. Resolution: operation+vault > vault > global. ADR-0008 §2 AQ-v0.2-6 MCP transport? stdio for v0.2 (CliAgentProvider native path); HTTP deferred to v0.4. Test client exercises tools in-process. ADR-0010 §1 AQ-v0.2-7 WikiPage "invalid" threshold + retry granularity? Invalid if ANY page fails: type∈enum, non-empty title, non-empty sources[] (incl. origin path), lang, non-empty content. Whole-batch retry. Dangling links do NOT invalidate. ADR-0007 §5 AQ-v0.2-8 $1/run cost-anomaly WARNING site? Inline in orchestrator, after the ingest_runs row/log line is written. Sets cost_anomaly=True. ADR-0009 §3

docs.title: 2. Locked schemas (the contract — backend/app/ingest/schemas.py)
docs.text: Normative. ai-agent-engineer implements these exactly. R = required, O = optional. Pydantic v2 BaseModel unless marked dataclass.

docs.title: PageType (str Enum)
docs.text: entity | concept | source | synthesis | comparison (plus overview and index reserved for auto-generated catalogue pages — NOT valid suggested_pages types; used only by K3/F3 writers.)

docs.title: Analysis (BaseModel)
docs.text: Field Type R/O Constraint topics list[str] R len ≥ 1 entities list[str] R len ≥ 0 (may be empty) language str R ISO-639-1, e.g. "en", "it" suggested_pages list[SuggestedPage] R len ≥ 1 summary str O short abstract for overview generation

docs.title: SuggestedPage (BaseModel)
docs.text: Field Type R/O Constraint title str R non-empty type PageType R in enum rationale str O why this page

docs.title: WikiFrontmatter (BaseModel, extra="allow")
docs.text: Field Type R/O Constraint type PageType R in enum title str R non-empty sources list[str] R non-empty; contains the origin relative path (F3) lang str R ISO-639-1, == Analysis.language (extra keys) Any O allowed for future frontmatter

docs.title: WikiPage (BaseModel)
docs.text: Field Type R/O Constraint title str R non-empty (mirrors frontmatter.title) type PageType R in enum (mirrors frontmatter.type) content str R non-empty Markdown body, frontmatter block stripped frontmatter WikiFrontmatter R typed; enforces I5 at the schema boundary

docs.title: Message (BaseModel) — used only by stubbed chat()
docs.text: Field Type R/O Constraint role Literal["user","assistant","system"] R backend-neutral (I6) content str R

docs.title: ProviderCapabilities (frozen dataclass)
docs.text: Field Type Constraint mode Literal["local","api","cli"] supports_tools bool supports_agentic_loop bool the ONLY routing signal (I6) max_context int tokens name str non-empty (audit only, never routing)

docs.title: Usage (frozen dataclass)
docs.text: Field Type Constraint input_tokens int ≥ 0 output_tokens int ≥ 0 total_cost_usd float 0.0 for local & cli

docs.title: 3. InferenceProvider ABC (backend/app/ingest/provider/base.py)
docs.text:
class InferenceProvider(abc.ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def analyze(self, source_text: str, vault_context: str) -> Analysis: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def generate(self, analysis: Analysis, retrieval_context: str) -> list[WikiPage]: ...
    @abstractmethod
    async def chat(self, messages: list[Message], retrieval_context: str) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def capabilities(self) -> ProviderCapabilities: ...

Rules (enforced in review + CI): - chat() is implemented on all three backends but raises NotImplementedError immediately (no network call) in v0.2. Signature locked now → non-breaking body change at v0.4 (F6). - Routing reads capabilities().supports_agentic_loop only. NEVER isinstance, type(...), class-name strings, or provider_type == "cli". (AC-K2-4, ADR-0007 §3.) - No model id / API key / endpoint URL appears outside backend/app/ingest/provider/ (AC-F17-4/8, ADR-0008 §3). Keys come from env inside api.py/cli.py only. - Usage is recorded out-of-band onto a run-scoped accumulator the orchestrator passes in; it is NOT part of the public return types (keeps WikiPage clean; lets the CLI path share the same accounting surface).

docs.title: Capability matrix (AC-F17-3)
docs.text: Provider mode supports_agentic_loop supports_tools route OllamaProvider local False model-dependent orchestrated ApiProvider api False True orchestrated CliAgentProvider cli True True delegated

docs.title: 4. Capability-aware routing + bounded loop (orchestrator.py)
docs.text:
Inserted at the ADR-0003 extension point inside ingest_file(); callers (watcher, REST) unchanged.

resolve provider from provider_config (operation="ingest")   # ConfigResolver, ADR-0008
caps = provider.capabilities()
if caps.supports_agentic_loop:            # CLI
    result = delegate_ingest(provider, source_text, vault_context, token_budget_cli)
else:                                     # Local / API — ORCHESTRATED LOOP
    analysis = await provider.analyze(source_text, vault_context)   # ONCE (AQ-1)
    ctx = retrieval_context
    for i in range(1, max_iter+1):                                  # I7 bound #1
        if accumulator.tokens >= token_budget: break                # I7 bound #2 (pre-call)
        pages = await provider.generate(analysis, ctx)
        errors = validate(pages)                                    # ADR-0007 §5
        if not errors:
            converged = True; break
        ctx = augment(ctx, errors)                                  # retry generate only
    write pages, parse links (K5), update index.md (K3) / overview.md (F3)
finalize accumulator → total_tokens, total_cost_usd
INSERT ingest_runs{...converged...}                                 # I7 log (ADR-0008 §4)
if total_cost_usd > 1.00: log WARNING; set cost_anomaly=True        # inline (AQ-8)

docs.title: I7 bounds (every loop)
docs.text: Loop max_iter token_budget cost logged fallback Orchestrated ingest 3 60 000 ingest_runs + log n/a CLI delegated n/a (provider-internal) 100 000 (passed to SDK; abort on exceed) ingest_runs (cost=$0.00) n/a Provider fallback 1 attempt — records fallback provider_name, converged=False exactly once, then surface IngestError

docs.title: Cost accounting points
docs.text:
1. After analyze() → add Usage to accumulator.
  2. After each generate() → add Usage.
  3. Before each generate() → check token_budget (stop if exceeded).
  4. At run end → finalize → write ingest_runs → inline anomaly check.

docs.title: 5. Data-model deltas (for D2 / make er)
docs.text:
All three tables ship in ONE Alembic migration (scope §2 K3/K5 decision). Full column tables in ADR-0008. Summary:

  • provider_config: id, scope (global|vault|operation), operation (ingest|chat|lint, null), vault_id (null at global), provider_type (local|api|cli), model_id, base_url (null), max_iter (default 3), token_budget, is_fallback (default false), created_at, updated_at. No API key column (§12 — env only). Seeded with one global row using §12 model ids via the Alembic data migration (never literals in app code, AC-F17-8).
  • ingest_runs: id, vault_id, page_id (FK pages, null), provider_name, provider_type, model_id, route (orchestrated|delegated), max_iter_used, total_tokens, total_cost_usd (numeric(10,4)), converged, cost_anomaly (default false), started_at, finished_at.
  • links (K5): id, source_page_id (FK pages, not null), target_title, target_page_id (FK pages, nullable — for v0.3 graph resolution), alias (null), dangling (default false), created_at.

provider_name and model_id in ingest_runs are audit metadata only — never read back into a routing decision (I6).

docs.title: 6. MCP tool contracts (backend/app/mcp/server.py, FastMCP, stdio)
docs.text:
All four tools reuse the ingest seam primitives (ADR-0010 §2): write_page does NOT write files directly — it validates, slugs, writes, then calls persist_metadata/upsert_vector/append_log/bump_version + K5 parse + K3 index, the same primitives the orchestrator uses (I1, I5). search_wiki reuses Qdrant + bge-m3 (I9).

Tool Input Output Notes search_wiki query: str list[PageRef] where PageRef = vector lookup on synapse_pages (I9); NOT F5 RAG write_page title: str, content: str, frontmatter: dict PageRef or structured tool-error validates type/title/sources (ADR-0007 §5); error (not exception) on missing field; updates index.md get_page title: str {title, type, content, frontmatter} or not-found tool-error structured error on unknown title list_pages type: str \| null list[PageRef] excludes soft-deleted (deleted_at != NULL); optional type filter

Schemas exported to docs/api/mcp-tools.json (AC-MCP-7/AC-D4u-2). CliAgentProvider consumes these over stdio during delegated ingest (AC-MCP-8).

docs.title: 7. Invariant-compliance statement
docs.text: Invariant How the v0.2 design honours it I6 (pluggable, never hardcode) All inference behind the ABC; routing reads capabilities().supports_agentic_loop only (no isinstance/class/type — AC-K2-4); provider selected from provider_config (operation>vault>global); model ids only in seeded DB rows (AC-F17-8); keys env-only, confined to provider/ (AC-F17-4); provider_name stored for audit, never routed on. Backends (incl. OpenAI-compat) are config (base_url), not new classes. I7 (bounded loops, cost logged) Orchestrated loop bounded by max_iter=3 AND token_budget=60k (dual stop, pre-call budget check); CLI budget=100k passed to SDK; fallback bounded to exactly 1; every run writes ingest_runs with total_cost_usd (0.00 for local/cli) + converged; inline $1 anomaly WARNING. Non-convergence test (AC-K2-5) stops at exactly 3. I1 (incremental only) write_page (both orchestrated and MCP) calls the same upsert primitives; K3 index + K5 links update per-write event; no rescan. I5 (Obsidian valid) Typed WikiFrontmatter enforces type/title/sources/lang at the schema boundary; validator rejects pages lacking them before write; index.md/overview.md keep valid frontmatter. Human re-check at EC-M2-13. I8 (docs-as-DoD) ADR-0007..0011 authored; component.mmd added, context/container updated; D3 ingest-routing.mmd + ingest-loop.mmd drafted; D2 to be regenerated via make er after models.py lands; D4 OpenAPI + mcp-tools.json. I9 (do not reinvent) OllamaProvider reuses running Ollama; search_wiki reuses Qdrant/bge-m3; FastMCP for the server; no new inference/search container. I2/I3/I4 Not exercised in v0.2 (no graph/chat-UI/editor); design introduces nothing that breaks them.

docs.title: EC coverage flag
docs.text:
The design satisfies all of EC-M2-1 through EC-M2-16 at the design level. The following are execution/runtime gates the design cannot itself satisfy — they require live infra and the human checkpoint, and are explicitly out of the architect's sign-off scope: - EC-M2-5 (3-provider live smoke matrix) and EC-M2-17 (human checkpoint) — require running Ollama + a real Anthropic key + claude-agent-sdk on TrueNAS. Design is complete and correct; execution is the qa/human gate. - EC-M2-13 (Obsidian open with real LLM content) — manual human verification.

No EC is unsatisfiable by design. No invariant is traded.

docs.title: 8. Handoffs
docs.text: To What ai-agent-engineer §2 locked schemas; §3 ABC; §4 routing+loop; §6 MCP contracts. Implement to these exactly. backend-engineer §5 data-model deltas → models.py + one Alembic migration (+ seed row); ConfigResolver (operation>vault>global); ingest_runs writer. tech-writer ADR-0007..0011 (format/index); D3 diagrams (drafted — finalize prose/labels); D1 context/container/component (authored — review); regenerate D2 via make er; D4 mcp-tools.json. product-manager / orchestrator This sign-off (§9) and the EC coverage flag (§7).

docs.title: 9. F17 abstraction — SIGN-OFF
docs.text:
VERDICT: APPROVED to implement.

The InferenceProvider ABC, capability-aware routing, bounded ingest loop, provider_config + ingest_runs + links data model, and MCP tool contracts are signed off as designed in this document and ADR-0007..0011. All 8 AQ questions are resolved. No invariant is traded for convenience; I6 and I7 are honoured with machine-checkable guardrails (static grep for provider leakage and routing-by-type; the non-converging-loop test).

Conditions on merge (architect review gate EC-M2-15 will verify): 1. orchestrator.py routing branch contains no isinstance/type/class-name test (CI grep). 2. No model id / key / endpoint outside provider/ (CI grep, AC-F17-4/8). 3. validate() and write_page share the ONE validator (ADR-0007 §5 / ADR-0010 §2). 4. The non-converging loop stops at exactly max_iter=3 with converged=False (AC-K2-5). 5. D2 regenerated by make er matches the live schema after the migration lands.

Signed: solution-architect · 2026-06-28

docs.title: Out-of-scope check — CLEAN
docs.text:
The following items are CONFIRMED NOT introduced in v0.2 (per architect review, QA static checks, and scope lock):

  • F4 (knowledge graph): no FA2, no igraph, no sigma.js. links table is data-only (graph edge source; I2 not yet exercised). CLEAN.
  • F1 (3-panel UI): no frontend code in v0.2. CLEAN.
  • F6 (multi-conversation chat): chat() is a stub raising NotImplementedError on all 3 backends; confirmed by T-SCH-001..020 and architect review §1. No chat endpoint, no conversation persistence. CLEAN.
  • F7 / F8 / F14: no streaming display, no LaTeX conversion, no context-window UI. CLEAN.
  • F10 (deep research): MCP search_wiki is local wiki search (Qdrant + bge-m3); SearXNG is NOT wired. ops/deep_research.py does not exist. CLEAN.
  • F9 (HITL review queue): not introduced. CLEAN.
  • F12 / F13 / F11: no multi-format ingest, no cascade delete, no clipper. CLEAN.

docs.title: chat() stub confirmation
docs.text: chat() is STUBBED on all 3 backends per §5 of the scope lock. Architect review §1 confirms: ollama.py:90, api.py:120, cli.py:97 all raise NotImplementedError(... v0.4 (F6)) with no network call. Signature is locked per ABC. Full F6 implementation is v0.4. CONFIRMED.

docs.title: No hardcoded provider/model IDs
docs.text: Architect review §1 confirms the only claude-* strings in app/ are in column/field doc comments and an Alembic seed default that is env-overridable (os.environ.get("DEFAULT_MODEL_ID", "claude-sonnet-4-6")). The only base_url literal is https://api.anthropic.com, confined to provider/api.py and env-overridable via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. No API key literals anywhere; all keys via os.environ inside api.py/cli.py only. CONFIRMED per CLAUDE.md §12 / I6.

docs.title: Early extras (not scope violations)
docs.text: One early extra noted: the /provider/config/{config_id} DELETE endpoint. The scope lock required GET and POST; DELETE is a logical extension of the CRUD surface and does not introduce any out-of-scope feature ID. Classified as a harmless early extra. Logged here for velocity calibration; does not require escalation.

docs.title: 4. Cost tracking (I7 / §11 confirmation)
docs.text:
• ingest_runs table has total_cost_usd Numeric(10,4) column; written on every ingest run (both orchestrated and delegated paths) per architect review §2.
  • $1.00/run anomaly threshold: cost_anomaly bool flag set + WARNING logged inline after the row write (AQ-v0.2-8, ADR-0009 §4). Verified by T-LOOP-* tests.
  • Sandbox cost: $0.00 (no live inference calls in CI). This is correct and expected.
  • Real cost will be logged to ingest_runs on the M2 TrueNAS smoke run (EC-M2-5/17).
  • CLI provider records total_cost_usd = 0.00 by convention (uses build-time agent credits, not runtime billing); documented in ADR-0009. tokens=0 + WARNING emitted when SDK metadata is absent.
  • CLAUDE.md §11 cost tracking: I7 COMPLIED.

docs.title: 5. Non-blocking follow-ups for v0.2.1 / v0.3
docs.text:
These three items do NOT block M2 sign-off. They are recorded here so they survive into the next sprint.

ID Owner Item Target NB-1 backend-engineer Widen fallback except clause to include httpx.HTTPStatusError for 5xx responses (currently catches only TimeoutError + ConnectionError; a literal HTTP 503 from httpx would bypass the single fallback and surface as IngestError without engaging the fallback per ADR-0009 §4). Filed by architect review §2. v0.3 hardening NB-2 backend-engineer T-CQ-009 I6 guard is a coarse whole-file substring check for the word "InferenceProvider". This forced avoidance of that word in a main.py docstring — the guard should be scoped to import statement lines only (e.g., grep "^from\|^import" backend/app/) rather than scanning all text. Zero functional impact; the guard still blocks accidental imports. v0.3 hardening NB-3 backend-engineer openapi.json info.version is "0.1.0" and info.description still says "walking skeleton (M1)" — stale strings set in backend/app/main.py. Routes and schemas are v0.2-accurate. Tech-writer confirmed non-blocking. Update version string and description before the v0.2 final commit. v0.2 final commit (before merge)

Note on DIAG-NB-1: the architect filed a cosmetic label-drift item (component.mmd cited ingest/config.py, ingest/parser/wikilinks.py, ingest/catalogue.py as illustrative paths, whereas the implementation lives at provider_config_service.py, wiki/links.py, wiki/index.py). The task brief confirms this is now FIXED by tech-writer. Not carried forward.

Note on NB-3: this item is a pre-merge action for the current sprint, not a v0.3 carry-forward. backend-engineer should resolve it before merging sprint/v0.2.

docs.title: 6. Human checkpoint — remaining conditions (EC-M2-17)
docs.text: The following conditions must be satisfied by Emanuele on the TrueNAS / M2 environment before Sprint 3 begins. No code change is required; these are runtime/live verification steps.

docs.title: Condition A — 3-provider live smoke matrix (EC-M2-5 / EC-M2-6)
docs.text:
Environment prerequisites: - OLLAMA_URL set to running Ollama on TrueNAS; model capable of JSON output configured in provider_config (global row, provider_type='local') - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set; ApiProvider global row configured - claude-agent-sdk installed and accessible in PATH; CliAgentProvider global row configured - Postgres 16 running; Alembic migrations applied (alembic upgrade head) - Qdrant running; bge-m3 embedding service reachable (EMBEDDING_URL set)

Run commands: 

# Option 1 — pytest live suite (runs all 3 providers sequentially)
cd backend
pytest tests/test_smoke_providers.py -m live -v

# Option 2 — standalone smoke script (more verbose output)
SYNAPSE_SMOKE_MOCK=0 python scripts/smoke_providers.py

Pass criteria per provider (asserted by the harness): - At least 1 schema-valid WikiPage written to vault/wiki/ with type, title, sources[] all non-empty and lang field present (F3) - vault/wiki/overview.md created or updated (K2/F3) - vault/wiki/index.md contains [[wikilink]] entry for each generated page (K3) - ingest_runs row present with total_cost_usd >= 0.00; Local/CLI = 0.00; API > 0.00 (I7) - Correct routing: Local/API → orchestrated loop; CLI → delegated via MCP tools (F17) - All wikilinks in generated pages have rows in the links table (K5)

docs.title: Condition B — Obsidian vault open (EC-M2-13)
docs.text: After the smoke matrix has produced real LLM-generated pages: 1. Open vault/wiki/ in Obsidian (any recent version). 2. Confirm: no error dialogs on open. 3. Confirm: all generated pages are visible in the file explorer. 4. Confirm: [[wikilinks]] within generated pages resolve to other generated pages (graph view or click-through in reading mode). 5. Confirm: index.md and overview.md open without parse errors.

docs.title: Condition C — Explicit sprint advancement approval
docs.text:
After completing A and B, Emanuele explicitly approves: "Sprint 3 (v0.3 / M3 — 'Graph live, no main-thread freeze') is approved to begin."

This confirmation is the final gate before the orchestrator unlocks Sprint 3 scope.

docs.title: 7. BACKLOG.md update note
docs.text: BACKLOG.md Sprint 2 items are updated to done / done-pending-live-smoke status in the accompanying BACKLOG.md commit. Velocity note is recorded there.

docs.title: 8. Velocity note
docs.text:
v0.2 was estimated at 10–14 solo evening sessions (the highest-complexity sprint in the roadmap per v0.2-scope §11). Delivery included:

Delivered as scoped: all 11 in-scope feature IDs + 5 D-artifact families fully complete.

Early extras: 1 (DELETE /provider/config/{id} endpoint — trivial, no feature ID, no out-of-scope risk). ADRs 0010 and 0011 were additional (beyond the 3 required); both document real decisions made this sprint and count as velocity, not overrun.

Bug found during QA: BUG-v0.2-1 (delegate_ingest return type). Found and fixed within the sprint. Does not count as scope overrun.

Verdict: ON SCOPE. No underrun (all IDs delivered). No overrun (no out-of-scope feature ID shipped). The early extras are micro-additions consistent with clean API design. The two additional ADRs reflect genuine architectural decisions that required documentation.

The mmdc CI render check (AC-D3-3) is the only deferred item that was explicitly listed as "best-effort" in the scope lock §11 velocity note. Carried forward to v0.3 devops tasks.

v0.3 boundary (F4 graph) is INTACT. No graph code shipped.

docs.title: 9. MILESTONE VERDICT
docs.text:
MILESTONE M2: MET-PENDING-HUMAN-CHECKPOINT

Definition: All 17 exit criteria are either fully verified (13/17) or verified at the mock/automated level with a documented live-run condition (4/17 pending-live). Zero exit criteria are NOT MET. Zero invariant violations. All 4 agent gate sign-offs are positive.

The milestone advances to the human checkpoint stage per CLAUDE.md §8 DoD: "green tests + architect review + docs gate + PM exit-criteria sign-off + human checkpoint before next sprint."

The first four are complete. The fifth (Emanuele's confirmation) is gated on the live TrueNAS smoke run described in §6 above.

Sprint 3 (v0.3 / M3 — "Graph live, no main-thread freeze") MUST NOT begin until EC-M2-17 is satisfied.

Remaining conditions for full M2 CLOSED: 1. NB-3: backend-engineer updates openapi.json info.version / info.description before merge. 2. Live smoke matrix (EC-M2-5/6): pytest tests/test_smoke_providers.py -m live -v passes on TrueNAS for all 3 providers. 3. Open vault/wiki/ in Obsidian after real pages generated (EC-M2-13). 4. Emanuele explicitly approves Sprint 3 (EC-M2-17).

Signed: product-manager · 2026-06-28

1. EC-M2-5 / EC-M2-17 — live 3-provider smoke matrix on TrueNAS (Emanuele).
  2. EC-M2-13 — open vault/wiki/ in Obsidian after real pages generated.
  3. EC-M2-15 — architect sign-off on D3/D7/D1u.
  4. EC-M2-16 — tech-writer sign-off on D3/D7/D1u.

Automated milestone gate: SIGNED OFF with the documented deferrals above.

docs.title: Sprint 2 — v0.2 — Scope Lock
docs.text: Milestone: M2 — "Agentic loop closed, 3 providers" Branch: sprint/v0.2 Locked by: product-manager on 2026-06-28 Status: LOCKED — no changes without PM + orchestrator approval Prerequisite: M1 CLOSED (v0.1-m1-closure.md — live demo passed, all EC items confirmed)

docs.title: 1. Sprint objective
docs.text:
Close the agentic inference loop end-to-end: an InferenceProvider is resolved from provider_config, capability-aware routing dispatches ingest to either the orchestrated loop (Local/API) or a delegated agent (CLI), and the wiki pages produced are schema-valid and written to vault/wiki/. The MCP standalone server becomes the programmatic interface used by the CLI provider during its own agent loop. Sequence diagrams (D3) and the first formally-required ADRs (D7) document every decision made this sprint.

v0.2 makes the FIRST real LLM inference calls in Synapse. Cost tracking (I7) is therefore a first-class acceptance gate — not a nice-to-have.

docs.title: 2. In-scope feature IDs
docs.text: Feature ID What is in scope for v0.2 Why needed for M2 F17 InferenceProvider ABC (base.py); OllamaProvider (ollama.py); ApiProvider (api.py — Anthropic + OpenAI-compat configurable base_url); CliAgentProvider (cli.py — claude-agent-sdk, permission_mode='acceptEdits'); provider_config Postgres table (scope: global / vault; per-operation override); capability-aware routing in orchestrator (capabilities() → branch); chat() method STUBBED on all 3 backends (see §5 decision) The defining feature of the sprint and of the whole project. I6 is the primary invariant in force. Without F17, nothing else in M2 is buildable. F3 Two-step CoT ingest — analyze(source_text, vault_context) → Analysis + generate(analysis, retrieval_context) → list[WikiPage]; source traceability (sources[] populated from original frontmatter and LLM reasoning); auto-generation of vault/wiki/overview.md after first ingest into a new vault; language-aware generation (detect source language, generate wiki page in same language) This is what the loop PRODUCES. Without F3, the InferenceProvider has no operations to call. Orchestrated loop for Local/API; delegated for CLI. K3 vault/wiki/index.md catalogue auto-maintained by the ingest loop: on each successful page write, index.md is updated (appended entry or regenerated section). LLM navigation entry-point. index.md is required by F3's auto-overview and by the MCP search tool. Partially exists as a stub from v0.1; v0.2 makes it live-updated by the loop. K5 [[wikilink]] dedicated parser: extract wikilinks from generated wiki pages; store as link-records in Postgres (pages → links table); validate that link targets exist (warn if dangling, do not error). No graph computation yet (F4 is v0.3), but the parser and link persistence layer must exist so graph edges have a source of truth in v0.2. F3's generate step produces wikilinks. Without the parser, links cannot be stored or validated. The graph (v0.3) will consume the links table. Deferring K5 past v0.2 would require schema migration mid-sprint-3. K2 (ingest op) Full ingest orchestrator in backend/app/ingest/orchestrator.py: (1) resolve provider from provider_config; (2) call capabilities(); (3) if supports_agentic_loop == True → delegate to CLI provider; otherwise → run orchestrated loop (analyze → generate → validate → if invalid augment & retry); bounded by I7 (max_iter=3, token_budget=60 000 tokens per run — see §6); log total_cost_usd per run. Watcher and POST /ingest call orchestrator exclusively. The M2 milestone title IS the ingest loop. This is the spine that wires F17 + F3 together. MCP server backend/app/mcp/server.py — FastMCP standalone server. Tools exposed (minimum): search_wiki(query) → list[PageRef], write_page(title, content, frontmatter) → PageRef, get_page(title) → Page, list_pages(type?) → list[PageRef]. Used by CliAgentProvider during its delegated ingest: the CLI agent calls these MCP tools to read/write vault/wiki/. CliAgentProvider requires MCP tools scoped to the vault. Without the MCP server, CLI delegation cannot write pages. Also the D4 artifact grows to include MCP tool schemas. D3 Sequence diagrams in docs/sequences/: (1) ingest-routing.mmd — full flow from watcher event to page written, including both branches (orchestrated loop vs. CLI delegation); (2) ingest-loop.mmd — detailed analyze → generate → validate → retry with I7 bounds annotated. Mermaid sequenceDiagram format. Required by CLAUDE.md §9 from v0.2. I8 mandates these before M2 sign-off. D7 Architecture Decision Records in docs/adr/. Required for v0.2: ADR-0007: InferenceProvider ABC design (why ABC, why 3 backends, capability-aware routing choice); ADR-0008: provider_config schema (global/vault/per-operation scope model, why Postgres not file-based); ADR-0009: bounded ingest loop defaults (why max_iter=3, why 60K token budget, how total_cost_usd is computed). Additional ADRs as needed for decisions made this sprint. Required by CLAUDE.md §9 "continuous from v0.2". First formally mandated sprint for D7. D1 (update) C4 component diagram (docs/architecture/component.mmd): add InferenceProvider layer (3 backends), orchestrator branching, MCP server, provider_config table. Context and container diagrams updated to reflect new containers/relationships. The provider layer is a major architectural addition; D1 must be current for I8 docs gate. D2 (update) ER diagram updated via make er: add provider_config table, links table (K5 wikilink records). Must match live Postgres schema. Schema changes in v0.2 require D2 update per I8. D4 (update) OpenAPI JSON updated: add new endpoints (POST /ingest replacing or extending the stub, GET /provider/config, POST /provider/config); add MCP tool schemas to docs/api/mcp-tools.json. Fix the deferred carry-forward item from v0.1 (202 schema for POST /ingest/trigger). Continuous per §9. MCP server is new in v0.2.

docs.title: K3 / K5 scope decision
docs.text:
Both K3 and K5 are FULLY in scope for v0.2, not partially.

Rationale: K3 is a direct output requirement of F3 — the ingest loop must write to index.md. Deferring it would leave a core Karpathy feature (the LLM navigation entry-point) as a stub while the loop runs. K5 is required to persist the wikilinks the generate step produces, and deferring the links table to v0.3 would require an Alembic migration mid-graph sprint. Bringing both in now is the correct call for a solo evening dev — one schema change event, not two.

docs.title: 3. Tempting-but-out-of-scope feature IDs
docs.text:
Any engineer proposing one of the items below must escalate to orchestrator before touching it. The PM will block and flag scope creep.

Feature ID Why tempting Why deferred Target sprint F4 — Knowledge graph v0.2 creates the links table (K5), so the graph "could" be built F4 requires FA2 + igraph + sigma.js viewer + I2 cache — a full sprint of work. v0.2's scope is already the largest single sprint in the roadmap. v0.3 F5 — 4-phase RAG retrieval MCP search tool looks like retrieval; provider has chat() method RAG requires graph expansion (F4) and is wired to chat (F6). The MCP search_wiki tool is a simple keyword/vector lookup, not the full 4-phase pipeline. v0.5 F1 — 3-panel UI Provider Selector UI is part of F17 Provider Selector is the FRONTEND component of F17. F17 backend is v0.2; F17 UI is v0.4. No frontend code in v0.2. v0.4 F6 — Multi-conversation chat chat() is on the ABC The chat() ABC method is STUBBED in v0.2 (see §5). The full chat endpoint, conversation persistence, cited-refs, regenerate, save-to-wiki — all v0.4. v0.4 F7 — think display Streaming is needed Depends on F6 chat UI. v0.4 F8 — LaTeX to Unicode Small feature, could be added Depends on F6 chat. v0.4 F14 — Configurable context window Provider has max_context in capabilities() The context budget fields are in the ABC but the UI configurator and per-operation override UI are v0.4. v0.2 uses the default token_budget defined in §6. v0.4 F9 — HITL review queue Post-ingest validation could queue to review ops/review.py is v0.5 work. v0.2 validates generated pages against schema and retries; it does NOT route to a human queue yet. v0.5 F10 — Deep Research SearXNG is running, tempting to wire now ops/deep_research.py is explicitly v0.5. The MCP server's search tool is local wiki search only, not SearXNG. v0.5 F2 — purpose.md injection Provider context is wired this sprint purpose.md is read by the orchestrator as vault_context input to analyze() — a MINIMAL read is acceptable in v0.2 to supply context. BUT the full F2 feature (structured injection, key-questions, thesis extraction into context) is v0.6 work. A plain file-read of purpose.md as raw string is permitted in v0.2 without counting as F2 delivered. v0.6 F12 — Multi-format ingest One could add PDF support to the orchestrator Markdown .md only in v0.2. F12 is v0.5. v0.5 F13 — Cascade deletion links table (K5) is being built Requires graph + wikilink resolution. v0.5. v0.5 F11 — Chrome clipper External client v0.6. v0.6 F15 — CI/CD pipeline A CI stage was noted as a carry-forward from v0.1 Add docker build CI stage (noted in v0.1-m1-closure process lessons) is IN SCOPE as a devops task, but full F15 (PWA + Tauri) is v0.6. The CI stage is a devops sub-task, not a new feature ID. CI stage: v0.2 devops sub-task; full F15: v0.6 F16 (rest) Only dataVersion partial was in v0.1 i18n, settings UI, GFM, multi-provider chat timeout — all v0.4. The provider_config persistence (in scope for F17 v0.2) is the ONLY F16-adjacent work permitted, and it is credited to F17, not F16. v0.4 D5 — UI screenshots No UI v0.3. v0.3 D6 — User/Deploy guides DEPLOY.md draft already exists D6 complete in v0.4. The v0.1 DEPLOY.md draft remains DRAFT-tagged. v0.4

docs.title: 4. Invariants in force — with emphasis on v0.2 firsts
docs.text: All 9 invariants apply. The two that are first-class acceptance gates this sprint:

docs.title: I6 — PLUGGABLE INFERENCE (the whole point of v0.2)
docs.text:
Every inference call — analyze(), generate(), chat() (stubbed) — MUST go through InferenceProvider. No model name, no HTTP endpoint, no API key appears outside the provider module files (ollama.py, api.py, cli.py). The orchestrator knows only the ABC interface and capabilities() output.

Enforcement: static analysis test asserting zero direct Ollama/Anthropic/SDK imports outside backend/app/ingest/provider/. Architect sign-off required. Any PR that hardcodes a provider or bypasses the ABC is rejected without discussion.

docs.title: I7 — BOUNDED LOOPS (first real loops exist in v0.2)
docs.text:
Every loop introduced this sprint (orchestrated ingest refinement, provider fallback) MUST have: - A max_iter cap (see §6 for defaults) - A token_budget cap (see §6 for defaults) - A total_cost_usd log entry on completion (even if $0.00 for Local)

The provider fallback loop is bound to exactly 1 retry (primary fail/timeout → fallback once → surface error). No recursive fallback chains.

pytest must include a test that mocks a non-converging provider (always returns invalid pages) and asserts the loop stops at max_iter without overrun.

docs.title: Remaining invariants — status in v0.2
docs.text: Invariant Status in v0.2 I1 — Incremental index only Inherited from v0.1; v0.2 must not break it. The orchestrator's write_page primitive writes to wiki/ only, never rescans. I2 — Graph layout server-side Not exercised yet (F4 is v0.3). No graph code introduced. The links table added in K5 is data-only. I3 — No per-token heavy work Not exercised (no chat UI in v0.2). The chat() stub returns a simple stream placeholder. I4 — CodeMirror 6 only Not exercised (no frontend in v0.2). I5 — Obsidian compatibility All wiki pages written by the loop must have valid YAML frontmatter (type, title, sources[]). Run the AC-K7-3 manual open check again after v0.2 ingest produces pages. I8 — Docs-as-DoD D3 + D7 are first-required this sprint. D1 + D2 + D4 must be updated. All required before M2 sign-off. I9 — Do not reinvent OllamaProvider reuses the already-running Ollama instance. No new inference containers. SearXNG NOT used in v0.2 (F10 deferred). bge-m3 embeddings unchanged.

docs.title: 5. chat() method scope decision
docs.text:
DECISION: chat() is IMPLEMENTED AS A STUB on all 3 backends in v0.2. It is NOT skipped from the ABC.

Rationale: - The ABC signature chat(messages, retrieval_context) → stream is a structural part of F17 and must be locked in v0.2 so the interface is complete and type-checked. - Omitting chat() from the ABC now means a breaking change at v0.4 when it is needed. - A stub that raises NotImplementedError at call time is sufficient and keeps the ABC complete. No chat endpoint, no chat UI, no conversation logic in v0.2. - The stub is tested: a unit test asserts the method exists on all 3 concrete providers and raises NotImplementedError when called (confirming it is wired but intentionally incomplete, not accidentally missing).

The full chat implementation (F6, streaming, cited-refs, conversation persistence) is v0.4.

docs.title: 6. Cost tracking and bounded-loop defaults (I7 compliance)
docs.text: v0.1 cost: $0.00 (no LLM calls). v0.2 introduces real inference spend. I7 requires total_cost_usd logged per run and per bounded loop.

docs.title: Defaults (proposal — architect must ratify in ADR-0009)
docs.text:
Parameter Default value Rationale max_iter (orchestrated ingest loop) 3 Analyze → generate → validate: 1 pass is the happy path. 2 retries covers malformed JSON or missing frontmatter fields. Beyond 3 is a provider quality problem, not a retry problem. token_budget (per-run, orchestrated loop) 60 000 tokens Covers a typical source doc analysis (5–10K) + generation of 3–5 wiki pages (20–30K) + 2 retries (×2 = headroom). Well within Anthropic API context windows. Tunable via provider_config. token_budget (CLI / delegated) 100 000 tokens CLI provider runs its own agent loop; higher headroom appropriate for agentic browsing + writing. provider_fallback_max 1 Primary fails → try fallback provider once → surface error. No chained fallbacks. total_cost_usd computation (input_tokens × input_price + output_tokens × output_price) per model from provider_config; Local (Ollama) = $0.00 always Price per model stored in provider_config; updated by user. Default seeded for current model IDs per CLAUDE.md §12. Cost anomaly flag threshold $1.00 per single ingest run Log WARNING if total_cost_usd > 1.00 for a single document ingest.

These defaults are configurable via provider_config.max_iter and provider_config.token_budget (per-vault or per-operation override). The hardcoded fallback is that if provider_config is absent, the values above are used.

docs.title: Acceptance criterion for I7
docs.text:
AC-I7-1: Every call to orchestrator.ingest_file() produces a log entry with keys: provider_name, max_iter_used, total_tokens, total_cost_usd, converged (bool).

AC-I7-2: A pytest test mocks a provider whose generate() always returns pages with missing required frontmatter. Assert the loop stops after exactly max_iter=3 iterations with converged=False. Assert no further provider calls are made after the cap.

AC-I7-3: For the CLI provider (delegated path), the token_budget is passed to the claude-agent-sdk invocation and the run is aborted if the budget is exceeded. Log the actual tokens used and total_cost_usd = 0.00 (CLI provider uses build-time agent credits, not runtime billing — document in ADR-0009).

docs.title: 7. M2 Exit Criteria (concrete, checkable)
docs.text: All items must be MET before PM sign-off and before advancing to Sprint 3.

docs.title: EC-M2-1 (F17 — ABC + 3 backends): Provider layer complete
docs.text:
• backend/app/ingest/provider/base.py defines InferenceProvider ABC with methods: analyze(), generate(), chat() (stub), capabilities(). All methods type-annotated (mypy strict). Abstract methods raise NotImplementedError in any non-implementing class.
  • ollama.py, api.py, cli.py each implement the full ABC (chat() stub included).
  • Static analysis test: zero direct Ollama/Anthropic/SDK imports outside provider/ directory.
  • Unit test: capabilities() returns correct {mode, supports_tools, supports_agentic_loop, max_context, name} dict for each provider; OllamaProvider.supports_agentic_loop == False; CliAgentProvider.supports_agentic_loop == True.

docs.title: EC-M2-2 (F17 — provider_config): Config table and resolution
docs.text:
• Postgres provider_config table exists (Alembic migration); columns: id, scope (global/vault/operation), vault_id (nullable), provider_type (local/api/cli), model_id, base_url (nullable), max_iter, token_budget, created_at, updated_at.
  • GET /provider/config and POST /provider/config endpoints operational and documented in OpenAPI.
  • Config resolution order: operation-scope > vault-scope > global-scope.
  • Unit test: resolution order verified with 3 conflicting rows in the test DB.

docs.title: EC-M2-3 (F17 — capability routing): Correct branch taken
docs.text:
• Integration test with OllamaProvider: supports_agentic_loop == False → orchestrator runs the analyze → generate → validate → retry loop.
  • Integration test with CliAgentProvider (mocked): supports_agentic_loop == True → orchestrator delegates; the CLI agent's ingest is invoked without the orchestrated loop body executing.
  • Static assertion: the routing branch reads capabilities() output — it does NOT check provider type string or class name.

docs.title: EC-M2-4 (I7 — bounded loop): Loop stops at max_iter
docs.text:
• pytest: mock provider always returns pages with invalid frontmatter (missing type field).
  • Assert: loop runs exactly 3 times (= max_iter default) then exits with converged=False.
  • Assert: no provider call is made after iteration 3.
  • Assert: log entry for the run includes total_cost_usd (any value, including 0.00) and converged=False.

docs.title: EC-M2-5 (F3 — 3-provider smoke matrix): Each provider ingests a fixture
docs.text:
Using a canonical fixture file (tests/fixtures/sample-source.md, a ~500-word Markdown document with valid frontmatter):

Provider Ingest outcome Pass condition Local (OllamaProvider) analyze() + generate() run; pages written to wiki/ At least 1 page in Postgres with sources[] populated; valid YAML frontmatter; [[wikilinks]] parseable by K5 parser API (ApiProvider — Anthropic) Same Same CLI (CliAgentProvider) Delegated to claude-agent-sdk agent At least 1 page in wiki/; page passes schema validation; index.md updated

All 3 providers must pass this smoke matrix on live infrastructure (TrueNAS / API key / claude-agent-sdk). This is a live-infra test, not a mocked test. Human checkpoint at M2 confirms the matrix was run.

docs.title: EC-M2-6 (F3 — content quality): Generated pages are schema-valid
docs.text:
• All pages written to vault/wiki/ have YAML frontmatter with type, title, sources[] present and non-empty (I5 + K6).
  • At least one [[wikilink]] per generated page (except single-topic sources may have zero — acceptable; warn in log, do not error).
  • The K5 parser extracts all wikilinks from generated pages without exception.
  • vault/wiki/index.md is updated with the new page entry after ingest.
  • vault/wiki/overview.md is created (or updated) after first ingest into the vault.
  • vault/wiki/ opens in Obsidian without errors after v0.2 ingest (human checkpoint, re-runs AC-K7-3 with actual LLM-generated content).

docs.title: EC-M2-7 (MCP server): Tools exposed and callable
docs.text:
• backend/app/mcp/server.py is a FastMCP standalone server.
  • Tools registered: search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages.
  • MCP server starts as a separate process / via make mcp and exposes tools via stdio (for CliAgentProvider) and HTTP (for future clients).
  • Integration test: call each of the 4 MCP tools via the FastMCP test client; assert correct return types and Postgres/filesystem state changes.
  • MCP tool schemas documented in docs/api/mcp-tools.json.

docs.title: EC-M2-8 (K5 — wikilink parser): Links stored in Postgres
docs.text:
• backend/app/ingest/parser/wikilinks.py parses [[Target Page|alias]] and [[Target Page]] syntax from generated wiki pages.
  • Postgres links table (Alembic migration): columns: id, source_page_id, target_title, alias (nullable), created_at.
  • After ingest, all wikilinks in generated pages have corresponding rows in the links table.
  • Dangling wikilinks (target page does not exist yet) are stored but flagged (dangling=True column); they do NOT cause an error.
  • Unit tests for parser: valid link, aliased link, multiple links, no links, nested brackets.

docs.title: EC-M2-9 (K3 — index.md): Catalogue auto-updated
docs.text:
• After every successful page write, vault/wiki/index.md is updated with an entry for the new page (title + wikilink + type).
  • index.md retains valid YAML frontmatter (I5).
  • If index.md is missing, it is recreated (not an error).
  • Unit test: ingest fixture → assert index.md contains wikilink to new page.

docs.title: EC-M2-10 (D3 — docs gate): Sequence diagrams present
docs.text:
• docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd — Mermaid sequenceDiagram: watcher event → orchestrator → resolve provider → capabilities() → branch (orchestrated loop path / CLI delegation path) → pages written / index.md updated. Both branches shown.
  • docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd — Mermaid sequenceDiagram: detailed orchestrated loop — analyze → generate → validate → [converged? yes: write; no: augment & retry, max_iter annotated] → log cost.
  • Both diagrams render without error (CI: mmdc render check).
  • Reviewed and approved by tech-writer.

docs.title: EC-M2-11 (D7 — docs gate): Required ADRs present
docs.text:
• docs/adr/ADR-0007-inference-provider-abc.md: InferenceProvider ABC design rationale.
  • docs/adr/ADR-0008-provider-config-schema.md: provider_config table scope model.
  • docs/adr/ADR-0009-bounded-loop-defaults.md: max_iter=3, token_budget defaults, cost logging.
  • Additional ADRs for any other arch decisions made this sprint (e.g., FastMCP choice, links table schema, wikilink parser approach).
  • Reviewed and approved by solution-architect.

docs.title: EC-M2-12 (D1/D2/D4 — docs gate): Existing artifacts updated
docs.text:
• D1: docs/architecture/component.mmd added; context.mmd + container.mmd updated to show InferenceProvider layer and MCP server.
  • D2: docs/er/schema.mmd regenerated via make er; reflects provider_config + links tables. ER matches live Postgres schema.
  • D4: docs/api/openapi.json regenerated; includes new provider config endpoints and fixes the 202 schema carry-forward from v0.1. docs/api/mcp-tools.json added.

docs.title: EC-M2-13 (I5 — Obsidian still valid): vault/wiki/ post-ingest
docs.text:
• After the smoke matrix (EC-M2-5) produces real pages, open vault/wiki/ in Obsidian.
  • No errors, all pages visible, [[wikilinks]] resolve correctly between generated pages.
  • This is the manual human checkpoint for I5 with real LLM-generated content.

docs.title: EC-M2-14 (QA gate):
docs.text:
• qa-test-engineer has run full test suite (unit + integration + smoke matrix); signed off green.
  • No open P0 or P1 bugs.
  • I7 bounded-loop test (EC-M2-4) passes.
  • I6 static analysis test (EC-M2-1) passes.

docs.title: EC-M2-15 (Architect gate):
docs.text:
• solution-architect has reviewed: base.py, orchestrator.py routing logic, provider_config schema, MCP server, K5 links table.
  • No invariant violations identified.
  • ADR-0007/0008/0009 approved.

docs.title: EC-M2-16 (Tech-writer gate):
docs.text:
• tech-writer has verified D3 (both sequence diagrams), D7 (all required ADRs), D1/D2/D4 updates are complete, internally consistent, and no diagrams are stale.
  • Signed off.

docs.title: EC-M2-17 (Human checkpoint):
docs.text:
• Human (Emanuele) has observed the 3-provider smoke matrix (EC-M2-5) running live.
  • Has confirmed vault/wiki/ opens in Obsidian with generated pages (EC-M2-13).
  • Explicitly approves advancement to Sprint 3 (v0.3 / M3 — "Graph live").

docs.title: 8. Required D-artifacts for the v0.2 docs gate
docs.text: Artifact Path How generated Gate requirement New in v0.2? D1 (updated) docs/architecture/context.mmd, container.mmd, component.mmd (NEW) Hand-authored Mermaid C4 Architect + tech-writer sign-off component.mmd is new D2 (updated) docs/er/schema.mmd make er from models.py Must match live schema; architect + tech-writer sign-off Updated: +provider_config, +links D3 (NEW) docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd, docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd Hand-authored Mermaid sequenceDiagram Tech-writer sign-off; mmdc render CI check YES — first sprint for D3 D4 (updated) docs/api/openapi.json, docs/api/mcp-tools.json (NEW) make openapi + FastMCP schema export Tech-writer sign-off; all new endpoints documented mcp-tools.json is new D7 (NEW) docs/adr/ADR-0007, ADR-0008, ADR-0009 + any additional Markdown, one file per decision Architect sign-off YES — first formally required sprint for D7

docs.text: Artifact First required sprint D5 — UI screenshots v0.3 D6 — User/Deploy guides (complete) v0.4 (DEPLOY.md draft already exists, remains DRAFT)

docs.title: 9. Carry-forward items from v0.1 addressed in v0.2
docs.text:
Per v0.1-pm-signoff §6 (non-blocking carry-forward):

Item Action in v0.2 OpenAPI 202 schema for POST /ingest/trigger Fix in v0.2 when extending the ingest endpoint; add response_model=IngestTriggerResponse, status_code=202 CI mmdc render check Add to CI pipeline alongside the docker build CI stage (devops sub-task) provider_config table Core v0.2 delivery (F17)

docs.title: 10. Sign-off register (to be completed at sprint end)
docs.text:
Gate Owner Status Date QA green qa-test-engineer PENDING — Architect review solution-architect PENDING — Tech-writer docs gate tech-writer PENDING — PM exit-criteria verdict product-manager PENDING — Human checkpoint Emanuele PENDING —

All 5 must be MET before sprint/v0.2 is merged and Sprint 3 begins.

docs.title: 11. Velocity note
docs.text:
v0.2 is the highest-complexity sprint in the roadmap: - First real LLM inference (introduces live cost dimension) - 3 distinct backends, each with its own ingest strategy - New Postgres tables (provider_config, links) - MCP standalone server - First D3 and D7 artifacts

For a solo evening developer, this is estimated at 10–14 evening sessions. If scope pressure develops, the FIRST candidates for partial deferral (with architect approval) are: 1. ApiProvider's OpenAI-compatible base_url variant (the Anthropic variant is P0; OpenAI-compat could slip to v0.3 if the ABC is designed for it) 2.
