The CI mmdc render check (keep it as a best-effort if pipeline time is a constraint)

The smoke matrix (EC-M2-5) with all 3 live providers is NON-NEGOTIABLE for M2 sign-off. That is the milestone's definition.

docs.title: Sprint 2 — v0.2 — User Stories and Acceptance Criteria
docs.text: Produced by: functional-analyst Sprint: v0.2 (M2 — "Agentic loop closed, 3 providers") Date: 2026-06-28 Source ACs: BACKLOG.md § Sprint 2 AC-F17-1..8, AC-K2-1..8, AC-F3-1..7, AC-K5-1..7, AC-K3-1..6, AC-MCP-1..8, AC-D3-1..4, AC-D7-1..5, AC-D1u-1..2, AC-D2u-1, AC-D4u-1..2 Exit criteria covered: EC-M2-1 through EC-M2-17 (v0.2-scope.md § 7) Invariants touched with heightened priority: I6 (pluggable inference), I7 (bounded loops) All 9 invariants apply; I1/I5/I8 verified again on real LLM-generated content this sprint. Prerequisite: M1 CLOSED — v0.1-m1-closure.md confirmed; F17 seam exists at backend/app/ingest/orchestrator.py

docs.title: Roles used in stories
docs.text: Role Who vault owner Emanuele — selects providers, drops files, reads wiki results the Synapse ingest orchestrator backend/app/ingest/orchestrator.py — dispatches work, runs loops a connected MCP client Claude Desktop / Claude Code connecting to the FastMCP server downstream sprint A future sprint (v0.3+) or agent that depends on this sprint's outputs

docs.title: US-F17 — InferenceProvider ABC and 3 backends
docs.text:
Feature ID: F17 Story: As the Synapse ingest orchestrator, I want every inference call to go through a single InferenceProvider abstract base class with three concrete backends (Local/Ollama, API/Anthropic, CLI/claude-agent-sdk), selected at runtime from provider_config, so that no model name, API key, or endpoint URL ever appears outside the provider module files and the provider can be swapped per vault or per operation without touching orchestrator logic (invariant I6).

Invariants touched: I6 (defining invariant — pluggable inference; no hardcoding anywhere), I7 (loops introduced by providers must be bounded — first real inference cost this sprint), I8 (D1 component diagram now must show the provider layer)

docs.title: AC-F17-1 — InferenceProvider ABC signature and mypy strict compliance
docs.text:
Given backend/app/ingest/provider/base.py has been authored, When mypy --strict backend/app/ingest/provider/base.py is run, Then the command exits 0 with no errors, and the file defines an abstract class InferenceProvider that declares the following abstract methods with exact signatures:

def analyze(self, source_text: str, vault_context: str) -> Analysis: ...
def generate(self, analysis: Analysis, retrieval_context: str) -> list[WikiPage]: ...
def chat(self, messages: list[Message], retrieval_context: str) -> AsyncIterator[str]: ...
def capabilities(self) -> ProviderCapabilities: ...

where Analysis, WikiPage, Message, and ProviderCapabilities are Pydantic models defined in the same or an adjacent module, and all four methods are decorated with @abstractmethod.

Testable assert:

import inspect, abc
from app.ingest.provider.base import InferenceProvider
abstract_methods = {
    name for name, val in inspect.getmembers(InferenceProvider)
    if getattr(val, "__isabstractmethod__", False)
}
assert abstract_methods == {"analyze", "generate", "chat", "capabilities"}

docs.title: AC-F17-2 — All 3 providers implement the ABC; chat() raises NotImplementedError
docs.text:
Given ollama.py, api.py, and cli.py each define a concrete class inheriting from InferenceProvider, When Python attempts to instantiate each class, Then no TypeError is raised (all abstract methods are implemented), and calling chat() on any of the three instances raises NotImplementedError immediately without making any external HTTP or subprocess call.

Testable asserts:

# For each provider class OllamaProvider, ApiProvider, CliAgentProvider:
provider = OllamaProvider(config=mock_config)
# Instantiation must not raise TypeError
import pytest
with pytest.raises(NotImplementedError):
    import asyncio
    asyncio.run(provider.chat(messages=[], retrieval_context=""))

The test must run against all three classes (parametrised or explicit); all three must pass.

docs.title: AC-F17-3 — capabilities() returns correct dict for each provider
docs.text:
Given instances of OllamaProvider, ApiProvider, and CliAgentProvider are constructed with minimal valid config, When capabilities() is called on each, Then each returns a ProviderCapabilities object (or dict) that satisfies:

Provider mode supports_agentic_loop supports_tools OllamaProvider "local" False model-dependent (not required True) ApiProvider "api" False True (Anthropic tool-use) CliAgentProvider "cli" True True

And all three results contain the keys mode, supports_tools, supports_agentic_loop, max_context, and name (non-empty string).

Testable assert:

caps = OllamaProvider(config).capabilities()
assert caps.supports_agentic_loop is False
assert caps.mode == "local"
assert isinstance(caps.name, str) and len(caps.name) > 0

caps = CliAgentProvider(config).capabilities()
assert caps.supports_agentic_loop is True
assert caps.mode == "cli"

docs.title: AC-F17-4 — Static analysis: zero provider imports outside provider/
docs.text:
Given the entire backend/app/ codebase (excluding backend/app/ingest/provider/), When a static grep is run for direct imports of Ollama, Anthropic, or claude-agent-sdk symbols, and for any hardcoded model IDs, API keys, or endpoint URLs, Then zero matches are found outside backend/app/ingest/provider/.

Testable assert (CI-enforced):

import subprocess, re
result = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rn",
     r"import ollama\|import anthropic\|from anthropic\|claude-agent-sdk\|claude-opus-4\|claude-sonnet-4\|sk-ant-",
     "backend/app/"],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)
# Filter out provider/ directory matches
non_provider_lines = [
    l for l in result.stdout.splitlines()
    if "ingest/provider/" not in l
]
assert non_provider_lines == [], f"Hardcoded provider references found: {non_provider_lines}"

This test must be in test_code_quality.py and must run on every CI push (I6 enforcement).

docs.title: AC-F17-5 — provider_config table exists after Alembic migration
docs.text:
Given backend/app/models.py defines the ProviderConfig SQLAlchemy model and an Alembic migration has been generated and applied, When information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'provider_config' is queried, Then the table exists and contains at minimum the following columns with compatible types:

Column Type id uuid, primary key scope varchar/text — values: 'global', 'vault', 'operation' vault_id uuid, nullable provider_type varchar/text — values: 'local', 'api', 'cli' model_id varchar/text, not null base_url varchar/text, nullable max_iter integer, not null, default 3 token_budget integer, not null created_at timestamp with time zone, not null updated_at timestamp with time zone, not null

Testable assert: introspect via SQLAlchemy inspect(engine).get_columns("provider_config") and assert all column names and nullable flags above.

docs.title: AC-F17-6 — Config resolution order: operation > vault > global
docs.text:
Given the provider_config table contains three rows: - Row A: scope='global', vault_id=NULL, model_id='model-global' - Row B: scope='vault', vault_id=, model_id='model-vault' - Row C: scope='operation', vault_id=, operation='ingest', model_id='model-operation'

When the config resolver is called with (vault_id=, operation='ingest'), Then it returns the config from Row C (operation scope wins).

And when it is called with (vault_id=, operation=None), Then it returns the config from Row B (vault scope wins when no operation override).

And when it is called with (vault_id=, operation=None), Then it returns the config from Row A (global fallback).

Testable assert (unit test with 3 conflicting rows in a test DB):

cfg = resolver.resolve(vault_id=test_vault_id, operation="ingest")
assert cfg.model_id == "model-operation"

cfg = resolver.resolve(vault_id=test_vault_id, operation=None)
assert cfg.model_id == "model-vault"

cfg = resolver.resolve(vault_id="unknown", operation=None)
assert cfg.model_id == "model-global"

docs.title: AC-F17-7 — Provider config REST endpoints operational and in OpenAPI
docs.text:
Given the FastAPI service is running with provider config endpoints added, When GET /provider/config is called with optional vault_id and operation query params, Then the response is HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing the resolved config (scope, provider_type, model_id, max_iter, token_budget).

And when POST /provider/config is called with a valid body, Then the response is HTTP 201 and the new config row appears in the database on subsequent GET /provider/config calls.

And when docs/api/openapi.json is inspected, Then both /provider/config paths (GET and POST) are present with correct request/response schemas.

Testable asserts:

resp = client.get("/provider/config", params={"scope": "global"})
assert resp.status_code == 200
assert "provider_type" in resp.json()

resp = client.post("/provider/config", json={
    "scope": "global", "provider_type": "local",
    "model_id": "llama3", "max_iter": 3, "token_budget": 60000
})
assert resp.status_code == 201

spec = json.loads(Path("docs/api/openapi.json").read_text())
assert "/provider/config" in spec["paths"]

docs.title: AC-F17-8 — No hardcoded model IDs anywhere in codebase
docs.text:
Given the full backend/ codebase, When a grep is run for known model ID strings (claude-opus-4-8, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5, llama3, mistral, gpt-4) outside of: - test fixture files under backend/tests/fixtures/ - provider_config seeding migration (Alembic version file) - CLAUDE.md and documentation files

Then zero matches are found in application source code (backend/app/).

Testable assert (CI-enforced, extends AC-F17-4 grep):

result = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-rn", r"claude-opus-4\|claude-sonnet-4\|claude-haiku",
     "backend/app/"],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)
assert result.stdout.strip() == "", f"Hardcoded model IDs found: {result.stdout}"

Model IDs must only appear in provider_config rows (seeded via Alembic data migration using values from CLAUDE.md §12, never as string literals in source code).

docs.title: US-K2-INGEST — Orchestrated ingest loop with capability-aware routing
docs.text:
Feature ID: K2 (ingest operation — full implementation) Story: As the Synapse ingest orchestrator, I want to resolve an InferenceProvider from provider_config, inspect its capabilities(), and route ingest to either the orchestrated analyze-generate-validate-retry loop (for Local/API providers) or a delegated agent loop (for CLI provider), so that wiki pages are produced by the most appropriate strategy for the selected provider without any conditional logic that inspects the provider's class name or type string (invariant I6).

Invariants touched: I6 (routing reads capabilities(), never class name / isinstance), I7 (orchestrated loop is bounded: max_iter=3, token_budget=60000; cost logged per run), I1 (each page write is an incremental upsert, never a rescan), I5 (generated pages must pass Obsidian validity: YAML frontmatter present)

docs.title: AC-K2-1 — OllamaProvider ingest produces valid wiki page
docs.text:
Given a test vault with provider_config set to OllamaProvider and the fixture file backend/tests/fixtures/sample-source.md (a ~500-word Markdown document with YAML frontmatter containing type, title, sources[]), When orchestrator.ingest_file("vault/raw/sources/sample-source.md") is called, Then at least one .md file appears in vault/wiki/ within 60 seconds, and the Postgres pages table contains a corresponding row with all of the following: - type is one of: entity, concept, source, synthesis, comparison (non-null) - title is a non-empty string - sources is a non-empty JSONB array containing at minimum the fixture file's relative path

Testable assert:

page = db.query(Page).filter(Page.title.isnot(None)).first()
assert page.type in {"entity", "concept", "source", "synthesis", "comparison"}
assert len(page.sources) >= 1
wiki_files = list(Path("vault/wiki").glob("*.md"))
assert len(wiki_files) >= 2  # at minimum 1 generated page + index.md

docs.title: AC-K2-2 — ApiProvider (Anthropic) ingest produces valid wiki page
docs.text:
Given the same fixture file as AC-K2-1, and provider_config set to ApiProvider with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set in environment, When orchestrator.ingest_file("vault/raw/sources/sample-source.md") is called, Then the same outcome as AC-K2-1: at least 1 page in Postgres with type, title, sources[] all non-null; at least 1 file in vault/wiki/ beyond seed files.

Additionally: sources[] in the generated page's frontmatter contains the originating fixture file path (F3 source traceability).

This is a live-infra test (requires real Anthropic API key). Marked @pytest.mark.live and run as part of EC-M2-5 smoke matrix only.

docs.title: AC-K2-3 — CliAgentProvider executes delegated path; orchestrated loop body does not run
docs.text:
Given provider_config set to CliAgentProvider and the fixture file, When orchestrator.ingest_file(...) is called, Then: 1. The orchestrated loop body (the analyze→generate→validate→retry sequence) does NOT execute — verified by asserting that provider.analyze() and provider.generate() are never called on the CLI provider instance. 2. The CLI agent writes at least 1 page to vault/wiki/ via the write_page MCP tool. 3. index.md is updated.

Testable assert (using spy/mock on the CliAgentProvider instance):

with patch.object(cli_provider, "analyze") as mock_analyze, \
     patch.object(cli_provider, "generate") as mock_generate:
    orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_path)
    mock_analyze.assert_not_called()
    mock_generate.assert_not_called()
pages_in_wiki = [p for p in Path("vault/wiki").glob("*.md")
                 if p.name not in {"index.md", "log.md", "overview.md"}]
assert len(pages_in_wiki) >= 1

docs.title: AC-K2-4 — Routing reads capabilities(), never provider class name or type string
docs.text:
Given a custom test provider class CustomAgentic that is NOT CliAgentProvider but returns capabilities().supports_agentic_loop = True, When provider_config is set to use this custom provider and orchestrator.ingest_file() is called, Then the orchestrator takes the delegated path (does not run the orchestrated loop), demonstrating that the routing branch is driven purely by the supports_agentic_loop capability flag.

Additionally, static analysis of orchestrator.py must find zero occurrences of isinstance(, type(, "CliAgentProvider", "cli" used in a routing conditional.

Testable asserts:

# Routing test with custom provider
result = subprocess.run(
    ["grep", "-n", r"isinstance\|type(\|\"CliAgentProvider\"\|== .cli.", 
     "backend/app/ingest/orchestrator.py"],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)
# Only allowed reference to "cli" is log output or config field read, not a routing branch
routing_lines = [l for l in result.stdout.splitlines()
                 if "capabilities()" not in l and "#" not in l]
assert routing_lines == []

docs.title: AC-K2-5 (I7) — Loop stops at max_iter=3 on non-converging provider
docs.text:
Given a mock provider whose generate() always returns pages missing the type frontmatter field (guaranteed to fail validation), When orchestrator.ingest_file(...) is called with this provider and max_iter=3, Then: 1. provider.generate() is called exactly 3 times (no more, no fewer). 2. provider.analyze() is called at most 3 times (once per iteration or once total — architect to decide; see AQ-v0.2-1 below). 3. After the 3rd failed iteration, the orchestrator exits with converged=False. 4. No further calls to the provider are made after the cap is reached. 5. A log entry is written with converged=False.

Testable assert:

mock_provider.generate.call_count == 3
assert last_log_entry["converged"] is False
assert last_log_entry["max_iter_used"] == 3

docs.title: AC-K2-6 (I7) — Every ingest run produces a structured log entry
docs.text:
Given any completed ingest run (converged or not, any provider), When the run completes (success or max_iter exhausted), Then a structured log entry is written (to Python logging at INFO level or to a dedicated ingest_runs Postgres table — architect to decide; see AQ-v0.2-2) containing all of the following keys with non-null values:

Key Type Notes provider_name str e.g. "OllamaProvider" max_iter_used int iterations actually consumed (1–max_iter) total_tokens int sum of input+output tokens across all iterations total_cost_usd float 0.00 for Local; computed for API/CLI (see ADR-0009) converged bool True if pages passed validation within max_iter

Testable assert:

# Capture structured log output during ingest
with capture_logs() as logs:
    orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_path)
entry = [e for e in logs if e.get("event") == "ingest_complete"][0]
assert all(k in entry for k in
           ["provider_name", "max_iter_used", "total_tokens", "total_cost_usd", "converged"])
assert isinstance(entry["total_cost_usd"], float)
assert entry["converged"] in (True, False)

docs.title: AC-K2-7 — Provider fallback: primary timeout triggers exactly one retry, then surfaces error
docs.text:
Given the primary provider is configured and a fallback provider is set in provider_config, When the primary provider raises a timeout exception (or HTTP 503) during analyze(), Then: 1. The orchestrator attempts the fallback provider exactly once. 2. If the fallback also fails, an error is surfaced to the caller (HTTP 500 with JSON error body from the REST endpoint; exception propagated from the library call). 3. No further retry beyond the one fallback attempt occurs. 4. The log entry records provider_name of the fallback provider used and converged=False.

Testable assert:

primary.analyze.side_effect = TimeoutError
fallback.analyze.side_effect = TimeoutError
with pytest.raises(IngestError):
    orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_path)
assert primary.analyze.call_count == 1
assert fallback.analyze.call_count == 1  # exactly one attempt on fallback

docs.title: AC-K2-8 — overview.md created or updated after first ingest into a new vault
docs.text:
Given a fresh vault with no prior LLM-generated pages (vault/wiki/ contains only seed files: index.md, log.md), When orchestrator.ingest_file(...) completes successfully for the first time, Then vault/wiki/overview.md exists, is parseable by python-frontmatter without error, has YAML frontmatter with at minimum type: overview and title, and contains at least one paragraph of Markdown prose (non-empty body, not just frontmatter).

And given a subsequent ingest into the same vault, When orchestrator.ingest_file(...) completes a second time, Then vault/wiki/overview.md is updated (its updated_at timestamp in frontmatter or filesystem mtime is newer than after the first ingest), and the file is still valid Obsidian-compatible Markdown.

Testable assert:

import frontmatter
from pathlib import Path

# After first ingest
overview = frontmatter.load("vault/wiki/overview.md")
assert overview.metadata["type"] == "overview"
assert len(overview.content.strip()) > 0

mtime_1 = Path("vault/wiki/overview.md").stat().st_mtime

# After second ingest
orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_path_2)
mtime_2 = Path("vault/wiki/overview.md").stat().st_mtime
assert mtime_2 > mtime_1

docs.title: US-F3 — Two-step CoT ingest, source traceability, language-aware
docs.text:
Feature ID: F3 Story: As the Synapse ingest orchestrator, I want the orchestrated ingest loop to call analyze() first (producing a structured Analysis object identifying topics, entities, language, and suggested page types) and then generate() (producing wiki pages with wikilinks and source traceability in frontmatter), so that every generated wiki page can be traced back to its source document and is written in the detected language of that document.

Invariants touched: I5 (generated pages must pass Obsidian YAML validity), I6 (analyze/generate called only through InferenceProvider ABC), I7 (validate-retry loop in orchestrator is bounded)

docs.title: AC-F3-1 — analyze() returns a valid Analysis Pydantic object
docs.text:
Given the fixture file backend/tests/fixtures/sample-source.md (English, ~500 words, valid frontmatter), When any concrete provider's analyze(source_text, vault_context) is called with the file's content and a mock vault_context string, Then the return value is an Analysis Pydantic model instance that passes Analysis.model_validate(result.model_dump()) without error, and contains all of:

Field Type Constraint topics list[str] len >= 1 entities list[str] len >= 0 (may be empty for abstract documents) language str valid ISO-639-1 code, e.g. "en" or "it" suggested_pages list[SuggestedPage] each has title: str and type: PageType

Testable assert:

result = provider.analyze(source_text=content, vault_context="")
assert isinstance(result, Analysis)
assert len(result.topics) >= 1
assert result.language in {"en", "it", "fr", "de", "es"}  # expand as needed
for page in result.suggested_pages:
    assert page.type in {"entity", "concept", "source", "synthesis", "comparison"}

docs.title: AC-F3-2 — generate() returns list[WikiPage] with required frontmatter fields
docs.text:
Given an Analysis object from a prior analyze() call (or a fixture Analysis object), When provider.generate(analysis, retrieval_context="") is called, Then the return value is a non-empty list[WikiPage] where each WikiPage satisfies:

Field Type Constraint title str non-empty type str one of: entity, concept, source, synthesis, comparison content str non-empty Markdown body frontmatter dict contains type, title, sources (list), lang lang str ISO-639-1 code matching analysis.language

Testable assert:

pages = provider.generate(analysis=fixture_analysis, retrieval_context="")
assert len(pages) >= 1
for page in pages:
    assert isinstance(page, WikiPage)
    assert page.type in {"entity", "concept", "source", "synthesis", "comparison"}
    assert "sources" in page.frontmatter
    assert isinstance(page.frontmatter["sources"], list)
    assert page.frontmatter["lang"] == fixture_analysis.language

docs.title: AC-F3-3 — sources[] contains the originating source file path
docs.text:
Given a fixture source file at relative path raw/sources/sample-source.md that is ingested via the orchestrator, When the generated wiki pages are written to vault/wiki/ and their frontmatter is parsed, Then every generated page's sources[] list contains the string "raw/sources/sample-source.md" (the relative path from vault root to the originating file).

The list may contain additional sources cited from retrieval context, but must always contain the originating path.

Testable assert:

for wiki_file in Path("vault/wiki").glob("entities/*.md"):
    doc = frontmatter.load(wiki_file)
    assert "raw/sources/sample-source.md" in doc.metadata["sources"], \
        f"{wiki_file} sources[] does not reference the origin: {doc.metadata['sources']}"

docs.title: AC-F3-4 — Language detection: EN and IT sources produce pages in matching language
docs.text:
Given two fixture files: - backend/tests/fixtures/sample-source-en.md (English content, ~500 words) - backend/tests/fixtures/sample-source-it.md (Italian content, ~500 words)

When each is ingested independently through the orchestrated loop, Then the generated wiki pages from the English fixture have lang: en in their frontmatter and their content field is predominantly English text (no Italian sentences), and the generated pages from the Italian fixture have lang: it and predominantly Italian content.

The lang field in each generated WikiPage.frontmatter must match the Analysis.language detected during analyze().

Testable assert:

# Ingest English fixture
pages_en = provider.generate(provider.analyze(en_content, ""), "")
for p in pages_en:
    assert p.frontmatter["lang"] == "en"

# Ingest Italian fixture
pages_it = provider.generate(provider.analyze(it_content, ""), "")
for p in pages_it:
    assert p.frontmatter["lang"] == "it"

docs.title: AC-F3-5 — vault/wiki/overview.md created on first ingest; valid Obsidian format
docs.text:
Given a fresh vault (no prior overview.md), When the first ingest completes via any provider, Then vault/wiki/overview.md exists and satisfies all of: 1. Parseable by python-frontmatter without exception. 2. frontmatter["type"] == "overview" (exact string). 3. frontmatter["title"] is a non-empty string. 4. frontmatter["sources"] is present and is a list (may be empty for auto-generated overview). 5. The Markdown body (content after frontmatter) is at least 50 characters (non-trivial prose). 6. The file opens in Obsidian without error (verified during EC-M2-13 human checkpoint).

Testable assert:

doc = frontmatter.load("vault/wiki/overview.md")
assert doc.metadata["type"] == "overview"
assert len(doc.metadata["title"]) > 0
assert isinstance(doc.metadata["sources"], list)
assert len(doc.content.strip()) >= 50

docs.title: AC-F3-6 — overview.md is updated on subsequent ingests; bounded length
docs.text:
Given vault/wiki/overview.md was created by a prior ingest, When a second ingest is performed with a different fixture file, Then: 1. vault/wiki/overview.md mtime increases (file was updated, not left stale). 2. The file remains valid Obsidian Markdown (parseable by python-frontmatter). 3. The file size does not exceed the limit imposed by the generation prompt's max token length (the overview generation prompt must include an explicit max-length instruction; the generated prose must not grow unboundedly across N ingests).

For the test, assert file size < 16384 bytes after 3 sequential ingests.

Testable assert:

mtime_before = Path("vault/wiki/overview.md").stat().st_mtime
orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_path_2)
mtime_after = Path("vault/wiki/overview.md").stat().st_mtime
assert mtime_after > mtime_before
assert Path("vault/wiki/overview.md").stat().st_size < 16384

docs.title: AC-F3-7 — Happy-path ingest converges in exactly 1 iteration (no retry)
docs.text:
Given the fixture file backend/tests/fixtures/sample-source.md (well-formed, full frontmatter) ingested through either OllamaProvider or ApiProvider, When the orchestrated loop runs, Then the loop exits with converged=True after exactly 1 iteration (the generated pages pass the frontmatter validator without requiring a retry).

This validates that the validation logic is not over-strict and that a normal source document does not trigger unnecessary retries.

Testable assert:

with capture_logs() as logs:
    orchestrator.ingest_file("vault/raw/sources/sample-source.md")
entry = next(e for e in logs if e.get("event") == "ingest_complete")
assert entry["converged"] is True
assert entry["max_iter_used"] == 1

docs.title: US-K5 — Wikilink parser and links table
docs.text:
Feature ID: K5 Story: As a downstream sprint, I want a dedicated [[wikilink]] parser that runs after each page write and stores all link records in a Postgres links table, so that Sprint 3 (v0.3) can compute knowledge graph edges from a reliable, schema-validated source of truth rather than parsing Markdown files at graph-build time.

Invariants touched: I1 (parser runs per-page write event, not on full rescan of wiki/), I5 (dangling wikilinks do not corrupt the vault; they are stored as warnings), I8 (links table appears in D2 ER diagram)

docs.title: AC-K5-1 — Simple wikilink parsed to (target_title, alias=None)
docs.text:
Given the Markdown content string "See [[Target Page]] for details.", When wikilinks.parse(content) is called, Then the result is a list containing exactly one tuple/object with target_title == "Target Page" and alias is None.

Testable assert:

from app.ingest.parser.wikilinks import parse
results = parse("See [[Target Page]] for details.")
assert len(results) == 1
assert results[0].target_title == "Target Page"
assert results[0].alias is None

docs.title: AC-K5-2 — Aliased wikilink parsed to (target_title, alias)
docs.text:
Given the content string "Read [[Long Title|short]] for an overview.", When wikilinks.parse(content) is called, Then the result contains exactly one entry with target_title == "Long Title" and alias == "short".

Testable assert:

results = parse("Read [[Long Title|short]] for an overview.")
assert results[0].target_title == "Long Title"
assert results[0].alias == "short"

docs.title: AC-K5-3 — Parser handles multiple links, zero links, and invalid bracket syntax without exception
docs.text:
Given the following content variations: - "[[Page A]] and [[Page B|alias]] and [[Page C]]" (3 links) - "No links here." (zero links) - "[[unclosed" (malformed — no closing brackets) - "[[]]" (empty title — should be ignored)

When wikilinks.parse(content) is called on each, Then: - 3-links case: returns a list of exactly 3 items, each with correct target_title - zero-links case: returns an empty list - malformed case: returns an empty list; no exception raised - empty-title case: returns an empty list; no exception raised

Testable assert:

assert len(parse("[[Page A]] and [[Page B|alias]] and [[Page C]]")) == 3
assert parse("No links here.") == []
assert parse("[[unclosed") == []  # no exception
assert parse("[[]]") == []  # empty title ignored

docs.title: AC-K5-4 — After ingest, all wikilinks in generated pages have links table rows
docs.text:
Given a fixture ingest that produces at least one wiki page containing wikilinks (verified by inspecting the generated content), When the ingest completes and the links table is queried, Then for every [[Target]] in every generated wiki page's content, a row exists in the links table with source_page_id matching the wiki page's UUID.

Testable assert:

# For each wiki page row in Postgres:
page_content = Path(f"vault/wiki/{page.file_path}").read_text()
expected_links = parse(page_content)
db_links = db.query(Link).filter(Link.source_page_id == page.id).all()
db_targets = {l.target_title for l in db_links}
parser_targets = {l.target_title for l in expected_links}
assert parser_targets.issubset(db_targets)

docs.title: AC-K5-5 — Dangling wikilinks stored with dangling=True; WARNING logged; no exception
docs.text:
Given a wiki page that contains [[Nonexistent Page]] where no page titled "Nonexistent Page" exists in the Postgres pages table, When the wikilink parser runs and attempts to resolve the target, Then: 1. A row is inserted into links with target_title = "Nonexistent Page" and dangling = True. 2. A WARNING-level log message is emitted containing the string "dangling" or "target not found" and the target title. 3. No exception is raised; the ingest run completes normally with converged=True (dangling links do not invalidate a page).

Testable assert:

with capture_logs() as logs:
    orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_with_dangling_link)

dangling_rows = db.query(Link).filter(Link.dangling == True).all()
assert len(dangling_rows) >= 1
assert dangling_rows[0].target_title == "Nonexistent Page"

warning_msgs = [l for l in logs if l["level"] == "WARNING" and "dangling" in str(l)]
assert len(warning_msgs) >= 1

docs.title: AC-K5-6 — links table schema matches ER diagram; Alembic migration runs cleanly
docs.text:
Given the links Alembic migration has been applied, When information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'links' is queried, Then the table contains exactly the columns defined in the ER diagram (D2):

Column Type Constraint id uuid primary key source_page_id uuid foreign key → pages.id, not null target_title text not null alias text nullable dangling boolean not null, default false created_at timestamp with time zone not null

Testable assert: introspect via SQLAlchemy inspect(engine).get_columns("links"); assert all column names match the above; assert foreign key constraint exists from links.source_page_id to pages.id.

docs.title: AC-K5-7 — Unit tests covering all parser edge cases
docs.text:
Given the backend/tests/test_wikilink_parser.py test file, When make test is run, Then the following test cases all pass: 1. Simple link: [[Target]] → (target_title="Target", alias=None) 2. Aliased link: [[Target|Alias]] → (target_title="Target", alias="Alias") 3. Multiple links on one line 4. Zero links (plain text) 5. Invalid bracket syntax (no crash, empty result) 6. Link with spaces in title: [[Multi Word Title]] → target_title="Multi Word Title" 7. Link at start of line: [[First]] without preceding text

Testable assert: all 7 (or more) parametrised test cases in the test file have status == PASSED in the pytest report.

docs.title: US-K3 — index.md catalogue auto-maintained
docs.text:
Feature ID: K3 Story: As a vault owner, I want vault/wiki/index.md to be automatically updated after every successful wiki page write, so that index.md is always a complete, type-organised catalogue of all generated pages and can serve as the LLM navigation entry-point for future query operations (F5) without manual curation.

Invariants touched: I5 (index.md must remain valid Obsidian YAML frontmatter at all times), I1 (index.md update is triggered per-page-write event, not by a separate rescan)

docs.title: AC-K3-1 — index.md updated with [[wikilink]] after ingest
docs.text:
Given a fixture ingest that produces at least one wiki page with title "Test Entity", When the ingest completes, Then vault/wiki/index.md contains the string [[Test Entity]] (or the exact title as a wikilink) somewhere in its body.

Testable assert:

index_content = Path("vault/wiki/index.md").read_text()
assert "[[Test Entity]]" in index_content

docs.title: AC-K3-2 — index.md retains valid YAML frontmatter (type: index, title: "Wiki Index")
docs.text:
Given vault/wiki/index.md exists and has been updated by at least one ingest, When it is parsed by python-frontmatter, Then the parse succeeds without exception, and: - frontmatter["type"] == "index" (exact string) - frontmatter["title"] == "Wiki Index" (exact string)

The YAML frontmatter block is present and intact after every update; no update operation corrupts or removes it.

Testable assert:

doc = frontmatter.load("vault/wiki/index.md")
assert doc.metadata["type"] == "index"
assert doc.metadata["title"] == "Wiki Index"

docs.title: AC-K3-3 — Entries in index.md are organised by page type
docs.text:
Given a fixture ingest producing pages of at least two different types (e.g. one "entity" page and one "concept" page), When vault/wiki/index.md is read, Then the file contains distinct Markdown section headers for each page type present (e.g. ## Entities, ## Concepts), and each page's wikilink appears under its correct section.

Testable assert:

content = Path("vault/wiki/index.md").read_text()
assert "## Entities" in content or "## Entity" in content
assert "[[Test Entity]]" in content  # appears under the entity section

docs.title: AC-K3-4 — Missing index.md is recreated without error
docs.text:
Given vault/wiki/index.md has been deleted (simulating a corrupted vault), When orchestrator.ingest_file(...) is called for any fixture file, Then vault/wiki/index.md is recreated from scratch with valid YAML frontmatter and at least one entry for the newly written page. No exception is raised.

Testable assert:

Path("vault/wiki/index.md").unlink(missing_ok=True)
orchestrator.ingest_file(fixture_path)
assert Path("vault/wiki/index.md").exists()
doc = frontmatter.load("vault/wiki/index.md")
assert doc.metadata["type"] == "index"

docs.title: AC-K3-5 — index.md never truncated; only appended/regenerated
docs.text:
Given vault/wiki/index.md contains N entries after a prior ingest, When a new ingest adds one more page, Then the resulting index.md contains at least N + 1 entries (the prior entries are preserved). The file is not truncated to just the new entry.

The acceptable strategies are: (a) append a new entry under the correct type section, or (b) regenerate the full index in one atomic write. Either is acceptable as long as prior entries are not lost.

Testable assert:

content_before = Path("vault/wiki/index.md").read_text()
prior_link_count = content_before.count("[[")
orchestrator.ingest_file(new_fixture)
content_after = Path("vault/wiki/index.md").read_text()
new_link_count = content_after.count("[[")
assert new_link_count >= prior_link_count + 1

docs.title: AC-K3-6 — Unit test: ingest fixture → index.md contains wikilink to new page
docs.text:
Given the unit test in backend/tests/test_index_catalogue.py that runs a mock ingest producing a single page with title "Fixture Page", When make test is run, Then the test asserts "[[Fixture Page]]" is present in vault/wiki/index.md and the test passes (status GREEN).

This is the minimum standalone unit test for K3; it must be independently executable without live Ollama/Anthropic infrastructure (using mock providers).

docs.title: US-MCP — FastMCP standalone server
docs.text:
Feature ID: MCP server Story: As a connected MCP client (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, or the CliAgentProvider subprocess), I want to call search_wiki, write_page, get_page, and list_pages tools via a FastMCP server, so that the CLI inference provider can read and write the wiki vault during its own agentic loop without bypassing the Synapse service layer (which enforces frontmatter validation, K5 wikilink parsing, K3 index updates, and Qdrant upsert).

Invariants touched: I6 (CliAgentProvider uses MCP tools, not direct filesystem writes), I1 (write_page goes through the same upsert primitives as direct ingest), I5 (write_page validates frontmatter before writing), I9 (MCP search uses Qdrant bge-m3 already running, not a new search service)

docs.title: AC-MCP-1 — MCP server starts via make mcp; all 4 tools registered
docs.text:
Given the make mcp target is defined and backend/app/mcp/server.py exists, When make mcp is run (or python -m app.mcp.server is invoked), Then the FastMCP server starts without error, and the FastMCP tool registry lists exactly (at minimum): search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages.

Testable assert (via FastMCP test client):

from fastmcp.testing import TestClient
from app.mcp.server import mcp_server
async with TestClient(mcp_server) as client:
    tools = await client.list_tools()
    tool_names = {t.name for t in tools}
    assert {"search_wiki", "write_page", "get_page", "list_pages"}.issubset(tool_names)

docs.title: AC-MCP-2 — search_wiki returns PageRef list with required fields
docs.text:
Given at least one page has been written to the wiki (via ingest or write_page), When search_wiki(query="<keyword from the page title or content>") is called via the MCP test client, Then the response is a non-empty list of PageRef objects where each contains: - id: UUID string - title: non-empty string - type: valid page type - relevance_score: float in [0.0, 1.0]

Testable assert:

result = await client.call_tool("search_wiki", {"query": "entity"})
assert len(result) >= 1
assert all(k in result[0] for k in ["id", "title", "type", "relevance_score"])
assert 0.0 <= result[0]["relevance_score"] <= 1.0

docs.title: AC-MCP-3 — write_page writes file, creates Postgres row and Qdrant vector, updates index.md; fails with descriptive error on missing frontmatter
docs.text:
Given a valid payload: title="MCP Test Page", content="# MCP Test Page\nContent.", frontmatter={"type": "entity", "title": "MCP Test Page", "sources": ["raw/sources/test.md"]}, When write_page(...) is called, Then: 1. vault/wiki/entities/mcp-test-page.md (or a slug-equivalent path) exists. 2. A Postgres pages row exists with title == "MCP Test Page". 3. A Qdrant point exists for the page UUID. 4. vault/wiki/index.md contains [[MCP Test Page]].

And given the same tool called without type in frontmatter, When write_page(title="Bad Page", content="content", frontmatter={"title": "Bad Page"}), Then the tool returns an error response (not an unhandled exception) with a human-readable message identifying the missing field.

Testable assert:

result = await client.call_tool("write_page", {
    "title": "MCP Test Page", "content": "Content.",
    "frontmatter": {"type": "entity", "title": "MCP Test Page", "sources": ["raw/sources/test.md"]}
})
assert Path("vault/wiki").glob("**/mcp-test-page.md").__next__().exists()

# Failure case
error_result = await client.call_tool("write_page", {
    "title": "Bad Page", "content": "content",
    "frontmatter": {"title": "Bad Page"}  # missing type
})
assert "type" in str(error_result).lower() or "missing" in str(error_result).lower()

docs.title: AC-MCP-4 — get_page returns full content and frontmatter; not-found for unknown title
docs.text:
Given a page titled "MCP Test Page" exists in the vault, When get_page(title="MCP Test Page") is called, Then the response contains title, content, frontmatter, and type with values matching the stored page.

And when get_page(title="Title That Does Not Exist") is called, Then the response is an error indicating the page was not found (not a Python exception propagated unhandled — a structured tool error).

Testable assert:

result = await client.call_tool("get_page", {"title": "MCP Test Page"})
assert result["title"] == "MCP Test Page"
assert "content" in result and len(result["content"]) > 0

not_found = await client.call_tool("get_page", {"title": "Does Not Exist"})
assert "not found" in str(not_found).lower() or "error" in str(not_found).lower()

docs.title: AC-MCP-5 — list_pages returns all non-deleted pages; type filter works
docs.text:
Given the vault contains 2 entity pages, 1 concept page, and 1 deleted page (deleted_at != NULL in Postgres), When list_pages() is called (no filter), Then the response contains exactly 3 entries (the deleted page is excluded).

And when list_pages(type="entity") is called, Then the response contains exactly 2 entries, all with type == "entity".

Testable assert:

all_pages = await client.call_tool("list_pages", {})
assert len(all_pages) == 3  # deleted page excluded

entity_pages = await client.call_tool("list_pages", {"type": "entity"})
assert len(entity_pages) == 2
assert all(p["type"] == "entity" for p in entity_pages)

docs.title: AC-MCP-6 — Integration test: all 4 tools pass DB and filesystem assertions
docs.text:
Given a test Postgres database and a test vault directory, When the FastMCP integration test in backend/tests/test_mcp_integration.py is run, Then all 4 tool calls (search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages) pass their DB and filesystem state assertions (as specified in AC-MCP-2 through AC-MCP-5).

This is one test file that exercises all tools sequentially, confirming end-to-end state propagation (write then read; write then search; write then list).

Testable assert: make test exit code 0; test_mcp_integration.py shows 0 failures.

docs.title: AC-MCP-7 — mcp-tools.json documents all 4 tools with input/output schemas
docs.text:
Given make mcp-schema (or equivalent) has been run to export FastMCP tool schemas, When docs/api/mcp-tools.json is read and parsed as JSON, Then the file contains a top-level key tools (or equivalent) with entries for all 4 tools, each containing at minimum: - name: matching the tool function name - description: non-empty string - inputSchema: valid JSON Schema object (has type: "object" and properties) - outputSchema or returns: describes the return type

Testable assert:

tools_doc = json.loads(Path("docs/api/mcp-tools.json").read_text())
tool_names = {t["name"] for t in tools_doc["tools"]}
assert {"search_wiki", "write_page", "get_page", "list_pages"}.issubset(tool_names)
for t in tools_doc["tools"]:
    assert "inputSchema" in t
    assert t["inputSchema"]["type"] == "object"

docs.title: AC-MCP-8 — CliAgentProvider communicates with MCP server via stdio during smoke matrix
docs.text:
Given the smoke matrix test (EC-M2-5) running the CliAgentProvider backend, When the CliAgentProvider subprocess is invoked with the MCP server in stdio mode, Then the CLI agent successfully calls write_page at least once during the ingest run (confirmed by at least one new entry in the pages Postgres table attributed to the CLI ingest run) and the subprocess exits without a non-zero exit code.

This is a live-infra test (requires working claude-agent-sdk + real Anthropic API key). Marked @pytest.mark.live and verified at EC-M2-5 human checkpoint.

Testable assert (human-observed, confirmed in sign-off register): - CLI subprocess exit code == 0 - At least 1 new Postgres row with provider_name = "CliAgentProvider" in the ingest log

docs.title: US-D3 — Sequence diagrams (ingest routing + ingest loop)
docs.text:
Feature ID: D3 (docs artefact — first sprint it is required) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want two Mermaid sequence diagrams documenting the ingest routing decision and the orchestrated loop, so that new engineers and agents have an accurate visual spec of the control flow this sprint introduced, satisfying the I8 docs-as-DoD gate for M2.

Invariants touched: I8 (D3 is a required M2 artefact; must pass mmdc render check in CI)

docs.title: AC-D3-1 — ingest-routing.mmd is a valid Mermaid sequenceDiagram showing both branches
docs.text:
Given docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd has been authored, When mmdc -i docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd -o /dev/null is run, Then the command exits 0, and the file content: 1. Begins with sequenceDiagram keyword (or a valid equivalent alias). 2. Names participants including at minimum: Watcher, Orchestrator, InferenceProvider, CliAgentProvider, MCPServer, WikiFS (or equivalent labels). 3. Contains two visually distinct branches: one labelled "supports_agentic_loop=True" (or equivalent) showing the CLI delegation path, and one showing the orchestrated loop path. 4. Shows index.md update as a terminal step on both branches.

Testable assert:

result = subprocess.run(
    ["mmdc", "-i", "docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd", "-o", "/dev/null"],
    capture_output=True
)
assert result.returncode == 0
content = Path("docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd").read_text()
assert "sequenceDiagram" in content
assert "agentic_loop" in content or "CLI" in content

Additionally: reviewed and approved by tech-writer (human gate, recorded in sign-off register).

docs.title: AC-D3-2 — ingest-loop.mmd is a valid Mermaid sequenceDiagram showing the bounded loop
docs.text:
Given docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd has been authored, When mmdc -i docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd -o /dev/null is run, Then the command exits 0, and the file content: 1. Begins with sequenceDiagram. 2. Shows the steps: analyze → generate → validate → [if invalid: augment + retry] → write pages. 3. Annotates the loop bound with max_iter (or equivalent text indicating the cap). 4. Shows log total_cost_usd as a final step.

Testable assert:

result = subprocess.run(
    ["mmdc", "-i", "docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd", "-o", "/dev/null"],
    capture_output=True
)
assert result.returncode == 0
content = Path("docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd").read_text()
assert "max_iter" in content or "max_iter" in content
assert "cost" in content.lower() or "total_cost" in content

Additionally: reviewed and approved by tech-writer (human gate).

docs.title: AC-D3-3 — Both diagrams pass CI mmdc render check
docs.text:
Given the CI pipeline has an mmdc render step, When the CI pipeline runs against a commit on sprint/v0.2, Then both docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd and docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd are rendered by mmdc with exit code 0 (no Mermaid parse errors). A non-zero exit from either file fails the CI build.

Testable assert (CI configuration check):

for diagram in [
    "docs/sequences/ingest-routing.mmd",
    "docs/sequences/ingest-loop.mmd"
]:
    result = subprocess.run(["mmdc", "-i", diagram, "-o", "/dev/null"],
                            capture_output=True)
    assert result.returncode == 0, f"mmdc render failed for {diagram}: {result.stderr}"

docs.title: AC-D3-4 — Diagrams are consistent with implementation (joint architect + tech-writer review)
docs.text:
Given both ingest-routing.mmd and ingest-loop.mmd pass AC-D3-1, AC-D3-2, AC-D3-3, When the solution-architect and tech-writer jointly review the diagrams against the actual orchestrator.py implementation, Then no discrepancy is found between a diagram step and the code behaviour (e.g., the diagram must not show analyze() being called for the CLI delegation path). Approval is recorded in the sign-off register before M2 sign-off.

This is a mandatory human gate (binary: approved / not approved).

docs.title: US-D7 — Architecture Decision Records (first formally required sprint)
docs.text:
Feature ID: D7 (docs artefact — required from v0.2 per CLAUDE.md §9) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want three required Architecture Decision Records (ADR-0007, ADR-0008, ADR-0009) present in docs/adr/, documenting the InferenceProvider ABC design, the provider_config schema, and the bounded-loop defaults, so that the rationale for every major v0.2 architectural decision is preserved and future engineers can understand why these choices were made without reading the entire codebase.

Invariants touched: I8 (D7 required from v0.2; must be approved by architect before M2 sign-off)

docs.title: AC-D7-1 — ADR-0007: InferenceProvider ABC design is present and approved
docs.text:
Given docs/adr/ADR-0007-inference-provider-abc.md has been authored, When it is reviewed by the solution-architect, Then the document is present, non-empty, and covers all of: 1. Why an ABC (abstract base class) was chosen over duck typing or a Protocol. 2. Why three backends (Local/API/CLI) and not two or four. 3. Why capability-aware routing was chosen (read capabilities()) over explicit config-based routing (read provider type string). 4. Why chat() is stubbed (NotImplementedError) in v0.2 rather than fully implemented or deferred to v0.3. 5. Status field set to "Accepted" and Date/Sprint/Decider populated.

Testable assert (automated structure check):

content = Path("docs/adr/ADR-0007-inference-provider-abc.md").read_text()
assert len(content) > 500  # non-trivial document
for keyword in ["ABC", "capability", "chat", "NotImplementedError", "Accepted"]:
    assert keyword in content, f"ADR-0007 missing expected content: {keyword}"

Approval by solution-architect is a mandatory human gate (recorded in sign-off register).

docs.title: AC-D7-2 — ADR-0008: provider_config schema is present and approved
docs.text:
Given docs/adr/ADR-0008-provider-config-schema.md has been authored, When it is reviewed by the solution-architect, Then the document covers all of: 1. Why Postgres was chosen over a YAML/JSON config file for provider_config. 2. Why three scope levels (global/vault/operation) and their resolution order. 3. Why vault_id is nullable at global scope. 4. Status "Accepted", Date/Sprint/Decider populated.

Testable assert (automated structure check):

content = Path("docs/adr/ADR-0008-provider-config-schema.md").read_text()
assert len(content) > 400
for keyword in ["global", "vault", "operation", "Postgres", "Accepted"]:
    assert keyword in content, f"ADR-0008 missing: {keyword}"

docs.title: AC-D7-3 — ADR-0009: bounded-loop defaults and cost logging is present and approved
docs.text:
Given docs/adr/ADR-0009-bounded-loop-defaults.md has been authored, When it is reviewed by the solution-architect, Then the document covers all of: 1. Rationale for max_iter=3 (happy path + 2 retries is sufficient; beyond 3 is a provider quality problem). 2. Rationale for token_budget=60000 (orchestrated) and 100000 (CLI delegated). 3. How total_cost_usd is computed for each backend type (Anthropic: input/output token pricing from provider_config; Ollama: always $0.00; CLI: $0.00 by convention, credits are build-time). 4. The $1.00/run anomaly WARNING threshold and why that value was chosen. 5. Status "Accepted", Date/Sprint/Decider populated.

Testable assert:

content = Path("docs/adr/ADR-0009-bounded-loop-defaults.md").read_text()
assert len(content) > 500
for keyword in ["max_iter", "token_budget", "total_cost_usd", "1.00", "Accepted"]:
    assert keyword in content, f"ADR-0009 missing: {keyword}"

docs.title: AC-D7-4 — All ADRs follow the established format
docs.text:
Given all ADR files present in docs/adr/ (including any additional ADRs authored this sprint beyond the three required), When each is read, Then each ADR contains all of the following section headers (case-insensitive): Status, Date, Sprint, Decider, Context, Decision, Consequences.

Testable assert:

import re
required_sections = {"status", "date", "sprint", "decider", "context", "decision", "consequences"}
for adr_file in Path("docs/adr").glob("ADR-00[0-9][0-9]*.md"):
    content = adr_file.read_text().lower()
    for section in required_sections:
        assert section in content, f"{adr_file.name} missing section: {section}"

docs.title: AC-D7-5 — ADRs are internally consistent with D3 sequence diagrams and implementation
docs.text:
Given all required ADRs (ADR-0007, ADR-0008, ADR-0009) and both D3 sequence diagrams are present, When the solution-architect and tech-writer review them jointly, Then no contradiction exists between: - ADR-0007's routing description and ingest-routing.mmd - ADR-0009's loop defaults and ingest-loop.mmd - Any ADR's claims and the actual behaviour in orchestrator.py and base.py

This is a mandatory joint human review gate (binary: consistent / not consistent). Approval recorded in sign-off register before M2 sign-off.

docs.title: US-D1u — C4 architecture diagrams updated
docs.text:
Feature ID: D1 (update — continuous per I8) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want the C4 architecture diagrams in docs/architecture/ to include the InferenceProvider layer, all 3 backends, the orchestrator routing, the MCP server, and the provider_config table, so that the component-level view of Synapse accurately reflects what was built in v0.2 and the I8 docs gate is satisfied.

Invariants touched: I8 (D1 must be updated whenever architecture changes; first component diagram this sprint)

docs.title: AC-D1u-1 — component.mmd is a valid Mermaid C4Component diagram showing InferenceProvider layer
docs.text:
Given docs/architecture/component.mmd has been authored (new file in v0.2), When mmdc -i docs/architecture/component.mmd -o /dev/null is run, Then exit code is 0 (no parse errors), and the file contains identifiers/labels for: InferenceProvider (or ProviderABC), OllamaProvider, ApiProvider, CliAgentProvider, IngestOrchestrator, MCPServer, ProviderConfig (or provider_config).

Testable assert:

result = subprocess.run(
    ["mmdc", "-i", "docs/architecture/component.mmd", "-o", "/dev/null"],
    capture_output=True
)
assert result.returncode == 0
content = Path("docs/architecture/component.mmd").read_text()
for label in ["InferenceProvider", "OllamaProvider", "ApiProvider",
              "CliAgentProvider", "MCPServer"]:
    assert label in content, f"component.mmd missing: {label}"

Reviewed and approved by architect and tech-writer (human gate).

docs.title: AC-D1u-2 — context.mmd and container.mmd updated; no stale elements
docs.text:
Given docs/architecture/context.mmd and docs/architecture/container.mmd from v0.1 have been updated, When each is passed to mmdc, Then both exit with code 0, and both files reference the MCP server as a new container or relationship. No v0.1-only elements remain that would mislead a reader about the v0.2 architecture (e.g., if the watcher was previously the only ingest trigger, the diagrams must now show the orchestrator and provider layer as co-participants).

Testable assert:

for f in ["docs/architecture/context.mmd", "docs/architecture/container.mmd"]:
    result = subprocess.run(["mmdc", "-i", f, "-o", "/dev/null"], capture_output=True)
    assert result.returncode == 0
    content = Path(f).read_text()
    assert "MCP" in content or "mcp" in content.lower(), \
        f"{f} missing MCP reference after v0.2 update"

docs.title: US-D2u — ER diagram updated with new tables
docs.text:
Feature ID: D2 (update — continuous per I8) Story: As a downstream sprint, I want docs/er/schema.mmd to reflect the provider_config and links tables added in v0.2, so that the ER diagram matches the live Postgres schema and the I8 docs gate is satisfied.

Invariants touched: I8 (D2 must be regenerated via make er whenever models.py changes)

docs.title: AC-D2u-1 — schema.mmd regenerated by make er; includes provider_config and links tables
docs.text:
Given backend/app/models.py has been updated with ProviderConfig and Link models, When make er is run, Then docs/er/schema.mmd is regenerated and contains entity labels and attributes for: - provider_config with columns: id, scope, vault_id, provider_type, model_id, base_url, max_iter, token_budget, created_at, updated_at - links with columns: id, source_page_id, target_title, alias, dangling, created_at

Additionally, make er exits 0 and the generated file is valid Mermaid erDiagram syntax (mmdc renders it without error).

Testable assert:

subprocess.run(["make", "er"], check=True)
content = Path("docs/er/schema.mmd").read_text()
assert "provider_config" in content
assert "links" in content
assert "source_page_id" in content
assert "dangling" in content
result = subprocess.run(["mmdc", "-i", "docs/er/schema.mmd", "-o", "/dev/null"],
                        capture_output=True)
assert result.returncode == 0

docs.title: US-D4u — OpenAPI and MCP tool schemas updated
docs.text:
Feature ID: D4 (update — continuous per I8) Story: As a connected MCP client or API consumer, I want docs/api/openapi.json to document the new provider config endpoints and the corrected 202 schema, and docs/api/mcp-tools.json to document all 4 MCP tools, so that external clients can integrate with Synapse v0.2 without reading source code.

Invariants touched: I8 (D4 is continuous; every new endpoint must appear in the spec)

docs.title: AC-D4u-1 — openapi.json updated; POST /ingest/trigger has correct 202 schema; provider config endpoints documented
docs.text:
Given make openapi has been run after adding provider config endpoints and fixing the 202 carry-forward from v0.1, When docs/api/openapi.json is inspected, Then: 1. paths["/ingest/trigger"]["post"]["responses"]["202"] contains a content key with a JSON Schema for IngestTriggerResponse (with at minimum task_id, status, page_id fields) — fixing the v0.1 carry-forward. 2. paths["/provider/config"] exists with GET and POST operations documented. 3. The file is valid OpenAPI 3.1 (passes openapi-spec-validator).

Testable assert:

spec = json.loads(Path("docs/api/openapi.json").read_text())
trigger_response = spec["paths"]["/ingest/trigger"]["post"]["responses"]["202"]
assert "content" in trigger_response  # not just a description string
assert "/provider/config" in spec["paths"]
assert "get" in spec["paths"]["/provider/config"]
assert "post" in spec["paths"]["/provider/config"]

docs.title: AC-D4u-2 — mcp-tools.json present; all 4 tools documented with input/output schemas
docs.text:
(This AC is the D4 counterpart of AC-MCP-7. Both must pass independently; AC-MCP-7 tests the FastMCP schema export; AC-D4u-2 tests that the file is committed to docs/api/.)

Given docs/api/mcp-tools.json has been generated and committed, When it is parsed as JSON, Then it is valid JSON, contains all 4 tool names (search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages), and each tool entry has inputSchema.type == "object".

Testable assert:

tools_doc = json.loads(Path("docs/api/mcp-tools.json").read_text())
tool_names = {t["name"] for t in tools_doc.get("tools", [])}
assert {"search_wiki", "write_page", "get_page", "list_pages"}.issubset(tool_names)

docs.title: Ambiguities and architect-escalation flags
docs.text: The following questions must be resolved by solution-architect before engineers begin implementation. Each is tagged with an AQ-v0.2 ID to distinguish from v0.1 AQs.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-1 — Analysis re-run on retry: is analyze() called once or per iteration? (blocks AC-K2-5)
docs.text:
The orchestrated loop is described as analyze → generate → validate → retry. It is ambiguous whether analyze() is called once (Analysis cached; only generate() is retried with augmented prompt) or called again on each retry iteration.

Calling analyze once is cheaper (fewer tokens) but means the retry only augments the generation prompt. Calling analyze again is more expensive but allows the provider to re-examine the source with the validation error as context.

Required action: architect to specify the retry strategy — single analyze or per-iteration analyze — and document in ADR-0007. AC-K2-5's assertion on analyze.call_count depends on this decision (either <= 1 or <= max_iter).

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-2 — Ingest run log: structured Python log or dedicated Postgres table? (blocks AC-K2-6)
docs.text:
AC-K2-6 requires a log entry with provider_name, max_iter_used, total_tokens, total_cost_usd, converged. It is unspecified whether this is written as a structured JSON log line (Python structlog / logging.info(event="ingest_complete", ...)) or persisted to a Postgres ingest_runs table.

A Postgres table enables dashboards and query-based cost analysis. A structured log is simpler but requires log aggregation to query. The test harness for AC-K2-6 differs significantly depending on the choice.

Required action: architect to decide storage mechanism for ingest run records and document in ADR-0009 (or a new ADR). AC-K2-6 test implementation is blocked on this.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-3 — Analysis and WikiPage Pydantic schemas: exact field definitions (blocks AC-F3-1, AC-F3-2)
docs.text:
The Analysis and WikiPage Pydantic models are referenced in AC-F17-1, AC-F3-1, and AC-F3-2 but their exact field schemas are not defined in BACKLOG.md or the scope lock.

Specifically unspecified: - Analysis.suggested_pages: list of what type? (SuggestedPage with title: str and type: PageType? Or a flat dict?) - WikiPage.frontmatter: typed dict or dict[str, Any]? - WikiPage.content: raw Markdown string (before or after frontmatter block stripped)? - Message type for chat(): simple {"role": str, "content": str} or full Anthropic Message type? - ProviderCapabilities: Pydantic model or TypedDict?

Required action: architect or ai-agent-engineer to define the exact Pydantic schemas for Analysis, WikiPage, Message, ProviderCapabilities in a schema file (backend/app/ingest/schemas.py or backend/app/models.py) before provider implementations begin. These schemas are the contract between orchestrator and providers; any divergence breaks both.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-4 — Token usage measurement per backend (blocks AC-K2-6, ADR-0009)
docs.text:
total_cost_usd computation requires knowing input and output token counts. Each backend exposes this differently: - Anthropic API: response.usage.input_tokens + response.usage.output_tokens returned in the API response body. - Ollama: response["eval_count"] (output tokens) and response["prompt_eval_count"] (input tokens) in /api/chat response. - CLI (claude-agent-sdk): The SDK may return cost via metadata in its response object or not at all. If the SDK does not expose token usage, the service must set total_cost_usd = 0.00 and log a WARNING that cost could not be computed.

Required action: ai-agent-engineer to confirm the exact SDK attributes for token usage in the claude-agent-sdk response, and document the cost computation logic in ADR-0009. The convention that "CLI cost = $0.00" (build-time credits, not runtime billing) must be explicitly stated and agreed by the architect.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-5 — provider_config.scope column: how is "operation" scope parameterised? (blocks AC-F17-5, AC-F17-6)
docs.text:
AC-F17-5 lists scope as values 'global', 'vault', 'operation'. For operation scope to be meaningful, there must be a way to identify the operation (ingest vs. chat vs. lint). The BACKLOG defines scope: global | vault | operation but does not define an operation column or enum. AC-F17-6 tests resolution with operation='ingest' but the schema as specified does not have an operation column — only scope and vault_id.

Required action: architect to clarify the schema. Options: (a) Add an operation column (ingest, chat, lint, etc.) to provider_config. (b) Encode operation in the scope value itself (e.g., scope='operation:ingest'). (c) Use a separate operation text column nullable for non-operation scope rows. This decision directly affects the Alembic migration and the resolution query in AC-F17-6.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-6 — MCP server transport: stdio-only, HTTP-only, or both simultaneously? (blocks AC-MCP-1, AC-MCP-8)
docs.text:
The scope lock (v0.2-scope.md §2) states: "Server starts in stdio mode (for CliAgentProvider) and HTTP mode (for external clients)." Running both simultaneously requires either two process instances or a multiplexing server.

FastMCP supports both transports but not always in the same process. The test client (AC-MCP-1 through AC-MCP-6) needs to know which transport to use for automated testing.

Required action: architect to specify whether: (a) The MCP server starts in stdio mode only and HTTP is deferred to v0.4. (b) The MCP server supports HTTP in addition to stdio via FastMCP's HTTP transport option. (c) Two separate make targets: make mcp-stdio and make mcp-http. This decision affects make mcp, AC-MCP-1's startup assertion, and the CliAgentProvider subprocess setup.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-7 — WikiPage validator: exact criteria for "invalid" to trigger retry (blocks AC-K2-5, AC-F3-7)
docs.text:
The validator in the orchestrated loop decides whether generate() output is "valid" or "invalid". The BACKLOG states: "check each WikiPage has required frontmatter (type, title, sources[])." But the threshold for triggering a retry is not fully specified: - Does a page with an empty sources[] list (but the list key present) count as valid? - Does a page with a type value not in the allowed enum count as invalid? - What happens if only some pages in the returned list are invalid — is the whole batch retried or just the invalid pages?

Required action: architect to define the validation schema precisely. Recommend: (a) Required non-null, non-empty: type (must be in enum), title (non-empty string), sources (non-empty list with at least the originating file path). (b) Partial batch failure: the whole batch is retried (simpler orchestrator logic). Document in the orchestrator's docstring and reference in ADR-0007. AC-K2-5 and AC-F3-7 test conditions depend on this exact definition.

docs.title: AQ-v0.2-8 — cost anomaly WARNING: at call site or in a separate monitoring hook? (blocks ADR-0009)
docs.text:
The scope lock §6 defines a $1.00/run anomaly WARNING threshold. It is not specified where this check is performed: (a) In orchestrator.py at the end of every ingest run (inline check after computing total_cost_usd). (b) In a separate monitoring module called as a post-ingest hook. (c) In the provider itself before returning results.

The test for this (asserting a WARNING is emitted when total_cost_usd > 1.00) depends on knowing where to capture the log output.

Required action: architect to specify the check location. Recommend: orchestrator inline, after the cost log entry is written. AC-K2-6's test can then mock the cost computation to return 1.01 and assert a WARNING was emitted.

docs.title: Sprint 3 — v0.3 / M3 — Architect PR Review + I2 Gate + Diagram Sign-off
docs.text: Reviewer: solution-architect (claude-opus-4-8) Date: 2026-06-28 Gate: EC-M3-14 (architect review) — the I2 gate (headline invariant of M3) Scope: implementation vs design (v0.3-architecture.md, ADR-0012..0015) + invariant compliance Inputs reviewed: engine.py, cache.py, GET /graph in main.py, models.py, alembic 0003, GraphViewer.tsx, graphStore.ts, graphTransform.ts, no-client-layout.test.ts, eslint.config.js, package.json (+ lockfile), test_graph_engine.py, component.mmd, graph-recompute.mmd, schema.mmd. Code was reviewed read-only; no implementation files were modified.

docs.title: 1. I2 — server-side layout, cached, NO client force layout (HEADLINE) — APPROVE
docs.text:
Server side (engine.py): - Force layout runs ONLY in GraphEngine.recompute() — g_weighted.layout_fruchterman_reingold(weights=..., niter=500). This is the python-igraph (R9, I9) force-directed layout explicitly sanctioned by ADR-0013 §1 as the "FA2-family" layout (layout_fruchterman_reingold / layout_drl). Not a deviation — it is exactly what my own ADR-0013 §1 architect note authorised. Library locked to igraph. ✅ - Determinism: igraph.set_random_number_generator(_SeedableRNG(FA2_SEED)), FA2_SEED=42 (env GRAPH_LAYOUT_SEED override). TestFA2Determinism.test_same_coords_two_runs asserts byte-stable coords across two runs (|Δ| < 1e-9). Matches ADR-0013 §2. ✅ - Coords persisted to pages.x/y via column-level UPDATE pages SET x,y WHERE id inside one transaction with the edges delete-then-insert (_persist_results). Matches ADR-0013 §3/§5. ✅

Cache (cache.py): - GraphCache debounces recompute on data_version bump (notify_bump → fire_at = clock()+window, default 5s; burst collapse resets the deadline). Injectable clock. Bounded queue: max 1 in-flight + 1 pending (_in_flight / _pending), exactly one follow-up after a run. Matches ADR-0014 §2/§3. ✅ - HIT (_marker == current_version) returns the in-process _snapshot with NO recompute — no FA2. ✅ - MISS runs ONE inline recompute behind the shared in-flight guard (no concurrent FA2). ✅

Endpoint (main.py GET /graph): - Synchronous 200. X-Graph-Cache: hit|miss set from the cached flag. No 202 path. Matches ADR-0014 §5/§6 and AQ-v0.3-3. ✅

Frontend (GraphViewer.tsx + graphTransform.ts + graphStore.ts): - buildGraphologyGraph assigns x: node.x, y: node.y verbatim from the server response; no layout function is called anywhere. Sigma is instantiated over the prebuilt graphology graph and renders the fixed positions; no rAF physics loop, no supervisor. ✅ - package.json dependencies = graphology, react, react-dom, sigma, zustand. No forceatlas2 / d3-force / graphology-layout- dependency. Confirmed absent from package.json and* package-lock.json (transitive deps included). ✅ - Enforcement is genuine and double-layered: - eslint no-restricted-imports blocks forceatlas2, d3-force, @antv/layout, graphology-layout (+ random/circular variants) — P0 message. - no-client-layout.test.ts scans BOTH the built dist/assets/*.js bundle AND non-test source imports for 10 forbidden patterns (forceAtlas2, fa2Worker, forceSimulation, d3-force, etc.), failing with a P0 escalation message on any hit. Both layers are real, not stubs. ✅

Verdict: I2 APPROVE. No client-side layout exists or can be introduced without tripping two independent gates. FA2 is server-side, seeded, deterministic, and cached. No P0 block.

docs.title: 2. Edge-weight formula (ADR-0012) — APPROVE
docs.text:
engine.py implements exactly: w = 3.0·direct + 4.0·shared + 1.5·aa + 1.0·same_type (lines computing w in recompute). ✅ - direct: both link orientations counted separately via pair_direct (canonical key). ✅ - shared: len(sources_A ∩ sources_B) over JSONB arrays; NULL→∅. ✅ - aa: Σ_{c∈N(A)∩N(B)} 1/ln(deg(c)), deg>1 guard, over the undirected unweighted resolved-link graph. Candidate-pair restricted (not O(N²)). ✅ - same_type: 1 iff both non-NULL and equal; NULL≠NULL. ✅ - Persisted iff w>0. Pairs canonicalised (smaller UUID first) via _canonical_key. ✅ - signals JSONB persists the per-term breakdown for audit. ✅

test_graph_engine.py asserts exact hand-computed values, not just smoke: - P3–P4 == 7.0 (direct 3 + source 4, AA 0, type 0) — exact. ✅ - signals isolation on P3–P4: direct=3.0, source=4.0, aa=0.0, type=0.0 — exact. ✅ - P1–P2 type signal == 1.0 (both entity); NULL-type pair type signal == 0.0 (NULL≠NULL). ✅ - Lower bounds for AA-bearing pairs: P1–P2 ≥ 11, P1–P4 ≥ 8, P2–P4 ≥ 5. ✅ - P3–P5 absent — and the QA fixture correctly applied the AQ-1 correction (P5 type=person, not concept), so weight=0 → not persisted. The architect-flagged fixture reconciliation was honoured. ✅

Verdict: APPROVE — formula is exact and exactly tested.

docs.title: 3. I1 — incremental (no rescan) — APPROVE
docs.text:
• _load_data issues SQL only against pages + links. No os.walk / glob / vault filesystem access in engine.py or cache.py. ✅
  • _persist_results does column-level UPDATE pages SET x,y per node + DELETE FROM edges WHERE vault_id then re-insert; no full pages-table rewrite. ✅
  • One ingest → that page's rows + ONE debounced recompute. FA2 repositioning all nodes is the documented and CORRECT global-layout semantics (ADR-0013 §4); AC-F4-9 asserts row-level incrementality, not coord stability. This is not an I1 violation. ✅

Verdict: APPROVE.

docs.title: 4. I4 — WebGL / bounded DOM — APPROVE
docs.text:
• Sigma renders into ONE <canvas> mounted in #sigma-container; overlays (StatusBar, Legend, one Tooltip) are a small fixed set of absolutely-positioned divs (~6 DOM nodes, ≪ 20). No per-node DOM. ✅
  • No editor / CodeMirror introduced (correctly deferred to v0.4). ✅
  • @tanstack/virtual-core is present in devDependencies — TanStack Virtual is available for the future node/search lists required by I4 (no lists exist in v0.3, so nothing to virtualise yet). ✅
  • Runtime fps/DOM Playwright assertions (T-E2E-G4-*) are DEFERRED-TO-LIVE — acceptable; the architecture makes G4 true by construction. ✅

Verdict: APPROVE.

docs.title: 5. I3 — Zustand selectors + shallow equality (pre-compliance for v0.4) — APPROVE
docs.text:
• graphStore.ts exports typed selectors; multi-field selectors (selectStatus, selectMeta) are consumed through useShallow hooks (useGraphStatus, useGraphMeta). ✅
  • No component calls useGraphStore() without a selector; GraphViewer subscribes via individual typed selectors. ✅

Verdict: APPROVE (pre-compliance confirmed).

docs.title: 6. Data model / migration / ER (D2) — APPROVE
docs.text:
• models.py: Page.x / Page.y (Double, nullable); new Edge model with vault_id, canonical source_page_id / target_page_id (FK→pages.id), weight (Double, NOT NULL), signals (JSONB nullable), created_at; unique (vault_id, source_page_id, target_page_id); indexes on both endpoints. Matches v0.3-architecture §3 and ADR-0012/0013 exactly. ✅
  • alembic/versions/0003_v0_3_graph.py: adds pages.x/y + creates edges with identical columns, FKs, unique constraint uq_edges_vault_pair, and both endpoint indexes. Migration matches the models — no drift. Single migration = one schema-change event (I8). ✅
  • docs/er/schema.mmd: 6 tables (PAGES, PROVIDER_CONFIG, VAULT_STATE, EDGES, INGEST_RUNS, LINKS), pages.x/y present, EDGES present. ER reflects reality; QA reports make er zero drift. ✅

Verdict: APPROVE.

docs.title: 7. Diagram sign-off (EC-M3-14) — APPROVE (with 2 non-blocking cosmetic notes)
docs.text:
docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd — APPROVE. Server-side seeded FA2, debounce/burst-collapse, bounded queue (1 in-flight + 1 pending), I1 read-only-from-Postgres, 4-signal note (×3/×4/×1.5/×1), one-txn coord+edge write, and both cache-hit/miss paths with correct X-Graph-Cache headers and cached flags. Client addNode with server coords + "NO client layout (ADR-0015)". No v0.4 features. - Non-blocking note D-1: line 49 draws the hit path as GC->>PG: SELECT pages.x/y + edges. The implementation serves a cache HIT from the in-process _snapshot (it does not re-query Postgres on a hit). The diagram's load-bearing claim — "NO FA2, pure read" — is correct; the source of the read (in-memory snapshot vs PG) is a cosmetic simplification. Optional polish for tech-writer; does not misrepresent I2.

docs/architecture/component.mmd — APPROVE. GraphEngine, GraphCache, coord/edge persistence, sigma viewer, graph Zustand store, Postgres pages (+x,y) + edges, I2 header annotation, ADR refs, notify_bump/recompute/GET-graph relations all present and accurate. No v0.4 chat/3-panel/ provider-selector/CodeMirror drawn. - Non-blocking note D-2: the store component gstore is labelled store/graph.ts; the real file is store/graphStore.ts. One-word label drift; optional fix for tech-writer.

Both notes are cosmetic. They do NOT block the gate (the diagrams correctly convey every invariant-relevant fact). Routed to tech-writer as optional polish via handoff.

docs.title: 8. main.py version check — PASS (v0.3 / M3)
docs.text: app/main.py: FastAPI(title="Synapse", version="0.3.0", ...), description references "M3 (graph live, no main-thread freeze)", the 4-signal formula, server-side FA2, GraphCache, and GET /graph. Not the stale "0.2.0 / M2". OpenAPI info.version = "0.3.0" (per DOCS_STATUS §5). No flag. ✅

docs.title: 9. Blocking defects
docs.text: NONE. No invariant is traded. No P0 client-layout. No formula/migration/ER drift. Version correct.

docs.title: 10. ARCHITECT VERDICT
docs.text:
APPROVED.

The v0.3 implementation faithfully realises the approved design and satisfies every in-scope invariant: I2 (headline), I1, I3, I4, I5, I7, I8, I9, I6. The 4-signal additive formula is implemented exactly and asserted with exact hand-computed values; server-side seeded FA2 is the only force layout in the repo and is deterministic and cached; client-layout is forbidden by two independent enforcement layers with zero forbidden dependencies in the lockfile; the data model, migration, and ER are mutually consistent (6 tables, zero drift); and main.py reports v0.3/M3.

Two cosmetic diagram notes (D-1 hit-path read source, D-2 store filename label) are routed to tech-writer as optional polish and do NOT hold the gate.

EC-M3-14 (architect gate): MET. The remaining open items for full-green M3 are the DEFERRED-TO-LIVE runtime/perf/D5 captures (G2 longtask, G4 fps/DOM, screenshots) and the EC-M3-17 human checkpoint — none of which are architect-gated.

— solution-architect · 2026-06-28

docs.title: Sprint 3 — v0.3 — Architecture & Design (F4 graph engine + I2 cache)
docs.text: Milestone: M3 — "Graph live, no main-thread freeze" Author: solution-architect · Date: 2026-06-28 Resolves: AQ-v0.3-1..7 · ADRs: 0012, 0013, 0014, 0015 Invariants owned this sprint: I2 (headline), I4, I1, I3 (pre-compliance)

docs.title: 1. AQ decision table (the 7 questions)
docs.text: AQ P Question DECISION ADR AQ-v0.3-1 P0 Edge-weight combining formula ADDITIVE over undirected page-pair: w = 3·direct_link_count + 4·shared_source_count + 1.5·adamic_adar + 1·same_type. Each term defined exactly (count / set-intersection / igraph AA / 0-1 indicator). Edge persisted iff w>0. 0012 AQ-v0.3-2 P1 FA2 determinism Fixed seed = 42 (GRAPH_LAYOUT_SEED override). Identical topology+weights → identical coords. Determinism assertion added to test_graph_engine.py. 0013 AQ-v0.3-3 P0 GET /graph sync 200 vs async 202 Synchronous 200. Hit → read coords (no FA2). Miss → one inline recompute then return. Never 202. (<2s for <500 nodes.) 0014 AQ-v0.3-4 P1 Incremental semantics "Incremental" = (a) one ingest mutates only that page's rows, (b) recompute is debounced (once per window). FA2 is global → existing coords MAY change; AC-F4-9 asserts row-level incrementality, NOT coord stability. 0013 AQ-v0.3-5 P0 edges table persisted? Yes, persisted in Postgres (edges table, written each recompute, read by GET /graph). Not computed per-request. 0012, 0014 AQ-v0.3-6 P1 Coord storage Columns on pages (pages.x, pages.y DOUBLE PRECISION NULL). Not a join table. Matches BACKLOG. Coord write stays inside the page-row upsert (I1). 0013 AQ-v0.3-7 P1 Cache trigger/detection In-process debounce on data_version bump (notify_bump() from ingest) + polling-reconcile fallback; injectable clock+tick for testing; default 5s window. No LISTEN/NOTIFY. 0014

docs.title: AQ-1 follow-up note to functional-analyst (one-line fixture fix)
docs.text: Per ADR-0012, two same-type pages get the +1 type term. v0.3-stories §1 expects edge P3–P5 weight 0 / absent, but P3 and P5 are both concept → the formula gives weight 1.0 (type only). Fixture correction: either set P5's type ≠ P3's type, or assert P3–P5 weight == 1.0. The formula (ADR-0012) is the source of truth; the QA fixture adjusts.
