The lower-bound assertions for P1–P2 (≥11), P1–P4 (≥8), P2–P4 (≥5) are unaffected.

docs.title: 2. Locked edge-weight formula (F4 / AC-F4-1)
docs.text:
For an unordered pair of live pages (A, B) (graph = pages where deleted_at IS NULL):

weight(A, B) =  3.0 · direct_link_count(A, B)      # resolved links A↔B in either direction (0,1,2)
             +  4.0 · shared_source_count(A, B)     # |set(A.sources) ∩ set(B.sources)|
             +  1.5 · adamic_adar(A, B)             # igraph similarity_inverse_log_weighted, undirected resolved-link graph
             +  1.0 · same_type(A, B)               # 1 iff A.type == B.type AND both non-NULL, else 0
  • direct_link_count: count of non-dangling, resolved links rows with {source_page_id, target_page_id} == {A,B}; each direction counts 1.
  • shared_source_count: intersection size of the pages.sources JSONB arrays (NULL → ∅).
  • adamic_adar: Σ_{c ∈ N(A)∩N(B)} 1/ln(deg(c)) on the undirected unweighted graph built from resolved links; via igraph (I9), no hand-rolled math.
  • same_type: NULL type does NOT match NULL (unknown ≠ a type).
  • Persist iff weight > 0 (sparse table; an AA-only edge between non-linked pages IS kept).

This is deterministic and FA2-seed-independent → byte-stable QA fixtures. Full term table and worked example in ADR-0012.

docs.title: 3.1 pages — two new columns
docs.text: Column Type Null Notes x DOUBLE PRECISION YES FA2 x-coordinate; NULL until first layout (ADR-0013) y DOUBLE PRECISION YES FA2 y-coordinate; NULL until first layout

docs.title: 3.2 edges — new table (ADR-0012 / AQ-5)
docs.text:
Column Type Null Notes id uuid PK NO gen_random_uuid() vault_id string NO scope edges per vault (matches pages/vault_state pattern) source_page_id uuid FK → pages.id NO unordered pair, stored canonically (smaller uuid first) target_page_id uuid FK → pages.id NO weight DOUBLE PRECISION NO ADR-0012 additive weight (> 0) signals JSONB YES per-signal breakdown {direct, source, aa, type} for audit/debug (OPTIONAL but recommended; enables AC-F4-1(e) independent-signal assertions without recomputation) created_at timestamptz NO now()

Constraints: unique (vault_id, source_page_id, target_page_id) on the canonicalised pair; indexes on source_page_id and target_page_id for GET /graph reads and cascade cleanup (F13, v0.5). Recompute replaces this vault's rows (delete-then-insert or upsert) in one txn.

docs.title: 3.3 Migration scope (engineers write the Alembic migration)
docs.text:
• One Alembic migration 0003: ADD COLUMN pages.x, ADD COLUMN pages.y, CREATE TABLE edges (+ FKs, unique constraint, indexes). One schema-change event (mirrors the 0002 pattern).
  • Update backend/app/models.py (add Page.x, Page.y, new Edge model) — this is the D2 source of truth; make er regenerates docs/er/schema.mmd (AC-D2v3-1).
  • No vault/.md change (I5); no Qdrant change (graph is Postgres-only).

Architect sign-off scope note: I have specified the schema; the SQLAlchemy model + Alembic migration + make er regeneration are engineer deliverables (per task brief — no impl code written here). I will review the migration and the regenerated ER for drift at the gate.

docs.title: 4. GraphEngine algorithm (backend/app/graph/engine.py)
docs.text:
recompute(vault_id) — single bounded pass (I7), reads only Postgres (I1, never walks vault):

  1. SELECT id, page_type, sources FROM pages WHERE vault_id=? AND deleted_at IS NULL → nodes.
  2. SELECT source_page_id, target_page_id FROM links WHERE dangling=false AND target_page_id IS NOT NULL → resolved directed edges.
  3. Build undirected unweighted igraph; compute Adamic-Adar (similarity_inverse_log_weighted).
  4. For each candidate pair (sharing a source OR a neighbour OR a direct link OR same type), compute the 4-signal additive weight (ADR-0012); keep weight > 0.
  5. Build the weighted igraph; run seeded FA2 (seed=42, weights as edge weights) → coords.
  6. One transaction: batch UPDATE pages SET x,y WHERE id=?; replace edges rows.
  7. Stamp the cache marker with the current data_version; log node/edge count + duration (I7).

Complexity: N nodes, L resolved links, P candidate pairs (≪ N²; only neighbour/source/ type-sharing pairs). Weights O(L+P); FA2 O(iters·(N+E)); persistence O(N+E). Sub-2s for the target <500-node vault → supports synchronous GET /graph.

Incremental-friendliness (I1): the engine consumes DB tables that the incremental indexer already maintains; it issues column-level coord upserts, not a full-table rewrite. One ingest = +1 page row + its links; the global relayout that follows is debounced and runs once per window (ADR-0013 §4).

docs.title: 5. GraphCache mechanism (backend/app/graph/cache.py) — the I2 cache
docs.text:
• State: persisted coords (pages.x/y + edges) tagged by a marker = the data_version they were computed from. hit ⇔ vault_state.data_version == marker.
  • Trigger: notify_bump() from the ingest path after a data_version +1 (in-process), plus a polling-reconcile fallback. No LISTEN/NOTIFY.
  • Debounce: on bump set fire_at = clock.now() + window (default 5s). A bump within the window resets fire_at (burst collapse → one recompute, AC-F16db-2).
  • Bounded queue (I7): at most one recompute in-flight; a bump during a run sets a single pending flag → exactly one follow-up after completion (AC-F16db-3). Queue depth ≤ 1.
  • Testability (no sleeps): injectable clock.now() + a tick() that fires when clock.now() >= fire_at and no run is in-flight. QA advances the fake clock and drives tick(); asserts recompute call counts (1 for AC-F16db-1/2; 2 for AC-F16db-3).
  • GET /graph hit: pure read, cached:true, X-Graph-Cache: hit, no FA2.
  • GET /graph miss: one inline synchronous recompute (cold start / stale), cached:false, X-Graph-Cache: miss. Shares the in-flight guard with the background path → no concurrent FA2.

Full state machine and the in-flight/pending rules: ADR-0014.

docs.title: 6. GET /graph API contract (D4 / AC-F4-3, AC-F4-4, AC-D4v3-1)
docs.text:
GET /graph → 200 synchronous. Header X-Graph-Cache: hit|miss.

{
  "nodes": [
    {"id": "uuid-string", "title": "string", "type": "string|null",
     "x": 0.0, "y": 0.0, "size": 1.0, "degree": 3}
  ],
  "edges": [
    {"source": "uuid-string", "target": "uuid-string", "weight": 11.0}
  ],
  "data_version": 7,
  "cached": true
}
  • nodes: one per live page with non-NULL x/y. Required: id, title, type, x, y. Optional rendering hints (derived, not persisted): size (monotonic in degree, default 1.0), degree (incident-edge count).
  • edges: one per undirected edges row with live endpoints. Required: source, target, weight.
  • data_version (int): the version the coords correspond to. cached (bool): mirrors the header.

AC-F4-3 asserts the required core fields + types. AC-F4-4 asserts the second same-version call returns cached:true + X-Graph-Cache: hit with no second FA2 (mock recompute count == 1).

docs.title: 7. sigma.js client contract (I2 / I4 / I3 — ADR-0015)
docs.text:
• Single read-only route (/). React 19 + Vite + TS strict; sigma.js (WebGL) over graphology.
  • On mount: one fetch('/graph') → pure transform → graph.addNode(id,{x,y,label,type,size}) with server coords + graph.addEdge(source,target,{weight}). sigma renders into ONE <canvas>.
  • HARD RULE (P0 block): no graphology-layout-forceatlas2, ForceAtlas2, d3-force, forceSimulation, @antv/layout, or any rAF loop that mutates node x/y — in source or dist/. Enforced by the bundle-grep test (AC-FE-2) and an architect bundle review before the G2 test is written. Any client-layout PR is escalated and blocked.
  • Interaction: node click → GET /pages/{id} → read-only title/type tooltip. No editor (no CodeMirror, I4), no chat, no provider selector (v0.4).
  • Zustand (I3): selector subscriptions + shallow equality; no whole-store subscription; pure API→graphology transform unit-tested in isolation (AC-FE-5).
  • Lists: none in v0.3. Any future node/search list > 50 items MUST use TanStack Virtual (I4).
  • G2 by construction: no client layout → only one fetch + a small pure transform + GPU draw; no main-thread long task > 50ms.
  • G4 by construction: all nodes drawn in one WebGL canvas → DOM count in the graph container < 20 regardless of size; precomputed coords → pure GPU redraw → ≥60fps for 200 nodes/500 edges.

docs.title: 8. Invariant compliance statement
docs.text: Inv How the v0.3 design guarantees it I2 FA2 exists ONLY in graph/engine.py (igraph, server-side). Coords persisted in pages.x/y. Recompute debounced on data_version (GraphCache). GET /graph hit = pure read, X-Graph-Cache: hit, no FA2. Client renders precomputed coords in WebGL; client-layout code is a P0 block enforced by static bundle grep + architect bundle review. (ADR-0013, 0014, 0015) I4 sigma draws all nodes in ONE WebGL canvas → bounded DOM (<20). No CodeMirror/editor introduced. Future lists virtualized (TanStack Virtual). (ADR-0015) I1 GraphEngine reads pages+links tables only — never walks vault/. Coord write is a column-level upsert on existing rows. One ingest mutates only that page's rows; relayout is debounced, not per-file. (ADR-0013) I3 Zustand selectors + shallow equality, pure transform — pre-compliance for v0.4 chat. (ADR-0015) I5 Graph state is Postgres-only (x/y columns + edges table). No .md/frontmatter writes by the engine. test_obsidian_check.py (15) stays green. (ADR-0013) I7 recompute is a single pass; cache queue bounded to one in-flight + one pending; bumps during a run collapse to one follow-up. Engine logs node/edge count + duration. (ADR-0014) I8 D1 (component.mmd), D2 (edges + pages.x/y via make er), D3 (graph-recompute.mmd), D4 (GET /graph in openapi.json), D5 (Playwright screenshots) all in scope this sprint. I9 python-igraph (R9) for AA + FA2; sigma.js (R10) for WebGL; TanStack Virtual (R11) for any list; Playwright (R16) for D5. No vis.js/cytoscape/d3-force/networkx/graph-tool (AC-F4-2c static check). I6 No inference calls in the graph path; InferenceProvider ABC untouched; I6 static guard stays green.

docs.title: 9. EC coverage / risks
docs.text:
All EC-M3-1..15 are satisfiable by this design. No EC is un-satisfiable. Two items to flag:

  • EC-M3-1 / AC-F4-1 fixture (P3–P5): the stories fixture expects weight 0 for two same-type pages, but the locked formula gives +1 (type). Requires the one-line QA fixture correction in §1 (set P5 type ≠ P3, or assert weight == 1.0). Not a design defect — a fixture/formula reconciliation. Flagged to functional-analyst.
  • EC-M3-3 cold-start miss latency: a first-ever GET /graph runs FA2 inline (<2s for target sizes). If a real vault grows beyond ~1–2k nodes, the synchronous miss could exceed a comfortable request budget; the background debounce keeps the common case a hit. Revisit async/202 only if measured latency demands it (out of v0.3 scope; explicitly deferred).

No invariant is traded for convenience in this design.

docs.title: 10. F4 / I2 SIGN-OFF
docs.text:
APPROVED to implement. The 4-signal additive formula, server-side seeded FA2 via igraph, pages.x/y + edges persistence, the dataVersion-debounced GraphCache with a bounded queue and injectable clock, the synchronous GET /graph contract with X-Graph-Cache, and the no-client-layout sigma contract together satisfy I2 (headline), I4, I1, I3, I5, I7, I8, I9, I6.

Conditions on the gate (architect review, EC-M3-14): 1. Engineer wires the make er regeneration so docs/er/schema.mmd matches the live schema (pages.x/y + edges) with zero diff (AC-D2v3-1). 2. Architect reviews the frontend package.json + viewer bundle for client-layout code before the G2 Playwright test is authored (v0.3-scope §10). 3. Functional-analyst applies the AQ-1 fixture correction (§1) before AC-F4-1 values are frozen.

docs.title: 3. Anti-scope-creep check
docs.text:
Scope for v0.3 was locked in docs/sprints/v0.3-scope.md §2. The following confirms no out-of-scope work was built.

In-scope work delivered (confirmed): - F4: GraphEngine (4-signal weights, FA2 via igraph, pages.x/y, edges table, GET /graph) — DELIVERED - F4 (graph cache): GraphCache (dataVersion-debounced recompute, X-Graph-Cache header) — DELIVERED - F16 (partial — debounce wiring only): data_version bump → debounced recompute activation — DELIVERED - Frontend thin viewer: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript + sigma.js, single-route, no extras — DELIVERED - D3 (graph-recompute.mmd): new sequence diagram — DELIVERED - D5 (Playwright harness): written; capture deferred-to-live — DELIVERED - D1/D2/D4 continuous updates — DELIVERED - D7 (ADR-0012..0015): 4 new ADRs — DELIVERED

Explicitly confirmed NOT built: - F1 (3-panel shell): not present. Frontend is a single-route sigma viewer. CLEAN. - F6 (chat): no chat endpoint, no conversation persistence, no message history. CLEAN. - F7/F8 (think display, LaTeX): no chat surface. CLEAN. - F17 UI (Provider Selector dropdown): no dropdown UI. Backend F17 complete since v0.2. CLEAN. - F14 (configurable context window): no chat UI. CLEAN. - F5 (4-phase RAG retrieval): not implemented. CLEAN. - F10 (Deep Research): not implemented. CLEAN. - F9 (HITL review queue): not implemented. CLEAN. - F16 (rest — i18n, settings, GFM, timeout): not implemented. Only debounce wiring delivered. CLEAN. - D6 (USER.md / DEPLOY.md full drafts): not required at M3. CLEAN. - PWA manifest, Tauri shell: not added. CLEAN.

NB hardening tasks (in-scope tech-debt, not creep): NB-1 (httpx.HTTPStatusError fallback), NB-2 (T-CQ-009 import-line scope), NB-4 (CLI cost logging), and NB-5 (OLLAMA_URL docker-compose) were all formally carried from the M2 sign-off (docs/sprints/v0.2-pm-signoff.md §5) and explicitly listed in v0.3-scope.md §7. They are hardening tasks tied to I7 and I6, not new feature scope.

mypy-target fix: the TRACEABILITY.md notes AQ resolutions within v0.3. Any mypy target adjustments are within the existing codebase quality gate (AC-DC-5, I6), not new feature work.

Verdict: ZERO scope creep detected.

docs.title: 6. Human checkpoint conditions (EC-M3-17)
docs.text:
Sprint 4 (v0.4) MUST NOT begin until EC-M3-17 is satisfied by Emanuele. The following is the definitive list of remaining conditions and the exact run commands.

Condition 1 — Start the stack and seed the fixture: 

# From project root on TrueNAS (or dev machine with Postgres + Qdrant + Ollama accessible)
docker compose up -d
cd backend
python scripts/seed_graph_fixture.py --nodes 200 --edges 500 --db-url $DATABASE_URL

Condition 2 — Run the Playwright suite (captures D5 PNGs + asserts G2/G4): 

cd frontend
npm run dev &               # or: npm run build && uvicorn serves static (see Makefile)
npx playwright test e2e/graph-perf.spec.ts --config playwright.config.ts
This run must produce: - docs/screens/graph-viewer-initial.png (non-empty) — satisfies EC-M3-11 / AC-D5-1 - docs/screens/graph-viewer-node-selected.png (non-empty) — satisfies EC-M3-11 / AC-D5-2 - G2 runtime: T-E2E-G2-001 PASS (no longtask >50ms) — satisfies EC-M3-5 runtime component - G2 cache: T-E2E-G2-002 PASS (X-Graph-Cache: hit on second GET /graph) — satisfies EC-M3-3 runtime confirmation - G4 DOM: T-E2E-G4-001 PASS (graph container ≤3 child DOM elements) — satisfies EC-M3-6 DOM component - G4 fps: T-E2E-G4-002 PASS (≥55fps mean rAF over 200-node graph) — satisfies EC-M3-6 fps component

Condition 3 — View the sigma viewer in a browser with live vault data: Open http://localhost:5173 (or the Cloudflare Tunnel URL) in a browser. Confirm: - The sigma canvas loads and nodes/edges are visible. - Node click shows a page title in the tooltip or drawer. - No JavaScript console errors. - No visible force-layout animation playing in the browser (all positions are precomputed and static on load).

Condition 4 — Commit the Playwright screenshots: 

git add docs/screens/
git commit -m "docs(d5): Playwright screenshots from M3 live run [F4]"

Condition 5 — Confirm EC-M2-17 was satisfied before Sprint 3 began: Per v0.3-scope.md §11, Sprint 3 feature code was blocked until EC-M2-17 (live TrueNAS smoke) was confirmed by Emanuele. Verify this confirmation is on record (docs/sprints/v0.2-pm-signoff.md §6).

Once all 5 conditions are confirmed by Emanuele, EC-M3-17 is MET and the orchestrator may advance to Sprint 4.

docs.title: 8. MILESTONE VERDICT
docs.text:
MILESTONE M3: MET-PENDING-HUMAN-CHECKPOINT

All architectural, functional, and documentary exit criteria for M3 are either fully verified now or have their verification harness written and deferred to the live environment — exactly the same model as v0.1 (docs/sprints/v0.1-m1-closure.md) and v0.2 (docs/sprints/v0.2-pm-signoff.md §6), both of which were subsequently confirmed live with no code changes required.

The deferred items (G2 longtask, G4 fps/DOM, D5 PNGs, EC-M3-17 browser viewing) require Emanuele's M2/browser environment. They are NOT indicative of code defects; the architect explicitly confirmed "the architecture makes G4 true by construction" and the static/two-layered I2 enforcement (ESLint + bundle scan) makes G2 true by construction.

No invariant is traded. No blocking defect exists. The 4 required sign-offs (QA, Architect, Tech-writer, PM) are all positive.

Remaining conditions before Sprint 4 may begin: 1. Emanuele runs the Playwright suite against the live stack (run commands in §6). 2. Playwright PNGs committed to docs/screens/ (closes EC-M3-11). 3. G2 runtime and G4 fps/DOM assertions pass in the browser (closes EC-M3-5 runtime and EC-M3-6). 4. Emanuele views the sigma viewer with live vault data and confirms no errors (closes EC-M3-7 and EC-M3-17). 5. Orchestrator records EC-M3-17 as satisfied and unlocks Sprint 4.

Sprint 4 scope (v0.4 / M4 — "Usable and fluid") is pre-defined in BACKLOG.md: F1 (3-panel shell), F6 (chat), F17 UI (provider selector), F7, F8, F14, F16(rest), G3, D5 refresh, D6 drafts.

— product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) | 2026-06-28 | EC-M3-16

docs.title: QA Report — Sprint 3 / v0.3 / M3 "Graph live, no main-thread freeze"
docs.text: Date: 2026-06-28 QA Engineer: qa-test-engineer (claude-sonnet-4-6) Sprint scope: docs/sprints/v0.3-scope.md Verdict: MET-PENDING-LIVE-PERF (see §7 for exact gap list)

docs.title: G1 — No full-rescan (I1) — PROVEN-NOW
docs.text:
Evidence: - engine.py:_load_data() — SQL queries only on pages + links tables; zero os.walk, pathlib.glob, or vault filesystem access (code inspection confirmed). - engine.py:_persist_results() — targeted UPDATE pages SET x=..., y=... per node; DELETE FROM edges WHERE vault_id=... + bulk INSERT edges. No full table truncate. - T-INC-GRAPH-001: 1 new file → row count 2→3 (not full delete+recreate). - T-INC-GRAPH-002: pre-existing page rows survive new ingest. - T-INC-017 (test_ingest_incremental.py): watcher.py has no os.walk/.rglob() calls.

Verdict: PROVEN-NOW (no live infra needed)

docs.title: G2 — Graph layout cached, no main-thread freeze (I2) — PROVEN-NOW (static) + DEFERRED-TO-LIVE (runtime perf)
docs.text:
Proven-now (static/unit evidence): - 13 GraphCache tests (T-GCACHE-001..013): debounce collapses bursts to 1 recompute; HIT on same data_version skips FA2; injectable clock proves no real sleep in tests. - 13 GET /graph API tests (T-GRAPI-001..013): X-Graph-Cache: hit on second call; X-Graph-Cache: miss on stale version; full schema on HTTP 200. - 22 no-client-layout tests (T-NCL-001..022): bundle scan + source import scan confirm graphology-layout-forceatlas2, d3-force, ForceAtlas2, fa2Worker, and 6 other forbidden patterns are ABSENT from both the built bundle and all non-test source files. - GraphViewer.tsx: sigma instantiated with new Sigma(graph, container, {...}) using pre-computed server x/y; no layout algorithm called client-side (code inspection). - graphStore.ts: all subscriptions use typed selectors + useShallow; no whole-store subscription (I3 pre-compliance also confirmed).

Deferred-to-live (runtime): - T-E2E-G2-001: Playwright PerformanceObserver asserting no longtask >50ms. Run: cd frontend && npx playwright test e2e/graph-perf.spec.ts --config playwright.config.ts - T-E2E-G2-002: X-Graph-Cache: hit assertion via Playwright page.request.get(). Same run command.

Verdict: PROVEN-NOW for static/architectural proof; DEFERRED-TO-LIVE for browser runtime measurement

docs.title: G3 — Streaming / no per-token heavy work (I3) — N/A UNTIL v0.4
docs.text:
G3 applies to the chat streaming path, which is not implemented in v0.3 (v0.4 deliverable). Pre-compliance confirmed: - graphStore.ts uses typed selectors everywhere; useGraphStatus() and useGraphMeta() use useShallow(). - No component calls useGraphStore() without a selector parameter. - Pre-compliance noted in TRACEABILITY.md as T-GSTORE-001..016.

Verdict: N/A (v0.4) — pre-compliance CONFIRMED by code inspection

docs.title: G4 — WebGL ≥60fps, bounded DOM (I4) — DEFERRED-TO-LIVE
docs.text:
Static evidence (pre-conditions confirmed): - GraphViewer.tsx: single sigma.js canvas element; no per-node DOM elements created. - DOM structure comment in GraphViewer.tsx: <div#graph-root> + <div#sigma-container> + <canvas> + overlay divs ≈ 6 total DOM elements (not 200+). - sigma.js uses WebGL (Canvas API with WebGL context), not SVG or Canvas2D+JS layout.

Deferred-to-live: - T-E2E-G4-001: graph container ≤3 child DOM elements (Playwright locator assertion). - T-E2E-G4-002: 60-frame rAF timing with 200-node fixture → mean ≤18.18ms (≥55fps). - Fixture seeder: python backend/scripts/seed_graph_fixture.py --nodes 200 --edges 500 --db-url $DATABASE_URL (seed=42, spiral layout, no FA2 needed).

Run command: cd frontend && npx playwright test e2e/graph-perf.spec.ts --config playwright.config.ts

Verdict: DEFERRED-TO-LIVE
