docs.title: Sprint 3 — v0.3 — Scope Lock
docs.text: Milestone: M3 — "Graph live, no main-thread freeze" Branch: sprint/v0.3 Locked by: product-manager on 2026-06-28 Status: LOCKED — no changes without PM + orchestrator approval Prerequisite: M2 DONE-PENDING-HUMAN-CHECKPOINT (docs/sprints/v0.2-pm-signoff.md) Sprint 3 MUST NOT begin until EC-M2-17 is satisfied (Emanuele's live TrueNAS confirmation).

docs.title: 1. Sprint objective
docs.text:
Make the knowledge graph live with no main-thread layout freeze. This sprint introduces the first frontend code in the project — a thin, focused sigma.js viewer page, not the full 3-panel shell (F1 is v0.4). The headline invariant is I2: FA2 layout runs server-side via igraph, coordinates are stored in Postgres, and the client renders precomputed coords in WebGL only. The browser NEVER runs a force-directed layout algorithm on the main thread.

This is also the first sprint where performance gates G1–G4 are mandatory acceptance criteria per the QA charter.

docs.title: 2. In-scope feature IDs
docs.text: Feature ID What is in scope for v0.3 Why needed for M3 F4 Knowledge graph engine: 4-signal edge weighting (direct-link ×3, source-overlap ×4, Adamic-Adar ×1.5, type-affinity ×1); server-side FA2 layout via igraph; node/edge coordinates stored in Postgres (pages.x, pages.y, edges table with weight column); GET /graph REST endpoint returning nodes + edges + precomputed coords; sigma.js WebGL viewer in a standalone viewer page The defining feature of M3 and the headline I2 implementation. Builds on the links table (K5) delivered in v0.2. F4 (graph cache) backend/app/graph/cache.py: dataVersion-debounced recompute — FA2 only re-runs when dataVersion bumps (not per file change); recompute is off the hot path; cached coords served directly from Postgres I2 mandates the layout is CACHED and debounced. Without this, every GET /graph would recompute, which is the bottleneck F4 is designed to fix. F16 (partial — debounce wiring) Wire the dataVersion bump (already in vault_state from v0.1) to trigger a debounced graph recompute job. The debounce window prevents bursts of ingests from firing repeated FA2 runs. This is the v0.3 activation of F16's dataVersion mechanism introduced in v0.1 F16's dataVersion was seeded in v0.1 precisely for this. Without the debounce wiring, I2 cannot be satisfied. This is a partial F16 activation, not the full F16 (i18n, settings, GFM — all v0.4). Frontend shell (thin viewer) A minimal React 19 + Vite + TypeScript frontend serving a single route: the sigma.js graph viewer. No 3-panel layout (F1 is v0.4). No chat UI. No CodeMirror editor. No provider selector. The viewer fetches GET /graph on load and renders nodes + edges using sigma.js WebGL. Node click triggers GET /pages/{id} and displays the page title in a tooltip or side drawer (read-only). TanStack Virtual is NOT required for this viewer (no long list). The sigma viewer is the only user-visible output of M3. Without a frontend, the graph has no surface and D5 cannot be produced. Keeping it thin enforces the F1 boundary and prevents scope creep. D3 (graph recompute sequence) docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd — Mermaid sequenceDiagram: watcher ingest → dataVersion bump → debounce timer fires → GraphEngine.recompute() → igraph FA2 → Postgres coords written → GET /graph serves cached result. Annotate: recompute is NOT triggered on every graph GET; only on dataVersion change with debounce. Required by CLAUDE.md §9 (D3 from v0.3 for graph). I8 mandates this before M3 sign-off. D5 (first UI screenshots) Playwright E2E test captures PNG screenshots of the sigma viewer with a real (or fixture) graph loaded: (1) initial load state, (2) graph rendered with nodes and edges visible, (3) node-click tooltip/drawer visible. Saved to docs/screens/. First sprint where D5 is required per CLAUDE.md §9. Cannot be satisfied without a working viewer. D1/D2/D4 (continuous updates) D1: update component.mmd to add GraphEngine + cache layer. D2: regenerate ER diagram via make er after adding x, y columns to pages and the edges table. D4: update openapi.json to include GET /graph endpoint with typed response. I8: every sprint must keep D-artifacts current. Schema changes in v0.3 require D2 update. New endpoint requires D4 update.

docs.title: Scope rationale — minimal frontend decision
docs.text: The sigma viewer is the minimum frontend needed to satisfy M3's milestone deliverable ("sigma viewer") and to produce D5 (UI screenshots). It is scoped as a single-page viewer with no routing, no editor, no chat, no tree panel, and no provider selector. Engineers must NOT add CodeMirror, the 3-panel shell, or any chat UI this sprint. Those belong to F1 and F6 (v0.4).

docs.title: 3. Tempting-but-out-of-scope feature IDs
docs.text:
Any engineer proposing one of the items below must escalate to orchestrator before touching it. The PM will block and flag scope creep.

Feature ID Why tempting Why blocked this sprint Target sprint F1 — 3-panel shell Viewer is a frontend; adding panels seems natural Full 3-panel (tree / chat / preview), resizable, activity panel — this is a full sprint of UX work. v0.4 milestone is specifically "Usable & fluid". Adding it now dilutes focus on I2. v0.4 F6 — Multi-conversation chat Frontend exists now; chat endpoint could be added chat() is stubbed on all 3 providers (confirmed v0.2). No chat endpoint, no conversation persistence, no message history. v0.4. v0.4 F7 / F8 — think display / LaTeX Chat-adjacent No chat in v0.3. v0.4 F17 (UI) — Provider Selector A dropdown could be added to the thin viewer Provider Selector UI is a v0.4 deliverable; wiring it to a pre-F1 viewer would require reworking the layout in v0.4 anyway. Backend F17 is complete. v0.4 F14 — Configurable context window Context budget in provider No chat UI in v0.3. v0.4 F5 — 4-phase RAG retrieval Graph expansion is ready once F4 exists F5 requires the full retrieval pipeline wired to chat (F6). Graph expansion is a retrieval phase, not a graph display feature. v0.5 F10 — Deep Research SearXNG is running ops/deep_research.py is v0.5. v0.5 F9 — HITL review queue Graph surfaces contradictions ops/review.py is v0.5. v0.5 F12 / F13 / F11 Multi-format, cascade delete, clipper All v0.5/v0.6. v0.5/v0.6 F2 — purpose.md full injection Viewer context F2 full feature is v0.6. The plain-string read of purpose.md in the orchestrator (permitted in v0.2) continues unchanged in v0.3. v0.6 F15 (rest) — PWA + Tauri There is now a frontend PWA and Tauri packaging are v0.6 work. The v0.3 frontend is served by Vite dev server or a static build step; no PWA manifest, no Tauri shell. v0.6 F16 (rest) — i18n, settings, GFM F16 partially activated Only the dataVersion debounce wiring is in scope. i18n (IT/EN), settings persistence, GFM rendering, multi-provider chat timeout — all v0.4. v0.4 D6 — User/Deploy guides Frontend exists D6 full drafts are v0.4. DEPLOY.md remains DRAFT-tagged. v0.4 D7 — new ADRs Graph decisions could be ADRs D7 is continuous but not mandated to produce new ADRs unless new architectural decisions are made (e.g., choice of igraph vs. graph-tool, FA2 debounce strategy). One ADR expected if a significant graph-layout decision is made; not a mandatory count this sprint. continuous

docs.title: 4. Invariants in force — I2 and I4 are first-class this sprint
docs.text: All 9 invariants apply. I2 and I4 are the DEFINING acceptance gates for M3.

docs.title: I2 — GRAPH LAYOUT IS SERVER-SIDE AND CACHED (headline invariant)
docs.text:
This is the raison d'etre of M3. Every component of I2 must be demonstrably satisfied:

  1. FA2 runs ONLY in backend/app/graph/engine.py via igraph (Python). NEVER in the browser.
  2. Coordinates (x, y per node) are written to Postgres after every successful FA2 run.
  3. Recompute is triggered ONLY by a dataVersion bump, with a debounce window. The second GET /graph call (with same dataVersion) serves cached coordinates — no re-layout.
  4. The GET /graph response is a static payload of precomputed coordinates. The client calls sigma.js graph.addNode(id, {x, y, ...}) with these coords; it does NOT call any sigma or graphology layout function (ForceAtlas2, random, circular, or any other).
  5. Violation of I2 is a P0 blocker. Any PR that introduces client-side layout code — even temporarily — MUST be escalated to solution-architect before merge.

docs.title: I4 — EDITOR IS CODEMIRROR 6; LONG LISTS VIRTUALIZED
docs.text:
The sigma.js viewer itself renders in WebGL (a canvas element), not as DOM nodes per graph node. This satisfies the "no unbounded DOM growth" principle of I4 for the graph.

In v0.3 the only list in the viewer is potentially a search/filter result list if one is added. If any list of nodes/pages is rendered in the DOM (e.g., a sidebar), it MUST be virtualized with TanStack Virtual if it can exceed 50 items. For the thin v0.3 viewer, if no such list is present, I4 is satisfied by default.

No CodeMirror editor is introduced this sprint (no editor surface exists yet).

docs.title: I1 — INCREMENTAL INDEX ONLY
docs.text: The graph engine reads the links table and pages table; it does NOT rescan vault/. Coord updates are column-level upserts on the pages table (x, y columns). This must be verified by a pytest integration test: ingest one file → coords appear for affected nodes only.

docs.title: I3 — NO PER-TOKEN HEAVY WORK IN CHAT
docs.text: No chat UI in v0.3. I3 is passively satisfied. Any Zustand store introduced for the sigma viewer must use selectors + shallow equality — no unconditional global re-renders on graph state changes.

docs.title: I5 — OBSIDIAN COMPATIBILITY
docs.text: The graph coord columns (x, y on pages) and edges table are Postgres-only — no new fields written to vault/ .md files. vault/wiki/ must remain a valid Obsidian vault. Verified by the existing test_obsidian_check.py suite (15 tests); confirm it stays green.

docs.title: I6 — PLUGGABLE INFERENCE
docs.text: No new inference calls are added by the graph engine. Graph computation is deterministic (igraph FA2) — not LLM-driven. The InferenceProvider ABC is not touched this sprint. The I6 static guard (T-CQ-009 scoped to import lines — NB-2 fix, see §7) must remain green.

docs.title: I7 — LOOPS ARE BOUNDED
docs.text: The graph recompute is a SINGLE PASS (not a loop), but the debounce and the background job queue must be bounded: max one queued recompute job at a time (subsequent dataVersion bumps during an in-progress recompute collapse into one follow-up run). Log recompute duration and node/edge count per run.

docs.title: I8 — DOCS-AS-DoD
docs.text: D3 (graph recompute sequence), D5 (Playwright screenshots), D1 update, D2 update, D4 update are all mandatory before M3 sign-off. The sprint cannot be declared done without them.

docs.title: I9 — DO NOT REINVENT
docs.text: igraph is the specified graph-tool backend (CLAUDE.md §6 stack, R9). sigma.js is the specified WebGL renderer (R10). TanStack Virtual for virtualized lists (R11). Do not introduce other graph libraries (vis.js, d3-force, cytoscape). Playwright for D5 (R16).

docs.title: 5. Performance gates G1–G4
docs.text: Per the QA charter, performance gates are mandatory from v0.3 onward. Each gate maps to a concrete, Playwright-testable assertion.

docs.title: G1 — No full-rescan (I1 proof)
docs.text:
Definition: a file change updates only the affected Postgres records (pages, links, coords) and Qdrant vectors. No full vault/ directory walk triggered by ingest.

Status in v0.3: G1 was proven green in v0.1/v0.2. The graph engine must not introduce a new rescan path. Verify: after adding a single file, only that file's page row and related link rows are updated; unrelated pages are not touched.

Test: test_incremental_graph_update — ingest file A (N nodes written), ingest file B, assert only B's node coord is new/updated; A's coords are unchanged.

docs.title: G2 — Graph layout cached; no main-thread long task on second open (I2 proof)
docs.text:
Definition: opening the sigma viewer for the first time triggers one FA2 layout computation (server-side). Opening it a second time (same dataVersion) serves precomputed coords with no layout recompute.

Playwright test (mandatory for M3 sign-off): 1. Load the sigma viewer → wait for graph to render. 2. Capture Playwright performance trace: assert no JavaScript long task >50ms on the main thread during graph render (all heavy work is precomputed; sigma.js renders from coords). 3. Reload the viewer → assert GET /graph response includes a cache-hit header or the same coordinates are returned without a new FA2 run (verify via backend log or response header X-Graph-Cache: hit). 4. Specifically assert: no call to graphology-layout-forceatlas2, sigma.ForceAtlas2, or any requestAnimationFrame-based layout loop is present in the client-side JavaScript bundle (static bundle analysis or Playwright network check).

docs.title: G3 — Streaming; no per-token heavy parse (I3 proof)
docs.text:
Status: chat streaming is NOT in scope for v0.3 (F6 is v0.4). G3 is formally N/A this sprint. It becomes mandatory from v0.4 onward when chat streaming is introduced.

Placeholder: QA must confirm at v0.4 that the Zustand store used in v0.3 for graph state does not inadvertently introduce patterns that would violate I3 when chat state is added. This is a pre-emptive design review task for v0.4, not a v0.3 gate.

docs.title: G4 — WebGL rendering at ≥60fps for large graphs; bounded DOM (I4 proof)
docs.text:
Definition: the sigma.js viewer renders a graph of at least 200 nodes and 500 edges at ≥60fps (16ms frame time) without adding unbounded DOM nodes.

Playwright test (mandatory for M3 sign-off): 1. Seed a fixture graph of 200 nodes / 500 edges in Postgres (test helper generates coords directly — no FA2 required for this perf fixture). 2. Load the sigma viewer. Assert the canvas element is present (sigma renders in a single WebGL canvas — NOT one DOM element per node). 3. Use Playwright's page.evaluate to read sigma's renderer FPS counter or measure requestAnimationFrame timing over 60 frames: assert mean frame time ≤16ms. 4. Assert DOM node count in the graph container does not exceed a fixed ceiling (e.g., <20 DOM elements regardless of graph size — all nodes are WebGL drawn).

If the project's test fixture graph from v0.2 is <200 nodes, a synthetic fixture must be generated. QA engineer owns this fixture.

docs.title: 6. M3 exit criteria
docs.text:
The following is the definitive checklist for M3 sign-off. All items must be MET. Any NOT MET item holds the sign-off.

EC Feature IDs Description EC-M3-1 F4 4-signal edge weighting implemented in graph/engine.py: direct-link ×3, source-overlap ×4, Adamic-Adar ×1.5, type-affinity ×1. Unit test asserts all 4 weights applied correctly on a fixture graph. EC-M3-2 F4 FA2 layout runs server-side via igraph. Coords written to Postgres (pages.x, pages.y columns; edges table with weight). make er regenerated. Verified by test_graph_layout_server_side. EC-M3-3 F4, F16, I2 Recompute is debounced on dataVersion: second GET /graph with same dataVersion returns cached coords without re-running FA2. Backend emits X-Graph-Cache: hit header on cache-hit. Test: two sequential GET /graph calls → only one FA2 invocation logged. EC-M3-4 F4 GET /graph REST endpoint returns JSON with: nodes (list of {id, title, type, x, y}), edges (list of {source, target, weight}), data_version (int), cached (bool). OpenAPI updated (D4). EC-M3-5 F4, I2, G2 Sigma.js viewer renders precomputed coords in WebGL. No call to any client-side force-layout function (static bundle check). No JavaScript long task >50ms on main thread during graph render (Playwright perf trace). EC-M3-6 F4, I4, G4 Graph of 200 nodes / 500 edges renders at ≥60fps. DOM node count in graph container <20 (all nodes WebGL drawn in sigma canvas). Playwright assertions per G4 definition. EC-M3-7 Frontend thin viewer Sigma viewer page loads via make dev or make build. Node click shows page title (tooltip or side drawer, read-only). No chat, no editor, no 3-panel shell. EC-M3-8 I1, G1 Graph coord update is incremental: ingest one new file → only that file's node coords added/updated in Postgres; no full table rewrite. test_incremental_graph_update green. EC-M3-9 I5, K7 vault/wiki/ remains a valid Obsidian vault. test_obsidian_check.py (15 tests) all green after v0.3 schema changes. No new fields written to .md frontmatter by the graph engine. EC-M3-10 D3, I8 docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd present, valid Mermaid sequenceDiagram, shows debounce + cache hit path + miss path. Reviewed by tech-writer and architect. EC-M3-11 D5, I8 Playwright captures ≥2 PNG screenshots to docs/screens/: (1) graph viewer initial state, (2) graph with nodes/edges rendered. Saved and committed. EC-M3-12 D1, D2, D4, I8 component.mmd updated (GraphEngine + cache); ER diagram regenerated via make er (pages.x/y + edges table); openapi.json updated (GET /graph). Zero drift verified by tech-writer. EC-M3-13 QA gate Full pytest suite green (0 failures, 0 regressions from v0.2). G2 and G4 Playwright tests green. ruff + black + mypy clean. EC-M3-14 Architect gate Architect reviews graph/engine.py, graph/cache.py, GET /graph endpoint, sigma viewer bundle (confirms no client-side layout). ADR for any new graph-architecture decisions approved. EC-M3-15 Tech-writer gate D3 + D5 + D1/D2/D4 updates verified consistent with implementation. DOCS_STATUS.md updated. EC-M3-16 PM gate This document. All EC-M3-1..15 MET. NB follow-up dispositions actioned (see §7). EC-M3-17 Human checkpoint Emanuele has viewed the sigma viewer in a browser with a real graph from live vault data. EC-M2-17 (live smoke) confirmed before this sprint begins.

docs.title: 7. NB follow-up disposition (from M2 sign-off §5)
docs.text:
Four NB items were carried forward from v0.2. Disposition for v0.3:

ID Item v0.3 disposition Rationale NB-1 Widen fallback except clause to catch httpx.HTTPStatusError for 5xx (ADR-0009 §4) FOLD INTO v0.3 — hardening task One-line fix in orchestrator.py. Low risk, no new feature ID. Prevents silent fallback bypass on HTTP 503. Backend-engineer owns. Target: first PR of the sprint. NB-2 T-CQ-009 I6 guard too coarse (whole-file substring; forced avoidance of "InferenceProvider" in docstrings) — scope to import lines only FOLD INTO v0.3 — hardening task One-line fix in the test. Zero functional impact. Avoids false negatives accumulating as codebase grows. Backend-engineer owns. NB-4 CLI cost logging: log SDK-reported total_cost_usd instead of $0 convention when API-key auth is used FOLD INTO v0.3 — hardening task The $0 convention was accepted as correct for build-time agent credits (ADR-0009). However, when CliAgentProvider is invoked with a real API key (user's runtime config), the SDK-reported cost should be logged. This is a targeted improvement to cli.py — not a new feature, not a contract change. ai-agent-engineer owns. NB-5 OLLAMA_URL not passed to backend container in docker-compose.yml / .env → Local provider unusable via make up FIX IMMEDIATELY — sprint blocker This blocks live Local provider testing via Docker, which is a core v0.3 testing path (graph ingest via Local provider populates the links table for graph computation). devops-engineer must fix before the first backend task starts. Add OLLAMA_URL to docker-compose.yml backend service env section and to .env.example.

NB-3 from v0.2 (openapi.json info.version string) was a pre-merge action for v0.2, not carried to v0.3. Verify it was resolved before the v0.2 branch was merged.

docs.title: 8. Required D-artifacts for v0.3 docs gate
docs.text:
Artifact Action Owner Gate D1 (component.mmd) Update: add GraphEngine, graph/cache.py, edges table solution-architect / tech-writer M3 D2 (schema.mmd) Regenerate via make er after adding pages.x/y + edges table backend-engineer M3 D3 (graph-recompute.mmd) NEW — graph recompute sequence diagram tech-writer M3 D4 (openapi.json) Update: add GET /graph with typed response schema backend-engineer M3 D5 (screens/) NEW — Playwright screenshots of sigma viewer qa-test-engineer M3 D7 (adr/) New ADR if graph-layout or igraph/debounce decision warrants one (at architect's discretion) solution-architect continuous

D6 (USER.md / DEPLOY.md) is NOT required at M3. It becomes required at M4 (v0.4).

docs.title: 9. Deferred to v0.4 — explicit list
docs.text:
The following are confirmed OUT for v0.3. Raising any of these before M3 sign-off requires orchestrator approval and PM scope-creep gate.

  • F1 — 3-panel shell (tree / chat / preview)
  • F6 — Multi-conversation chat
  • F7 — Reasoning think display
  • F8 — LaTeX to Unicode
  • F14 — Configurable context window
  • F17 (UI) — Provider Selector dropdown UI
  • F16 (rest) — i18n, settings persistence, GFM rendering, multi-provider chat timeout
  • G3 — Streaming perf gate (N/A until v0.4 chat)

docs.title: 10. Velocity calibration note (from M2)
docs.text:
M2 was the largest sprint in the roadmap and delivered all 11 in-scope feature IDs ON SCOPE with one harmless early extra and one in-sprint bug fixed. v0.3 is scoped as a medium sprint: one backend feature cluster (F4 graph engine + cache), one thin frontend (sigma viewer), and the hardening tasks (NB-1/2/4/5). The mmdc CI render check (deferred from v0.2 as "best-effort") should be resolved this sprint as part of the D3 docs gate work.

Estimated complexity: medium (lower than v0.2). Key risk: I2 enforcement — any deviation from server-side layout discovered late in the sprint would require a rewrite. The architect must review the sigma viewer bundle before the G2 Playwright test is written.

docs.title: 11. Sign-off required before Sprint 3 work begins
docs.text:
1. EC-M2-17: Emanuele confirms live TrueNAS smoke (conditions in v0.2-pm-signoff.md §6).
  2. NB-5 fix: OLLAMA_URL added to docker-compose.yml by devops-engineer.
  3. This scope document reviewed by solution-architect.

No sprint 3 feature code ships until all three conditions are met.

docs.title: Sprint 3 — v0.3 — User Stories + Acceptance Criteria
docs.text: Milestone: M3 — "Graph live, no main-thread freeze" Authored by: functional-analyst Date: 2026-06-28 Source of truth for AC IDs: BACKLOG.md §Sprint 3 Exit criteria reference: docs/sprints/v0.3-scope.md §6 (EC-M3-1..17) Invariants with heightened priority: I2 (server-side cached layout — HEADLINE), I4 (WebGL + virtualization) All 9 invariants apply.

docs.title: How to read this document
docs.text:
Each user story block contains: - Story header: "As a [role], I want [capability], so that [outcome]." - Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, each mapped to a BACKLOG.md AC ID. - Invariants touched — priming engineers on which non-negotiables are exercised. - D-artifacts impacted. - Planned test target(s) — forward reference for qa-test-engineer.

Binary pass/fail interpretation: every AC is either fully met or not met. No partial credit. Criteria phrasing such as "correct" or "valid" is resolved by the exact assertions listed in the Given/When/Then body.

docs.title: 0. Pre-start prerequisite stories (NB hardening)
docs.text: These stories are carried from M2 non-blocking follow-ups (NB-1, NB-2, NB-4, NB-5). They are hardening tasks and not new feature IDs. They must be complete before any F4 backend feature PR is merged.

docs.title: US-NB5 — Docker Compose OLLAMA_URL fix (pre-start blocker)
docs.text:
As the devops-engineer, I want OLLAMA_URL to be wired through docker-compose.yml into the synapse-backend container, so that the Local provider (OllamaProvider) is reachable when a developer runs make up and v0.3 graph-ingest testing can proceed via Docker.

Invariants touched: I9 (reuse existing Ollama service — requires correct env wiring).

D-artifacts: none.

AC-NB5-1 Given docker-compose.yml has been updated, when the backend service starts, then the backend container environment block includes the entry OLLAMA_URL: ${OLLAMA_URL}. Test target: test_docker_env.py or static YAML parse in test_code_quality.py.

AC-NB5-2 Given .env.example has been updated, when a developer inspects it, then the file contains the line OLLAMA_URL=http://localhost:11434 with a comment explaining the default. Test target: static file assertion in test_docs.py or test_code_quality.py.

AC-NB5-3 Given NB5-1 and NB5-2 are satisfied and a Local provider_config row is present, when make up is running and POST /ingest/trigger is called with a valid file path, then the backend container successfully calls Ollama (confirmed by response or Docker log entry containing "OllamaProvider" and no ConnectionRefused error). Test target: test_smoke_providers.py (@pytest.mark.live) or manual smoke on dev machine.

docs.title: US-NB1 — Fallback except clause widened to httpx.HTTPStatusError
docs.text:
As the backend-engineer, I want the provider fallback in orchestrator.py to also catch httpx.HTTPStatusError, so that an HTTP 503 from the inference provider correctly triggers the single fallback retry instead of surfacing as an IngestError without engaging the fallback.

Invariants touched: I7 (bounded fallback — exactly one retry, then surface).

D-artifacts: none.

AC-NB1-1 Given the primary provider raises httpx.HTTPStatusError (e.g., HTTP 503), when the orchestrator executes the fallback clause, then the fallback provider is invoked exactly once; if the fallback also raises any exception, the error is surfaced as IngestError with no further retry. Test target: test_bounded_loop.py — mock primary raises HTTPStatusError(503), mock fallback also raises, assert IngestError raised, assert primary called exactly 1 time and fallback exactly 1 time.

AC-NB1-2 Given the primary raises httpx.HTTPStatusError and the fallback succeeds, when the orchestrator executes, then the ingest result contains pages_written > 0 and converged = True with no exception. Test target: test_bounded_loop.py — mock primary raises HTTPStatusError, mock fallback returns valid WikiPage list.

docs.title: US-NB2 — T-CQ-009 I6 guard scoped to import lines only
docs.text:
As the backend-engineer, I want the T-CQ-009 static guard to check only ^from and ^import lines (not entire file text), so that the word "InferenceProvider" may appear in docstrings and comments outside provider/ without triggering a false positive.

Invariants touched: I6 (the guard enforces I6 — must not weaken its detection power).

D-artifacts: none.

AC-NB2-1 Given T-CQ-009 has been updated, when the file backend/app/main.py contains the word "InferenceProvider" in a docstring or comment (not an import), then T-CQ-009 passes without error. Test target: test_code_quality.py — inject a docstring containing "InferenceProvider" in a fixture module outside provider/; assert T-CQ-009 does not flag it.

AC-NB2-2 Given T-CQ-009 has been updated, when any file outside backend/app/ingest/provider/ contains a line matching ^from.*InferenceProvider or ^import.*InferenceProvider, then T-CQ-009 fails (negative test). Test target: test_code_quality.py — add a temporary fixture file with a forbidden import line; assert T-CQ-009 raises AssertionError on it.

docs.title: US-NB4 — CLI cost logging uses SDK-reported cost when API key is present
docs.text:
As the ai-agent-engineer, I want CliAgentProvider to use the SDK-reported total_cost_usd from ClaudeCodeOutput metadata when a real Anthropic API key is configured, so that the ingest_runs table reflects accurate cost for runtime CLI provider usage while preserving the $0.00 convention for build-time / no-key usage.

Invariants touched: I7 (cost accuracy for I7 cost-logging requirement).

D-artifacts: none.

AC-NB4-1 Given SDK output metadata contains a cost field with a positive float value, when CliAgentProvider writes the ingest_runs row, then ingest_runs.total_cost_usd equals the SDK-reported value (not 0.00). Test target: test_bounded_loop.py or new test_cli_cost.py — mock SDK output with total_cost_usd = 0.0342; assert DB row has total_cost_usd == Decimal("0.0342").

AC-NB4-2 Given SDK output metadata is absent or cost equals 0 (build-time / no API key path), when CliAgentProvider writes the ingest_runs row, then ingest_runs.total_cost_usd == Decimal("0.00") and a WARNING log entry is emitted containing the text "total_cost_usd". Test target: test_bounded_loop.py or test_cli_cost.py — mock SDK output with no cost metadata; assert DB row has 0.00 and caplog contains WARNING with "total_cost_usd".

AC-NB4-3 Given SDK output metadata contains cost = 0 exactly (not absent, but explicitly zero), when CliAgentProvider writes the ingest_runs row, then ingest_runs.total_cost_usd == Decimal("0.00") and the WARNING is emitted (same path as AC-NB4-2, the guard checks for absence OR zero). Test target: test_cli_cost.py — mock SDK output with total_cost_usd = 0.0; assert row has 0.00 and WARNING emitted.

docs.title: US-F4-ENGINE — 4-signal weighted graph construction
docs.text:
As the GraphEngine (backend/app/graph/engine.py), I want to compute edge weights between wiki pages using four independent, additive signals — direct-link (×3), source-overlap (×4), Adamic-Adar (×1.5), and type-affinity (×1) — so that the knowledge graph reflects the strength of relationships between pages in a principled, auditable way.

Invariants touched: I1 (graph is built from Postgres tables, no vault/ filesystem rescan), I9 (igraph is the specified library — R9).

D-artifacts: D2 (edges table added to schema), D3 (graph-recompute sequence touches engine).

Fixture specification for weight testing (QA must implement):

Use a 5-node fixture graph loaded directly into the pages and links tables. Nodes:

page_id title type sources[] P1 "Alpha" entity ["doc_a.md"] P2 "Beta" entity ["doc_a.md"] P3 "Gamma" concept ["doc_b.md"] P4 "Delta" entity ["doc_a.md", "doc_b.md"] P5 "Epsilon" concept ["doc_c.md"]

Links (wikilinks — rows in the links table):

source_page_id target_title dangling P1 "Beta" False P2 "Alpha" False P3 "Delta" False P4 "Alpha" False

Expected edge weights (additive — all applicable signals sum per edge):

  • Edge P1-P2 (bidirectional links + shared source "doc_a.md" + same type "entity"):
  • direct-link: P1→P2 exists (×3) + P2→P1 exists (×3) = 6
  • source-overlap: both have "doc_a.md" (×4) = 4
  • Adamic-Adar: common neighbours via links table — P4 links to P1, P3 links to P4 which links to... (computed by igraph AA on the directed/undirected adjacency; exact value depends on igraph implementation — see AQ-v0.3-2 below)
  • type-affinity: both "entity" (×1) = 1
  • Minimum expected weight (without AA contribution, AA adds >= 0): 6 + 4 + 1 = 11 + AA

The QA test must verify each signal's contribution INDEPENDENTLY by constructing edge pairs that isolate each signal. Specifically:

  • Edge P3-P5 (no link, no shared source, different type): no contribution from any signal. Expected weight = 0 (this edge should NOT appear in the edges table).
  • Edge P1-P4 (P4→P1 link only, shared source "doc_a.md", same type "entity"):
  • direct-link: P4→P1 exists (×3) = 3
  • source-overlap: both have "doc_a.md" (×4) = 4
  • type-affinity: both "entity" (×1) = 1
  • Expected minimum weight: 8 + AA (AA from any shared neighbours)
  • Edge P2-P4 (no direct link, shared source "doc_a.md", same type "entity"):
  • direct-link: 0
  • source-overlap: both have "doc_a.md" (×4) = 4
  • type-affinity: both "entity" (×1) = 1
  • Expected minimum weight: 5 (AA may add further)

DESIGN QUESTION for architect (AQ-v0.3-1): Are the 4 signals always SUMMED per edge (additive model), or is any signal multiplicative / capped? The AC text above assumes additive. If the architect changes this to a different combining formula, the QA fixture expected values must be recomputed. Functional analyst flags this as AQ-v0.3-1.

AC-F4-1 Given a fixture graph with 5 nodes and the links/sources/types described in the fixture specification above has been loaded into the test database, when GraphEngine.build_weights() is called, then: (a) The edges table contains a row for P1-P2 with weight >= 11 (direct + source + type minimum contribution, before AA). (b) The edges table contains a row for P1-P4 with weight >= 8. (c) The edges table contains a row for P2-P4 with weight >= 5. (d) The edges table does NOT contain a row for P3-P5 (or if it does, weight == 0). (e) Each signal's contribution to at least one edge is independently verifiable via a pytest parametrize fixture that zeroes out all other signals and asserts non-zero contribution of the target signal. Test target: test_graph_engine.py (new) — unit test with SQLite in-memory + fixture inserts. EC reference: EC-M3-1.

docs.title: US-F4-LAYOUT — Server-side FA2 layout via igraph
docs.text:
As the GraphEngine, I want to run the ForceAtlas2 layout algorithm using python-igraph on the server, writing x and y float coordinates for every page to Postgres, so that the client can render the graph from precomputed positions without ever executing a layout algorithm in the browser (satisfying invariant I2 completely).

Invariants touched: I2 (FA2 runs server-side, coords in Postgres, NEVER in browser), I1 (coord update is incremental — only affected page rows updated), I9 (igraph R9 is the specified tool, not graph-tool, not d3-force).

D-artifacts: D2 (pages table gains x, y float columns; edges table is new — must appear in schema.mmd after make er), D3 (graph-recompute.mmd shows FA2 call).

AC-F4-2 Given GraphEngine.recompute() has been called on a seeded test graph, when the Postgres pages table is queried, then every page that was in the igraph Graph object has a non-NULL x (float) and y (float) value in the pages table; no page has both x IS NULL and y IS NULL after a successful recompute. Test target: test_graph_engine.py — call engine.recompute() with 5-node fixture; assert all 5 pages have non-NULL x/y via SQLAlchemy query. EC reference: EC-M3-2.

AC-F4-2b (I2 no-client-layout static check) Given the frontend bundle has been built via make build, when the bundle output files (dist/) are scanned for forbidden strings, then NO file in the bundle contains any of the following substrings: graphology-layout-forceatlas2, ForceAtlas2, d3-force, forceSimulation, requestAnimationFrame when followed within 100 characters by layout or force. Test target: test_graph_engine.py or frontend/tests/bundle-check.test.ts — grep/scan the Vite build output directory. EC reference: EC-M3-5 (G2 static bundle check).

AC-F4-2c (igraph version pin and no alternative library) Given the backend requirements are installed, when the module backend/app/graph/engine.py is imported, then the import succeeds using igraph (python-igraph); the module does NOT import graph_tool, networkx, vis, cytoscape, or d3. Test target: test_graph_engine.py — assert import igraph succeeds; assert that engine.py source text does not contain the string "import graph_tool" or "import networkx" (static check). EC reference: EC-M3-2, I9.

DESIGN QUESTION for architect (AQ-v0.3-2): FA2 in igraph requires a seed for deterministic coordinate output. Should the engine always seed FA2 with a fixed integer (e.g., seed=42) so that two calls with identical graph topology produce identical coordinates (enabling byte-stable regression tests)? Or is non-determinism acceptable (test only checks non-NULL, not specific values)? Functional analyst flags this as AQ-v0.3-2. Impact: if seed is fixed, add seed=42 parameter to igraph FA2 call and add a determinism assertion to the test. If non-deterministic, remove the determinism test and accept only non-NULL + range checks.

docs.title: US-F4-CACHE — Debounced FA2 recompute on dataVersion change
docs.text:
As the GraphCache (backend/app/graph/cache.py), I want to monitor vault_state.data_version and trigger exactly one FA2 recompute after a configurable debounce window when the version changes, collapsing bursts of rapid ingests into a single recompute run, so that the graph coordinates remain accurate without wasting compute on intermediate states and without any unbounded job queue (satisfying I2's "debounced on dataVersion" clause and I7's bounded-job requirement).

Invariants touched: I2 (cached + debounced), I7 (bounded queue — max 1 queued job), I1 (recompute reads DB tables, not filesystem).

D-artifacts: D3 (graph-recompute.mmd shows debounce collapse and cache-hit/miss paths).

AC-F16db-1 Given vault_state.data_version is at version N, when data_version is updated to N+1 (simulated by direct DB write in test), then GraphCache detects the change (via polling or event) and, after the debounce window elapses, exactly one call to GraphEngine.recompute() is recorded. Test target: test_graph_cache.py (new) — mock GraphEngine.recompute(); mock clock; bump data_version once; advance clock past debounce window; assert recompute called exactly 1 time. EC reference: EC-M3-3.

AC-F16db-2 Given GraphCache is monitoring data_version, when data_version is bumped 5 times in rapid succession within a single debounce window, then exactly 1 call to GraphEngine.recompute() is made (not 5), verified by a mock call counter. Test target: test_graph_cache.py — mock clock; bump version 5 times within debounce window; advance clock past window end; assert recompute called == 1. EC reference: EC-M3-3, I2 (debounce).

AC-F16db-3 Given GraphEngine.recompute() is currently in-flight (simulated by a blocking mock), when data_version is bumped again during the in-flight recompute, then the in-flight recompute is NOT interrupted; after it completes, exactly 1 additional recompute is triggered (the deferred bump). Total calls: 2 (first + one deferred), not N. Test target: test_graph_cache.py — use asyncio or threading mock; assert the in-flight recompute completes before the second starts; assert exactly 2 total calls on the mock. EC reference: I7 (bounded queue), I2.

AC-F16db-4 Given GraphCache is running, when GET /status is called, then the response JSON includes data_version equal to the current vault_state.data_version value (no regression from v0.1 AC-F16dv-3). Test target: test_api.py — extend existing T-API-004 to assert data_version still present after v0.3 changes. EC reference: v0.1 regression gate.

docs.title: US-F4-API — GET /graph returns precomputed graph payload
docs.text:
As the vault owner (Emanuele) querying the Synapse REST API, I want GET /graph to return a typed JSON payload containing precomputed node coordinates, weighted edges, the current data_version, and a cache-hit indicator, so that the frontend can render the graph immediately from server-computed data without triggering any layout computation.

Invariants touched: I2 (endpoint serves precomputed coords, emits cache header), I8 (D4 OpenAPI must document this endpoint and its response schema).

D-artifacts: D4 (openapi.json updated with GET /graph response schema).

Response schema (to be locked by architect — AQ-v0.3-3): The GET /graph response must conform to: 

{
  "nodes": [
    {"id": "<uuid>", "title": "<string>", "type": "<string>", "x": <float>, "y": <float>}
  ],
  "edges": [
    {"source": "<uuid>", "target": "<uuid>", "weight": <float>}
  ],
  "data_version": <int>,
  "cached": <bool>
}
The X-Graph-Cache response header is set to "hit" when the response is served from cached coordinates (no FA2 recompute triggered) and "miss" when FA2 was triggered.

DESIGN QUESTION for architect (AQ-v0.3-3): Should GET /graph block until FA2 is complete (synchronous), or return HTTP 202 with a job_id when FA2 is not yet done and coords are stale? Impact on test strategy is significant: if synchronous, test can assert full payload; if async, test must poll. Functional analyst recommends synchronous for v0.3 simplicity (graph is fast on igraph for the expected vault sizes), but flags this as AQ-v0.3-3 for architect decision before engineering begins.

AC-F4-3 Given a seeded graph with at least 3 nodes and 2 edges in Postgres with x/y coords set, when GET /graph is called, then: (a) HTTP 200 is returned. (b) Response body is valid JSON with keys: nodes, edges, data_version, cached. (c) nodes is a non-empty array; each node object has keys: id, title, type, x, y with correct types (string, string, string, float, float). (d) edges is a non-empty array; each edge object has keys: source, target, weight with correct types (string, string, float). (e) data_version is an integer >= 1. (f) cached is a boolean. Test target: test_api.py (extend) + test_graph_api.py (new) — ASGI integration test with pre-seeded pages/edges rows. EC reference: EC-M3-4.

AC-F4-4 Given GET /graph has been called once (cache-miss, FA2 run logged), when GET /graph is called a second time without any data_version change, then: (a) Response cached field is true. (b) X-Graph-Cache response header equals "hit". (c) Backend logs do NOT contain a second FA2 invocation (verified by asserting the mock GraphEngine.recompute() call count remains 1 after the second GET /graph call). Test target: test_graph_api.py — use mock GraphEngine; call GET /graph twice; assert recompute called exactly once; assert second response has cached=True and header X-Graph-Cache=hit. EC reference: EC-M3-3 (cache-hit verification).

AC-D4v3-1 Given GET /graph has been added to FastAPI and make openapi has been run, when docs/api/openapi.json is inspected, then the file contains a path entry for /graph with a GET operation that documents: - 200 response schema matching the structure above (nodes array, edges array, data_version int, cached bool). - All node fields (id, title, type, x, y) typed in the schema. - All edge fields (source, target, weight) typed in the schema. Test target: test_docs.py (extend) — assert openapi.json contains /graph path with correct response schema; run via pytest or CI make openapi + parse check. EC reference: EC-M3-4, EC-M3-12, I8.

docs.title: US-F4-G1 — Incremental coord update preserves unaffected pages
docs.text:
As the GraphEngine, I want to update only the page coordinate rows affected by a new ingest event, so that ingesting one file does not rewrite the entire pages table and the I1 incremental-index invariant is preserved for graph coordinates.

Invariants touched: I1 (incremental only — no full table rewrite on a single ingest).

D-artifacts: none directly, but the graph-recompute.mmd (D3) should annotate that recompute writes coords to Postgres for all nodes in the igraph graph (which itself is built from DB rows). The "incremental" guarantee at the coord level means coord rows are not unnecessarily zeroed or rewritten for nodes unrelated to the triggering ingest.

DESIGN CLARIFICATION (for architect — AQ-v0.3-4): The graph recompute is a full FA2 run over all current pages (the igraph graph includes all non-deleted pages). Coords for ALL nodes are therefore rewritten on every recompute. The "incremental" guarantee of I1 at the graph level means: (a) the recompute is DEBOUNCED (not triggered per-file-change), and (b) new page rows for newly-ingested files are added incrementally without deleting and recreating unrelated rows. The coord UPDATE (via FA2) touches all rows because FA2 is a global layout, but it does so only once per debounce window — not per ingest event. Functional analyst recommends the test assert that: after ingesting one new file, only one new pages row is added (the new file's page); no other pages rows are deleted or recreated. The coord columns (x, y) of existing pages may change after the debounced FA2 recompute — this is acceptable because FA2 is a global layout algorithm. Please confirm this interpretation via AQ-v0.3-4 before QA writes the test.

AC-F4-9 Given a vault with N pre-existing pages all having non-NULL x/y coordinates in Postgres, when one new file is ingested via the watcher (adding one new page row), then: (a) Exactly one new row is added to the pages table (the new page); no existing page rows are deleted or have their id, title, type, sources changed. (b) The new page row exists in the pages table with the correct file_path and title. (c) The Postgres pages table row count increases by exactly 1. Test target: test_incremental_graph_update.py (new) — seed N pages with x/y; trigger one ingest; assert table count N+1; assert all N pre-existing page ids still present with unchanged id/title/type. EC reference: EC-M3-8, G1.

docs.title: 6. F4 Frontend — sigma.js WebGL viewer (thin, read-only)
US-FE-VIEWER — Sigma.js viewer renders precomputed graph coordinates in WebGL
docs.text:
As the vault owner (Emanuele) opening the graph viewer in a browser, I want to see all wiki pages rendered as nodes and all weighted links as edges in a WebGL canvas, with positions pre-calculated server-side, so that the browser never freezes on a layout computation and the graph is immediately visible without waiting for an in-browser physics simulation (satisfying I2 and I4 completely from the client side).

Invariants touched: I2 (client NEVER calls layout — receives precomputed coords only), I3 (Zustand selectors + shallow equality pre-wired — no whole-store subscription), I4 (sigma.js renders all nodes in one WebGL canvas element, not one DOM node per node).

D-artifacts: D5 (Playwright screenshots of the viewer — first required sprint).

AC-FE-1 Given make dev has been run (Vite dev server started) and a seeded graph is available from GET /graph, when the viewer URL (/ or /graph) is loaded in a headless Playwright browser, then: (a) The page returns HTTP 200. (b) No console errors appear within 5 seconds of page load. (c) A <canvas> element is present in the DOM (sigma.js WebGL canvas). Test target: frontend/tests/graph-viewer.spec.ts (Playwright) — assert page loads, no console errors, canvas present. EC reference: EC-M3-7.

AC-FE-2 Given the frontend bundle has been built via make build, when the contents of the dist/ directory are scanned, then NO file contains any of the following import strings or function calls: graphology-layout-forceatlas2, @antv/layout, d3-force, forceSimulation, fa2Worker, randomLayout, circularLayout called within a layout context. Test target: frontend/tests/bundle-check.test.ts (vitest) — read all .js files in dist/ and assert none of the forbidden strings are present. EC reference: EC-M3-5 (I2 static bundle check, G2).

AC-FE-3 Given the sigma viewer is loaded and the Zustand store for graph data is initialized, when the graph state is inspected via Playwright, then: (a) The Zustand store is created with a selector pattern (no whole-store useStore() subscription without a selector function) — verified by vitest unit test on the store. (b) Object.is shallow equality is used as the equality function (not deep equality or no-equality), verified by store unit test asserting that a re-render is NOT triggered when an unrelated slice of state changes. Test target: frontend/tests/graph-store.test.ts (vitest) — unit test on the Zustand store. EC reference: I3 pre-compliance for v0.4 chat integration.

AC-FE-4 Given the frontend TypeScript source is being checked, when tsc --noEmit and eslint . and prettier --check . are run on the frontend/src/ directory, then all three tools exit with code 0 (no type errors, no lint errors, no formatting violations). Test target: CI make lint-frontend step or frontend/tests/ vitest wrapper that calls these tools. EC reference: EC-M3-13 (QA gate — TypeScript strict + eslint + prettier).

AC-FE-5 Given a mock GET /graph response is provided to the store in a vitest test environment, when the data transformation function (API response → graphology Graph object) is called, then: (a) Each node in the response is added to the graphology graph with id, x, y, label, type properties. (b) Each edge in the response is added to the graphology graph with source, target, weight. (c) Node count in the graphology graph equals the node count in the mock response. (d) Edge count in the graphology graph equals the edge count in the mock response. Test target: frontend/tests/graph-transform.test.ts (vitest) — pure function unit test. EC reference: EC-M3-5, AC-FE-5 in BACKLOG.md.

docs.title: US-FE-CLICK — Node click shows page title in read-only tooltip
docs.text:
As the vault owner, I want to click a graph node and see the corresponding wiki page's title and type displayed in a read-only tooltip or side drawer, so that I can identify what each node represents without leaving the graph view.

Invariants touched: I2 (node click triggers GET /pages/{id} fetch — not a re-layout), I4 (no editor surface introduced).

D-artifacts: D5 (graph-viewer-node-click.png if tooltip is implemented; optional per BACKLOG §D5 scope).

AC-F4-8 Given the sigma viewer is loaded with a seeded graph and at least one node is visible, when a node is clicked (Playwright page.locator('canvas').click({...}) or a simulated sigma click event), then: (a) A tooltip or side drawer element becomes visible in the DOM. (b) The tooltip/drawer contains the page title text matching the clicked node's title. (c) No edit controls (CodeMirror editor, textarea, contenteditable) are present in the tooltip. (d) No chat UI elements (chat input, message history) are present in the viewer page. Test target: frontend/tests/graph-viewer.spec.ts (Playwright) — click node; assert tooltip visible; assert title text present; assert no editor/chat elements. EC reference: EC-M3-7.

docs.title: 8. I2 / G2 — No main-thread freeze (Playwright perf test)
US-G2-PERF — Graph render imposes no long tasks on the browser main thread
docs.text:
As the vault owner opening the graph viewer, I want graph rendering to produce no JavaScript long tasks exceeding 50ms on the main thread, so that the browser remains responsive and the bottleneck #2 from llm_wiki is demonstrably fixed.

Invariants touched: I2 (the invariant's raison d'etre — no client-side layout = no main thread freeze), I4 (WebGL rendering is off-main-thread for draw calls).

D-artifacts: D5 (G2 Playwright test is co-located in the screenshot E2E suite).

AC-F4-6 Given the sigma viewer is loaded in Playwright with a 50-node / 100-edge fixture graph (sufficient to trigger any layout loop if one existed), when Playwright performance tracing is active during page load and initial graph render, then NO JavaScript long task exceeding 50ms duration is recorded on the main thread during the period between page navigation start and the sigma canvas first-paint event. (Implementation note: use page.on('metrics', ...) or Chrome Performance API via page.evaluate to collect long tasks; assert array of long tasks with duration > 50 is empty.) Test target: frontend/tests/perf.spec.ts (Playwright) — co-located with screenshot suite. EC reference: EC-M3-5 (G2).

docs.title: 9. I4 / G4 — 200-node WebGL render at ≥60fps with bounded DOM
US-G4-PERF — Large graph renders at 60fps in sigma.js WebGL with minimal DOM
docs.text:
As the vault owner viewing a large knowledge graph, I want the sigma.js renderer to sustain 60fps (mean frame time ≤16ms) for a graph of 200 nodes and 500 edges, and to use a single WebGL canvas element rather than one DOM node per graph node, so that the UI remains fluid regardless of vault size.

Invariants touched: I4 (sigma WebGL renders in a single canvas — bounded DOM growth), I2 (all positions are precomputed — no layout computation during rendering).

D-artifacts: D5 (G4 Playwright test co-located in perf.spec.ts).

AC-F4-7 Given a fixture graph of exactly 200 nodes and 500 edges has been seeded in Postgres with pre-set x/y coordinates (QA-generated synthetic fixture, no FA2 required for this test), when the sigma viewer is loaded in Playwright and the canvas is visible, then: (a) page.evaluate(() => document.querySelectorAll('[data-graph-node]').length) returns 0 OR the DOM node count within the sigma graph container element is < 20 (all nodes are WebGL-drawn, not DOM-represented). (b) Over 60 consecutive animation frames (measured via requestAnimationFrame in the page context), the mean frame time is ≤16ms and no single frame time exceeds 33ms (2x budget). Test target: frontend/tests/perf.spec.ts (Playwright) — page.evaluate for DOM count and rAF timing loop. EC reference: EC-M3-6 (G4).

docs.title: 10. I5 / K7 — Obsidian compatibility maintained after v0.3 schema changes
US-OBS-COMPAT — Graph schema changes do not corrupt the Obsidian vault
docs.text:
As the vault owner, I want the graph engine's changes to Postgres (adding x, y columns and the edges table) to have zero impact on the vault/ filesystem and the .md files in wiki/, so that vault/wiki/ remains a valid Obsidian vault after v0.3 is deployed.

Invariants touched: I5 (Obsidian compatibility is non-negotiable), I1 (graph coords live in Postgres, not in .md frontmatter).

D-artifacts: D2 (schema.mmd updated — but the .md files are unchanged).

AC-OBS-V0.3 Given GraphEngine.recompute() has been run and coords have been written to Postgres, when the existing test_obsidian_check.py suite (15 tests from v0.2) is run, then all 15 tests pass with 0 failures (no regression from v0.2 green status). Test target: backend/tests/test_obsidian_check.py — existing suite, re-run unchanged. EC reference: EC-M3-9, I5.

docs.title: US-D3-V3 — graph-recompute.mmd documents the full cache + debounce flow
docs.text:
As the tech-writer and future engineers reading the codebase, I want a Mermaid sequenceDiagram in docs/sequences/ that shows the complete graph recompute lifecycle — from watcher ingest through dataVersion bump, debounce collapse, FA2 execution, coord persistence, and both GET /graph paths (cache-hit and cache-miss) — so that the I2 architecture is documented in code and any engineer can understand how to safely modify the graph pipeline.

Invariants touched: I8 (D3 is a mandatory DoD artifact from v0.3 onward).

D-artifacts: D3 (graph-recompute.mmd is new in this sprint).

AC-D3v3-1 Given docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd has been authored, when the file is parsed as a Mermaid diagram, then: (a) The file exists at the exact path docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd. (b) The file contains the keyword sequenceDiagram on a non-comment line. (c) The diagram includes participant/actor labels for: Watcher, dataVersion, GraphCache, GraphEngine, Postgres, and Client (or equivalent names). (d) The diagram contains two distinct paths annotated as cache-miss and cache-hit (e.g., alt cache-miss / else cache-hit or equivalent Mermaid opt/alt blocks). (e) The diagram contains an annotation or note indicating the debounce collapse behaviour (multiple dataVersion bumps → single recompute). (f) The diagram contains a note or annotation stating "FA2 runs ONLY server-side" or equivalent wording (explicitly calling out the I2 constraint). Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py (extend) — file existence + keyword checks. EC reference: EC-M3-10, I8.

AC-D3v3-2 Given docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd exists, when the mmdc CLI render check is run (mmdc -i docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd -o /tmp/check.png), then the command exits with code 0 and produces a non-empty output file. (Note: this also resolves the v0.2 carry-forward AC-D3-3 for the existing sequence diagrams.) Test target: CI mmdc step (devops-engineer to wire in CI) — resolves v0.2 deferred item. EC reference: EC-M3-10, AC-D3v3-2 (mmdc CI carry-forward from v0.2).

AC-D3v3-3 Given docs/sequences/graph-recompute.mmd exists and has been reviewed, when the solution-architect and tech-writer have reviewed it jointly, then both have confirmed in the gate sign-off register that the diagram is consistent with the implementation in graph/engine.py and graph/cache.py. Test target: MANUAL GATE — recorded in sign-off register. EC reference: EC-M3-10, EC-M3-14, EC-M3-15.

docs.title: US-D5 — Playwright captures sigma viewer screenshots as docs/screens/ artifacts
docs.text:
As the tech-writer and PM verifying the docs gate, I want Playwright E2E tests to automatically capture PNG screenshots of the sigma viewer and commit them to docs/screens/, so that D5 (UI screenshots) is satisfied for M3 and the milestone visual evidence is part of the repository.

Invariants touched: I8 (D5 is mandatory from v0.3; must be committed).

D-artifacts: D5 (docs/screens/graph-viewer-initial.png, docs/screens/graph-viewer-rendered.png).

AC-D5-1 Given make screenshots (or make test with screenshot capture included) has been run, when docs/screens/graph-viewer-initial.png is inspected, then: (a) The file exists at docs/screens/graph-viewer-initial.png. (b) The file size is > 0 bytes (non-empty PNG). (c) The file is committed to the repository (not gitignored). Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py (extend) — assert file exists and is non-empty. EC reference: EC-M3-11, I8.

AC-D5-2 Given make screenshots has been run against a sigma viewer with a seeded graph loaded, when docs/screens/graph-viewer-rendered.png is inspected, then: (a) The file exists at docs/screens/graph-viewer-rendered.png. (b) The file size is > 0 bytes. (c) The file is committed to the repository. (Playwright screenshot capture of the rendered sigma canvas is considered sufficient proof that the canvas is present; pixel-level node count assertion is NOT required here — the G4 test handles DOM/WebGL verification.) Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py + frontend/tests/graph-viewer.spec.ts. EC reference: EC-M3-11, I8.

AC-D5-3 Given the Playwright screenshot capture test exists, when make screenshots is run in headless mode (no display required), then the command exits with code 0 and both PNG files are written. Test target: frontend/tests/graph-viewer.spec.ts (Playwright headless) + make screenshots target in Makefile. EC reference: EC-M3-11.

AC-D5-4 Given the G2 Playwright perf test (AC-F4-6) and G4 Playwright perf test (AC-F4-7) exist, when the full E2E suite is run via make test or a separate make screenshots target, then the screenshot capture and the performance assertions run in the same Playwright invocation (same npx playwright test command), sharing the browser context setup. Test target: frontend/tests/perf.spec.ts + frontend/tests/graph-viewer.spec.ts — both registered in playwright.config.ts. EC reference: EC-M3-11, BACKLOG AC-D5-4.

docs.title: US-DOCS-V3 — D1/D2/D4 artifacts updated to reflect v0.3 schema and API changes
docs.text:
As the tech-writer and architect verifying the docs gate, I want the C4 component diagram, ER diagram, and OpenAPI reference to be updated for v0.3 schema additions (pages.x/y, edges table, GET /graph), so that D-artifacts remain the single source of truth for the system architecture and the I8 docs-as-DoD invariant is satisfied.

Invariants touched: I8 (every sprint must keep D-artifacts current).

D-artifacts: D1, D2, D4.

AC-D1v3-1 Given docs/architecture/component.mmd has been updated, when the file is parsed as a Mermaid C4Component diagram, then: (a) The file contains a component representation labelled "GraphEngine" (or equivalent). (b) The file contains a component representation labelled "GraphCache" (or equivalent). (c) The file contains a component or boundary reference to the sigma viewer or frontend graph route. (d) A solution-architect and tech-writer have jointly confirmed the diagram is consistent with the implementation (MANUAL gate recorded in sign-off register). Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py (extend) — assert component.mmd contains the required label strings; MANUAL gate for diagram accuracy. EC reference: EC-M3-12, I8.

AC-D2v3-1 Given make er has been run after adding pages.x, pages.y (float columns) and the edges table (id, source_page_id, target_page_id, weight) to backend/app/models.py, when docs/er/schema.mmd is inspected, then: (a) The file was generated by make er (not hand-edited — verified by CI make er + diff). (b) The diagram contains an entity "pages" that includes the columns x and y. (c) The diagram contains an entity "edges" with at minimum columns id, source_page_id, target_page_id, weight. (d) The ER diagram is consistent with the live Postgres schema after Alembic migration (CI: run migrations + make er + compare against committed file — zero diff). Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py + backend/tests/test_models_schema.py — extend to assert new columns; CI make er must produce identical output to committed schema.mmd. EC reference: EC-M3-12, I8.

docs.title: 14. Ambiguities flagged to solution-architect (AQ register for v0.3)
docs.text:
The following design questions must be resolved by the solution-architect BEFORE engineering begins on the affected ACs. Until resolved, the ACs are considered BLOCKED.

AQ ID Blocks ACs Question Recommended resolution Urgency AQ-v0.3-1 AC-F4-1 Are the 4 edge-weight signals ADDITIVE (summed per edge) or do they use a different combining formula (e.g., max, product, weighted product)? The AC fixture expected values assume additive. The BACKLOG.md spec says "×3", "×4", "×1.5", "×1" — these are multipliers on what base signal? Is it: base_signal=1 for presence, then multiplied? Or is weight = sum of (multiplier × signal_value) for each contributing signal? Functional analyst recommends: for presence-based signals (direct-link, source-overlap, type-affinity), base = 1 if condition is met, 0 otherwise; weight = sum over all contributing signals of (multiplier × base). For Adamic-Adar, multiply the igraph-computed AA coefficient by 1.5. Total edge weight = sum of all applicable signal contributions. Architect to confirm or revise formula and publish in ADR before test fixture values are finalized. P0 — blocks AC-F4-1 unit test values AQ-v0.3-2 AC-F4-2 Should igraph FA2 be seeded with a fixed integer (e.g., seed=42) for deterministic output, or is non-deterministic output acceptable? Recommend: fix seed=42 for reproducibility in tests and regression detection. Non-deterministic coords make regression testing harder. If architect wants non-deterministic (e.g., for better layout variety on vault changes), the unit test must only check non-NULL + coordinate range rather than exact values. P1 — blocks AC-F4-2 determinism assertion AQ-v0.3-3 AC-F4-3, AC-F4-4 Should GET /graph block (synchronous, return 200 with full payload only when FA2 is complete) or return HTTP 202 + job_id when FA2 is running and coords are stale? Functional analyst recommends: synchronous for v0.3. Graph recompute on igraph for expected vault sizes (<500 nodes) should complete in <2s. Return 200 when coords are ready. If no coords exist yet (first ever call), trigger FA2 synchronously and return 200. Add a "stale" indicator in the response if desired but do not return 202. P0 — blocks AC-F4-3 test design AQ-v0.3-4 AC-F4-9 "Incremental" at the graph coord level: FA2 is a global layout algorithm that repositions ALL nodes on every recompute. Does AC-F4-9 assert (a) only 1 new pages row is added per ingest (correct), or (b) only the new page's coords change while others remain frozen (incorrect — FA2 moves all nodes)? Confirm interpretation: AC-F4-9 tests row-level incrementality (exactly 1 new row added per file ingest), NOT coord-value stability across recomputes. Coord values for existing pages WILL change after FA2 reruns — this is expected and correct. Document this explicitly in test_incremental_graph_update.py to avoid confusion. P1 — clarifies AC-F4-9 assertion scope AQ-v0.3-5 AC-F4-1, AC-D2v3-1 Is the edges table stored persistently in Postgres (written after every FA2 run and read directly for GET /graph) or computed in-memory on every graph request? Recommend: store in Postgres edges table (as specified in BACKLOG.md §F4 scope). This enables GET /graph to be purely a read (no recompute) when cache=hit. In-memory computation would require FA2 on every GET /graph, violating I2 cache intent. Architect to confirm and lock schema: edges(id uuid pk, source_page_id uuid FK pages.id, target_page_id uuid FK pages.id, weight float, created_at timestamp). P0 — blocks D2 ER update and GET /graph implementation AQ-v0.3-6 AC-F4-2, AC-D2v3-1 Are x/y coordinates stored as columns on the existing pages table (pages.x float, pages.y float) or in a separate graph_coords table (page_id FK, x float, y float, layout_version int)? Functional analyst notes: BACKLOG.md explicitly says "pages.x, pages.y columns" and "coords stored in Postgres pages table". This resolves the AQ in favor of columns on pages. Architect to confirm no change to this decision. If a separate table is chosen instead, Alembic migration and D2 diagram must be updated accordingly. P1 — BACKLOG already specifies columns on pages; confirm no change AQ-v0.3-7 AC-F16db-1 How does GraphCache detect dataVersion changes: (a) polling vault_state table on a configurable interval (e.g., every 2 seconds), (b) Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY, or (c) in-process event emitted by the watcher after each ingest? Recommend: polling (option a) for simplicity in v0.3; the watcher is in the same process, so option c is also viable. LISTEN/NOTIFY (option b) adds Postgres dependency complexity. Architect to decide and document in the ADR if this decision is not already covered by existing ADRs. Debounce window is configurable (default 5s per BACKLOG spec). P1 — affects GraphCache implementation and test mock strategy

docs.title: Appendix A — Story-to-EC mapping
