docs.title: Sprint 4 — v0.4 / M4-HARD — PM Scope Lock: Post-Human-Testing Hardening Increment
docs.text: Author: product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Date: 2026-06-29 Gate: Hardening increment scope confirmation — NOT a new milestone Branch: sprint/v0.4 (continuing; no new sprint cut) Prerequisite: M4 originally declared MET-WITH-DEFERRALS (docs/sprints/v0.4-pm-signoff.md); M4-EXT shipped (commits f7c7865 / 59765f3 / 65a6407); EC-M4-HCP human testing completed and produced the feedback items below. This document supersedes nothing in v0.4-pm-scope.md or v0.4-pm-signoff.md. It adds to them.

docs.title: 0. Context and Mandate
docs.text:
M4 was declared MET. The developer (Emanuele) conducted human browser testing (EC-M4-HCP) and found usability problems. The orchestrator has already shipped a first-pass fix for most of them (commits 65a6407, f7c7865, 59765f3). This hardening increment retroactively locks that scope, classifies what is done vs in-progress vs deferred, adds two new PM rulings (Point A — nav labels; Point B — M5 placeholder strategy), and defines acceptance criteria for the in-progress items so they can pass through the full gate chain.

This is a hardening pass. It does NOT advance the milestone number. Sprint 5 (M5) remains blocked until M4-HARD exits cleanly (4 sign-offs) and Emanuele confirms EC-M4-HCP.

docs.title: 1a. Already-done items (retroactively locked; require gate verification only)
docs.text:
These items were implemented by the orchestrator before this scope-lock document was written. They are IN SCOPE for M4-HARD. The gate chain (QA + Architect + Tech-Writer + PM) must verify them before M4-HARD is declared done.

Feature ID Description Invariants Evidence F1-HARD-SETTINGS Settings panel redesigned from flat list into 9-section left-nav: General / LLM Models / Embeddings / Source Watch / API+MCP / Output / Interface / Maintenance / About. Visual direction: nashsu/llm_wiki reference app. I3 (no per-token), I6 (provider config from API, not hardcoded) Commit 65a6407; SettingsPanel.tsx F1-HARD-COLLAPSE Left and right panels gain collapse/expand chevron buttons implemented via react-resizable-panels usePanelRef. Satisfies human-tester feedback that panels were not collapsible. I3 (no main-thread layout on each frame) Commit 65a6407 F1-HARD-PROVIDER-EDIT Settings > LLM Models section is now read-write: user can add a new provider (POST /provider/config) and delete an existing one (DELETE /provider/config/{id}). Previously read-only "CONFIGURAZIONE PROVIDER" was a usability blocker. I6 (model_id read from provider_config; no hardcoded IDs anywhere), I3 Commit 65a6407 F1-HARD-MCP-STUB API+MCP settings section added. Current content: "MCP server configuration — coming in M5" placeholder. This is correct M4 behavior — full MCP config is M5 scope. None violated Commit 65a6407; en.json settings.apiMcp F1-HARD-NAV-ORDER Nav rail order changed to: logo → Chat → Wiki → Sources → Search → Graph → Lint → Review → Deep Search → (spacer) → Settings. Default section on load is Chat (was Graph). Matches developer's explicit request and improves new-user onboarding. I3 (Zustand selector discipline) Commit 65a6407; NavRail.tsx TOP_ITEMS / M5_ITEMS order F1-HARD-EMBED-STUB Vector Embeddings settings section added. Content: "Vector embeddings configuration — coming in M5" placeholder. Correct M4 behavior. None violated Commit 65a6407; en.json settings.embeddings F1-HARD-CONV-HISTORY Conversation history length selector in Output settings section. Options: 2 / 4 / 6 / 8 / 10 / 20 messages. Value persisted in localStorage (F16-settings). I3 (no per-token), I7 (context budget) Commit 65a6407; en.json settings.output

docs.title: 1b. In-progress items (raised during human testing; require implementation + gate)
docs.text:
These two items were raised by Emanuele during the human testing session AFTER the first-pass fix was shipped. They are the primary reason this scope-lock document exists. Both are formally IN SCOPE for M4-HARD. Neither may be deferred to M5.

Feature ID Description PM Ruling F1-HARD-NAV-LABELS Nav rail items must display text labels beside the icons. See §2 for the chosen form and rationale. IN SCOPE — P0 F1-HARD-M5-PLACEHOLDER M5 nav items (Search, Lint, Review, Deep Search) must be removed from the nav rail entirely until M5 builds the logic. See §2 for rationale. IN SCOPE — P0

docs.title: 1c. Scope log — locked 2026-06-29
docs.text:
Locked feature IDs for M4-HARD: F1-HARD-SETTINGS, F1-HARD-COLLAPSE, F1-HARD-PROVIDER-EDIT, F1-HARD-MCP-STUB, F1-HARD-NAV-ORDER, F1-HARD-EMBED-STUB, F1-HARD-CONV-HISTORY, F1-HARD-NAV-LABELS, F1-HARD-M5-PLACEHOLDER

Total: 7 already-done items + 2 in-progress items = 9 work items.

These are all extensions of F1 (shell / nav rail), F1-NAV, and F16-rest (settings). No new feature IDs from CLAUDE.md §4 are introduced. No M5 work is started.

docs.title: 2. PM Rulings on the Two New Feedback Points
Ruling on Point B — M5 nav placeholders: REMOVE, do not label
docs.text:
DECISION: Remove Search, Lint, Review, and Deep Search from the M4 nav rail entirely. Do not show them as greyed-out disabled items with or without labels.

Rationale:

The current M4 nav rail shows these four items in a separate group below a separator, with disabled: true styling (color #30363d — near-invisible on the dark rail) and title tooltip "Coming in M5". The human tester found them confusing: grey icons with no visible label and no click behavior are not informative — they create uncertainty about whether the UI is broken or the feature is intentionally absent.

Two options were considered:

Option A — Keep them, add visible "coming soon" label text. Option B — Remove them from the rail until M5 ships the logic.

Option A fails for M4 because it requires rendering 4 additional nav items whose sole purpose is to communicate absence. A "coming soon" label next to a disabled icon does not help the user accomplish any task and adds visual noise in an already new UI. The developer has confirmed the items are non-functional; showing them in M4 is purely aspirational chrome. It also sets a precedent that every M6 feature should appear as a placeholder in M5, and so on — this is scope-creep of UI surface area.

Option B is the correct approach for M4: the nav rail should contain only sections the user can actually use. When M5 ships Search, Lint, Review, and Deep Search logic, those items are added back to the rail with full functionality. This matches how mature products handle progressive disclosure: features appear when they work.

The separator between active and placeholder items is also removed (there is nothing to separate). The final M4 nav rail is: Chat / Wiki / Sources / Graph / (spacer) / Settings.

Note: the i18n translation keys for these items (nav.search, nav.lint, nav.review, nav.deepSearch, nav.comingSoon) are RETAINED in en.json and it.json so M5 can use them without introducing new keys. Only the component entries in NavRail.tsx M5_ITEMS array are removed (or the M5_ITEMS array is rendered as an empty block).

docs.title: Ruling on Point A — Nav label form: PERSISTENT LABELS BESIDE ICONS (wider rail)
docs.text:
DECISION: Each nav item renders the icon plus a short text label below or beside the icon. The rail is widened from 48px to approximately 72px to accommodate. Labels are the existing i18n translation keys (already present in en.json and it.json). There is no tooltip-only or expandable-rail option at this stage.

Rationale:

Three forms were considered:

Form 1 — Wider rail with persistent labels beside/below icons. Form 2 — Icon-only with hover tooltips. Form 3 — Expandable/collapsible rail (collapsed = icon-only, expanded = icon + label).

Form 2 is rejected because it requires the user to hover each item to discover what it does. The human tester's exact complaint was "non parlanti" — the icons do not speak for themselves. Tooltips are a discoverability aid, not a substitute for visible labels. The icons used (message-square for Chat, files for Wiki, download for Sources, share for Graph, gear for Settings) are standard but not universal — a user unfamiliar with them cannot navigate without labels.

Form 3 would require state management for the collapsed/expanded rail toggle, an additional Zustand slice or localStorage key, and a persistence mechanism. It introduces more complexity than the problem warrants in a hardening pass. It also creates an inconsistent initial state (collapsed by default means the user sees unlabeled icons on first load — the same problem we are trying to fix).

Form 1 is the correct approach for M4-HARD. It is a CSS and layout change: widen the rail, add a with the label text below each icon, keep the existing i18n key bindings. The implementation is contained to NavRail.tsx and its CSS. No new Zustand state, no new API calls, no new i18n keys.

Implementation note for frontend-engineer: the rail width change from 48px to ~72px must not break the react-resizable-panels layout. The rail is a fixed-width flex child; verify the panels still resize correctly after the width change (F1 AC-F1-7 panel resize test must still pass). The label font should be 10–11px to keep the rail compact. The active-state highlight (currently a rounded rectangle around the icon only) should extend to cover both icon and label.

docs.title: F1-HARD-SETTINGS — 9-section settings panel
docs.text:
• AC-HARD-SET-1: The Settings section renders a two-column layout: a left sub-nav listing all 9 sections (General, LLM Models, Embeddings, Source Watch, API+MCP, Output, Interface, Maintenance, About) and a right content pane showing the selected section's content.
  • AC-HARD-SET-2: Clicking a sub-nav item switches the right pane without a page reload or full component remount. Active sub-nav item is visually highlighted.
  • AC-HARD-SET-3: All 9 section titles and sub-nav labels are i18n translation keys present in both en.json and it.json (keys: settings.nav.general, settings.nav.llmModels, etc.). No hardcoded display strings.
  • AC-HARD-SET-4: Sections with placeholder content (Embeddings, Source Watch, API+MCP, Interface) render a descriptive placeholder text referencing M5 (already in en.json: settings.embeddings.comingSoon, settings.apiMcp.comingSoon). These do NOT render empty white space — they communicate clearly that the feature is coming.
  • AC-HARD-SET-5: Settings panel is accessible via the Settings nav rail item. Sub-nav keyboard navigation (arrow keys) works as expected.
  • AC-HARD-SET-6: vitest test: renders all 9 sub-nav items; clicking each switches the active pane; active item has correct aria-current or aria-selected attribute.

docs.title: F1-HARD-COLLAPSE — Panel collapse/expand
docs.text:
• AC-HARD-COL-1: The left panel and the right panel each have a visible chevron button (or equivalent collapse trigger). Clicking the button collapses that panel to a minimal width (0px or a defined collapsed width) without removing it from the DOM.
  • AC-HARD-COL-2: Clicking the collapsed panel's expand trigger (or the chevron in its collapsed state) restores the panel to its previous width.
  • AC-HARD-COL-3: Collapse/expand state is preserved in localStorage (consistent with F16-settings AC-F16-settings-1: panel widths persist).
  • AC-HARD-COL-4: Collapsing a panel does not trigger a force layout or cause a long task on the main thread (I3). vitest: collapse action → assert no layout recompute signal.
  • AC-HARD-COL-5: vitest test: collapse left panel → assert panel width is at collapsed minimum; expand → assert panel width is restored.

docs.title: F1-HARD-PROVIDER-EDIT — Editable LLM Models section
docs.text:
• AC-HARD-PROV-1: The LLM Models settings section renders the list of existing provider configs from GET /provider/config. Each row shows: provider type, model_id, scope (vault/global), and a Delete button.
  • AC-HARD-PROV-2: An "Add provider" form (or expandable row) allows the user to select provider type, enter a model_id, optionally enter a base_url, and select scope. Submitting calls POST /provider/config. On success (HTTP 201 or 200), the new row appears in the list without a page reload. On failure, an error toast shows the response detail.
  • AC-HARD-PROV-3: Clicking Delete on a provider row calls DELETE /provider/config/{id}. On success (HTTP 200 or 204), the row is removed from the list. A confirmation prompt is shown before the DELETE is issued (text: i18n key settings.llmModels.confirmDelete).
  • AC-HARD-PROV-4: No model_id or provider type is hardcoded in any component. The list is entirely driven by GET /provider/config. (I6 reinforced.)
  • AC-HARD-PROV-5: vitest test: mock GET /provider/config with 2 providers → assert 2 rows rendered. Mock POST → assert new row appears. Mock DELETE → assert row removed.
  • AC-HARD-PROV-6: If the user attempts to delete the last remaining provider, a warning is shown ("This is the last configured provider. Chat and ingest will fail without a provider.") but the delete is still permitted — the system does not enforce a minimum of 1 provider at the UI level.

docs.title: F1-HARD-NAV-ORDER — Nav rail order and default section
docs.text:
• AC-HARD-ORD-1: The nav rail renders items in the following top-to-bottom order (after M4-HARD-M5-PLACEHOLDER items are removed — see below): Chat, Wiki, Sources, Graph, (spacer), Settings.
  • AC-HARD-ORD-2: The default active section on first load (no localStorage state) is Chat. Verified by vitest: fresh Zustand store → activeSection === "chat".
  • AC-HARD-ORD-3: The section previously active is restored from localStorage on page reload (F16-settings). If the restored section is one of the removed M5 items, the default (chat) is used instead — no broken state.

docs.title: F1-HARD-NAV-LABELS — Persistent text labels beside icons (Point A ruling)
docs.text:
• AC-HARD-LBL-1: Every nav rail item renders both an icon AND a visible text label. The label is positioned below the icon (or to its right, designer's choice, but it must be persistently visible — not hidden unless hovered).
  • AC-HARD-LBL-2: The rail width is increased from 48px to accommodate the labels. Target: approximately 72px. The exact value is at the frontend-engineer's discretion provided all labels are fully readable without truncation at 72px.
  • AC-HARD-LBL-3: Label text is sourced from the existing i18n translation keys (nav.chat, nav.wiki, nav.sources [maps to ingest section], nav.graph, nav.settings). No new i18n keys are required. Labels switch language when the locale changes (i18n already wired; this is a layout change only).
  • AC-HARD-LBL-4: The active-state visual indicator (currently a rounded highlight on the icon button) is extended to encompass both icon and label. The active state must remain clearly distinguishable in both EN and IT locales (label lengths differ).
  • AC-HARD-LBL-5: Font size for labels is 10–11px (do not exceed 12px — keeps the rail visually compact and does not push items into scroll territory at typical viewport heights).
  • AC-HARD-LBL-6: The react-resizable-panels layout adjustment (rail now ~72px) does not break the F1 AC-F1-7 panel resize Playwright test. The test may need its width assertions updated to account for the new rail width — this is an expected test update, not a regression.
  • AC-HARD-LBL-7: vitest test: render NavRail → assert each item's rendered output contains both an SVG icon element and a non-empty label span with the correct i18n key value for the "en" locale.
  • AC-HARD-LBL-8: The ingest badge (running-count dot) remains functional and visible after the label is added. It does not overlap the label text.

docs.title: F1-HARD-M5-PLACEHOLDER — Remove M5 nav items from M4 rail (Point B ruling)
docs.text:
• AC-HARD-M5P-1: The nav rail in M4 does NOT render Search, Lint, Review, or Deep Search nav items. None of these appear as enabled OR disabled items in the DOM in M4.
  • AC-HARD-M5P-2: The separator line between "active items" and "M5 placeholder items" is also removed (there is nothing to separate in M4).
  • AC-HARD-M5P-3: The M5_ITEMS array in NavRail.tsx is emptied (all 4 items removed) OR the M5 group rendering block renders nothing. The data structure may be retained as a comment or empty array for M5 to populate.
  • AC-HARD-M5P-4: The i18n keys nav.search, nav.lint, nav.review, nav.deepSearch, and nav.comingSoon are RETAINED in en.json and it.json. Only the rendered components are removed, not the translation keys. This preserves M5's ability to add items without touching the i18n files.
  • AC-HARD-M5P-5: The Section type in graphStore.ts is NOT changed — "search", "lint", "review", "deep-search" may remain as valid type members to avoid downstream TypeScript churn. The nav rail simply does not render buttons for them. If the active section is restored from localStorage as one of these values, the component falls back to "chat" (AC-HARD-ORD-3).
  • AC-HARD-M5P-6: vitest test: render NavRail → assert no button with data-section="search", data-section="lint", data-section="review", or data-section="deep-search" is in the DOM.
  • AC-HARD-M5P-7: Playwright E2E test: load the app → assert the nav rail renders exactly 5 interactive items: Chat, Wiki, Sources, Graph, Settings (4 top + 1 bottom). Assert no greyed or disabled items are present.

docs.title: Already-done stubs (MCP, Embeddings, Conv-History) — gate-only ACs
docs.text:
These were already implemented. The QA gate must verify them; no new code is expected.

  • AC-HARD-MCP-1: Settings > API+MCP section renders with the placeholder text (settings.apiMcp.comingSoon i18n key). No interactive elements other than the sub-nav item itself. Verified by vitest render test.
  • AC-HARD-EMBD-1: Settings > Embeddings section renders with the placeholder text (settings.embeddings.comingSoon i18n key). Verified by vitest render test.
  • AC-HARD-CONV-1: Settings > Output section renders a conversation history length selector with options 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 20. Selected value is persisted in localStorage under a stable key. Verified by vitest: change value → assert localStorage updated.
  • AC-HARD-CONV-2: The conversation history length value is read by the chat message assembler when constructing the messages array sent to the provider. If the selected value is 4, at most the last 4 messages (2 user + 2 assistant turns) are included. Verified by unit test: mock 10 messages + history=4 setting → assert sent array length is at most 4 (plus system prompt).

docs.title: 4. Anti-Scope-Creep Gate
docs.text:
The following items are explicitly OUT of M4-HARD. Any engineer proposing this work will be blocked and the orchestrator escalated.

What Deferred to Rationale MCP server configuration UI (actual settings, not placeholder) M5 Full MCP config requires M5 backend work (F17 MCP transport finalization). Placeholder only in M4. Vector embeddings configuration UI (actual settings) M5 Qdrant/bge-m3 settings require M5 work. Placeholder only in M4. Search functionality (semantic or keyword) M5 — F5 4-phase retrieval is M5. The Search nav item is REMOVED from M4 rail (see Point B ruling). Lint operation UI M6 — K2 lint Lint-fix loop is M6. Nav item removed from M4. Review queue UI M5 — F9 Async HITL queue is M5. Nav item removed from M4. Deep Search UI M5 — F10 Deep Research loop is M5. Nav item removed from M4. Expandable/collapsible rail (Form 3) Backlog (no sprint assigned) Not needed to fix the usability problem. May be revisited in M6 polish. Multi-format file upload (PDF, DOCX, etc.) M5 — F12 F12 boundary is unchanged. F1-UPLOAD accepts .txt/.md only. Any new ADR Only if architect decides If the nav-label width change or M5-removal triggers an architectural decision, architect decides whether an ADR is needed. PM does not pre-approve one. Additional settings sections beyond the 9 defined Not scoped The 9 sections are complete for M4. Any new section must be proposed with a feature ID and escalated to orchestrator.

docs.title: 5. DoD Gate Checklist for M4-HARD
docs.text:
All 4 sign-offs required before M4-HARD is declared done and EC-M4-HCP can be confirmed.

# Gate Agent responsible Required evidence 1 All ACs in §3 green (automated tests) qa-test-engineer vitest + Playwright; all new ACs PASS; ruff + black + mypy + ESLint + Prettier clean 2 No regression on previously-passing M4 gates qa-test-engineer T-NCL-001..022 (no-client-layout), T-OBS-001..015 (Obsidian compat), T-E2E-G3-001 (streaming perf) all still PASS 3 Architect gate: review rail width change (layout impact on I3/I4), M5 item removal (Zustand Section type safety), provider add/delete endpoints (I6) solution-architect Architect APPROVED (written or inline) 4 Tech-writer gate: i18n files updated if any new keys added; D5 screenshots refreshed to show new nav rail with labels (at minimum one new PNG replacing an old nav screenshot); docs/USER.md updated if the nav changes affect user journey steps tech-writer DOCS_STATUS note or inline PASS 5 PM sign-off (this document is the scope record; exit verdict to orchestrator after gates 1–4) product-manager Exit verdict delivered to orchestrator

Exit verdict options: M4-HARD DONE (all gates met) or M4-HARD NOT MET with precise gap list.

Once M4-HARD is DONE, Emanuele performs the final EC-M4-HCP browser confirmation (all 8 original conditions plus the two M4-EXT conditions remain in force, with the updated UI). Sprint 5 is unblocked only after that confirmation is recorded.

docs.title: The one pending D5 screenshot — ruling
docs.text:
The collapsed-panel screenshot (docs/screens/shell-collapsed-panel.png) could not be auto-captured because the Playwright selector lacked the data-testid attribute on the collapse chevrons. The orchestrator subsequently added data-testid="collapse-left-btn" and data-testid="collapse-right-btn" to AppShell.tsx, resolving the root cause.

Ruling: this is a non-blocking follow-up, not a gate blocker.

Rationale: (1) F1-HARD-COLLAPSE is functionally verified by AC-HARD-COL-1..5, all of which are GREEN in the 371/371 vitest suite. (2) The existing shell-3panel.png shows the collapse chevron buttons visually — the feature is not invisible in the docs. (3) The root cause (missing testid) is now fixed; the PNG recapture requires only a make screenshots run, not any code change. (4) The tech-writer explicitly deemed this non-blocking in their PASS-WITH-PENDING verdict. Deferring a screenshot recapture is within the scope of a follow-up item; it does not change whether the feature works.

Carry-forward item: CF-HARD-1 (see §6-PM carry-forward table below).

docs.title: Human checkpoint requirement
docs.text:
EC-M4-HCP (Emanuele's manual browser confirmation) is still required before Sprint 5 opens.

This PM sign-off verifies automated gates and agent sign-offs. It does NOT substitute for the human checkpoint. Emanuele must confirm in the browser: - All 8 original EC-M4-HCP conditions (including labeled nav rail, 9-section settings, panel collapse, editable provider list, conversation history selector) - The two M4-EXT conditions (upload and scheduled import)

Sprint 5 is unblocked only after EC-M4-HCP is recorded by the orchestrator.

PM scope-lock and exit-criteria sign-off: M4-HARD MET-WITH-FOLLOWUPS | 2026-06-29 product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Carry-forwards: CF-HARD-1 (collapsed-panel screenshot, M5 pre-start), CF-HARD-6 (persist guard, M5 conditional) Human checkpoint: EC-M4-HCP still required before Sprint 5

docs.title: 6. Velocity Note
docs.text:
M4-HARD is a small increment: 2 items in-progress, 7 retroactive verifications. It does not change the M5 scope or timeline. Sprint 5 scope (F5, F9, F10, F12, F13) is unchanged.

The primary lesson from this hardening pass: UI skeleton features (nav rail) need a labeled-icon standard defined at the start of the sprint, not discovered in human testing. This is a calibration note for Sprint 5: define and document the nav UX standard before the frontend engineer builds it, not after.

docs.title: §Stories — User Stories for M4-HARD (functional-analyst, 2026-06-29)
docs.text: Authored by: functional-analyst These stories operationalise the PM's §3 acceptance criteria for engineers and QA. Binary pass/fail only. No partial compliance. Invariants primed per story to alert engineers before code is written.

docs.title: US-HARD-SET — 9-section settings panel (F1-HARD-SETTINGS)
docs.text:
As a Synapse user, I want the Settings section to present a two-column layout with a left sub-nav listing all 9 configuration areas and a right pane showing the selected area's content, so that I can navigate to any setting without leaving the page or losing my context.

Invariants touched: I3 (no heavy re-renders — sub-nav switch must not remount heavy components), I6 (provider config displayed from GET /provider/config, never hardcoded).

D-artifacts: D5 (screenshots refresh required), D6 (USER.md nav journey steps).

Source files: frontend/src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx, frontend/src/locales/en.json, frontend/src/locales/it.json.

ACs: AC-HARD-SET-1 through AC-HARD-SET-6.

docs.title: US-HARD-COL — Panel collapse/expand (F1-HARD-COLLAPSE)
docs.text:
As a Synapse user, I want to collapse the left and right panels with a single click so that I can reclaim screen space when I only need to focus on one panel.

Invariants touched: I3 (collapsing must not trigger a main-thread layout recalculation or long task), I4 (react-resizable-panels manages the collapse; no custom DOM measurement loop).

D-artifacts: D5 (screenshots showing collapsed state).

Source files: frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx (or equivalent panel host).

ACs: AC-HARD-COL-1 through AC-HARD-COL-5.

docs.title: US-HARD-PROV — Editable LLM provider list (F1-HARD-PROVIDER-EDIT)
docs.text:
As a Synapse user, I want to add a new inference provider and delete an existing one from the Settings > LLM Models section, so that I can manage which AI backends the system uses without editing configuration files.

Invariants touched: I6 (every provider displayed from GET /provider/config; no model_id hardcoded in any component), I3 (list update on POST/DELETE must not cause full re-render of unrelated settings sections).

D-artifacts: D4 (POST /provider/config and DELETE /provider/config/{id} must be in OpenAPI), D5 (screenshot of editable provider list).

Source files: frontend/src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx (LLM Models section).

ACs: AC-HARD-PROV-1 through AC-HARD-PROV-6.

docs.title: US-HARD-MCP — MCP settings placeholder (F1-HARD-MCP-STUB)
docs.text:
As a Synapse user opening Settings > API+MCP, I want to see a clear placeholder message indicating that MCP server configuration is coming in M5, so that I know the section is intentional and not a rendering error.

Invariants touched: none violated; confirms M4 scope boundary.

D-artifacts: none (no new diagram; i18n key already present).

Source files: frontend/src/locales/en.json (settings.apiMcp.comingSoon), frontend/src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx.

ACs: AC-HARD-MCP-1.

docs.title: US-HARD-ORD — Nav rail order and default section (F1-HARD-NAV-ORDER)
docs.text:
As a new Synapse user loading the app for the first time, I want to land on the Chat section by default and see navigation items in a logical task-first order (Chat, Wiki, Sources, Graph, Settings), so that the most common task is immediately accessible.

Invariants touched: I3 (Zustand activeSection is a scalar; selector discipline means no whole-store re-render on section change).

D-artifacts: D5 (nav screenshots showing correct order).

Source files: frontend/src/components/nav/NavRail.tsx, frontend/src/store/graphStore.ts.

ACs: AC-HARD-ORD-1 through AC-HARD-ORD-3.

docs.title: US-HARD-EMBD — Embeddings settings placeholder (F1-HARD-EMBED-STUB)
docs.text:
As a Synapse user opening Settings > Vector Embeddings, I want to see a clear placeholder message indicating that embeddings configuration is coming in M5, so that I understand the section is reserved and not broken.

Invariants touched: none violated; confirms M4 scope boundary.

D-artifacts: none.

Source files: frontend/src/locales/en.json (settings.embeddings.comingSoon), frontend/src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx.

ACs: AC-HARD-EMBD-1.

docs.title: US-HARD-CONV — Conversation history length selector (F1-HARD-CONV-HISTORY)
docs.text:
As a Synapse user, I want to choose how many past conversation messages are included when I send a new chat message, so that I can trade off context richness against token cost by selecting a history length (2 / 4 / 6 / 8 / 10 / 20).

Invariants touched: I3 (selector is a simple controlled input; no re-render cost), I7 (history length directly limits the messages array sent to the provider; the setting is a token-budget enforcement mechanism).

D-artifacts: none (UI only; no new API endpoint).

Source files: frontend/src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx (Output section), frontend/src/locales/en.json (settings.output.*).

ACs: AC-HARD-CONV-1 through AC-HARD-CONV-2.

docs.title: US-HARD-LBL — Persistent text labels beside nav icons (F1-HARD-NAV-LABELS) — P0
docs.text:
As a Synapse user, I want each navigation rail item to show a visible text label below its icon at all times (not just on hover), so that I can identify each section at a glance without needing to memorize icon meanings.

Invariants touched: I3 (label render is static text; zero per-token work), I4 (rail width change must not break react-resizable-panels layout or introduce a main-thread layout measurement loop). Verifying AC-HARD-LBL-6 requires that the existing F1 AC-F1-7 panel resize Playwright test still passes — the test may need its expected rail-width constant updated but must not be deleted.

D-artifacts: D5 (nav screenshot refresh required — at minimum one new PNG replacing old nav screenshot), D6 (USER.md nav section updated if wording describes icon-only items).

Source files: frontend/src/components/nav/NavRail.tsx, frontend/src/styles/nav-rail.css (or equivalent inline styles).

ACs: AC-HARD-LBL-1 through AC-HARD-LBL-8.

docs.title: US-HARD-M5P — Remove M5 placeholder items from nav rail (F1-HARD-M5-PLACEHOLDER) — P0
docs.text:
As a Synapse user, I want the navigation rail in M4 to show only sections that are fully functional (Chat, Wiki, Sources, Graph, Settings), so that I do not encounter disabled or greyed-out items that create uncertainty about whether the application is broken.

Invariants touched: I3 (fewer items = fewer renders; no impact on Zustand scalar discipline). TypeScript safety: the Section union type in graphStore.ts retains "search", "lint", "review", "deep-search" as valid members so downstream code does not require a mass-refactor at M5.

D-artifacts: D5 (nav screenshot must show exactly 5 interactive items).

Source files: frontend/src/components/nav/NavRail.tsx (M5_ITEMS array emptied), frontend/src/store/graphStore.ts (Section type UNCHANGED), frontend/src/locales/en.json (nav.search / nav.lint / nav.review / nav.deepSearch / nav.comingSoon RETAINED, not deleted).

ACs: AC-HARD-M5P-1 through AC-HARD-M5P-7.

§Stories authored by: functional-analyst (claude-sonnet-4-6) | 2026-06-29 Stories cover all 9 M4-HARD feature IDs. Handoff: user stories → frontend-engineer (all 9 items are frontend-only); TRACEABILITY.md M4-HARD section → qa-test-engineer (fill Test IDs after writing tests).

PM scope-lock: M4-HARD locked 2026-06-29 | product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Feature IDs: F1-HARD-SETTINGS, F1-HARD-COLLAPSE, F1-HARD-PROVIDER-EDIT, F1-HARD-MCP-STUB, F1-HARD-NAV-ORDER, F1-HARD-EMBED-STUB, F1-HARD-CONV-HISTORY, F1-HARD-NAV-LABELS, F1-HARD-M5-PLACEHOLDER Ruling on Point A (nav labels): persistent labels beside icons, wider rail (~72px) Ruling on Point B (M5 placeholders): REMOVE from M4 nav rail entirely Handoff: acceptance criteria (§3) → functional-analyst Handoff: exit-criteria verdict (§5) → orchestrator after all 4 sign-offs

docs.title: M4 Closure Record — Sprint 4 (v0.4) "Usable & fluid"
docs.text:
Date: 2026-06-29 · Verified live on: MacBook Pro (Docker Desktop, Ollama.app) · Verdict: MET

The PM gate verdict (v0.4-hard-scope.md §6-PM) was MET-WITH-FOLLOWUPS, blocked only on EC-M4-HCP — the human browser-testing checkpoint. Emanuele conducted that testing during the closure session, found two blocking usability defects, both were fixed, and the v0.4.0 release was cut and pushed. This record clears EC-M4-HCP.

docs.title: Human checkpoint — what was actually exercised
docs.text:
Emanuele ran the full stack in the browser and tested the M4 surface. The session is the EC-M4-HCP checkpoint in action: real usage surfaced real defects that the automated gates could not (both were live-infra / UX issues, not logic bugs).

EC-M4-HCP condition Result 3-panel shell + labeled nav rail; sections switch without reload ✅ confirmed Sources → ingest runs list + Run Ingest ✅ confirmed Chat streams token-by-token, no freeze ✅ confirmed Settings → LLM Models editable (add/delete), persists ✅ confirmed — defect found + fixed (see below) Graph → sigma viewer, structural edges, hover-dim ✅ confirmed docs/USER.md exists ✅ (already met) Upload drag-drop in Sources ✅ confirmed Settings → Source Watch schedule card ✅ confirmed Nav rail shows text labels at ~72px ✅ confirmed Settings renders 9-section left-nav ✅ confirmed — defect found + fixed (Embeddings section)

docs.title: Two defects found in live testing and fixed
docs.text:
1. Local (Ollama) provider could not be configured (commit 3e6bcaf). The Settings → LLM Models "Add provider" form hid the base_url field for provider_type=local, so the Ollama server URL was stored as NULL and OllamaProvider raised at runtime unless OLLAMA_URL env was set. The form now shows base_url for both local and api, with type-specific placeholders (qwen2.5:3b, http://localhost:11434). This is what blocked the developer from pointing at his installed qwen2.5:3b model.

  2. Embeddings section was a dead "coming in M5" stub (commit 3e6bcaf). Replaced with a live read-only display of EMBEDDING_URL / EMBEDDING_MODEL / EMBEDDING_DIM via a new GET /config/embedding endpoint, so the developer can confirm bge-m3 is wired without opening .env.

Both slipped the sandbox gate because neither is verifiable without a human driving the provider form against a live Ollama — exactly the class of defect the human checkpoint exists to catch.

docs.title: Dev-environment improvement (not an M4 defect)
docs.text: To make the human checkpoint repeatable on macOS, docker-compose.dev.yml was added (commits 196cf99, e4f3d1a): a local-dev override that adds Qdrant + the Vite dev server as containers so the whole stack starts with one command. Ollama stays on the host. Production docker-compose.yml is unchanged (I9 respected).

docs.title: Release
docs.text: v0.4.0 — Usable & fluid (M4) tagged on sprint/v0.4 and published: https://github.com/Emanuele-Chiummo/llm-wiki-synapse/releases/tag/v0.4.0

docs.title: Gate chain (final)
docs.text: Gate Verdict QA PASS-WITH-NOTES (460 pytest / 370 vitest green; ruff/black/eslint/tsc clean) Architect GREEN — I1–I9 all respected; ER zero drift; F17 sound Tech-writer PASS-WITH-PENDING (D2/D4/D5/D6/D7 current) PM MET-WITH-FOLLOWUPS → cleared by this record Human (EC-M4-HCP) ✅ CLEARED — this document

docs.title: Carry-forwards into M5 (non-blocking, tracked)
docs.text:
• TRACEABILITY.md stale PENDING rows → GREEN (GAP-HARD-1, GAP-HARD-5, AC-F4-GUX-, M4-Phase0-)
  • docs/sprints/v0.4-architect-review.md — create to close the audit trail
  • graphViewer.test.ts (3 AC-F4-GUX vitest items), test_seed_demo.py (T-SEED items)
  • CF-HARD-1: recapture docs/screens/shell-collapsed-panel.png
  • AC-HARD-SET-5: settings left-nav arrow-key handler

M4 is MET. Sprint 5 (v0.5 / M5 — "Feature parity core") is cleared to start.

docs.title: Sprint 4 — v0.4 / M4 — PM Scope Lock and Phase Plan
docs.text: Author: product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Date: 2026-06-28 Gate: Sprint start — formal scope confirmation for M4 "Usable and fluid" Branch: sprint/v0.3 (to be cut to sprint/v0.4 after EC-M3-17 human checkpoint) Prerequisite: M3 MET-PENDING-HUMAN-CHECKPOINT (docs/sprints/v0.3-pm-signoff.md §8) Inputs: BACKLOG.md §Sprint 4, CLAUDE.md §4/§8/§9/§11, TRACEABILITY.md, ADR-0016

docs.title: 1. M4 Scope Confirmation
docs.text: Sprint 4 / M4 scope is LOCKED to exactly the following feature IDs. No other work is in scope. Any proposal outside this list must be escalated to the orchestrator and explicitly approved before a single line of code is written.

docs.title: 1a. Baseline M4 features (from BACKLOG.md §Sprint 4)
docs.text: Feature ID Description Invariants in force F1 3-panel shell: tree / chat / preview, resizable panels, activity panel, scenario templates I3, I4 (CodeMirror 6; no WYSIWYG; all long lists virtualized) F6 Multi-conversation persistent chat, cited-refs, regenerate, save-to-wiki I3 (no per-token heavy parse; Zustand selectors) F7 Reasoning <think> display: streaming, collapsed by default I3 F8 LaTeX to Unicode conversion (parse at stream END, not per-token) I3 F14 Configurable context window (4K–1M); budget 60/20/5/15 allocation I7 (bounded budget) F17-UI Provider Selector UI: per-vault/global dropdown, wired to existing backend F17 provider_config I6 (no hardcoded provider) F16-rest Remaining F16 sub-features: i18n IT/EN, settings persistence, .obsidian auto-gen, GFM rendering, multi-provider chat timeout I5 (.obsidian auto-gen must not break Obsidian compat) G3 Streaming performance gate: first mandatory sprint; no per-token heavy parse; Zustand selector pattern (pre-wired in v0.3 viewer store); assert with Playwright I3 (defining test) D5-update UI screenshots refreshed: 3-panel shell, provider selector, chat; make screenshots target updated I8 D6 USER.md + DEPLOY.md draft versions (functional; not final — final is v0.6) I8 NB-6 mmdc CI render check — carried from v0.2/v0.3; devops-engineer must resolve before M4 sign-off I8 NB-7 Cosmetic: graph-recompute.mmd hit-path read source (tech-writer, optional P3 polish) I8 NB-8 Cosmetic: component.mmd store filename label store/graphStore.ts (tech-writer, optional P3 polish) I8

docs.title: 1b. Phase 2 additions — stakeholder-requested (logged 2026-06-28)
docs.text:
These two items were explicitly requested by stakeholder Emanuele and are hereby added to the locked M4 scope. Visual direction: inspired by nashsu/llm_wiki navigation style. They are assigned to Phase 2 (navigation + operations + provider/settings) and do NOT touch the chat feature set (Phase 3 boundary intact).

Feature ID Description Invariants in force F1-NAV Left Navigation Rail: persistent app navigation with switchable sections — Pages (file tree), Graph, Ingest, Settings. Chat section reserved for Phase 3. Restructures the shell's left region from a flat page tree into a section navigator. I3 (no per-token work), I4 (virtualized page list within Pages section) F1-INGEST-VIEW Ingest Activity View: read-only list of recent ingest runs from the existing ingest_runs table (status, pages_created, total_cost_usd [I7], timestamps, errors) plus a "Run Ingest" trigger button (POST /ingest/trigger). Requires a new backend endpoint GET /ingest/runs. This is a history + trigger view ONLY — it is NOT the F9 async HITL review queue. The F9 boundary is explicit: F9 = async queue for reviewing AI-generated pages (Create / Deep-Research / Skip actions, pre-generated queries) — that is M5 work and must not be started here. I7 (cost field from bounded-loop log)

Backend work item arising from F1-INGEST-VIEW:

Work Item Description BE-INGEST-RUNS New endpoint: GET /ingest/runs — returns a paginated list of recent ingest runs from ingest_runs table. Response fields per run: id (uuid), vault_id (uuid), status (enum: running / completed / failed / converged_false), provider_type (string), pages_created (int), iterations_used (int), total_cost_usd (decimal), started_at (timestamptz), completed_at (timestamptz nullable), error_message (text nullable). Query params: limit (default 20, max 100), offset (default 0), vault_id (optional filter). Requires D4 (openapi.json) regeneration after adding endpoint.

docs.title: 1c. GraphUX (M4-graph) — work already done, now formally tracked
docs.text:
This bucket captures architectural and frontend graph-rendering work that was executed pragmatically at the v0.3 → v0.4 boundary before this formal sprint kickoff. It is now formalized in the M4 sprint record so it is tested, gated, and cannot slip through untracked. The implementing ADR is ADR-0016 (docs/adr/0016-obsidian-graph-rendering.md).

Sub-items within GraphUX (M4-graph):

Sub-ID Description ADR reference M4-GUX-1 Engine structural-only edges: KEEP direct-link + shared-source as edge generators; demote Adamic-Adar and same-type to weight modulators only. New edge inclusion gate. Eliminates type-clique hairball on synthetic fixture. ADR-0016 §1 M4-GUX-2 Per-edge kind field ("link" or "source") added to edges table (optional column) and GraphEdgeResponse. Backward-compatible (additive field; existing clients ignore it). ADR-0016 §4 M4-GUX-3 Node size formula revised: size = 1.0 + 1.0 * sqrt(structural_degree). degree field redefined to structural_degree. Replaces 1 + ln(1+weighted_degree). ADR-0016 §2 M4-GUX-4 Migrations 0004 (edges.kind TEXT nullable) and 0005 (pages.pinned BOOLEAN). ER schema.mmd and openapi.json updated. ADR-0016 §5 M4-GUX-5 Server-side near-circular layout envelope (_compress_to_disc, aspect ratio ~1.04). Stays server-side; I2 intact. ADR-0016 §3 M4-GUX-6 Single-node DRAG with position persistence: PATCH /pages/{id}/position endpoint; pages.pinned column; GraphCache.patch_node_position (no relayout, no data_version bump). Engine preserves pinned coords across recomputes. I2-compatible (single-node direct manipulation, NOT a force layout; no-client-layout guard test must still pass). ADR-0016 §4 (API extension) M4-GUX-7 Frontend graph viewer rewritten Obsidian-style: color-by-type (CVD-safe palette + redundant legend encoding), node size proportional to connections, hover-dim (hovered node + neighbors highlighted, rest faded), accessible halo labels (~16:1 contrast AAA), label LOD, prefers-reduced-motion media query, aria-live selected-node announcements. ADR-0016 §3, CLAUDE.md §3 I4 M4-GUX-8 Realistic demo dataset: seed_demo_vault.py generating 140-node scale-free graph, so node-size variation is visible in D5 screenshots. ADR-0016 §Consequences

All M4-GUX items are subject to the same DoD gate as the rest of M4: QA green, architect sign-off, tech-writer sign-off, PM exit sign-off.

docs.title: 1d. Scope log — locked 2026-06-28; amended 2026-06-28
docs.text:
Locked feature IDs for M4: F1, F1-NAV, F1-INGEST-VIEW, F6, F7, F8, F14, F17-UI, F16-rest, G3, D5-update, D6, NB-6, NB-7, NB-8, M4-GUX-1..8, BE-INGEST-RUNS

Total: 14 baseline + 2 Phase 2 additions + 1 backend work item + 8 GraphUX sub-items = 25 work items.

Amendment rationale: F1-NAV and F1-INGEST-VIEW added at stakeholder Emanuele's explicit request on 2026-06-28. Both are extensions of F1 (shell restructuring + operations visibility) and are correctly scoped to Phase 2. No previously out-of-scope features are introduced. The F9 boundary is explicitly maintained: Ingest Activity View is read-only history + trigger; F9 async HITL queue (Create / Deep-Research / Skip) remains M5.

docs.title: 2. Acceptance Criteria Per Feature
docs.text: The following are crisp, testable acceptance criteria handed to functional-analyst for elaboration into user stories. Each AC maps 1-to-1 to its feature ID.

docs.title: F1 — 3-panel shell
docs.text:
• AC-F1-1: The frontend renders exactly 3 resizable panels: left (file tree), center (chat/main), right (preview/graph). Panel resize uses CSS or a drag handle; no layout calculated in JS on every frame.
  • AC-F1-2: All long lists in the tree panel (more than 50 items) are virtualized using TanStack Virtual. No non-virtualized list rendering more than 50 DOM nodes at once (I4).
  • AC-F1-3: The editor/preview panel uses CodeMirror 6 exclusively. No ProseMirror, no contentEditable WYSIWYG element in the DOM (I4).
  • AC-F1-4: The graph sigma viewer from v0.3 is embedded in the 3-panel shell (right panel or as a tab), not removed. Its no-client-layout bundle assertion (T-NCL-001..022) must still pass.
  • AC-F1-5: An activity panel (status bar or overlay) shows: current vault, active provider name, last ingest timestamp, data_version.
  • AC-F1-6: At least 2 scenario templates are selectable from the UI (e.g. "Summarize document", "Find related pages"); each pre-fills the chat input with a template prompt.
  • AC-F1-7: Playwright E2E test: 3 panels are visible in the DOM; resizing left panel to 200px does not cause a layout reflow longer than 16ms (captured via CDP PerformanceMetrics).

docs.title: F1-NAV — Left Navigation Rail
docs.text:
Visual direction: nashsu/llm_wiki-inspired persistent side navigation. The left region of the shell becomes a navigator bar with labeled sections rather than a plain file tree.

  • AC-F1-NAV-1: The shell's left region contains a persistent vertical navigation rail with at minimum 4 section entries: Pages (file tree), Graph (sigma viewer), Ingest (ingest activity view), Settings (settings panel). A Chat section item is present in the rail but renders as "coming in Phase 3" or is disabled, clearly signalling its reserved status — it must NOT open a chat panel in Phase 2.
  • AC-F1-NAV-2: Clicking a rail section switches the main content area to that section's view without a full page reload. The active section item is visually indicated (highlighted/selected state).
  • AC-F1-NAV-3: The rail is persistent across view switches — it does not collapse or disappear when the active section changes.
  • AC-F1-NAV-4: The Pages section renders the virtualized file tree from F1 (AC-F1-2 still applies: TanStack Virtual for lists >50 items). The Graph section renders the sigma viewer (AC-F1-4 still applies). The Settings section renders the settings panel (F17-UI, F14, F16-rest).
  • AC-F1-NAV-5: Rail section labels are translation keys (appear in locales/en.json and locales/it.json per F16-i18n) — no hardcoded display strings in the component.
  • AC-F1-NAV-6: The activity panel (AC-F1-5: vault name, active provider, last ingest timestamp, data_version) is visible regardless of which rail section is active — it must not be hidden when switching views.
  • AC-F1-NAV-7: Playwright E2E test: assert all 4 section nav items are in the DOM; click "Graph" → sigma canvas visible; click "Pages" → file tree visible; click "Settings" → settings panel visible; click "Ingest" → ingest list visible. No full page reload between switches (no navigation event fired).
  • AC-F1-NAV-8: vitest test: active section state in Zustand store updates correctly on nav item click; no selector re-computation fires for unrelated store slices when nav state changes (I3 Zustand selector discipline).

docs.title: F1-INGEST-VIEW — Ingest Activity View
docs.text:
Boundary note: This view is READ-ONLY history + a trigger button. It is EXPLICITLY NOT the F9 async HITL review queue. F9 (Create / Deep-Research / Skip actions, pre-generated queries, async queue management) is M5 and must not be started here. Any engineer proposing F9 features inside this view during M4 will be blocked.

  • AC-F1-IV-1: The Ingest section (accessible via the nav rail) renders a list of recent ingest runs fetched from GET /ingest/runs. Each list row displays: status (color-coded badge: running / completed / failed / converged_false), provider type, pages created count, total cost in USD (formatted to 4 decimal places to honour I7 cost visibility), started_at timestamp (human-readable relative format, e.g. "2 minutes ago"), and error_message (truncated to 80 chars with an expand control if present).
  • AC-F1-IV-2: The list is paginated or virtualized: at most 20 rows rendered per page; a "Load more" control or scroll-triggered pagination fetches the next batch. If TanStack Virtual is used, no more than 40 DOM rows are mounted at once.
  • AC-F1-IV-3: A "Run Ingest" button triggers POST /ingest/trigger with the current vault_id. On success (HTTP 202), a transient success toast is shown and the list auto-refreshes (re-fetches GET /ingest/runs). On failure, an error toast shows the error message from the response body.
  • AC-F1-IV-4: While an ingest run has status "running", its row displays a loading indicator (spinner or pulsing badge). The list polls or uses a short refresh interval (configurable, default 5s) while any run is in "running" status; polling stops when no "running" rows are visible.
  • AC-F1-IV-5: The view is read-only. There are no "approve", "reject", "skip", or "deep-research" actions on any row. These are F9 features and must not appear here.
  • AC-F1-IV-6: The backend endpoint GET /ingest/runs exists and returns the fields specified in BE-INGEST-RUNS (§1b). It is documented in docs/api/openapi.json (D4 updated via make openapi). Pydantic response model validated by pytest schema test.
  • AC-F1-IV-7: vitest test: mock GET /ingest/runs response with 3 runs (completed, failed, running) → assert all 3 rows render with correct status badge and cost display.
  • AC-F1-IV-8: Playwright E2E test: navigate to Ingest section; assert list renders; assert "Run Ingest" button is present; assert clicking it fires a POST /ingest/trigger request (network assertion via CDP or Playwright route intercept).

docs.title: BE-INGEST-RUNS — GET /ingest/runs backend endpoint
docs.text:
• AC-BE-IR-1: GET /ingest/runs returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body: {items: [...], total: int, limit: int, offset: int}. Each item contains all fields from the ingest_runs table as specified in §1b: id, vault_id, status, provider_type, pages_created, iterations_used, total_cost_usd, started_at, completed_at (nullable), error_message (nullable).
  • AC-BE-IR-2: Query parameters: limit (int, default 20, max 100), offset (int, default 0), vault_id (uuid, optional filter). Invalid limit/offset values return HTTP 422 with a validation error body.
  • AC-BE-IR-3: Results are ordered by started_at DESC (most recent first).
  • AC-BE-IR-4: The endpoint is added to the FastAPI router and appears in the regenerated openapi.json with a correct Pydantic response schema (D4 zero-drift gate applies on next make openapi run).
  • AC-BE-IR-5: pytest integration test: seed 3 ingest_runs rows with different statuses; call GET /ingest/runs; assert all 3 returned in started_at DESC order; assert total = 3.

docs.title: F6 — Multi-conversation persistent chat
docs.text:
• AC-F6-1: Conversations are persisted in Postgres (new conversations and messages tables). A page refresh restores the last active conversation.
  • AC-F6-2: Multiple named conversations can be created, switched between, and deleted. Switching is <100ms (measured by vitest timer mock).
  • AC-F6-3: Each assistant message displays [n] citation markers that link to the source page (page title shown on hover). Citation data is stored with the message.
  • AC-F6-4: A "regenerate" button re-issues the last user prompt to the provider and replaces the last assistant message. The prior message is removed from the UI (not kept in history).
  • AC-F6-5: A "save to wiki" button on an assistant message triggers POST /ingest with the message content as source text; the ingest result (page title + link) is shown inline.
  • AC-F6-6: Message history list is virtualized (TanStack Virtual) — no more than 30 DOM nodes rendered at once regardless of conversation length (I3, I4).

docs.title: F7 — Reasoning <think> display
docs.text:
• AC-F7-1: When an assistant message contains <think>…</think> tags, the reasoning block is extracted and rendered in a separate collapsible section ("Reasoning"), collapsed by default.
  • AC-F7-2: The <think> block is NOT stripped from the stored message content; it is stored in full and re-parsed on render (no irreversible mutation of stored data).
  • AC-F7-3: Streaming: the <think> block accumulates during stream and collapses when </think> is received. The main content begins rendering after </think>. No heavy parse during stream (I3).
  • AC-F7-4: vitest test: a fixture message string with <think>…</think> renders the reasoning section collapsed and the main content visible.

docs.title: F8 — LaTeX to Unicode
docs.text:
• AC-F8-1: LaTeX expressions in assistant messages are converted to Unicode at stream END (on the done or final chunk event). No conversion is triggered per-token (I3).
  • AC-F8-2: The conversion covers at minimum: Greek letters (α β γ … Ω), common operators (∑ ∏ ∫ ∂ ∇), arrows (→ ← ↔ ⇒ ⇔), and fractions/superscripts/subscripts for common cases.
  • AC-F8-3: Unconvertible LaTeX (complex display math) is left as a fenced code block, not silently dropped.
  • AC-F8-4: vitest test: fixture string with 5 LaTeX expressions converts correctly after stream END; no conversion fires during incremental stream appends.

docs.title: F14 — Configurable context window
docs.text:
• AC-F14-1: A settings UI (settings panel or modal) exposes a context window size selector: 4K, 8K, 16K, 32K, 64K, 128K, 256K, 512K, 1M (or numeric input validated against these bounds).
  • AC-F14-2: The selected size is persisted in the provider_config table (new column context_window_tokens). Default is 32K if not set.
  • AC-F14-3: The retrieval assembler (F5 stub or the chat message builder) respects the budget split: 60% conversation history, 20% retrieved context, 5% system prompt, 15% generation headroom. The split is computed from the configured window, not hardcoded to absolute token counts.
  • AC-F14-4: If the conversation history exceeds its 60% allocation, the oldest messages are truncated (not the most recent). The truncation boundary is computed server-side.
  • AC-F14-5: Unit test: mock a 10-message conversation against a 4K window; assert messages[0..N] are truncated to keep total ≤ 60% × 4096, and the split percentages are correct.

docs.title: F17-UI — Provider Selector
docs.text:
• AC-F17-UI-1: A Provider Selector dropdown is visible in the settings panel or activity panel. It lists all configured providers from GET /provider/config.
  • AC-F17-UI-2: Selecting a provider posts to POST /provider/config with the selected provider_type and scope=vault (current vault) or scope=global per a sub-toggle. The selection persists across page refresh.
  • AC-F17-UI-3: The active provider name is shown in the activity panel (feeds AC-F1-5).
  • AC-F17-UI-4: The dropdown correctly shows 3 provider types: Local (Ollama), API (Anthropic / OpenAI-compatible), CLI (claude-agent-sdk). Each shows its model_id from provider_config.
  • AC-F17-UI-5: Changing the provider in the UI does not require a page reload; the next ingest or chat uses the newly selected provider. vitest test: simulate provider change → assert store updates + next mock request uses new provider_type.
  • AC-F17-UI-6: No model ID or provider type is hardcoded in any frontend component; all values come from the GET /provider/config response (mirrors backend AC-F17-4 / I6).

docs.title: F16-rest — Remaining F16 sub-features
docs.text:
• AC-F16-i18n-1: The UI renders in Italian when the browser locale is it or it-IT and in English otherwise. Language detection uses navigator.language. At minimum the following strings are translated: panel titles, button labels (Ingest, Chat, Save, Settings), error messages.
  • AC-F16-i18n-2: Language strings are NOT hardcoded inline; they live in a locales/en.json and locales/it.json file. vitest test: every key in en.json has a corresponding key in it.json.
  • AC-F16-settings-1: User settings (active provider, preferred language, panel widths, context window size) are persisted in localStorage and restored on page load.
  • AC-F16-settings-2: A "Reset settings" action clears localStorage and resets UI to defaults without requiring a hard reload.
  • AC-F16-obsidian-1: The backend generates vault/wiki/.obsidian/app.json on startup if absent, and regenerates it if the vault_id changes. Generated config remains a valid Obsidian app.json (JSON-parseable, contains "legacyEditor": false). Extends AC-K7-1 (v0.1).
  • AC-F16-gfm-1: Markdown in assistant messages and wiki preview is rendered as GitHub Flavored Markdown: tables, strikethrough, task lists, and autolinks are supported. No custom parser; use a library (e.g. marked or remark with GFM plugin).
  • AC-F16-timeout-1: Each provider call (ingest and chat) respects a configurable timeout (default 60s for chat, 120s for ingest). Timeout is stored in provider_config as timeout_seconds. On timeout, the UI shows a user-visible error ("Provider did not respond in Xs") and the backend logs the timeout as a provider failure (feeds the I7 bounded-fallback mechanism).

docs.title: G3 — Streaming performance gate
docs.text:
• AC-G3-1: Playwright test (new, mandatory): open a chat, send a message, observe streaming. Assert no JS long task >50ms on the main thread during the stream (PerformanceObserver + CDP). This is the first mandatory G3 test; must not be left deferred-to-live at M4 sign-off unless a live environment waiver is explicitly granted by the orchestrator.
  • AC-G3-2: Markdown and LaTeX parsing fires EXACTLY ONCE on the done event (stream end). Verified by a vitest spy: the parse function call count equals 1 regardless of how many token chunks were emitted.
  • AC-G3-3: Zustand store update during streaming appends to a raw string buffer; no selector re-computation fires per token. Verified by vitest: 100 simulated token appends → selector call count = 0 during stream; = 1 after stream ends.
  • AC-G3-4: The message history list does not re-render all existing messages when a new token arrives. Verified by vitest render-count spy: existing messages' render count does not increase during streaming of a new message.

docs.title: D5-update — Screenshots refreshed
docs.text:
• AC-D5-upd-1: Playwright captures updated PNG screenshots: docs/screens/shell-3panel.png (3-panel shell loaded), docs/screens/shell-provider-selector.png (provider dropdown open), docs/screens/shell-chat-streaming.png (chat with at least one assistant message visible).
  • AC-D5-upd-2: The v0.3 graph screenshots (graph-viewer-initial.png, graph-viewer-rendered.png or node-selected.png) are REPLACED with updated versions that reflect the ADR-0016 Obsidian-style rendering (structural edges only, node-size variation, hover-dim). If v0.3 screenshots were never committed (deferred-to-live), this is their first committed version.
  • AC-D5-upd-3: make screenshots target runs all Playwright capture tests in one command and exits 0.

docs.title: D6 — User guide and Deploy guide (drafts)
docs.text:
• AC-D6-1: docs/USER.md exists and covers at minimum: what Synapse does, how to start it (docker compose up), how to drop a file into vault/raw/sources/ and see a wiki page, how to use the chat, how to select a provider, how to open vault/wiki/ in Obsidian.
  • AC-D6-2: docs/DEPLOY.md exists and covers at minimum: prerequisites (Docker, Postgres, Qdrant, bge-m3, Ollama), environment variables (.env.example walkthrough), first-run steps, and how to configure a provider. The v0.1 DRAFT-tagged DEPLOY.md is promoted to a proper draft.
  • AC-D6-3: Both files pass a basic lint gate: no broken Markdown (checked by markdownlint or equivalent), no placeholder "[TODO]" strings left in the committed version.
  • AC-D6-4: Reviewed and approved by tech-writer before M4 sign-off.

docs.title: NB-6 — mmdc CI render check
docs.text:
• AC-NB6-1: mmdc is installed in CI (package.json devDependency or CI action step).
  • AC-NB6-2: CI step runs mmdc -i docs/sequences/*.mmd -o /tmp/mmd-render/ and exits 0.
  • AC-NB6-3: T-DOCS-MANUAL-003 sentinel is replaced with a real mmdc assertion (or updated to assert mmdc exit code = 0).

docs.title: NB-7 / NB-8 — Cosmetic diagram fixes (optional P3)
docs.text:
• AC-NB7-1 (optional): graph-recompute.mmd hit-path updated to show in-process snapshot source; reviewed by architect.
  • AC-NB8-1 (optional): component.mmd store label updated to store/graphStore.ts; reviewed by tech-writer.

docs.title: M4-GUX — GraphUX formalization
docs.text:
• AC-GUX-1: Regression test: engine.py produces ZERO edges for a fixture graph where the only signal between P3 and P5 is same_type. The ADR-0012 §5 fixture row "P3–P5 (type-only) → weight 1, stored" is updated to "P3–P5 → absent". T-GENG fixture expectations updated accordingly (this is the one-line functional-analyst correction flagged in ADR-0016 §6).
  • AC-GUX-2: The 200-node synthetic fixture (seed_demo_vault.py, type = i % 4) produces strictly fewer than 4900 edges under the new structural-only gate. vitest/pytest assertion: len(edges) < 500 on the 200-node fixture (actual number depends on link density; this is a sanity lower bound).
  • AC-GUX-3: GET /graph response for any graph includes kind field on every edge object ("link" or "source"). Schema validation test (Pydantic + pytest).
  • AC-GUX-4: PATCH /pages/{id}/position accepts {x: float, y: float} and updates pages.x, pages.y, and pages.pinned = true without triggering a data_version bump or FA2 recompute. Test: assert data_version unchanged after PATCH.
  • AC-GUX-5: Engine preserves pinned coords: if pages.pinned = true for a node, its x/y are not overwritten by the next FA2 recompute. Test: pin a node, trigger recompute, assert node's coords unchanged.
  • AC-GUX-6: Frontend hover-dim: when a node is hovered, all non-neighbor nodes/edges transition to a faded opacity (≤0.2). Playwright or vitest-jsdom assertion on CSS opacity/class of a non-neighbor element.
  • AC-GUX-7: Frontend accessibility: the graph container has an aria-live region that announces selected node title. Verified by vitest-axe or Playwright accessibility check (no critical a11y violations).
  • AC-GUX-8: prefers-reduced-motion: when (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) is active, the hover-dim transition duration is 0ms (no animation). vitest: mock media query, assert transition-duration = 0.
  • AC-GUX-9: The no-client-layout bundle assertion T-NCL-001..022 still passes after all GraphUX frontend changes. The drag implementation (PATCH /pages/{id}/position) must not import or call any layout function.

docs.title: 3. Anti-Scope-Creep: What Is Explicitly OUT of M4
docs.text:
The following items are deferred and MUST NOT be started in Sprint 4 without explicit orchestrator approval. Any engineer proposing this work will be blocked.

Deferred to Feature IDs Reason M5 (v0.5) F5 — 4-phase RAG retrieval, [n] citation resolution (full pipeline) Requires dedicated sprint; chat in M4 uses a stub retrieval path M5 (v0.5) F9 — Async HITL review queue. BOUNDARY EXPLICIT: the M4 Ingest Activity View (F1-INGEST-VIEW) is read-only history + a trigger button. F9 = async queue for reviewing AI-generated pages (Create / Deep-Research / Skip actions, pre-generated queries, queue management UI). These are completely different features. Any engineer adding F9 actions (approve, reject, skip, deep-research) to the Ingest view during M4 is out of scope and will be blocked. M4 delivers K8 "human curates" only via save-to-wiki (F6) + ingest trigger button; full async review queue is M5 M5 (v0.5) F10 — Deep Research loop (SearXNG multi-query) M5 work item per CLAUDE.md §8 M5 (v0.5) F12 — Multi-format ingest (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, images, AV) M5 work item M5 (v0.5) F13 — Cascade deletion M5 work item M5 (v0.5) D3-update — sequence diagrams for deep-research + cascade-delete Tied to F10/F13 M6 (v0.6) F2 — purpose.md injection as provider context M6 work item M6 (v0.6) F11 — Chrome MV3 web clipper M6 work item M6 (v0.6) F15-rest — PWA manifest, Tauri v2, full CI/CD pipeline M6 work item M6 (v0.6) K2 lint op — lint-fix loop M6 work item M6 (v0.6) Full MkDocs site Optional M6 deliverable Never Force layout on UI main thread Violates I2; escalate to architect Never Per-token Markdown/LaTeX parse in chat Violates I3; escalate to architect Never ProseMirror or any WYSIWYG editor Violates I4; escalate to architect Never Hardcoded provider or model ID Violates I6; escalate to architect

docs.title: 4. Phasing Recommendation
docs.text:
M4 is the largest sprint in the roadmap: it encompasses a full web UI shell (F1), a new chat feature set (F6/F7/F8), provider configuration UI (F17-UI), settings (F16-rest), a performance gate (G3), documentation deliverables (D5/D6), devops carry-forward (NB-6), and the GraphUX formalization (M4-GUX). It spans an estimated 2 weeks of evening development.

To deliver visibly, keep the human in the loop, and preserve the ability to verify each layer before building on it, the following 4-phase delivery order is recommended.

docs.title: Phase 0 — GraphUX formalization (already done; needs gating only)
docs.text:
Duration: 1–2 sessions (gating + test fixes, no net new code)

Work: - Functional-analyst updates TRACEABILITY.md: the ADR-0012 §5 fixture expectation for type-only pairs P3–P5 changes from "weight == 1.0, present" to "absent" (AC-GUX-1). This is the open handoff flagged in ADR-0016 §6. - QA engineer runs the existing T-GENG suite and confirms it passes with the new engine. If any test is RED due to the structural-gate change, it is fixed here before P1 begins. - Architect and tech-writer review and sign off ADR-0016 (already written; sign-off is the gate). - D2 regenerated (make er) and D4 regenerated (make openapi) to include the edges.kind column and PATCH /pages/{id}/position endpoint. - D5: seed_demo_vault.py runs on the live stack; captures updated graph screenshots that show node-size variation and structural-only edges. These satisfy the outstanding v0.3 deferred-to-live D5 condition AND provide the baseline for M4 D5-update.

Exit condition for Phase 0: T-GENG suite green with ADR-0016 changes; architect + tech-writer sign off ADR-0016; D2 + D4 zero drift; at least 1 committed graph PNG showing structural-only rendering; EC-M3-17 human checkpoint satisfied by Emanuele.

docs.title: Phase 1 — 3-panel shell + graph wired in (F1)
docs.text:
Duration: 3–4 sessions

Work: - Build the 3-panel shell (F1): resizable panels, activity panel, scenario templates. - Wire the existing sigma graph viewer (from v0.3 + M4-GUX) into the right panel. - Implement TanStack Virtual for the file tree list (I4). - Verify T-NCL-001..022 still green (no-client-layout), T-GSTORE, T-GTRANS still green. - Playwright: capture docs/screens/shell-3panel.png (first D5-update screenshot).

Exit condition for Phase 1: 3-panel shell renders in browser; graph viewer embedded and functional; no I2/I3/I4 violations; AC-F1-1..7 green.

docs.title: Phase 2 — Navigation + Operations + Provider/Settings
docs.text:
(F1-NAV, F1-INGEST-VIEW, BE-INGEST-RUNS, F17-UI, F14, F16-rest settings + .obsidian + timeout + i18n)

Duration: 3–4 sessions (expanded from 2–3 due to nav rail and ingest view additions)

Rationale: the left navigation rail (F1-NAV) gives the shell its permanent structural skeleton; every subsequent view (Graph, Pages, Settings, Ingest, and later Chat) plugs into it. Building it in Phase 2 — before chat in Phase 3 — means Phase 3 simply registers the Chat section rather than restructuring the shell. The Ingest Activity View (F1-INGEST-VIEW) is a natural companion to the provider/settings work because it surfaces the I7 cost data and gives Emanuele a way to verify the backend is working before chat exists. Provider config must also be wired before Phase 3's chat() call so Phase 3 can test against real provider switching. Visual direction for nav rail and ingest view: nashsu/llm_wiki-inspired.

Work: - Left Navigation Rail (F1-NAV): vertical rail with Pages / Graph / Ingest / Settings sections; Chat section item present but disabled/reserved; section switching without page reload; rail labels in locales/en.json + locales/it.json. - Ingest Activity View (F1-INGEST-VIEW): list from GET /ingest/runs; status badges; cost column (USD, 4dp); relative timestamps; "Run Ingest" button; polling while "running" rows exist. NOTE: read-only + trigger ONLY. No approve/reject/skip/deep-research actions — those are F9 (M5). - Backend: GET /ingest/runs endpoint (BE-INGEST-RUNS): paginated, vault_id filter, DESC order; Alembic migration if ingest_runs table does not already have all required columns. Regenerate openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate). - Provider Selector dropdown (F17-UI): reads GET /provider/config; posts to POST /provider/config. - Settings panel: context window (F14), provider (F17-UI), language (F16-i18n), panel widths. - Settings persistence via localStorage (F16-settings). - .obsidian auto-gen hardening (F16-obsidian): extend AC-K7-1 to cover vault_id change. - Multi-provider chat timeout (F16-timeout): provider_config.timeout_seconds column + Alembic migration. - i18n IT/EN (F16-i18n): locales/en.json + locales/it.json (includes F1-NAV section labels). - NB-6 (mmdc CI render check): devops-engineer wires mmdc in CI — target this phase since it is infrastructure, not UI, and should not block P3. - Playwright: capture docs/screens/shell-provider-selector.png and docs/screens/shell-ingest-view.png.

Phase 2 — confirmed phase order rationale: Phase 2 = navigation shell structure + operations visibility + provider/settings Phase 3 = chat (plugs into the nav rail's Chat section slot; provider config already wired) This order is correct and unchanged from the original plan; the two new items are additive.

Exit condition for Phase 2: Nav rail renders with all 4 sections switchable; Ingest view shows run history and Run Ingest button fires correctly; GET /ingest/runs endpoint live and in openapi.json; provider selector visible and functional; settings persist across reload; .obsidian generated correctly; i18n switches language; mmdc CI green; AC-F1-NAV-1..8, AC-F1-IV-1..8, AC-BE-IR-1..5, AC-F17-UI-1..6, AC-F14-1..5, AC-F16-i18n-1..2, AC-F16-settings-1..2, AC-F16-obsidian-1, AC-F16-timeout-1 green.

docs.title: Phase 3 — Chat + streaming + performance gate (F6, F7, F8, G3)
docs.text:
Duration: 3–4 sessions

Rationale: chat is the most user-visible feature. With F1 (shell) and F17-UI (provider config) already working, the chat feature can be wired end-to-end immediately. G3 is gated at the end of this phase (not deferred-to-live this sprint).

Work: - Conversations and messages tables: Alembic migration; REST endpoints (POST /chat, GET /conversations, POST /conversations, DELETE /conversations/{id}). - Chat streaming: SSE or WebSocket; Zustand buffer appends raw string per token; parse fires on stream END only (I3, AC-G3-2). - <think> display (F7): collapsible reasoning section. - LaTeX-to-Unicode (F8): fires on stream END. - GFM rendering (F16-gfm): marked or remark; fires on stream END. - Cited-refs (F6): [n] markers; citation stored with message. - Regenerate + save-to-wiki (F6). - Message list virtualization (F6, TanStack Virtual). - G3 Playwright test (AC-G3-1): no long task >50ms during stream. This test is MANDATORY in P3; it must not be deferred-to-live. If the live environment is unavailable, the orchestrator must grant an explicit waiver (not automatic). - D5-update: capture shell-chat-streaming.png. - NB-7 and NB-8 (cosmetic diagram fixes): tech-writer can fold these into P3 documentation pass as optional polish.

Exit condition for Phase 3: Chat streams end-to-end; G3 Playwright test green; F7/F8 pass vitest; message list virtualizes; D5 screenshots committed; AC-F6-1..6, AC-F7-1..4, AC-F8-1..4, AC-G3-1..4, AC-D5-upd-1..3 green.

docs.title: Docs thread (threaded throughout all phases)
docs.text:
• D6 USER.md and DEPLOY.md: tech-writer drafts in parallel with P1–P3; must be complete and reviewed (AC-D6-1..4) before the M4 DoD gate.
  • D1 component.mmd: updated by tech-writer to show 3-panel shell, chat components, and Provider Selector as new components. Target: end of Phase 1 or Phase 2.
  • D2 (make er): regenerated whenever a schema migration lands (P2 timeout migration, P3 conversations/messages migration). Zero-drift is a hard gate.
  • D4 (make openapi): regenerated whenever new endpoints are added. Zero-drift is a hard gate.
  • D7 ADRs: any architectural decision made during M4 that is not already covered by an existing ADR requires a new ADR before PM sign-off. Candidates: chat WebSocket vs. SSE transport choice, conversations table schema, GFM library selection.

docs.title: 5. DoD Gate Checklist for M4
docs.text:
The following checklist is the definitive Definition of Done for M4. All items must be MET before the PM signs off on M4 and the orchestrator may advance to Sprint 5.

# Gate Agent responsible Required evidence 1 All ACs in §2 green (automated where possible, MANUAL where not) QA gate (qa-test-engineer) pytest + vitest + Playwright all green; ruff + black + mypy + ESLint + Prettier clean 2 G3 Playwright streaming test green (no deferred-to-live unless explicit orchestrator waiver) QA gate T-E2E-G3-001 PASS in CI or on live machine with waiver on record 3 G2 and G4 tests from v0.3 either green (live run) or waiver re-issued by orchestrator QA gate T-E2E-G2-001/002 and T-E2E-G4-001/002 status on record 4 Architect gate: reviews 3-panel shell (I3/I4), chat streaming (I3), Provider Selector (I6), PATCH /position (I2-compatible), ADR-0016 formalization solution-architect docs/sprints/v0.4-architect-review.md: APPROVED 5 Tech-writer gate: D1 updated (new components), D2 zero drift (make er), D4 zero drift (make openapi), D5 screenshots committed (at least 3 PNGs), D6 USER.md + DEPLOY.md reviewed, NB-7/NB-8 cosmetic fixes applied (if chosen) tech-writer DOCS_STATUS.md or equivalent: DOCS GATE UP-TO-DATE 6 NB-6 mmdc CI render check green (no deferred-to-live) devops-engineer CI pipeline shows mmdc step exit 0; T-DOCS-MANUAL-003 replaced 7 ADR-0016 signed off by architect and tech-writer solution-architect + tech-writer docs/adr/0016-obsidian-graph-rendering.md status updated to "Accepted + Reviewed" 8 TRACEABILITY.md updated: M4 sprint coverage section added; ADR-0012 P3–P5 fixture expectation corrected per ADR-0016 §6 functional-analyst (stub) + QA TRACEABILITY.md §Sprint 4 section present with all AC IDs and test IDs 9 BACKLOG.md updated: all M4 items marked done or done-pending-live-demo; M4-GUX sub-items added as formal entries; no M4 item left in "in-progress" product-manager BACKLOG.md §Sprint 4 updated 10 I5 regression: vault/wiki/.obsidian/app.json still valid; test_obsidian_check.py 15/15 green QA gate T-OBS-001..015 green 11 Human checkpoint (EC-M4-HCP): Emanuele confirms in browser: 3-panel shell with nav rail loads (Pages/Graph/Ingest/Settings sections switchable); Ingest view shows run list and Run Ingest button works (run appears in list); chat sends a message and streams a response; provider selector changes the active provider; graph viewer shows structural-only rendering with node-size variation Emanuele (human) Recorded in M4 closure document

M4 verdict is either MET (all 11 gates satisfied) or NOT MET with a precise gap list. The PM will deliver the verdict to the orchestrator only when all 11 gates are resolved.

docs.title: 6. Open Handoff: TRACEABILITY Update Required (ADR-0016 §6)
docs.text:
ADR-0016 §6 explicitly flags a mandatory TRACEABILITY correction that must be performed by the functional-analyst before Phase 0 can be signed off.

The correction: ADR-0012 §5 documents a worked fixture with a type-only pair P3–P5 (entity pages sharing no links and no shared source). Under the old inclusion rule this pair produced weight == 1.0 and was stored as an edge. Under ADR-0016 §1's structural gate this pair produces NO edge (the +1 type term no longer materializes an edge without a structural tie).

Required TRACEABILITY change: in the Sprint 3 coverage section for AC-F4-1 (T-GENG-001..012), the expected value for the P3–P5 pair must change from "weight == 1.0, present" to "absent (no structural tie)". The lower-bound assertions for P1–P2 (≥11), P1–P4 (≥8), and P2–P4 (≥5) are UNAFFECTED (those pairs are structural via direct link or shared source and their stored weights are identical).

If the T-GENG test suite itself hardcodes the old expectation, the QA engineer must update the test fixture to match ADR-0016 before Phase 0 sign-off. This is a one-line correction, not a re-design. Failing to fix it would leave a GREEN test that asserts the wrong behavior (an invariant violation: the type-clique hairball would be silently re-introduced on the next engine.py edit).

docs.title: 7. Velocity Note
docs.text:
Sprint 3 was ON SCOPE (see v0.3-pm-signoff.md §5). Sprint 4 is deliberately LARGE: it is the "Usable and fluid" milestone, which by definition requires the first end-to-end user-facing UI. The 4-phase delivery plan in §4 is designed to make this manageable:

  • Phase 0 is close to zero new code (gating only) and must complete quickly.
  • Each phase has a clear exit condition that delivers something human-visible.
  • G3 streaming perf is called out explicitly as non-deferrable, preventing the pattern of "all Playwright tests deferred to live" that accumulated in v0.3.

Scope amendment impact on velocity: Phase 2 now carries 2 additional items (F1-NAV, F1-INGEST-VIEW) plus a backend endpoint (BE-INGEST-RUNS). Phase 2 duration estimate increases from 2–3 sessions to 3–4 sessions. Total sprint remains within the 2-week M4 envelope. Phase 3 (chat) duration is unchanged. If during execution Phase 2 runs long, the FIRST escalation is to the orchestrator — not to silently defer ACs.

If during execution any phase proves larger than estimated, the FIRST escalation action is to bring this to the orchestrator — not to silently defer ACs or reduce the scope of a phase without PM approval.

Sprint 5 boundary remains intact: F5 retrieval, F9 review queue, F10 deep research, F12 multi-format, F13 cascade delete are all M5 work. No M5 item may be started during M4. The Ingest Activity View (F1-INGEST-VIEW) is read-only + trigger and does NOT encroach on F9.

docs.title: 8. M4-EXT — Stakeholder Extension (added before EC-M4-HCP closes)
docs.text:
Logged: 2026-06-28 | Requested by: Emanuele (stakeholder, explicit) Status: IN SCOPE for M4 — added as M4-EXT before the human checkpoint closes. These two features extend M4 before the EC-M4-HCP gate is satisfied; they are not Sprint 5 work. Sprint 5 (M5) scope is unchanged.

Emanuele requested two additional features after the M4 sign-off but before the human checkpoint (EC-M4-HCP) has been confirmed. Both are extensions of existing M4 scope: Feature U extends the Ingest section (F1-INGEST-VIEW), Feature S extends the Settings section (F16-rest). Neither touches F9 (async HITL review queue), F10 (deep research), or F12 (multi-format ingest) — those remain M5.

docs.title: M4-EXT scope log — feature IDs added
docs.text:
New locked feature IDs: F1-UPLOAD (Feature U) and F1-SCHED (Feature S).

Updated total locked feature IDs for M4: F1, F1-NAV, F1-INGEST-VIEW, F1-UPLOAD, F1-SCHED, F6, F7, F8, F14, F17-UI, F16-rest, G3, D5-update, D6, NB-6, NB-7, NB-8, M4-GUX-1..8, BE-INGEST-RUNS

Total: 27 work items (previously 25; +2 from M4-EXT).

docs.title: M4-EXT: Feature U — F1-UPLOAD — Document upload from the UI
docs.text:
Scope summary: A drag-and-drop / file-picker in the Ingest section that uploads a document from the user's browser into vault/raw/sources/ and triggers ingest. The upload path reuses the existing ingest pipeline (watcher or POST /ingest/trigger). This sprint accepts TEXT (.txt) and MARKDOWN (.md) files only.

F12 boundary — explicit and non-negotiable: Feature U accepts .txt and .md files ONLY in v0.4. Multi-format parsing (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, images, AV) is F12 and remains M5.
