The file-type gate must be enforced both client-side (accept attribute) AND server-side (Content-Type + extension validation); any file that is not text/plain or text/markdown is rejected with HTTP 415 and a clear error message. This boundary is stated here so engineers do not conflate F12 with Feature U. The upload implementation MUST NOT add any dependency on pypdf, unstructured, python-docx, python-pptx, openpyxl, or any AV library — those belong to F12/M5.

Backend endpoint:

POST /ingest/upload — multipart/form-data - Request fields: file (binary, required), vault_id (uuid string, optional — defaults to current active vault). - The backend sanitizes the filename: strips path components, normalizes unicode, replaces characters outside [a-zA-Z0-9._-] with _, truncates to 200 chars. Path traversal is explicitly blocked (reject any filename containing /, \, ..). The sanitized file is written to vault/raw/sources/<sanitized-name>. - After writing the file, the backend triggers ingest via the existing ingest path (POST /ingest/trigger with the new file path, or by directly invoking the orchestrator). - Response on success: HTTP 202 with {file_path: "vault/raw/sources/<sanitized-name>", ingest_run_id: <uuid>}. - On unsupported file type: HTTP 415 with {detail: "Unsupported file type. Only .txt and .md files are accepted in this version. PDF/DOCX/PPTX support is F12 (M5)."}. - On path-traversal attempt: HTTP 400 with {detail: "Invalid filename."}. - On file too large (configurable limit, default 10 MB): HTTP 413. - The endpoint appears in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate, same make openapi run).

Acceptance criteria — F1-UPLOAD:

  • AC-U-1: The Ingest section contains a drag-and-drop zone and/or a file-picker button labeled (i18n key) "Upload document". Only .txt and .md files are selectable/droppable; other file types show a client-side validation error before any network request is made.
  • AC-U-2: On valid file selection the frontend calls POST /ingest/upload (multipart). On HTTP 202 response: a success toast shows the sanitized filename and ingest_run_id; the ingest runs list (AC-F1-IV-1) refreshes to show the new run.
  • AC-U-3: POST /ingest/upload writes the file to vault/raw/sources/ with the sanitized filename and triggers ingest. The ingest run appears in GET /ingest/runs within 10 seconds.
  • AC-U-4 (path-traversal safety): POST /ingest/upload with a filename containing /, \, or .. returns HTTP 400 "Invalid filename". No file is written. Unit test covers: ../../etc/passwd, ../secrets, foo/bar.md, foo\bar.md.
  • AC-U-5 (file type gate): POST /ingest/upload with a .pdf, .docx, .xlsx, .png, or any non-text/non-markdown MIME type returns HTTP 415 with the exact error message referencing F12/M5. The client-side check (accept attribute) is a UX guard only; the server-side check is authoritative. Unit test: submit .pdf → assert HTTP 415 body.
  • AC-U-6 (size limit): POST /ingest/upload with a file larger than the configured limit (default 10 MB, env var UPLOAD_MAX_BYTES) returns HTTP 413. Unit test covers this.
  • AC-U-7 (I1 incremental): uploading the same file twice (identical content + name) does NOT create a duplicate Postgres row or Qdrant vector (the watcher/ingest path already enforces I1 via mtime/hash; verify this still applies on the upload path).
  • AC-U-8 (I5 vault integrity): the uploaded file is written ONLY to vault/raw/sources/; it is NEVER written directly to vault/wiki/. The wiki entry is produced solely by the ingest pipeline, preserving the K1 3-layer invariant.
  • AC-U-9: POST /ingest/upload is documented in openapi.json (D4 updated via make openapi). Pydantic request/response models validated by pytest schema test.
  • AC-U-10: vitest test: mock POST /ingest/upload → HTTP 202 → assert success toast rendered and ingest list refresh triggered.
  • AC-U-11: Playwright E2E test: upload a valid .md fixture via the file-picker; assert POST /ingest/upload fired (route intercept); assert the ingest runs list refreshes.

docs.title: M4-EXT: Feature S — F1-SCHED — Scheduled folder import (in Settings)
docs.text:
Scope summary: A "Automatic import" sub-section in the Settings panel where the user configures a source folder and a frequency. The backend periodically scans that folder and imports new documents into vault/raw/sources/ (then the watcher indexes them, satisfying I1). Configuration is persisted in Postgres. A "Run now" action triggers an immediate scan.

Critical constraint — container-mounted paths: The backend runs inside a Docker container. It cannot see arbitrary host filesystem paths. The source folder configured by the user MUST be a path that is mounted as a Docker volume. The acceptance criteria, UI hint text, and documentation must state this explicitly. The Settings UI must show a helper note: "This folder must be a path mounted into the Synapse container (e.g. /data/import). Arbitrary host paths are not accessible. See DEPLOY.md for volume configuration." The backend must validate that source_dir is an absolute path and that the directory exists and is readable from inside the container; if not, the save is rejected with HTTP 422 and a message explaining the container constraint.

Bounded scheduler — I7 compliance: The scheduler is subject to I7 (bounded loops). Each scan run has a max_files_per_run cap (configurable, default 50) and a scan_timeout_seconds cap (configurable, default 300 s). total_cost_usd is NOT applicable to the scan itself (no inference in folder scanning); however, each ingest triggered by the scan DOES log cost through the existing ingest pipeline. The scheduler MUST NOT trigger overlapping scan runs (if a scan is already running, skip and log a warning).

Backend endpoints:

GET /import-schedule — returns the current scheduled import configuration for the active vault. Response fields: enabled (bool), source_dir (string or null), frequency (enum: hourly / daily / weekly / manual), interval_minutes (int — derived from frequency or custom), last_run_at (timestamptz or null), last_status (enum: ok / error / never_run or null), last_error_message (text or null), max_files_per_run (int), scan_timeout_seconds (int). HTTP 200 or HTTP 404 if no schedule configured for the vault (returns a default-empty response object).

PUT /import-schedule — creates or replaces the import schedule for the active vault. Request body: {enabled: bool, source_dir: string, frequency: "hourly"|"daily"|"weekly"|"manual", interval_minutes: int|null, max_files_per_run: int|null, scan_timeout_seconds: int|null}. Validation: source_dir must be absolute, must exist and be readable inside the container (checked at save time); if not, HTTP 422 with {detail: "source_dir is not accessible from the container. Mount the folder as a Docker volume and use its container-side path."}. Returns HTTP 200 with the persisted schedule object.

POST /import-schedule/run-now — triggers an immediate scan run (outside the scheduler cadence). Returns HTTP 202 with {scan_run_id: <uuid>}. If a scan is already in progress, returns HTTP 409 with {detail: "A scan is already running."}.

All 3 endpoints appear in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate applies on next make openapi).

Persistence — new import_schedules table (Alembic migration): Columns: id (uuid pk), vault_id (uuid FK → vault, unique per vault), enabled (bool, default false), source_dir (text), frequency (text enum), interval_minutes (int), last_run_at (timestamptz nullable), last_status (text nullable), last_error_message (text nullable), max_files_per_run (int, default 50), scan_timeout_seconds (int, default 300), created_at (timestamptz), updated_at (timestamptz).

Acceptance criteria — F1-SCHED:

  • AC-S-1: The Settings panel contains an "Automatic import" sub-section. It exposes: an enabled toggle, a source folder text input, a frequency selector (Hourly / Daily / Weekly / Manual), and a "Save" button. A "Run now" button triggers an immediate scan regardless of the schedule. A "Last run" status line shows last_run_at (relative timestamp) and last_status (ok / error / never run).
  • AC-S-2 (container path constraint — UI): The source folder input shows a persistent helper text: "Must be a container-mounted path (e.g. /data/import). See DEPLOY.md." This text is an i18n key and appears in both locales/en.json and locales/it.json.
  • AC-S-3 (container path constraint — backend): PUT /import-schedule validates that source_dir is an absolute path that exists and is readable from within the container. If the path is inaccessible, HTTP 422 is returned with a message that explicitly references the Docker volume mount requirement. No schedule is saved for an inaccessible path. Unit test: submit a path that does not exist on the filesystem → assert HTTP 422 with container constraint message.
  • AC-S-4: GET /import-schedule returns the persisted configuration for the active vault. vitest test: mock response with all fields → assert Settings sub-section renders correctly with enabled state, folder path, frequency, last-run info.
  • AC-S-5 (I7 bounded scan): each scheduled or manual scan run respects max_files_per_run (default 50) and scan_timeout_seconds (default 300). If the scan runs longer than the timeout, it is aborted, last_status is set to "error", and the error is logged. The scheduler does NOT start a new scan if one is already running (skip + warn). Unit test with mock filesystem: assert scan stops at max_files_per_run files even when more are present.
  • AC-S-6 (I1 incremental): the scan imports only NEW files (files not already in the ingest_runs or pages table for the vault). Files already indexed are skipped. Unit test: pre-seed 3 already-indexed files + 2 new; run scan; assert only 2 new files are ingested.
  • AC-S-7 (I5 vault integrity): the scan copies files into vault/raw/sources/ and triggers ingest via the existing ingest path. Files from source_dir are NEVER written directly to vault/wiki/. Same K1 3-layer rule as AC-U-8.
  • AC-S-8: POST /import-schedule/run-now returns HTTP 202 with a scan_run_id. If a scan is already running, it returns HTTP 409. The "Run now" button in the UI triggers this endpoint and shows a toast on 202 or an error on 409.
  • AC-S-9: last_run_at and last_status are updated in the import_schedules table at the end of each scan run (both scheduled and manual). The Settings UI refreshes these values after a "Run now" call (polls or refetches GET /import-schedule after the 202).
  • AC-S-10: The import_schedules Alembic migration runs cleanly on a fresh DB. D2 (schema.mmd) is regenerated via make er to include the new table. D4 (openapi.json) is regenerated via make openapi to include the 3 new endpoints.
  • AC-S-11: pytest integration tests: (a) PUT /import-schedule with a valid mounted path → HTTP 200 → GET /import-schedule returns same data; (b) POST /import-schedule/run-now → HTTP 202; (c) second POST /import-schedule/run-now while first still running → HTTP 409.
  • AC-S-12: Playwright E2E test: navigate to Settings → Automatic import; fill source folder
  • frequency; click Save; assert PUT /import-schedule fired (route intercept); click "Run now"; assert POST /import-schedule/run-now fired; assert last-run status updates.

docs.title: M4-EXT: Out-of-scope boundaries (explicit)
docs.text:
The following are OUT of scope for both Feature U and Feature S, and must not be started during M4 under any circumstance:

Out of scope Deferred to Rationale Multi-format parsing: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, images, AV F12 / M5 Feature U accepts .txt/.md only in v0.4. F12 is a separate M5 feature with dedicated parser dependencies (pypdf, unstructured, etc.). Any engineer adding multi-format parsing to the upload path during M4 is out of scope and will be blocked. F9 — Async HITL review queue (approve/reject/skip actions, pre-generated queries) M5 Reaffirmed: NOT part of Ingest section work. The upload trigger and the scheduled import trigger both feed the existing ingest pipeline; they do not create a review queue. F10 — Deep Research loop (SearXNG multi-query) M5 Not introduced by folder scanning. SearXNG queries originate only from the F10 deep-research path. Arbitrary host-path access (non-mounted paths) Never (container constraint) The backend container cannot access host paths that are not mounted as Docker volumes. No implementation can bypass this; it is an infrastructure reality, not a TODO.

docs.title: M4-EXT: Impact on M4 exit criteria and EC-M4-HCP
docs.text:
The EC-M4-HCP human checkpoint (Gate 11 in §5) now additionally requires Emanuele to confirm the following two browser conditions before the checkpoint can be closed:

  • EC-M4-HCP-U: Emanuele can upload a .md or .txt file from the browser via the Ingest section drag-and-drop or file-picker. The file appears in vault/raw/sources/ and a new ingest run is visible in the runs list within 10 seconds.
  • EC-M4-HCP-S: Emanuele can open Settings → Automatic import, configure a source folder (must be a mounted container path) and a frequency, save the configuration, and trigger a "Run now" scan. The last-run status updates after the scan completes.

Gate 11 wording in §5 is hereby amended: the existing 6 browser conditions are unchanged; EC-M4-HCP-U and EC-M4-HCP-S are added as conditions 7 and 8. All 8 conditions must be satisfied before M4 is fully closed and Sprint 5 is unblocked.

DoD additions for M4-EXT: - D4 (openapi.json) must be regenerated to include POST /ingest/upload, GET /import-schedule, PUT /import-schedule, POST /import-schedule/run-now. Zero-drift gate applies. - D2 (schema.mmd) must be regenerated to include the import_schedules table. Zero-drift gate. - docs/DEPLOY.md must document how to configure a Docker volume mount for the scheduled import source folder (this is the missing content that makes AC-S-2/AC-S-3 actionable). This is already a conditional requirement under AC-D6-2; the volume-mount documentation is now also required for AC-S-2.

PM sign-off: scope-locked 2026-06-28 | product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Scope amendment: Phase 2 additions logged 2026-06-28 — Left Navigation Rail (F1-NAV) and Ingest Activity View (F1-INGEST-VIEW) added at stakeholder Emanuele's explicit request; visual direction: nashsu/llm_wiki-inspired. Total locked feature IDs updated accordingly. M4-EXT amendment: Feature U (F1-UPLOAD) and Feature S (F1-SCHED) added 2026-06-28 at stakeholder Emanuele's explicit request; added before EC-M4-HCP closes; EC-M4-HCP extended to 8 conditions. F12 boundary explicit. I1/I5/I7 invariant notes recorded. Handoff: acceptance criteria (§2 + §8) → functional-analyst for user story elaboration Handoff: exit criteria verdict (§5 + §8 EC-M4-HCP amendments) → orchestrator at M4 sign-off Next action: Phase 0 begins; human checkpoint EC-M3-17 must be satisfied before any Phase 1 code is written.

docs.title: Sprint 4 — v0.4 / M4 — PM Exit-Criteria Sign-Off
docs.text: Author: product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Date: 2026-06-28 Milestone: M4 "Usable & fluid" Branch: sprint/v0.4 Verdict: M4 MET — WITH DOCUMENTED DEFERRALS (M5) AND ONE OPEN GAP (D6) Next action: EC-M4-HCP human checkpoint by Emanuele before Sprint 5 begins.

docs.title: 1. Per-Criterion Assessment — DoD Gate Checklist (§5)
docs.text: Each gate maps to docs/sprints/v0.4-pm-scope.md §5.

docs.title: Gate 1 — All ACs in §2 green
docs.text:
Verdict: MET-WITH-DEFERRALS

Evidence: - QA gate confirmed GO after in-sprint fixes: NavTree virtualization fix (F1/AC-F1-2), Chat disabled in Phase 2 (AC-F1-NAV-1 respected), token label fix (F14/AC-F14-1). - ruff + black + mypy + ESLint + Prettier reported clean. - Deferred ACs (not gaps — PM-approved deferrals to M5): - AC-F6-3: [n] citation markers — messages.citations not populated. Button present in UI but citation data is empty. This is a known M5 item (F5 full pipeline). - AC-F6-5: save-to-wiki — button disabled in UI ("coming in M5"). No ingest triggered. - AC-F7 partial: chat-think-block D5 screenshot deferred (qwen2.5:3b emits no ). - F17 (CLI): CliAgentProvider.chat() raises honest NotImplementedError. AC-F17-2 intent preserved: explicit not-yet signal, not a silent failure.

docs.title: Gate 2 — G3 Playwright streaming test green
docs.text:
Verdict: MET

Evidence: T-E2E-G3-001 PASS. Mandatory gate confirmed GO by QA. No deferred-to-live waiver needed. 0 main-thread long tasks during live stream confirmed. Parse-once at stream end verified (I3). Zustand buffer appends raw string per token; selector fires exactly once on done event (AC-G3-2, AC-G3-3 green). Existing messages do not re-render on token arrival (AC-G3-4 green).

docs.title: Gate 3 — G2 and G4 tests from v0.3 status
docs.text:
Verdict: MET (waiver re-issued from M3)

Evidence: G2 static (T-NCL-001..022) confirmed green post all GraphUX changes. G4 fps test (T-E2E-G4-001/002) remains DEFERRED-TO-LIVE — waiver inherited from M3 (live TrueNAS environment required). No regression introduced. QA confirmed no T-NCL violation.

docs.title: Gate 4 — Architect gate
docs.text:
Verdict: MET

Evidence: docs/adr/0016-obsidian-graph-rendering.md accepted; docs/adr/0017-three-panel-shell.md, 0018-navrail-ingest-provider.md, 0019-chat-streaming.md all produced and approved. Architect APPROVED across all phases: - M4-GUX: ADR-0016 formalized + signed off. - Phase 1 (F1 shell): I3/I4 reviewed, APPROVED. - Phase 2 (nav-rail, ingest view, provider selector, settings): APPROVED after retiring redundant MainTabs component. - Phase 3 (chat streaming): APPROVED-WITH-NITS. Nits on record: - P1: capability heuristic (non-blocking nit) - P2: optimistic think reconstruction on reconnect (non-blocking nit) - P3: token_budget stream-stop test (non-blocking nit) These are carried to M5 hardening. None are blocking for M4 sign-off. - PATCH /pages/{id}/position: I2-compatible confirmed, T-NCL guard retained.

docs.title: Gate 5 — Tech-writer gate (D-artifacts)
docs.text:
Verdict: MET-WITH-GAP (D6 partial)

Evidence: - D1: component.mmd updated for 3-panel shell, chat components, Provider Selector. PASS. - D2: schema.mmd regenerated via make er after migrations 0004 (edges.kind), 0005 (pages.pinned), 0006 (ingest_runs view fields), 0007 (conversations/messages). Zero drift. - D4: openapi.json regenerated after adding GET /ingest/runs, PATCH /pages/{id}/position, POST /chat/stream, GET /conversations endpoints. Zero drift. - D5: 10 PNGs committed in docs/screens/ — covers: graph-obsidian.png, graph-obsidian-node-selected.png, shell-3panel.png, shell-3panel-selected.png, ingest-section.png, settings-section.png, provider-selector-open.png, navrail-graph-active.png, chat-streaming.png, chat-conversation.png. AC-D5-upd-1..2 green. chat-think-block screenshot deferred (no from qwen2.5:3b during the capture run — acceptable; block is tested by vitest). - D6: PARTIAL GAP. - docs/USER.md: DOES NOT EXIST. AC-D6-1 NOT MET. - docs/DEPLOY.md: EXISTS but is v0.1-only draft. No v0.4 content (chat, provider selector, 3-panel UI). AC-D6-2 PARTIAL — prerequisites section exists but is not a v0.4 draft. - AC-D6-3 and AC-D6-4 cannot be assessed for USER.md (file absent). - ADRs 0017/0018/0019 reviewed and consistent (tech-writer approved). PASS. - NB-7 (cosmetic graph-recompute.mmd): status: cosmetic, optional. Not shipped in M4. Carried. - NB-8 (cosmetic component.mmd store label): status: cosmetic, optional. Not shipped in M4. Carried.

D6 gap assessment: docs/USER.md is absent. This is a genuine gap against AC-D6-1. docs/DEPLOY.md was not promoted to a v0.4 draft. This is a gap against AC-D6-2. However, no feature code is missing because of this; the gap is documentation-only. The verdict below treats D6 as a CONDITIONAL PASS: M4 MET is declared with the explicit condition that USER.md + DEPLOY.md v0.4 draft are delivered by tech-writer before the EC-M4-HCP human checkpoint is confirmed to the orchestrator. The human checkpoint (Gate 11) already requires Emanuele to confirm features in browser — D6 delivery can be parallelized with that.

docs.title: Gate 6 — NB-6 mmdc CI render check
docs.text:
Verdict: MET

Evidence: .github/workflows/ci.yml now includes: - npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli step - mmdc loop over all docs/architecture/.mmd, docs/er/.mmd, docs/sequences/*.mmd - exits non-zero on parse error T-DOCS-MANUAL-003 sentinel replaced by real mmdc assertion. AC-NB6-1..3 green. Note: CI branch trigger list in ci.yml still shows only sprint/v0.1, sprint/v0.2, main. This is a devops nit — the mmdc logic is wired; the branch filter needs expanding to sprint/v0.3, sprint/v0.4 before next push. Carried as NB-9 (non-blocking for M4 verdict).

docs.title: Gate 7 — ADR-0016 signed off by architect and tech-writer
docs.text:
Verdict: MET

Evidence: docs/adr/0016-obsidian-graph-rendering.md status updated to Accepted + Reviewed. All M4-GUX sub-items gated through this ADR.

docs.title: Gate 8 — TRACEABILITY.md updated: Sprint 4 coverage + ADR-0012 P3–P5 fixture correction
docs.text:
Verdict: MET

Evidence: TRACEABILITY.md Sprint 4 section present with all M4 AC IDs and test IDs. The ADR-0012 §5 P3–P5 fixture expectation corrected from "weight == 1.0, present" to "absent (no structural tie)" per ADR-0016 §6 requirement. The T-GENG suite updated to match the new structural-only gate. Green.

docs.title: Gate 9 — BACKLOG.md updated
docs.text:
Verdict: MET (this update — see §3 below)

All M4 items are now marked done or done-with-deferral. Deferred items moved to M5. Carried nits logged. BACKLOG.md Sprint 4 section updated by this PM sign-off.

docs.title: Gate 10 — I5 regression: Obsidian compatibility
docs.text:
Verdict: MET

Evidence: test_obsidian_check.py 15/15 green (T-OBS-001..015). vault/wiki/.obsidian/app.json generated correctly including vault_id change handling (F16-obsidian AC met). No wikilink syntax regressions. I5 intact.

docs.title: Gate 11 — Human checkpoint (EC-M4-HCP)
docs.text:
Verdict: PENDING — conditions below

Emanuele must confirm the following in browser before Sprint 5 may begin:

Condition What to verify EC-M4-HCP-1 3-panel shell loads in browser with nav rail showing Pages / Graph / Ingest / Settings icons. All 4 sections are switchable without page reload. EC-M4-HCP-2 Ingest section shows list of recent ingest runs with status badge, cost (4dp), and relative timestamp. Run Ingest button is present. EC-M4-HCP-3 Chat section opens (nav rail Chat icon active); a message can be sent; the response streams token by token with no UI freeze; at least one assistant message appears in the message list. EC-M4-HCP-4 Provider selector dropdown is accessible in Settings; changing the provider persists across page refresh. EC-M4-HCP-5 Graph section shows the sigma viewer with structural-only edges, node-size variation proportional to connections, and hover-dim behavior. EC-M4-HCP-6 docs/USER.md exists and covers the core user journey (prerequisite condition — tech-writer must deliver before this checkpoint is closed).

Sprint 5 is BLOCKED until Emanuele confirms all 6 conditions above and records confirmation in this document (or a designated closure note).

docs.title: 2. Overall Verdict
docs.text:
M4 MET — with documented M5 deferrals and one conditional documentation requirement.

M4 is declared MET on the following basis: - All feature code for F1, F1-NAV, F1-INGEST-VIEW, F6, F7, F8, F14, F17-UI, F16-rest, G3, M4-GUX (1-8), and BE-INGEST-RUNS is shipped and gate-tested. - The three G3 performance invariants (I3) are verified by automated tests — this sprint's primary non-negotiable gate is GREEN. - All 4 sign-offs required by CLAUDE.md DoD are recorded: QA GO, Architect APPROVED, Tech-Writer PASS (with D6 conditional), PM sign-off (this document). - M5 deferrals are explicitly PM-approved, documented, and do not represent unfinished in-scope work: - F5 4-phase retrieval + [n] citation population → M5 - messages.citations population → M5 (F5 dependency) - save-to-wiki button → M5 (button disabled, not removed) - CliAgentProvider.chat() → M5 (honest NotImplementedError, not silent failure) - Selected-page context injection into chat → M5 - chat-think-block D5 screenshot → M5 (no model available during capture) - D6 is the one genuine gap: USER.md absent, DEPLOY.md not promoted. This is a documentation-only gap with no feature regression. M4 MET is declared conditional on tech-writer delivering docs/USER.md and promoting docs/DEPLOY.md before EC-M4-HCP is confirmed to the orchestrator.

Milestone M4 "Usable & fluid" is closed upon Emanuele's EC-M4-HCP confirmation.

docs.title: 3. BACKLOG.md Updates (recorded here; applied in BACKLOG.md directly)
docs.text: See BACKLOG.md Sprint 4 section — all items updated to done / done-with-deferral / backlog-M5 status. See also Sprint 5 section for newly moved items.

docs.title: 4. Velocity Note
docs.text:
Sprint 4 was LARGE by design (see v0.4-pm-scope.md §7). Scope was EXECUTED within the 2-week envelope with the 4-phase delivery plan intact. The 2 Phase 2 additions (F1-NAV, F1-INGEST-VIEW) were absorbed without phase slippage. G3 was not deferred-to-live — the pattern of accumulated live-run deferrals from v0.3 was broken. One documentation gap (D6) accumulated; this is the primary velocity lesson for Sprint 5: tech-writer work on USER.md and DEPLOY.md must be started at Sprint 5 kickoff and not left to the final phase.

Sprint 5 scope estimate: F5 (retrieval + citations) is the most complex item; F9 (review queue), F10 (deep research), F12 (multi-format), F13 (cascade delete) are individually scoped but collectively large. Recommend the orchestrator consider a 3-phase plan for M5 as M4 was for M4. Velocity note: M4 proved that 4 features + 2 additions + 1 backend endpoint + 8 GUX sub-items can be delivered in 2 weeks with disciplined phasing. M5 carries 5 major features — phasing is mandatory.

docs.text:
Item Type Notes D6 — USER.md Documentation gap Must be delivered before EC-M4-HCP is closed D6 — DEPLOY.md v0.4 Documentation gap Must be promoted before EC-M4-HCP is closed NB-7 — graph-recompute.mmd cosmetic Optional polish Tech-writer, non-blocking NB-8 — component.mmd store label Optional polish Tech-writer, non-blocking NB-9 — CI branch filter (v0.3/v0.4 missing) Devops nit Add sprint/v0.3, sprint/v0.4 to ci.yml trigger list Architect P1 nit — capability heuristic Backend nit Non-blocking; M5 hardening Architect P2 nit — optimistic think reconstruction Frontend nit Non-blocking; M5 Architect P3 nit — token_budget stream-stop test QA nit Non-blocking; M5 G4 fps Playwright test Live-run deferred Inherited from M3; requires TrueNAS live run G2 runtime Playwright test Live-run deferred Inherited from M3; T-E2E-G2-001/002 status chat-think-block D5 screenshot Screenshot qwen2.5:3b emits no ; needs -capable model

docs.title: v0.5 Architect Review — M5 Phase 1 (F5 retrieval + [n] citations)
docs.text: Reviewer: solution-architect · Date: 2026-06-29 · Branch: sprint/v0.5 Scope: pre-phase-gate sign-off of the Phase-1 implementation against ADR-0022 (frozen contract) and the 9 invariants. Test execution is owned by qa-test-engineer (parallel) and was deliberately NOT run here. Reviewed: backend/app/rag/retrieval.py, backend/app/chat/stream.py, backend/app/main.py (GET /search, POST /ingest/from-text), backend/app/ingest/provider/cli.py, frontend/src/components/chat/decorateCitations.ts, MarkdownView.tsx, backend/app/qdrant_client.py, app/models.py, app/migrations/versions/, docs/api/openapi.json.

docs.title: Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS
docs.text:
The implementation is a faithful, invariant-clean realisation of ADR-0022. All ten Do-NOT rejection triggers HOLD. The frozen data structures (PageRef / Citation / RetrievalContext) match §2.3 exactly. The one accepted limitation (§4) is honestly preserved, not silently worsened. No invariant is traded for convenience.

The conditions are documentation-only (one ADR code-reference amendment + one inline provenance note), not code changes. They do not block the phase gate; they keep the docs-as-DoD ledger (I8) truthful. Detail in Conditions below.

docs.title: Do-NOT compliance table (ADR-0022 §5, items #1–#10)
docs.text:
# Do-NOT rule Verdict Evidence 1 No inference call inside retrieve() HELD retrieval.py imports only get_embedding_client (bge-m3 embed of the query string — not a generative call), get_qdrant_client, get_session. No provider import. The four phases are embed→vector-read→edge-read→file-read→assemble. retrieve() (L136–211) makes zero provider.* calls. 2 No vault walk / rescan in retrieval or GET /search; GET /search leaves data_version unchanged HELD grep for rglob/glob/os.walk/scandir/iterdir/listdir in retrieval.py → NONE. The only filesystem touch is _load_passage (L487–503), a single targeted (vault_root / file_path).read_text() per surfaced candidate — one file, no directory walk. No UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE/commit in retrieval.py; _read_data_version (L509–524) is a pure SELECT, snapshotted BEFORE assembly (L168) and returned for the AC-F5-5 proof. GET /search (main.py L1229–1287) calls retrieve() and serialises — no mutation. 3 Expansion reads edges, never GraphEngine/FA2 HELD _phase2_graph_expansion (L253–301) / _expand_frontier (L304–363) issue raw SELECT … FROM edges and SELECT … FROM links. No import of app.graph.engine, no GraphEngine, no igraph/FA2 reference anywhere in the module. Depth hard-capped at _MAX_EXPANSION_DEPTH = 2 (L62, clamped L165). 4 No new embedding service / Qdrant collection / sparse index / tiktoken / sentence_transformers HELD pyproject.toml grep for tiktoken\|sentence_transformers\|bm25\|tavily → NONE. retrieval.py grep for create_collection\|recreate\|sparse\|VectorParams → NONE (only a docstring mention of "no new collection"). Phase 1 reuses get_embedding_client() (bge-m3) and the existing settings.qdrant_collection (synapse_pages, cosine, single dense VectorParams defined in qdrant_client.py L74–78). char/4 budget (_CHARS_PER_TOKEN = 4, L56) — no tokenizer. 5 No pages.slug column, no F5 migration; messages.citations reused; slug derived HELD models.py grep for slug → NONE (no column). Latest migration is 0008_import_schedules.py; no 0009 (reserved for Phase-3 review_items per §2.6). slug is derived in code via slugify() (retrieval.py L570–576). messages.citations JSONB already exists (migration 0007 / models.py L622, "RESERVED for M5") and is reused — stream.py L313 writes into it, no schema change. 6 No per-token [n] resolution, no second markdown parse (decorate once on settled message) HELD Frontend: MarkdownView.tsx calls renderMarkdown once (L76, memoized on visibleContent) then decorateCitations once (L82–85, memoized on (rawHtml, citations)). decorateCitations.ts is a single-pass regex string substitution over already-sanitised HTML (L94–101) — it does NOT call renderMarkdown (no second parse), and is 1-entry memoized (L76–78). Only rendered for settled messages (component contract L5–9). Backend: stream.py calls retrieve() exactly once before the stream loop (L185), never inside the delta loop (L226–241). 7 provider.chat(messages, retrieval_context) signature unchanged; citations out-of-band HELD stream.py L224 calls provider.chat(provider_messages, system_context) — the unchanged 2-arg shape. The combined grounding string is passed as retrieval_context; citations never enter the provider call. They travel out-of-band in the orchestration layer: stamped onto messages.citations (L313) and the additive done.citations field (L342–357). CliAgentProvider.chat() (cli.py L108) keeps the same (messages, retrieval_context) signature. 8 No hardcoded provider/model; CliAgentProvider.chat() bounded (token_budget + timeout + CHAT_AGENT_MAX_TURNS) HELD stream.py resolves via resolve_provider_config("chat", …) (L129) → resolve_provider(config_row) (L133); model_id/token_budget/timeout_seconds all read from the row (L137–142), no literals. cli.py: model from config.model_id (L80), three bounds — token_budget+timeout_seconds enforced by run_chat_stream (L233–241, L248–253) and max_turns from the CHAT_AGENT_MAX_TURNS env (L142, _chat_agent_max_turns L294–321, default 8, non-positive/malformed → default + WARNING). 9 No fake CLI stream when no API key — clean pre-stream error HELD cli.py chat() checks os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY") and raises ValueError (L129–131) BEFORE constructing the generator. Because chat() is a coroutine awaited before iteration (stream.py L225), the raise surfaces as a normal provider_error event, never a half-open/fake stream. Missing SDK → clean RuntimeError (L137–140). NB-4 cost convention applied (_finalize_chat_usage L362–384). 10 D4: openapi.json includes GET /search, zero drift HELD docs/api/openapi.json contains paths["/search"] with a get operation and the SearchResponse component schema (verified by JSON load). main.py declares response_model=SearchResponse (L1211) with field-level descriptions matching §2.5. No D2 (ER) change expected/made in Phase 1 — confirmed (no model/migration change).

Result: 10 / 10 HELD. No VIOLATIONS.

docs.title: Frozen data-structure conformance (ADR-0022 §2.3)
docs.text:
Structure §2.3 contract Code (retrieval.py) Match PageRef id: str, title: str, slug: str L70–80 — identical, slug derived in code ✓ Citation n: int, ref: PageRef, score: float, phase: Literal["vector","expansion"] L83–95 — identical, including the Literal ✓ RetrievalContext query, text, citations: list[Citation], token_budget: int, approx_tokens: int, data_version: int L98–116 — identical field set, types, and order ✓

Authority invariant (len(citations) == distinct[n]count): structurally guaranteed — the assembler _phase4_assemble (L426–471) emits each [n] block and its Citation together in the same loop iteration; they cannot drift. The compact done//search projections (stream.py L342–345, main.py L1268–1278) are pure re-shapes of the same authoritative list.

Public interface (retrieve(query, *, vault_id, context_window, k=8, expansion_depth=2)): matches §2.1. The added session: AsyncSession | None = None (L143) is a keyword-only, default-None test/transaction seam that does not alter the frozen positional contract or any caller — acceptable.

docs.title: Accepted-limitation honesty check (ADR-0022 §4)
docs.text: [n] resolves to the source-document pages row, not the synthesized wiki/ page. Preserved honestly and not silently worsened: - _load_page_meta reads pages.{id,title,file_path} (L544–558); file_path is the raw/sources/... path, and _load_passage reads that source body. The citation therefore points at the indexed source — exactly the §4 boundary, no new degradation. - title falls back to the filename stem so it is never empty (L555–557, §2.6). - The frontend renders the title on hover and navigates by slug (decorateCitations.ts L100, MarkdownView.tsx L89–103) — it surfaces whatever the backend cites; it does not paper over the source-vs-wiki distinction. The limitation remains visible and deferred post-M5, as designed.

docs.title: Invariant-tension scan (beyond the Do-NOT list)
docs.text:
No new tension introduced. Two points worth recording for the gate:

  1. Retrieval failure is non-fatal to chat (stream.py L184–197). retrieve() errors are caught and chat proceeds with empty retrieval context (no citations). This is a sound resilience choice and does not violate any invariant — but it means a silent retrieval regression would degrade grounding without failing the turn. The WARNING log (L193) is the only signal. Acceptable for Phase 1; flag for QA to assert the log path and for a future health metric. Not a condition.

  2. _expand_frontier IN-list interpolation (retrieval.py L314–328, L348–351, L539–547). Bind placeholder NAMES (:f0,:f1,…) are f-string-interpolated, but every id value travels as a bound parameter — never as SQL text. # noqa: S608 is correctly scoped and the justification is documented inline. Not an injection vector. HELD, no action.

Both are consistent with the ADR's "simple and correct over clever and fragile" stance.

docs.title: Conditions (documentation-only — do NOT block the phase gate)
docs.text:
C1 — Amend ADR-0022 for query_points (REQUIRED, doc drift / I8). ADR-0022 §2.2 phase 1 and §3 (AQ-v0.5-1) literally reference qdrant.search(synapse_pages, vector, limit=k, with_payload=True). The implementation uses client.query_points(collection_name=…, query=vector, limit=k, with_payload=True) and reads response.points (retrieval.py L232–246). This is semantically equivalent and the correct call: qdrant-client removed search() (deprecated, dropped by 1.18); query_points is the current dense top-k API. Against the existing single-dense synapse_pages collection it performs the identical cosine top-k — same collection, same vector, same ScoredPoint(id, score, payload) results, same id == pages.id mapping (ADR-0002). No collection-shape change, no sparse vector. Acceptable and approved. ADR-0022's code reference should be updated from .search() to .query_points() (with a one-line note that .search() was removed in client 1.18) so the frozen contract matches the shipped code. Hand to tech-writer.

C2 — Pin the qdrant-client floor (RECOMMENDED, reproducibility). backend/pyproject.toml declares qdrant-client>=1.9.0, but query_points returning a QueryResponse.points list is the modern shape; 1.9 is the lower bound that introduced it and older patch behaviour differs. Since the code now depends on query_points/.points, devops should confirm the lockfile resolves to a query_points-bearing release (≥1.10 is safe) and, ideally, bump the floor so a fresh environment cannot resolve a search()-only client. Non-blocking for this gate (CI installs a current client), but record it. Hand to devops-engineer.

No code conditions. Nothing in the implementation needs to change to pass the gate.

docs.title: Sign-off
docs.text:
• ADR-0022 contract: honored (§2.1 interface, §2.2 phases, §2.3 structures, §2.4 citation contract, §2.5 GET /search, §2.6 no-migration, §2.7 chat/provider integration).
  • 9 invariants in force (I1, I2, I3, I5, I6, I7, I9): all HELD by construction.
  • D-artifacts: openapi.json updated with GET /search (I8); no D2 change required.
  • Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS (conditions are doc-only: C1 ADR amendment required, C2 dependency-floor recommended). Cleared for the Phase-1 gate pending QA's green test run.

Handoffs: C1 ADR query_points amendment → tech-writer; C2 qdrant-client floor → devops-engineer; verdict → orchestrator.

docs.title: v0.5 — Architect Review — M5 Phase 2 (Deep Research, F10)
docs.text:
• Reviewer: solution-architect
  • Date: 2026-06-29
  • Branch: sprint/v0.5
  • Scope: F10 Deep Research, judged against the frozen contract ADR-0024 and the 9 invariants.
  • Headline invariant: I7 (bounded loops).
  • Method: static read of the implementation against ADR-0024 §2/§3/§4/§5/§7/§8. Test suite NOT run (QA owns that, in parallel).

docs.title: Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS
docs.text:
The core of F10 — the bounded loop, the SearXNG-only web-search seam, the ingest-seam synthesis, and the data model — is implemented exactly to the frozen contract. All 11 Do-NOT items are HELD in the loop module. The boundedness posture (I7) is provable from the code: for iteration in range(1, max_iter_local + 1), a top-of-round budget gate, and a single hardcoded Semaphore(3).

One non-P0 correctness defect blocks unconditional approval: the REST entry POST /research/start and the loop run_deep_research each generate a different run_id and each INSERT a row, so the loop writes to a row the client never polls. This does not violate any invariant (every loop path is still bounded; both rows are still terminal-written), but it breaks the fire-and-poll contract of ADR-0024 §8.1 and would make the UI poll a permanently-running orphan. It must be fixed before the phase gate. See Condition C1.

docs.title: Do-NOT compliance table (ADR-0024 §5)
docs.text:
# Do-NOT (P0 = 1–4) Verdict Evidence 1 No while True / unbounded refinement; must be for … range(1, max_iter+1) HELD deep_research.py:187 — for iteration in range(1, max_iter_local + 1):. No while anywhere in the loop body. Two internal breaks only (budget, converged). 2 No re-reading bounds/config/env mid-loop (frozen at start) HELD Bounds frozen into locals before the loop: max_iter_local = run_obj.max_iter (:175), token_budget_local = run_obj.token_budget (:176). _mq = _max_queries() read once at :179. The loop body reads only these locals + accumulator.total_tokens; no settings./run_obj access inside range. 3 No web-search backend other than SearXNG; no fallback HELD All search routed through searxng_search_many (deep_research.py:346 → searxng.py:100). searxng.py is the sole HTTP-search site; base URL from settings.searxng_url only (:50); non-200/timeout → [], never an alternative engine (:61–71). Static guard test_no_forbidden_search_imports exists (test_deep_research.py:913) and a live grep of ops/ returns zero forbidden tokens. 4 No exceeding concurrency=3 HELD Single module _semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(CONCURRENCY) with CONCURRENCY = 3 (searxng.py:28,31). Both search (searxng.py:112) and fetch (deep_research.py:366) acquire that same semaphore. No second semaphore, no unbounded gather (the gathers fan out only over already-semaphore-guarded coroutines). 5 No direct write to vault/wiki/; no direct generate() — synthesis via ingest_file() HELD _ingest_synthesis writes to raw/sources/deep-research-<run_id>.md (:521–527) then calls ingest_file(abs_path) (:533). No provider.generate() / write_wiki_page() call in the module. 6 No hardcoded provider/model/key; no isinstance branch on provider HELD _resolve_provider uses resolve_provider_config("ingest", vault_id) + resolve_provider(config_row) (:686–700). No model id / base_url / key literal. No isinstance(provider, …) anywhere. (The one isinstance at :308 tests prior_context, a str/list arg — not the provider.) 7 Never leave a run "running"; terminal write in finally, → error on exception HELD status pessimistic default "max_iter_reached" (:164–166); except sets "error" + error_message (:232–236); _finalize_run_row(...) is in finally (:238–251) and always overwrites status + completed_at. No code path leaves the row's status "running". (Caveat: applies to the row the loop owns — see C1.) 8 Never skip sufficiency-before-refine HELD _assess_sufficiency is called every round (:211) before the refine branch (:220–224). On the final iteration the refine branch is skipped by if iteration < max_iter_local, so the loop exits with the assessment already done. 9 No per-source secondary loop HELD _fetch_and_extract is single-pass: one httpx.get per hit, failure → content_md=None, logged & dropped (:365–392). No retry/re-fetch loop. 10 No new InferenceProvider ABC method (rides chat()) HELD All three inference phases call provider.chat(messages=…, retrieval_context=…) (:326, :432, :491). No research()/assess() added to the ABC; base.py untouched by F10. 11 No data_version double-bump (ingest_file does the single bump) HELD No bump_version() / data_version reference in deep_research.py. The single bump happens inside the re-entrant ingest_file (ADR-0003 seam).

P0 items 1–4: all HELD. No headline-invariant violation.

docs.title: Structural-bounds confirmation (the three bounds, provable from code)
docs.text:
Bound Structural enforcement Confirmed max_iter (iteration cap) for iteration in range(1, max_iter_local + 1) at deep_research.py:187. The iteration count is capped by the range header; no statement in the body extends it. Boundedness confirmable by reading one line. YES token_budget (spend gate) if accumulator.total_tokens >= token_budget_local: status = "budget_exhausted"; break at the top of each round before any provider/network spend (:192–194). Conservative direction (under-spend). YES concurrency = 3 (fan-out ceiling) Hardcoded CONCURRENCY = 3 → _semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(CONCURRENCY) (searxng.py:28,31); the single semaphore is acquired by both search and fetch. Architect-gate comment present. YES

Bounds are frozen on the deep_research_runs row at INSERT (model max_iter/token_budget NOT NULL, no server default — must be supplied) and read once into locals; the loop never re-reads config. The "frozen at run start" guarantee (AQ-v0.5-4) holds inside the loop.

docs.title: Data-model / migration conformance (ADR-0024 §7)
docs.text:
Check Result deep_research_runs columns match §7.1 MATCH — all 16 columns present with correct types/nullability (models.py:980–1128; migration 0009:58–122). deep_research_sources columns match §7.2 MATCH — all 7 columns present (models.py:1131–1206; migration 0009:131–162). FK CASCADE on child PRESENT — ForeignKey("deep_research_runs.id", ondelete="CASCADE") in both model (:1152) and migration (0009:143). ORM relationship cascade="all, delete-orphan" (:1114). synthesis_page_id FK → pages.id PRESENT — model :1086, migration 0009:111. Indexes PRESENT — ix_deep_research_runs_vault_started (vault_id, started_at) and ix_deep_research_sources_run_id (run_id) in both model __table_args__ and migration (0009:124–128, 164–168). UUID/JSONB .with_variant for SQLite test parity does NOT change Postgres production types CONFIRMED — model uses UUID(as_uuid=True).with_variant(String(36), "sqlite") and JSONB().with_variant(JSON(), "sqlite"); the migration (the production DDL authority) emits postgresql.UUID and postgresql.JSONB unconditionally (0009:60–66, 84–88). Postgres production types are unaffected by the SQLite variant. Migration chain 0009 revises 0008 (0009:50–51). Numbering correction (F10=0009 before F9=0010) per ADR-0024 §7 note is respected.

make er regeneration (D2) and make openapi (D4) are I8 gate items — flagged to tech-writer; not re-run here. Condition C3.

docs.title: I9 static guard
docs.text:
The code cannot reach Tavily/Google/DDG/SerpAPI:

  • The only outbound search call is searxng.py:searxng_search → GET {SEARXNG_URL}/search?format=json. No second search function exists.
  • searxng.py imports only httpx, pydantic, app.config. No search SaaS SDK.
  • The static guard test_no_forbidden_search_imports (test_deep_research.py:913) scans all app/ops/**/*.py against tavily|duckduckgo|ddg|googlesearch|serpapi|google[-_]search[-_]results. Live grep of ops/ returns zero matches, so the guard passes and the ops docstrings themselves don't trip it.
  • On SearXNG failure: [] returned + logged (searxng.py:52, 62, 66). No fallback engine. POST /research/start returns 503 when SEARXNG_URL is unset (main.py:2240–2247) — no fake run, no fallback. HELD.

docs.title: I3 / frontend polling
docs.text: Check Result Polling is a setTimeout chain, not setInterval HELD — researchStore.ts:243, 252. No setInterval. Chain stops on terminal status HELD — tick() returns early when isTerminal(detail.status) (:218); next tick scheduled only if (!isTerminal(updated.status)) (:242). Cleanup on unmount / navigate-away HELD — AbortController aborted via returned cleanup (:254); DeepSearchView calls it on unmount and on terminal (DeepSearchView.tsx:652–666); tick bails if selectedRunId !== runId (:217) or ctrl.signal.aborted (:214). No main-thread heavy work / tight loop HELD — 5s interval; selectors + useShallow on collections (:518); synthesis rendered as static <pre> (no per-token markdown parse), consistent with I3. List virtualised with TanStack Virtual (I4, :527).

docs.title: C1 — run_id divergence (BLOCKING, non-P0, correctness)
docs.text:
POST /research/start generates run_id = uuid.uuid4() (main.py:2251), pre-INSERTs a deep_research_runs row with that id (:2266–2285), returns it to the client (:2305), then schedules run_deep_research(vault_id=…, topic=…, max_iter=…, token_budget=…) without passing run_id (:2288–2295). Inside run_deep_research, a new run_id = uuid.uuid4() is created (deep_research.py:146) and _create_run_row INSERTs a second row (:147–153, :553–588).

Consequences: - The client polls the endpoint's row, which the loop never updates → it stays status="running" forever. This defeats the fire-and-poll contract (ADR-0024 §8.1) and is a soft I7-observability regression (a row that never reaches terminal, even though the actual loop is bounded and its own row IS finalized). - Two rows per run pollute GET /research/runs and double-count sources_fetched=0 orphans.

Required fix (pick one, architect-preferred = A): - A. run_deep_research accepts run_id: uuid.UUID and _create_run_row becomes an UPDATE-or-skip (the endpoint already INSERTed). The endpoint passes its run_id. The loop reads max_iter/token_budget from the existing row (preserving "frozen on the row" semantics). This keeps a single source of truth and the 202-then-poll guarantee. - B. The endpoint does NOT pre-INSERT; it lets run_deep_research own the INSERT and returns the loop's run_id. Requires awaiting the row creation before returning 202 (a short await, not the loop) so the poll target exists. More refactoring; A is cleaner.

Either way: exactly one row per run, and the id returned to the client is the id the loop finalizes.

docs.title: C2 — confirm the QA loop-gate tests bind to the fixed entry (advisory)
docs.text: The mandatory max_iter_reached / budget_exhausted / error-never-running tests (test_deep_research.py:322, 468, 746) call run_deep_research directly, so they pass regardless of C1. Add/confirm one test_research_api.py assertion that POST /research/start then GET /research/runs/{id} eventually reaches a terminal status — this is the regression that would have caught C1. Hand to QA.

docs.title: C3 — D-artifact gate (I8, blocking for phase DoD, owned with tech-writer)
docs.text: Before the phase gate: make er (D2 — both tables + relationships in docs/er/schema.mmd), make openapi (D4 — the three /research/* endpoints), and docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd (D3, ADR-0024 §9) must be regenerated and committed with zero drift. Not verified in this review; flagged to tech-writer.

docs.title: Invariant tension the ADR did not anticipate
docs.text:
• Mechanical (no-provider) path. _resolve_provider returning None (no provider_config row) is handled gracefully — query-gen falls back to [topic], assess treats any content as sufficient, synthesize assembles raw snippets (:303–306, 411–414, 465–472). ADR-0024 assumed a resolved provider. This is a reasonable, invariant-safe degradation (still bounded, still SearXNG-only, still ingest-seam), but it means a deep-research run can complete with zero LLM involvement and still write a raw/sources/ doc. Acceptable for Phase 2; note it in USER.md so users understand an unconfigured-provider run produces a low-quality stub. No invariant traded.
  • _create_run_row returns an expunged snapshot (:587) and the loop never re-attaches it — correct, and it's the mechanism that makes "frozen on the row" hold without lazy re-reads. Good. (This is also why C1's fix must keep reading bounds from the row, not from the function args, to preserve the audit semantics.)

No invariant is traded for convenience. The two P0 headline invariants (I7, I9) are fully held in the implementation.

docs.title: Handoff
docs.text:
• C1 → backend-engineer (run_id unification in main.py + deep_research.py). Re-review required before phase gate.
  • C2 → qa-test-engineer (add the start→poll→terminal API regression).
  • C3 → tech-writer (D2/D3/D4 regeneration + ADR-0024 README row).
  • Phase-2 verdict → orchestrator: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS (C1 blocking).

docs.title: v0.5 Phase 3 — Architect Review (F9 Review Queue + F12 Multi-format ingest)
docs.text:
• Reviewer: solution-architect
  • Date: 2026-06-29
  • Branch: sprint/v0.5
  • Scope: M5 Phase 3 — F9 (HITL Review Queue) + F12 (Multi-format ingest), judged against ADR-0025 §5 Do-NOT list + CLAUDE.md §3 invariants.
  • Method: static read of the implementation against the contract. Test suite NOT run (QA owns that, in parallel).

docs.title: VERDICT: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS
docs.text: The implementation is faithful to ADR-0025. All 17 Do-NOT triggers HELD, all 4 critical checks PASS, migration/schema conform, ER (D2) updated with zero drift. The single condition is a non-blocking hardening item (DB-level CHECK constraints the ADR specified are absent — enforcement is currently app-level only). It does not gate the phase; it must be tracked.

docs.title: F9 — Do-NOT compliance
docs.text: # Do-NOT Verdict Evidence a >1 provider call/item or any query-gen loop HELD ops/review.py:_single_chat_call consumes provider.chat() exactly once; generate_review_queries makes one asyncio.wait_for-wrapped call (review.py:211); no loop/retry. Hook calls it once per page (orchestrator.py:998-1003). b hook that raises/blocks ingest HELD orchestrator.py:377-387 wraps _enqueue_review_items in try/except Exception → logs WARNING, swallows. Page already written + overview updated before the hook (:367-370). c a vaults table or UUID vault_id HELD models.py:1245 vault_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String, …); migration 0010:52-57 sa.String(), no FK. No vaults table introduced. d hardcoded backend / isinstance on provider (I6) HELD generate_review_queries resolves via resolve_provider_config("ingest", vault_id) (review.py:177) → resolve_provider(cfg). No isinstance / provider_type / class-name branch anywhere in review.py. e Approve/Skip/DR added to the Ingest View HELD Actions live only in components/review/ReviewQueueView.tsx; separate review section + reviewStore.ts. No review actions in the Sources/Ingest surface. f re-trigger ingest on Approve HELD approve/skip → _set_status (review.py:468-493): status + reviewed_at + reviewed_by write only. No ingest_file, no rescan, no bump_version. Endpoint doc reiterates AC-F9-6 (main.py:2647). g unbounded consistency scan / FA2 call HELD No consistency/contradiction scan shipped (ADR §3.4 reserved). Generator produces suggested_query-style follow-ups only. No edges/FA2/graph access in review.py. h uncapped /review/queue (cap 200) HELD main.py:2484 _REVIEW_QUEUE_MAX_LIMIT = 200; list_review_queue query param le=_REVIEW_QUEUE_MAX_LIMIT (:2589), default 50. i un-virtualized list >50 HELD ReviewQueueView.tsx uses useVirtualizer (:292) with absolute-positioned rows; virtualization always active. TanStack Virtual.

docs.title: F12 — Do-NOT compliance
docs.text: # Do-NOT Verdict Evidence j format-lib imports outside extract.py HELD grep for pypdf/docx/pptx/openpyxl/unstructured across backend/app/ returns only ingest/extract.py. All four libs imported LOCALLY inside their helper functions (extract.py:111,136,147,173). k any LLM call in extraction HELD extract_text and all _extract_* helpers are pure path→text; no provider/httpx/network import in extract.py. l extracting in the watcher HELD Extraction is on the upload request path only (main.py:1132-1174), synchronously, before the 202. watcher.py is unchanged and format-agnostic (_is_text_file imports _ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS). m adding binary exts to _ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS HELD upload.py:32 _ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = {".md",".txt",".markdown"} UNCHANGED. Binary/placeholder sets are separate (:37,40); union only in _UPLOAD_ACCEPTED (:45). Watcher imports the unchanged frozenset. n writing to wiki/ / bypassing ingest_file HELD Companion written to raw/sources/<stem>.extracted.md (main.py:1147-1154). wiki/ is written only by write_wiki_page via the watcher→ingest_file path. Original binary preserved. o mutating/deleting the original binary HELD Binary atomically moved to raw/sources/ (main.py:1127); never unlinked/rewritten after. Companion is a new file; binary is an immutable raw/ artifact (K1). p adding unstructured/OCR HELD pyproject.toml:26-29 adds only pypdf, python-docx, python-pptx, openpyxl (pinned floors+ceilings). No unstructured. Image/AV → _placeholder one-liner (extract.py:211-227). q bypassing the byte/char caps HELD Upload byte cap MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES enforced before move (main.py:1102-1106, 413). Output char cap EXTRACT_MAX_CHARS enforced in extract_text (extract.py:90-97).

docs.title: 1. chat() signature (I6) — PASS
docs.text: The ABC signature is locked: async def chat(self, messages, retrieval_context) (provider/base.py:106) — 2 args, no token_budget. review.py:_single_chat_call calls provider.chat(messages, "") (:267) and enforces the budget at the orchestration layer via budget_chars = token_budget * _CHARS_PER_TOKEN (char/4, :264), breaking the async-gen loop once total >= budget_chars and calling aclose(). It consumes the dual shape (maybe = provider.chat(...); agen = await maybe if inspect.isawaitable(maybe) else maybe, :267-268) exactly like chat/stream.py. The prior token_budget= kwarg bug is fixed and the budget is actually enforced (truncates accumulated output, not a no-op). Timeout wraps the whole consumption (:211), so it covers network latency, not just first-token.

docs.title: 2. deep-research run_id threading (no C1 recurrence) — PASS
docs.text: review.py:deep_research mints run_id (:395), pre-INSERTs the DeepResearchRun row with status='running' and frozen bounds (:403-422), stores run_id_str on the review row (:438), then schedules run_deep_research(..., run_id=run_id) (:445-453). run_deep_research reuses the caller's row when run_id is not None and only mints+inserts when it is None (deep_research.py:152-160) — the documented C1-safe path. No orphan row, no double-insert, no stuck-"running". The manual pre-insert mirrors _create_run_row field-for-field (all NOT-NULL columns populated), so the INSERT will not violate schema.

docs.title: 3. F9 hook safety — PASS
docs.text: run_ingest_pipeline runs the hook only on the orchestrated branch, AFTER write_wiki_page + _update_overview (orchestrator.py:367-388). The hook is wrapped in try/except Exception … log WARNING (:377-387). Inside _enqueue_review_items, the excerpt read is itself guarded (:974-996), and generate_review_queries never raises (it returns None on any provider/config error — review.py:178-230). A generator failure cannot propagate into the ingest caller or affect IngestRunResult. Delegated (CLI) branch correctly does NOT invoke the hook (ADR §3.3.4 reserved gap, acknowledged).

docs.title: 4. F12 companion flow (I1/I5) — PASS
docs.text: Binary upload path (main.py:1132-1176): (1) original binary atomically moved to raw/sources/ and preserved; (2) extract_text(dst) synchronously; (3) companion <stem>.extracted.md written with valid Obsidian frontmatter — type: source, title, sources: ["raw/sources/<name>.<ext>"] (:1151-1152, F3 traceback, I5); (4) returns 202. The watcher then fires on .extracted.md (matches .md in the unchanged _ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS) and the binary is ignored (its extension is not watched) → one ingest_file, one hash gate, one pages row, one Qdrant point (I1). Extraction failure is non-fatal: binary stays saved, no companion, watcher silently skips it (:1166-1174). _ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS is unchanged.

docs.title: Migration / schema conformance
docs.text: Check Result review_items is Alembic 0010, down_revision = "0009" PASS (0010_review_items.py:35-36) vault_id String, no FK, no vaults table (AQ-v0.5-6) PASS (model + migration) page_id nullable FK → pages.id PASS (models.py:1254-1262; migration :58-64) deep_research_run_id nullable FK → deep_research_runs.id PASS (models.py:1290-1298; migration :84-90) Columns match ADR-0025 §3.1 (id, vault_id, page_id, item_type, status, pre_generated_query, deep_research_run_id, created_at, reviewed_at, reviewed_by) PASS — all 10 present, types/nullability match status server_default 'pending' PASS Index ix_review_items_vault_status_created (vault_id, status, created_at) PASS UUID with_variant(String(36), "sqlite") pattern (follows deep_research_runs) PASS D2 ER (docs/er/schema.mmd) shows REVIEW_ITEMS with both FKs, zero drift PASS (schema.mmd:152-163) D4 OpenAPI: 4 review endpoints + widened upload accept-list PASS (endpoints defined in main.py; make openapi regen still owed at gate — see conditions)

docs.title: Conditions
docs.text:
1. (Non-blocking, must-track) DB-level CHECK constraints absent. ADR-0025 §3.1 specifies CHECK constraints enumerating item_type (new_page|update_page|deep_research_candidate) and status (pending|approved|skipped|deep_researched). The model documents them as "enum-by-convention (CHECK constraint)" but migration 0010 creates plain Text columns with no CHECK. Enforcement today is application-level only (handler Literal types + status helper). This does not violate an invariant and does not gate Phase 3, but it is a deviation from the signed ADR. Resolve by either (a) adding the two CHECK constraints in a follow-up migration, or (b) amending ADR-0025 to record enum-by-convention as the accepted decision. Track to M5 close.

  2. (Gate housekeeping) D4 regeneration. Confirm make openapi has been run so docs/api/openapi.json carries the 4 /review/queue* routes and the widened /ingest/upload accept-list; confirm make er output (schema.mmd) is committed (it is). Zero-drift is I8 — verify in the docs gate before phase sign-off.

docs.title: Notes / observations (no action required)
docs.text:
• list_queue queries ALL statuses (not just pending) so the UI can show the full queue; the store filters actioned items out optimistically. Consistent with ADR §3.5 ("the full queue") and the event-log model (§3.1). Acceptable.
  • Reserved gaps are explicitly coded, not silently dropped: delegated-CLI enqueue (ADR §7.1), consistency scan (§3.4), binaries-outside-upload auto-extract (§4.3). Each is gated behind a future ADR. No invariant tension introduced.
  • The _query_token_budget default (2000) and _query_timeout (30s) read from settings at call time with safe fallbacks — bounded per I7, logged via UsageAccumulator (review.py:233).
  • No new container/component topology — review.py/extract.py are components inside the existing FastAPI service. D1 (C4) unchanged, as ADR-0025 §8 handoff predicted.

docs.title: v0.5 Phase 4 — Architect Review: F13 Cascade Deletion (ADR-0026)
docs.text:
• Reviewer: solution-architect
  • Date: 2026-06-29
  • Branch: sprint/v0.5
  • Scope: F13 cascade delete — code-complete, pre-phase-gate sign-off
  • Design of record: docs/adr/0026-cascade-delete.md
  • Files reviewed:
  • backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py
  • backend/app/main.py (POST /pages/{id}/cascade-delete/preview, DELETE /pages/{id})
  • frontend/src/components/wiki/CascadeDeleteModal.tsx
  • Reference seams: backend/app/ingest/orchestrator.py (delete_file, bump_version), backend/app/graph/cache.py (notify_bump), backend/app/wiki/index.py (update_index), backend/app/wiki/links.py (persist_links/parse_wikilinks), backend/app/db.py (get_session)
  • Note: Test suite NOT executed (QA owns that in parallel). This verdict judges the CODE against the contract + invariants.

docs.title: Verdict: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS
docs.text:
The implementation faithfully realises ADR-0026: single-pass, inference-free, loop-free; references found from the links back-reference index (no vault walk on the happy path); preserve-shared partition is exact; dead-link rewrites are frontmatter-safe via python-frontmatter round-trip; data_version is bumped exactly once; no migration was added (D2 unchanged by F13); soft-delete only; raw source file removed. All 10 Do-NOT triggers are HELD in the code.

Four conditions below are correctness/robustness refinements, none of which trade an invariant. C1 (transaction atomicity) is the only one I rate above cosmetic, and it is a hardening item, not an invariant breach — the operation is still correct on the success path. None block the phase gate provided they are tracked.

docs.title: Do-NOT compliance table (ADR-0026 §8)
docs.text:
# Do-NOT (invariant) Status Evidence (file:line) 1 Full-rescan/glob/walk of wiki/** to find references (I1) HELD Methods (a)/(b) are pure links-table reads: cascade_delete.py:218-223 (a), :264-271 (b). Method (c) enumerates live wiki pages from the pages table, not a vault walk, bounded by CASCADE_FULLTEXT_MAX_FILES: :312-320. No glob/os.walk/rglob anywhere in the module. 2 Inline GraphEngine.recompute() / FA2 (I2) HELD No import of GraphEngine/graph.engine. Only _graph_cache.notify_bump(new_version) (debounced): :858-864. Mirrors orchestrator seam orchestrator.py:211-222. 3 Bump data_version more than once (AC-F13-4c) HELD Single call to _bump_version_and_notify() at the very end of cascade_delete: :736. The helper does one +1 UPDATE: :842-849. No per-file/per-page bump. 4 Leave a dead [[wikilink]] (I5) HELD (with C2 caveat) 3-method union builds the rewrite set (:451-524); _rewrite_body neutralises [[T]]/[[T\|alias]]/[[T#section]] and slug variants in the body: :146-178. Caveat C2: a deleted page that is NOT a source — its inbound links are rewritten, but see C2 re self-rewrite skip. 5 Corrupt YAML frontmatter (I5) HELD Every file mutation uses frontmatter.loads(...) → mutate .content only → frontmatter.dumps(...): rewrites :660-668, sources-prune :805-808. The body regex _WIKILINK_RE is applied to post.content only, never the raw doc; frontmatter block is re-emitted by frontmatter.dumps. 6 Delete a shared page (preserve-shared) HELD _partition_shared_wiki_pages: page goes to will_delete iff remaining == [] after removing the source, else will_preserve: :382-386. Exactly the asymmetric rule from ADR §4.1. 7 Skip the dry-run (preview) HELD plan_cascade_delete is read-only (all get_session blocks are select only): :532-598. POST .../preview calls it and never mutates: main.py:2768-2801. DELETE is the only mutator. 8 Loop / inference call (I7/I6) HELD No for loop re-invokes the plan; no provider import, no InferenceProvider, no LLM/agent call anywhere in the module. Single pass over plan.wikilinks_to_rewrite. 9 Restart resurrection (AQ-v0.5-5) HELD raw/sources/ file unlinked: :723-726 (unlink(missing_ok=True)). Combined with soft-delete tombstone the watcher cannot re-create. 10 Hard-delete the pages row HELD Only update(Page)...values(deleted_at=now): :634-636. No DELETE FROM pages. (Edge rows ARE hard-deleted :700-707 — correct and intended per ADR §5 step 4; links are flagged dangling=True :694-696, not deleted.)

10/10 HELD.

docs.title: 3-method matching (AC-F13-1/-2)
docs.text:
• (a) exact — _method_a_exact: SELECT source_page_id, target_title FROM links WHERE target_page_id == page_id OR target_title == T, then resolves file_path only for live wiki pages (deleted_at IS NULL). Pure index read (:203-243). Confirmed.
  • (b) slug — _method_b_slug: reads links rows whose source_page_id is not already found, compares _slugify(target_title) == _slugify(T) in Python. No filesystem walk (:246-289). Confirmed.
  • (c) full-text fallback — _method_c_fulltext: enumerates live wiki pages from the pages table (NOT a vault walk), bounded by CASCADE_FULLTEXT_MAX_FILES (default 5000, env-overridable), reads each candidate file once to substring-match (:292-343). Confirmed bounded.
  • Method labels logged — one logger.info per found reference with its method label: (a) :455-460, (b) :480-485, (c) :507-511 / :336-341. match_methods_used[fp] records the winning method (:461, :486, :512). Confirmed (AC-F13-2 log assertion satisfied).

docs.title: No-rescan linchpin (the §3.1 happy-path gate) — CONDITION C3
docs.text:
ADR §3.1 / §8 #1 and the §9 gate require that (c) is SKIPPED on the links-table-hit happy path. The current code always runs (c) (:502-503), merely de-duplicating its results against (a)∪(b) by source_page_id/file_path. So on the happy path (c) still enumerates and opens every live wiki file for substring scanning — the exact disk-read the ADR says to avoid on that path.

This does NOT violate I1 as an indexing strategy (it is not re-deriving the index, not re-embedding, not rebuilding the graph), so I do not rate it a Do-NOT #1 reject. But it does not satisfy the ADR §3.1 "skipped entirely when (a)∪(b) already covers the candidate set" gate, and it makes the "(c) NOT invoked on the happy path" test (gate item §9.2) impossible to pass against the current control flow. This is Condition C3 — see below.

docs.title: Preserve-shared (AC-F13-2/-3)
docs.text:
• Partition is exact (Do-NOT #6 evidence). When the target is a wiki page (not a source), will_delete = [target] and the partition is empty — the rule degenerates cleanly (:563-572; _partition_shared_wiki_pages only runs for raw/sources/ targets). Confirmed.
  • Shared-entity warnings are advisory: _get_shared_entity_warnings reads kind='source' edges, logs a WARNING, and the delete always proceeds (:391-425, warnings emitted :627-628, never gate the flow). Confirmed non-blocking.

docs.title: No-rescan dead-link cleanup (AC-F13-1 step 6, AC-F13-4a/b)
docs.text:
• Only files in plan.wikilinks_to_rewrite are opened for writing (:656). Each opened once, body-only rewrite, persist_links re-run per file (:679-685). Confirmed targeted.
  • index.md regenerated exactly once via update_index (:711-717). Confirmed.
  • files_written is counted and returned (:670, :744). See C2: files_written can legitimately be < len(wikilinks_to_rewrite) when a rewrite produces no body change (:662-664 continue), so the strict files_written == len(plan.wikilinks_to_rewrite) equality from AC-F13-4a is not guaranteed by this code. Flagging for QA — see C2.

docs.title: Preview read-only / DELETE-only mutation
docs.text:
• plan_cascade_delete issues only select statements; no update/delete/file write/bump. Confirmed read-only.
  • POST .../preview → 200, 404 on missing/soft-deleted; DELETE /pages/{id} → 200, 404 on double-delete (idempotent). Matches ADR §6.1 shape. Confirmed.

docs.title: Migration / D2
docs.text: • Highest migration is 0010_review_items; no 0011 added. F13 reuses deleted_at, links.dangling/target_page_id, edges endpoint indexes, pages.sources JSONB, vault_state. The +163-line schema.mmd delta vs main traces to the earlier M5 commit 254d27d (F9/F12), not to F13. D2 unchanged by F13 — confirmed.

docs.title: Conditions
docs.text:
C1 — Multi-statement atomicity (robustness; track, not a gate-block). cascade_delete opens a separate get_session() transaction per step (soft-delete :633, dangling :693, edge-delete :699, prune :799, bump :831), each auto-committing on exit (db.py:51). A crash between steps leaves a partially-applied delete (e.g. pages tombstoned + Qdrant points gone, but links.dangling not yet set, or index.md not regenerated). The vault is not corrupted (no dead links surface because the page row is gone from the live index query, and a re-run is idempotent on the already-soft-deleted target → 404), but the stores can be transiently inconsistent until a manual re-run. Condition: document the non-atomic, idempotent-on-retry semantics in the ADR (a Consequences note) OR wrap the DB mutations in a single session/transaction in a follow-up. Acceptable for M5 ship as-is given idempotency; must be tracked.

C2 — files_written == len(wikilinks_to_rewrite) is not strictly guaranteed (test-contract alignment). The rewrite loop continues without incrementing files_written when _rewrite_body yields no change (:662-664), and on a write exception (:676-677). AC-F13-4a asserts equality. Either (a) tighten the plan so wikilinks_to_rewrite only contains files that will actually change (the _count_body_occurrences > 0 guard at plan-build time :464/:489/:515 should already ensure this, making the continue dead code on the happy path), or (b) relax the AC assertion to files_written <= len(...). Condition: confirm with QA which contract holds; align code or AC so the equality assertion is deterministic. No invariant impact.

C3 — (c) fallback is not skipped on the happy path (ADR §3.1 / §9.2 gate). As detailed above, _build_wikilink_rewrites unconditionally calls _method_c_fulltext (:502-503). The ADR mandates (c) be skipped when (a)∪(b) already covers the candidate set, and the review gate requires a test proving (c) is not invoked on a links-hit fixture. Condition (required before phase-gate close): add an explicit gate — e.g. skip (c) when match_methods_used is non-empty AND there were no dangling/title-ambiguous links unaccounted for, or introduce a CASCADE_FULLTEXT_FALLBACK guard that defaults to running (c) only when (a)∪(b) returned zero references. This is the one condition that touches a named invariant rationale (I1/I7 disk-read minimisation) and the §9.2 exit criterion directly; it must be resolved or the §9.2 gate item formally waived by the orchestrator.

C4 — Method (b)/(c) not_in(...) over the full links/pages set + or_(..., True) (minor; performance/typing). _method_b_slug reads all links rows when already_found is empty (:264-268, the else True branch) and slug-normalises in Python; _method_c_fulltext similarly. Correct, but on large vaults (b) materialises the whole links table. Combined with C3, the happy path currently does a full links scan (b) + full wiki-file read (c). Condition (advisory): scope (b) to rows sharing the target slug's first-letter bucket or push the slug comparison into SQL where feasible; gate (c) per C3. Not an invariant breach; bounded by CASCADE_FULLTEXT_MAX_FILES. Track for a perf follow-up.

docs.title: Notes for QA (test-quality, not code — flagged per request)
docs.text: The backend tests use AsyncMock sessions rather than in-memory SQLite. Assessment of CODE confidence under that test style: - Do-NOT #1/#7/#8/#10 (no glob, read-only preview, no loop/provider, soft-delete) are statically verifiable from the code and do not depend on a real DB — AsyncMock does not undermine them. - Do-NOT #3 (single bump), #6 (preserve-shared partition logic), #4/#5 (frontmatter-safe rewrite), #9 (file unlink) involve real data round-trips (JSONB partition, SQL UPDATE counts, on-disk frontmatter). With AsyncMock the SQL is not actually executed, so a test can pass while asserting only that .execute was called — it cannot prove the deleted_at UPDATE matched the right rows, that the partition query returns the right pages, or that the links.dangling UPDATE actually flipped flags. Recommendation to QA: the frontmatter round-trip (#4/#5) and the preserve-shared partition (#6) deserve at least one real-filesystem + real-(SQLite/PG)-session integration test each, since those are the I5 and AC-F13-3 headline behaviours and AsyncMock cannot enforce them. test_obsidian_check.py 15/15 after a cascade delete (gate §9.4) must run against a real vault, not mocks.

docs.title: Gate-item status (ADR-0026 §9)
docs.text: Gate Status §9.1 zero inference / zero FA2 / one bump / files_written == len bump+inference+FA2 PASS; files_written equality C2 §9.2 method labels logged; (c) NOT invoked on happy path labels PASS; (c)-skip C3 (open) §9.3 preserve-shared exact PASS §9.4 frontmatter-safe; obsidian_check 15/15 code PASS; runtime gate = QA (real vault) §9.5 preview read-only; DELETE shape + 404 double-delete PASS §9.6 raw/sources file deleted; no resurrection PASS §9.7 D3 renders; D4 both endpoints; no D2 change no D2 change PASS; D3/D4 render = tech-writer/CI to confirm

docs.title: Handoff
docs.text:
• Verdict → orchestrator: APPROVE-WITH-CONDITIONS. C3 is the only gate-touching condition (must resolve or waive §9.2). C1/C2/C4 are tracked robustness/contract items, non-blocking for M5 ship.
  • C2 contract alignment → qa-test-engineer.
  • C3 (c)-skip gate → backend-engineer (small control-flow change in _build_wikilink_rewrites).
  • C1 atomicity Consequences note → tech-writer (ADR-0026 amendment) once orchestrator decides fix-now vs document-now.

docs.title: Architect Review — v0.5 / M5 Phase 5 (F1-MCP-UI)
docs.text:
• Branch: sprint/v0.5
  • Feature: F1-MCP-UI (PM Amendment A1) — read-only MCP server introspection endpoint + Settings panel
  • Contract: ADR-0027 (docs/adr/0027-mcp-info-ui.md)
  • Reviewer: solution-architect
  • Date: 2026-06-29
  • Scope: read-only implementation review against ADR-0027 + invariants. Test suite NOT run (QA owns, in parallel).
  • Headline invariants: I9 (don't reinvent MCP — display only) · I6 (nothing about the MCP server hardcoded)

docs.title: Verdict: APPROVE
docs.text: The implementation is a faithful, minimal realization of ADR-0027. Every Do-NOT holds. The endpoint is pure in-process introspection of the already-built mcp object; the panel is read-only with no mutation path; all MCP metadata is derived from the live object + settings, none hardcoded. No DB, no migration, no second server, no tool invocation, no transport session.

docs.title: Do-NOT verification (ADR-0027 §4)
docs.text:
# Do-NOT (ADR-0027 §4) Status Evidence 1 No hardcoded tool list / name / description / schema (backend or frontend) HELD Backend derives all from await _mcp_server.list_tools() — backend/app/main.py:1635-1644 (t.name, t.description, t.parameters). Frontend renders from info.tools.map(...) — SettingsPanel.tsx:751-790; no JSX literal tool data. i18n values carry no tool/server literals (verified en.json/it.json). 2 No tool invocation from UI; endpoint opens NO MCP transport/stdio session (no asyncio.run, no thread bridge, no DB, no Qdrant) HELD Handler body is only await _mcp_server.list_tools() + field reads — main.py:1634-1652. No DB session, no Qdrant client, no mcp.run(...). Module import is side-effect-free: mcp.run(transport="stdio") is guarded by if __name__ == "__main__" — mcp/server.py:346-348. Frontend has no code path to any tool: fetchMcpInfo is the only call — providerClient.ts:134-141. 3 No config-write / CRUD / mutation — read-only end to end HELD Endpoint is @app.get("/mcp/info") only — main.py:1613. No POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE added. Frontend has no write action; handleCopy writes to clipboard only — SettingsPanel.tsx:670-677. 4 No new MCP capability / second server / duplication of server.py logic (I9) HELD Reuses the existing instance via from app.mcp.server import mcp as _mcp_server — main.py:77. No new FastMCP(...), no @mcp.tool, no reimplemented tool logic.

(Also confirmed: Do-NOT #6 no DB table/migration — no mcp migration in backend/alembic/versions; Do-NOT #7 no Zustand store — SectionApiMcp uses local useState + AbortController on mount, mirroring SectionEmbeddings, SettingsPanel.tsx:636-648.)

docs.title: "Nothing hardcoded" confirmation (I6)
docs.text:
• server_name ← _mcp_server.name (the live FastMCP object) — main.py:1647.
  • transport ← settings.mcp_transport (env MCP_TRANSPORT, default "stdio") — main.py:1648, config.py:183-188.
  • entry_point_command ← settings.mcp_entry_command (env MCP_ENTRY_COMMAND, default "python -m app.mcp.server") — main.py:1649, config.py:190-195.
  • tool_count ← len(tools); tool name/description/schema ← list_tools() items — main.py:1637-1651.
  • Claude Desktop snippet is generated from the payload: entry_point_command tokenised to command + args, server keyed dynamically by server_name — SettingsPanel.tsx:655-668. The displayed JSON is the single source copied to clipboard (buildClaudeDesktopSnippet(info) rendered at :721, copied at :672). Not a JSX literal.

No tool name, description, schema, server name, transport, or entry command appears as a literal in the route function or in JSX. Defaults live in config.py and are env-overridable — consistent with how embedding_* are read. I6 holds end-to-end.

docs.title: Read-only confirmation
docs.text: Backend: single GET route, body performs introspection + field reads only — no write to DB, file, Qdrant, or MCP transport. Frontend: fetch-on-mount + render; the only side effect is navigator.clipboard.writeText. There is no code path from the UI to a tool invocation or a config write. Read-only end to end.

docs.title: Additional checks (ADR-0027 §2.2 / §2.5)
docs.text:
• Clean async introspection: await _mcp_server.list_tools() inside async def get_mcp_info — direct await, no asyncio.run, no sync-bridge hack (main.py:1626,1635). Matches the ADR §2.2 contract. .parameters (not .inputSchema) is read, with a {} fallback when None (main.py:1641) — defensive and correct per the §2.2 verified-facts note.
