docs.text: AC Description Test(s) Status AC-F13-1 cascade_delete executes all 7 steps in order T-CD-009 (soft-delete), T-CD-010 (Qdrant), T-CD-007/T-CD-013 (rewrite), T-CD-015 (index), T-CD-011 (data_version) PASS AC-F13-2 3-method matching; method label logged T-CD-002 (method a), test_method_a_result_reflected_in_plan; method b via slug tests; method c via T-CD-026 (xfail — method c invoked when it should not be) PARTIAL — DEFECT-F13-001 AC-F13-3 Shared-entity warnings advisory; deletion proceeds T-CD-016 PASS AC-F13-4 I1: only referencing files written; data_version +1 once; no FA2 T-CD-011 (bump exactly once), T-CD-012 (no FA2), T-CD-013/T-CD-021 (files_written == rewrites), T-CD-026 (xfail — method c called) PARTIAL — DEFECT-F13-001 AC-F13-5 DELETE /pages/{id} → 200 correct shape; 404 on double-delete T-CD-018, T-CD-019, T-CD-019b, T-CD-020 PASS AC-F13-6 Frontend: two-step modal; shared-entity warnings before confirm; cancel/ESC/backdrop no-op CascadeDeleteModal.test.tsx (all cancel/ESC/backdrop/confirm/back tests) PASS AC-F13-7 3 scenarios: 3 rewrites, shared-overlap, double-delete T-CD-021, T-CD-016, T-CD-019b PASS

docs.title: 3.1 Dead-wikilink rewrite (I5 frontmatter integrity)
docs.text:
What the original tests cover: - T-CD-007 (test_dead_wikilink_plain_rewrite): real-filesystem test that writes ref.md, calls cascade_delete, reads back the file, and asserts [[Target]] is gone and parsed YAML keys (title, type) are correct. This is NOT mock-only. The assertion is on parsed metadata values.

What was missing: - No test asserted byte-identical frontmatter. The ADR §4.3 claims "byte-for-byte via frontmatter.dumps" but this is false — PyYAML sorts keys alphabetically on dumps.

QA addition (T-CD-024 — test_frontmatter_integrity_after_rewrite): - Writes a multi-key frontmatter file with non-alphabetical key order. - Calls cascade_delete on a real tmp_path. - Reads file back and asserts: (a) parsed key values unchanged, (b) dead links gone, (c) unrelated links untouched. - Status: PASS (semantic integrity confirmed).

QA addition (T-CD-024b — test_frontmatter_key_order_preserved_xfail): - Same setup, additionally asserts the raw frontmatter block bytes are identical. - Status: XFAIL (strict) — confirms DEFECT-F13-002. - The assert produces: original "title: Ref Order type: concept sources: - raw/sources/doc.md tags: - alpha created_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' " vs. after "created_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' sources: - raw/sources/doc.md tags: - alpha title: Ref Order type: concept ". - Note also: list item indentation changes (- item → - item). This is a second formatting drift that can cause noisy diffs in Obsidian git history.

docs.title: 3.2 Preserve-shared sources[] mutation (real filesystem)
docs.text:
What the original test covers (T-CD-005 — test_multi_source_page_in_will_preserve): - Creates concept.md with sources: [doc.md, other.md] in tmp_path. - Asserts wiki_id not in delete_point_ids (preserved = not Qdrant-deleted). - Does NOT read concept.md back to verify the sources[] prune happened on disk.

QA addition (T-CD-025 — test_preserve_shared_sources_pruned_in_file): - Same setup. - After cascade_delete, reads concept.md from disk. - Asserts: (a) raw/sources/doc.md removed from sources[], (b) raw/sources/other.md still present, (c) title and type values intact, (d) wiki_id not deleted. - Status: PASS — _prune_sources correctly writes to the real file and the prune is proven on the filesystem. - Key finding: the existing T-CD-005 was sufficient to prove Qdrant behavior but the file-level mutation was untested. T-CD-025 closes this gap and confirms the implementation is correct. - Note: T-CD-025 also exhibits DEFECT-F13-002 (key order reordering) on concept.md since _prune_sources uses the same frontmatter.dumps() path, but the semantic values are correct.

docs.title: 3.3 Method (c) not invoked on links-table-hit (I1)
docs.text:
What the original test inventory states: - T-CD-004 is listed in the inventory comment at line 20 but has no corresponding test body.

QA addition (T-CD-026 — test_method_c_not_called_on_links_table_hit): - Patches _method_c_fulltext and asserts it is not called when method (a) finds the reference. - Status: XFAIL (strict) — confirms DEFECT-F13-001. - Root cause: _build_wikilink_rewrites calls _method_c_fulltext unconditionally at every invocation. The comment in the production code says "We still run (c) to catch hand-edited files absent from the links table" which contradicts ADR-0026 §3.1: "(c) is skipped entirely when (a)∪(b) already covers the candidate set". The ADR §9 (architect-review gate) explicitly requires this as a precondition for PR approval.

docs.title: 4.1 I1 — No full-rescan
docs.text:
• Static proof: grep -n "glob\|walk\|listdir\|scandir" cascade_delete.py returns zero results. No vault walk in the production code.
  • Primary path (methods a/b): References are found exclusively via SELECT ... FROM links WHERE target_page_id = :id OR target_title = :T (method a) and slug-normalized comparison over links rows (method b). Both are pure index reads.
  • Method (c) fallback: Bounded by CASCADE_FULLTEXT_MAX_FILES=5000. When invoked, it queries the pages table (not a filesystem walk) to enumerate live wiki pages, then reads each file once. This is I1-safe by the ADR §3.1 definition.
  • DEFECT-F13-001: The fallback is invoked unconditionally, even when the links-table hit is complete. This is a behavioral deviation from ADR §3.1 (extra SQL query to enumerate live wiki pages on the happy path). Not a hard I1 violation (no vault walk; references are not computed from the walk) but violates the ADR architect-review gate.
  • Verdict on I1: Functionally correct (no full-rescan used to identify changes); ADR §3.1 happy-path gate not met.

docs.title: 4.2 I2 — data_version bumped exactly once; no inline FA2
docs.text:
• T-CD-011: _bump_version_and_notify called exactly once (mock call count = 1). PASS.
  • T-CD-012: GraphEngine constructor not called. PASS.
  • Code inspection: cascade_delete imports _graph_cache.notify_bump() (debounced) not GraphEngine.recompute(). PASS.
  • _bump_version_and_notify wraps bump + notify_bump(). No FA2 or recompute() call path. PASS.

  • T-CD-017 (test_preview_returns_200): API test asserts 200 with correct shape. PASS.
  • T-CD-020 (test_preview_404_on_unknown): 404 when page not found. PASS.

  • T-CD-010 (test_qdrant_delete_point_called): asserts delete_point called once per page in will_delete. PASS.
  • T-CD-014 (test_raw_source_file_deleted): creates real file in tmp_path/raw/sources/, asserts it does not exist after delete. PASS.
  • Double-delete (T-CD-019b): second DELETE returns 404 (idempotent). PASS.

  • Preview error: error text shown; confirm disabled. PASS.
  • Shared-entity warnings rendered BEFORE other sections (DOM order checked with compareDocumentPosition). PASS.

  • ESC key: onCancel called; cascadeDelete not called. PASS.

docs.title: DEFECT-F13-001 — P1 — I1: method (c) called on happy path
docs.text:
• Filed against: backend-engineer
  • Location: backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py, function _build_wikilink_rewrites, lines 500-503
  • Description: _method_c_fulltext is called unconditionally regardless of whether method (a) or (b) already found all references. On the happy path (links-table hit), this issues an extra SQL query to enumerate all live wiki pages from the pages table. While no file read or full vault walk occurs in this case (because already_found filters all candidates), the extra DB query violates the ADR §3.1 gate: "(c) is skipped entirely when (a)∪(b) already covers the candidate set".
  • ADR reference: ADR-0026 §3.1 and §9 (architect-review gate bullet 2).
  • Test: T-CD-026 (test_method_c_not_called_on_links_table_hit) — xfail(strict).
  • Fix: Add an early-return guard before the _method_c_fulltext call. Simplest implementation: if all_found_ids and not b_results: return rewrites, match_methods_used (skip (c) when (a) found hits and (b) found nothing new). A more complete guard would check whether all live wiki pages enumerable by the pages table are a subset of all_found_ids, but the common case (links table is up-to-date) is captured by the simpler guard.
  • Risk: Without the guard, every cascade delete issues an extra paginated SQL query. With CASCADE_FULLTEXT_MAX_FILES=5000 this can be significant on large vaults.

docs.title: DEFECT-F13-002 — P1 — I5: frontmatter key order corrupted by PyYAML sort
docs.text:
• Filed against: backend-engineer
  • Location: backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py, all uses of frontmatter.dumps() in cascade_delete() (step 4, line 667) and _prune_sources() (line 808)
  • Description: python-frontmatter uses PyYAML's default Dumper which sorts mapping keys alphabetically. This means any file rewritten by F13 has its YAML frontmatter key order changed, regardless of the author's original ordering. Observed: title, type, sources, tags, created_at → created_at, sources, tags, title, type. Also: list items lose the 2-space indent (- item → - item). Both changes are semantically equivalent but create unnecessary diffs in Obsidian's git plugin, violate the user's formatting preferences, and break any key-order-dependent tooling.
  • ADR reference: ADR-0026 §4.3: "The frontmatter block is re-emitted byte-for-byte via frontmatter.dumps". The claim is false.
  • Test: T-CD-024b (test_frontmatter_key_order_preserved_xfail) — xfail(strict); T-CD-024 (test_frontmatter_integrity_after_rewrite) — PASS (semantic values correct).
  • Fix options (in preference order):
  • Switch to python-frontmatter with ruamel.yaml handler: frontmatter.loads(raw, handler=frontmatter.YAMLHandler()) using a ruamel.yaml CommentedMap preserves key order and list formatting.
  • Alternative: read the raw frontmatter block as a string, extract and replace only the sources: key value when pruning, leaving all other keys byte-identical (body-regex approach, limited to the specific key being mutated).
  • Last resort: after frontmatter.dumps(), compare with original and only write if body actually changed; accept the key-order change as a one-time cost per file touched by F13.
  • Affected paths: Every WikilinkRewrite file write (step 4) and every _prune_sources() call (step 5). Files that are NOT touched by F13 are unaffected.

docs.title: 6. Obsidian compatibility check
docs.text: The Obsidian check (test_obsidian_check.py) was not run against a live vault in this gate (no vault data available in CI). Static check: - DEFECT-F13-002 means that after a cascade delete, any rewritten wiki file will have its YAML keys sorted. test_obsidian_check.py validates frontmatter YAML syntax (parseable), not key order. So the check would still pass syntactically but the vault will show unnecessary git diffs. - No dead [[wikilinks]] will remain after a cascade delete (T-CD-021 proves this on real files). Obsidian check gate would pass on that dimension once DEFECT-F13-001 and DEFECT-F13-002 are fixed.

docs.title: 7. Flakiness report
docs.text:
Both suites run twice with identical results. No flakes detected. The 3 pre-existing skips in pytest are unrelated to F13. The 2 xfailed tests are deterministic (strict xfail).

The 3 pre-existing warnings in pytest (RuntimeWarning: Event loop is closed in test_mcp_tools.py) are carry-forward from prior sprints and unrelated to F13.

docs.title: 8. Handoffs
docs.text: To Item backend-engineer DEFECT-F13-001: add early-return guard in _build_wikilink_rewrites before _method_c_fulltext call when all_found_ids is non-empty backend-engineer DEFECT-F13-002: replace frontmatter.dumps() with a key-order-preserving serializer (ruamel.yaml recommended) in cascade_delete step 4 and _prune_sources step 5 orchestrator Phase 4 gate: FAIL. Milestone must not advance until both defects are fixed and T-CD-024b + T-CD-026 flip from xfail to pass product-manager M5 Phase 4 is NOT signed off. DEFECT-F13-001 (I1 gate) and DEFECT-F13-002 (I5 gate) are P1 blockers

docs.text:
Three tests added to backend/tests/test_cascade_delete.py in TestQARealFilesystem:

Test ID Function Purpose Status T-CD-024 test_frontmatter_integrity_after_rewrite Real-filesystem I5 proof: semantic values correct after rewrite PASS T-CD-024b test_frontmatter_key_order_preserved_xfail I5 byte-identical gate — xfail removed after DEFECT-F13-002 fix PASS (normal test) T-CD-025 test_preserve_shared_sources_pruned_in_file Real-filesystem preserve-shared proof: sources[] pruned on disk PASS T-CD-026 test_method_c_not_called_on_links_table_hit I1 architect-review gate: method (c) skipped on happy path — xfail removed after DEFECT-F13-001 fix PASS (normal test)

docs.title: 10. Re-verification (2026-06-29)
docs.text: Re-verifier: qa-test-engineer (claude-sonnet-4-6)

docs.title: Fix confirmation — DEFECT-F13-001 (I1: method (c) skipped on happy path)
docs.text:
Location of fix: backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py, _build_wikilink_rewrites, lines 526-528.

Fix implemented: Added guard if not all_found_ids: before the _method_c_fulltext call. When method (a) and/or (b) finds at least one referencing page via the links index, all_found_ids is non-empty, the condition is False, and _method_c_fulltext is not called. When both (a) and (b) return nothing, all_found_ids is empty, the condition is True, and the full-text fallback fires as intended.

Correctness verdict: The fix is production logic, not a test-only workaround. The guard is in the production function _build_wikilink_rewrites. Method (c) still fires on the no-links-hit path (empty links table) as required by ADR-0026 §3.1 last-resort semantics.

Residual note — no-links-hit path test coverage: There is no dedicated test asserting that when (a) and (b) both return empty, method (c) does fire and its results are incorporated. The existing T-CD-026 proves the happy-path skip; the unhappy path is exercised indirectly by the session mock returning empty results on calls 4+ in several tests. This is a test-coverage gap (not a defect), filed as advisory: a future test test_method_c_fires_when_links_table_empty would complete coverage of ADR-0026 §3.1.

Test T-CD-026 result: PASS (normal test, no xfail marker). method_c_called assertion confirms _method_c_fulltext was not called on the links-hit fixture.

docs.title: Fix confirmation — DEFECT-F13-002 (I5: frontmatter byte-identical after wikilink rewrite)
docs.text:
Location of fix (two changes):

  1. backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py, new helper _rewrite_body_preserving_frontmatter (lines 181-200): Splits the raw file on ---\n fences (max 2 splits), keeps the frontmatter block byte-for-byte as the middle part, runs _rewrite_body on the body only, and reassembles. No PyYAML round-trip whatsoever. Returns None if the body is unchanged (no write needed).

  2. backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py, cascade_delete step 4 (line 689): Changed from frontmatter.loads/dumps round-trip to _rewrite_body_preserving_frontmatter(raw, plan.target_title or ""). The frontmatter block is now untouched for dead-wikilink rewrites.

  3. backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py, _prune_sources (line 836): The sources[] mutation still uses frontmatter.dumps (a PyYAML round-trip is unavoidable when mutating a structured key), but now passes sort_keys=False to PyYAML so the existing key order is respected. Key order is preserved; only the sources value changes. This is the documented minimum-diff approach for intentional sources[] mutations.

Correctness verdict: Both changes are production logic fixes. The wikilink rewrite path is now byte-identical in the frontmatter block. The _prune_sources path minimises diff to the sources[] value only (not key reordering). The ADR §4.3 claim "byte-for-byte via frontmatter.dumps" is now accurate for the rewrite path (via raw split, not PyYAML); _prune_sources intentionally mutates content so byte-identity is impossible but key order is now preserved with sort_keys=False.

Test T-CD-024b result: PASS (normal test, no xfail marker). The raw frontmatter block extracted via split("---\n", maxsplit=2)[1] is byte-identical before and after the dead-wikilink rewrite.

docs.text:
Suite Run 1 Run 2 Flakes Backend pytest 674 passed, 3 skipped, 0 xfail/xpass 674 passed, 3 skipped, 0 xfail/xpass None Frontend vitest 526 passed (28 files) 526 passed (28 files) None

The 3 pre-existing skips in pytest are carry-forward from prior sprints, unrelated to F13. The 5 warnings in run 1 (aiosqlite event-loop teardown, FastMCP coroutine, act() warnings in React tests) are identical carry-forwards from prior sprints; none are new and none indicate test failures.

docs.text:
PASS — M5 Phase 4 (F13 Cascade Deletion) SIGNED OFF.

Both P1 defects are closed. All acceptance criteria AC-F13-1..7 are met. Full test suites green twice with no flakes. Static analysis clean across all layers. The ADR-0026 §3.1 and §4.3 invariants are now correctly enforced in production code, not just tested around.

grep confirms no apiMcp.comingSoon key PASS AC-F1-MCP-UI-7 I9: no second MCP server, no tool call, no logic duplication Code inspection: handler calls only await _mcp_server.list_tools(), no DB/Qdrant, no tool execution; vitest: 4 tools rendered, copy button present test_mcp_info_*.py (14 tests, all pass); SettingsPanel.test.tsx §11 (copy button, tool names) PASS

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
The get_mcp_info() handler at line 1626 of backend/app/main.py: - Calls await _mcp_server.list_tools() to source the tool list; builds McpToolInfo from t.name, t.description, t.parameters — no string literal for any tool name, description, or schema. - Returns server_name=_mcp_server.name — derived from the live FastMCP object. - Returns transport=settings.mcp_transport — from app/config.py, env var MCP_TRANSPORT, default "stdio". - Returns entry_point_command=settings.mcp_entry_command — from app/config.py, env var MCP_ENTRY_COMMAND, default "python -m app.mcp.server". - No DB query (get_session is not called). No Qdrant call. No MCP stdio transport opened.

Test test_mcp_info_tools_match_live_registry specifically verifies that the returned tool names equal exactly {t.name for t in await mcp.list_tools()} — a dynamic check that would fail if the handler returned a hardcoded list.

Tests test_mcp_info_entry_point_command_from_settings and test_mcp_info_transport_from_settings assert the response values equal settings.mcp_entry_command and settings.mcp_transport respectively.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
SectionApiMcp in frontend/src/components/settings/SettingsPanel.tsx: - Calls fetchMcpInfo(signal) on mount; renders from info.tools list exclusively — no JSX literal for any tool name or description. - buildClaudeDesktopSnippet(info) tokenises info.entry_point_command and keys the snippet by info.server_name — both from the API response. - No Zustand store created for this panel (local useState only, mirrors SectionEmbeddings).

The mcpClient.test.ts suite includes a dedicated describe block ("Claude Desktop config snippet — tokenisation contract") with 6 tests verifying that the snippet is derived from the payload and not from hardcoded strings (including a test with a custom server_name: "custom-server" that asserts the hardcoded key "synapse" does NOT appear).

docs.title: 4. Read-Only Confirmation (Do-NOT list, ADR-0027 §4)
docs.text: Do-NOT Verified clean No hardcoded tool list backend or frontend Confirmed (§3 above) No hardcoded server name / transport / entry command in handler Confirmed (§3 above) No MCP tool invocation from UI Confirmed — frontend makes one fetch() call to GET /mcp/info only; no calls to search_wiki/write_page/get_page/list_pages No config-write / CRUD Confirmed — panel is display-only; no PUT/POST/DELETE calls No second MCP server or server.py duplication Confirmed — handler imports _mcp_server from app.mcp.server and introspects it No DB table or migration Confirmed — no new SQLAlchemy model, no Alembic migration, D2 unchanged No Zustand store for the panel Confirmed — SectionApiMcp uses useState only

docs.text:
docs/api/openapi.json contains: - Path /mcp/info with operationId: get_mcp_info_mcp_info_get - Response schema McpInfoResponse with all 5 fields (server_name, transport, entry_point_command, tool_count, tools) - Schema McpToolInfo with 3 fields (name, description, input_schema)

Zero drift confirmed. D2 is unchanged (no schema migration for this feature).

docs.title: NOTE-1 — D5 Screenshot Not Yet Captured (AC-F1-MCP-UI-9)
docs.text:
The Playwright spec frontend/e2e/shell-m5-phase5-mcp-ui.spec.ts was created and defines the capture contract for docs/screens/settings-api-mcp.png. The spec cannot execute in this CI-headless session (no running backend/frontend servers). The screenshot must be captured in the next make screenshots run with the live stack.

Resolution path: run cd frontend && npx playwright test e2e/shell-m5-phase5-mcp-ui.spec.ts against the live stack, then commit docs/screens/settings-api-mcp.png. AC-F1-MCP-UI-9 and EC-M5-22 remain open until the PNG is committed.

This is a documentation gap only. The backend endpoint, frontend panel, and all unit/integration tests are green. The UI panel itself is functional and verified by the vitest suite.

docs.title: Sprint 5 — v0.5 — PM Scope Lock
docs.text: Milestone: M5 — "Feature parity core" Author: product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Date: 2026-06-29 Gate: Sprint start — formal scope confirmation Branch: sprint/v0.5 (cut from sprint/v0.4 after EC-M4-HCP cleared) Prerequisite: M4 CLOSED — docs/sprints/v0.4-m4-closure.md (EC-M4-HCP cleared 2026-06-29; v0.4.0 tagged and released; all 4 gate sign-offs on record) Inputs: CLAUDE.md §3/§4/§7/§8/§9, BACKLOG.md §Sprint 5, docs/sprints/v0.4-m4-closure.md

docs.title: 1. Sprint Objective
docs.text:
Deliver "feature parity core" — the set of features that transform Synapse from a usable prototype into a system that can handle real-world research workflows. The headline theme is depth over breadth: F5 retrieval closes the chat-quality loop; F10 Deep Research adds autonomous web-sourcing; F9 HITL review surfaces AI outputs for human curation; F12 multi-format ingest removes the .txt/.md constraint; F13 cascade deletion makes the vault self-healing.

The headline invariant for M5 is I7 (bounded loops). F10 Deep Research is the most loop-intensive feature in the entire roadmap — its multi-query SearXNG loop has explicit max_iter and token_budget caps, an assess-sufficiency gate before each query, and a hardcoded concurrency ceiling of 3. Any PR that introduces an unbounded loop anywhere in M5 work is a P0 blocker.

The headline reuse rule for M5 is I9: SearXNG is the only permitted web-search backend. Tavily must never be introduced. Qdrant + bge-m3 are the embedding stack. The existing OllamaProvider / ApiProvider / CliAgentProvider must not be bypassed — all new inference calls go through InferenceProvider.

docs.title: 2. Locked Feature IDs for v0.5
docs.text: Locked scope — exactly the following feature IDs. No other work is in scope. Any proposal outside this list must be escalated to the orchestrator and explicitly approved before a single line of code is written.

docs.title: 2a. Primary M5 features (per CLAUDE.md §8)
docs.text: Feature ID Description Key invariants F5 4-phase retrieval: tokenized keyword → graph-expansion → token-budget assembly → context assembly; [n] citation markers attached to retrieved passages I1 (no rescan), I3 (no per-token work), I9 (reuse Qdrant + bge-m3) F9 Async HITL review queue: actions Create / Deep-Research / Skip; pre-generated queries per reviewed item; async queue management; K8 "human curates" principle I7 (queue is bounded), I6 (review calls go through InferenceProvider) F10 Deep Research loop: SearXNG multi-query → fetch → assess sufficiency → refine → synthesize → auto-ingest; concurrency=3; I9: SearXNG only I7 (max_iter + token_budget; concurrency ≤3), I9 (SearXNG; never Tavily) F12 Multi-format ingest: PDF (pypdf), DOCX (python-docx), PPTX (python-pptx), XLSX (openpyxl), images (unstructured), AV; extracted text fed into existing ingest pipeline I1 (incremental), I5 (raw/ layer preserved), I6 (InferenceProvider not bypassed) F13 Cascade deletion: 3-method matching (exact title, slug, wikilink), preserve shared entities, cleanup index.md + dead wikilinks in vault/wiki/ I1 (targeted deletions only; no rescan), I5 (vault integrity preserved)

docs.title: 2b. Documentation deliverables (per CLAUDE.md §9)
docs.text: Artifact ID Description From sprint D3 (update) New sequence diagrams: docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd and docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd; both required before M5 sign-off v0.5 per §9 D5 (update) Playwright screenshots refreshed for M5 UI surfaces: HITL review queue panel, Deep Research trigger + results, multi-format file upload, cascade delete confirmation v0.5

docs.text:
Locked feature IDs for M5:

Primary: F5, F9, F10, F12, F13

Documentation: D3-update (deep-research.mmd + cascade-delete.mmd), D5-update

M4 carry-forward (AC-level): F6 AC-F6-3, F6 AC-F6-5, F17 CliAgentProvider.chat()

Total: 5 primary features + 2 doc artifacts + 3 AC carry-forwards + 7 nits = 17 items.

Locked: 2026-06-29 by product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6).

Amendment A1 — MCP Configuration UI (F1-MCP-UI) Amended: 2026-06-29 Requested by: Emanuele Chiummo (developer / stakeholder — explicit request) Authorised by: product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Amendment type: stakeholder-requested scope addition; formally recorded per anti-scope-creep gate rules.

Rationale: The Settings > API + MCP section currently shows a "coming in M5" placeholder (i18n key settings.apiMcp.comingSoon, stub SectionApiMcp in SettingsPanel.tsx) — a promise made during M4-HARD that was never placed in the M5 scope-lock. This amendment closes the gap by making the promise real. The MCP server itself (4 tools: search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages; stdio transport; entry point python -m app.mcp.server) was fully delivered in M2 (v0.2). F1-MCP-UI surfaces the existing server — it does NOT add new MCP capability. A lightweight GET /mcp/info backend endpoint is required to provide server metadata to the UI at runtime (server name, transport, entry-point command, tool list with schemas). This is the only new backend work; it is read-only and additive.

Scope addition: F1-MCP-UI (sub-item of F1 + F17; extension of existing Settings shell) Phase placement: new Phase 5 (after F13/Phase 4). See §7 amendment note. New EC-M5-HCP condition: EC-M5-HCP-7 added to §9.

Updated total: 5 primary features + F1-MCP-UI + 2 doc artifacts + 3 AC carry-forwards + 7 nits = 18 items.

docs.title: F5 — 4-phase RAG retrieval
docs.text:
• AC-F5-1: backend/app/rag/retrieval.py implements all 4 phases in sequence: (1) tokenized keyword search over Qdrant (bge-m3 vectors + BM25 hybrid if available); (2) graph-expansion: for each top-k result, follow edges in the graph (links + shared-source) up to depth 1 or 2; (3) token-budget allocation per F14 20%-retrieved-context slot; (4) context assembly — passages ranked by relevance, truncated to budget, returned as RetrievalContext with page refs.
  • AC-F5-2: Each retrieved passage carries a citation index n (1-based). The assembled context string includes [n] markers inline. The RetrievalContext object maps each n to a PageRef (id, title, url-friendly slug).
  • AC-F5-3: The retrieval pipeline calls only Qdrant (bge-m3) and reads from the Postgres links and edges tables — no new embedding service introduced (I9). Static import check confirms no direct bge-m3 or Ollama embedding calls outside the existing Qdrant wrapper.
  • AC-F5-4: The token budget for retrieved context (20% of configured window per F14) is respected. If candidate passages exceed the budget, the lowest-ranked passages are dropped. Unit test: 10 fixture passages totaling 2× budget → assert assembled context ≤ budget.
  • AC-F5-5: Graph-expansion phase uses the existing edges table (Postgres) — does NOT trigger a graph recompute or FA2 run (I2 preserved). Verified: data_version is unchanged after a retrieval call.
  • AC-F5-6: GET /search?q=<query>&vault_id=<id> endpoint returns the top retrieval results with citation indices. Response documented in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate).
  • AC-F5-7: Unit tests cover: 0-result query, single-result, multi-page expansion, budget overflow truncation. Integration test: seed 5 pages with known wikilinks → retrieval returns linked pages in expansion phase.
  • AC-F5-8: After F5 ships, the chat endpoint passes the assembled RetrievalContext to the active provider's chat() call so that [n] markers appear in assistant messages (unblocks F6 AC-F6-3 and F17 CliAgentProvider.chat()).

docs.title: F6 (AC-F6-3 + AC-F6-5) — Citations and save-to-wiki
docs.text:
• AC-F6-3: (Carry-forward unblocked by F5) Each assistant message in the chat UI displays [n] citation markers that link to source pages. Citation data is stored with the message in the messages table (JSONB citations field). On render, [n] is displayed as a clickable superscript that shows the page title on hover.
  • AC-F6-5: (Carry-forward) The "save to wiki" button on an assistant message is enabled and wired. Clicking it calls POST /ingest with the message content as source text. On 202, an inline status shows the resulting page title and wikilink. vitest test: mock POST /ingest → assert status rendered.

docs.title: F17 — CliAgentProvider.chat()
docs.text:
• AC-F17-CHAT-1: CliAgentProvider.chat(messages, retrieval_context) is implemented (not NotImplementedError). The method delegates the chat turn to the claude-agent-sdk subprocess with the assembled RetrievalContext injected as a system tool or context message. The agent loop is bounded by max_iter and token_budget per I7.
  • AC-F17-CHAT-2: The streaming interface is consistent with OllamaProvider and ApiProvider: the method yields token chunks; the chat endpoint SSE stream is unchanged.
  • AC-F17-CHAT-3: Unit test: mock claude-agent-sdk output → verify stream chunks emitted, total_cost_usd logged correctly (SDK metadata or $0 convention per NB-4).

docs.title: F9 — Async HITL review queue
docs.text:
• AC-F9-1: backend/app/ops/review.py implements an async review queue backed by a new Postgres table review_items. Columns: id (uuid), vault_id (uuid FK), page_id (uuid FK → pages, nullable), item_type (enum: new_page / update_page / deep_research_candidate), status (enum: pending / approved / skipped / deep_researched), pre_generated_query (text nullable), created_at, reviewed_at (nullable), reviewed_by (text nullable).
  • AC-F9-2: The ingest pipeline enqueues a review_item for each newly generated wiki page (item_type=new_page). This is non-blocking: the page is written to vault/wiki/ immediately (existing behaviour); the review queue entry is advisory.
  • AC-F9-3: REST endpoints:
  • GET /review/queue — list pending items (paginated, vault_id filter);
  • POST /review/queue/{id}/approve — sets status=approved, reviewed_at=now();
  • POST /review/queue/{id}/skip — sets status=skipped;
  • POST /review/queue/{id}/deep-research — sets status=deep_researched and triggers F10 deep research for the item's pre_generated_query (or the page topic if query absent). All endpoints documented in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate).
  • AC-F9-4: Pre-generated queries: when a review item is created, the ingest pipeline calls the active InferenceProvider to generate 1–3 research questions for the new page. These are stored in pre_generated_query (newline-separated). Bounded: max 1 generation call per item; if it fails or times out, query is left null (I7). The generation call goes through InferenceProvider (I6).
  • AC-F9-5: Review queue UI: a "Review" nav section (previously showing "coming in M5" placeholder) is activated. It renders the pending queue as a list with: page title / item type / pre-generated query (if present) / action buttons (Approve, Skip, Deep-Research). List is virtualized (TanStack Virtual) if >50 items (I4).
  • AC-F9-6: The review queue is NOT a blocker for normal operation. Pages are already written to vault/wiki/ when they appear in the queue. Approve = human confirmation only; does not re-trigger ingest. Skip = remove from queue. Deep-Research = trigger F10 and remove from queue.
  • AC-F9-7: Boundary from M4 Ingest View: the F1-INGEST-VIEW (ingest runs list + Run Ingest button) is not modified. F9 review queue is a separate section on the nav rail, not a modification of the Ingest section.
  • AC-F9-8: Integration test: seed 2 review_items; call approve on 1, skip on 1; assert both statuses updated; assert deep-research endpoint triggers F10 stub call.

docs.title: F10 — Deep Research loop
docs.text:
• AC-F10-1: backend/app/ops/deep_research.py implements the full loop: (1) generate 2–5 SearXNG search queries from the research topic (via InferenceProvider); (2) execute queries against SearXNG (I9: never any other search backend); concurrency=3 (asyncio gather with semaphore); (3) fetch and parse result pages (html → markdown); (4) assess sufficiency: call InferenceProvider to judge whether collected content answers the research topic; if yes → proceed to synthesize; if no → generate follow-up queries (up to max_iter); (5) synthesize: call InferenceProvider to produce a synthesis document; (6) auto-ingest: feed synthesis into the ingest pipeline (not directly into vault/wiki/; must go through the orchestrated loop to satisfy I6).
  • AC-F10-2 (I7 — headline invariant): Every deep research run is strictly bounded: max_iter (configurable, default 3 refinement rounds), token_budget (configurable, default 100K tokens), max_queries_per_iter (default 5), concurrency (hardcoded 3, not configurable beyond 3 without architect approval). total_cost_usd logged per run in a new deep_research_runs table. Any PR that removes or weakens these bounds is a P0 blocker.
  • AC-F10-3 (I9): SearXNG is the only web search backend. The SearXNG base URL is read from SEARXNG_URL env var (already in the stack). No Tavily, DuckDuckGo, or Google API may be introduced. Static import test: no tavily, ddg, or google package imports anywhere in the codebase.
  • AC-F10-4: The deep research run is triggered via:
  • POST /research/start — body: {vault_id, topic, max_iter?, token_budget?}; returns HTTP 202 with {run_id: uuid};
  • GET /research/runs — list of past runs with status + cost;
  • GET /research/runs/{id} — details of a run including queries used, sources fetched, synthesis text. All endpoints in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate).
  • AC-F10-5: F9 integration: when F9's "Deep-Research" action fires, it calls POST /research/start with the pre_generated_query as topic. The run_id is stored on the review_item row for traceability.
  • AC-F10-6: New Postgres tables: deep_research_runs (id, vault_id, topic, status, queries used JSONB, sources_fetched int, total_cost_usd, started_at, completed_at, error_message) and deep_research_sources (id, run_id, url, title, fetched_content_md, relevance_score). Alembic migration. D2 regenerated via make er.
  • AC-F10-7: Unit tests: mock SearXNG returning 3 results → assert 3 fetch calls; mock InferenceProvider returning "sufficient" on first assess → assert loop stops after 1 round; mock InferenceProvider returning "insufficient" for max_iter rounds → assert loop stops at max_iter, status="max_iter_reached".
  • AC-F10-8: Playwright E2E: navigate to "Deep Search" nav section (previously placeholder); enter a topic; assert POST /research/start fires; assert run appears in runs list.

docs.title: F12 — Multi-format ingest
docs.text:
• AC-F12-1: The backend ingest pipeline accepts the following additional file types at POST /ingest/upload (and via the watcher on vault/raw/sources/):
  • PDF (.pdf): text extracted via pypdf; images in PDF are skipped with a log warning.
  • DOCX (.docx): text + basic structure via python-docx.
  • PPTX (.pptx): slide text via python-pptx (one logical document per presentation).
  • XLSX (.xlsx): cell text (sheet name + row data as markdown table) via openpyxl.
  • Images (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp): OCR via unstructured (if available) or "image file: no text extracted" placeholder if unstructured not installed.
  • Audio/Video (.mp3, .mp4, .wav, .m4a): "AV file: transcript not available in this release" placeholder (full transcription is out of scope). The extracted text is passed into the existing analyze()/generate() ingest loop unchanged — no new LLM calls are added for extraction.
  • AC-F12-2: The server-side file-type gate in POST /ingest/upload is extended: the HTTP 415 rejection now only applies to file types with no extractor at all. The accepted MIME type list is updated to include PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and image MIME types. The F1-UPLOAD client-side accept attribute is updated to match.
  • AC-F12-3 (I1): Uploading the same multi-format file twice (same mtime/hash) does not create a duplicate Postgres row or Qdrant vector. The existing content-hash check covers this regardless of file type.
  • AC-F12-4 (I5): Extracted text for multi-format files is stored in vault/raw/sources/ as a companion .extracted.md file alongside the original binary. The original binary is preserved. vault/wiki/ is only written by the ingest pipeline. K1 3-layer rule intact.
  • AC-F12-5: New Python dependencies (pypdf, python-docx, python-pptx, openpyxl, unstructured) are added to backend/pyproject.toml. Docker image rebuilds cleanly. All new dependencies are pinned versions.
  • AC-F12-6: Unit tests: fixture PDF with 2 pages → assert extracted text contains content from both pages; fixture DOCX → assert paragraphs extracted; fixture XLSX → assert sheet rows present as markdown table; unsupported file type → HTTP 415.
  • AC-F12-7: The format extractor is in a new module backend/app/ingest/extract.py behind a dispatch function extract_text(file_path) -> str. Static guard: no format-specific imports (pypdf, docx, pptx, openpyxl) outside ingest/extract.py.

docs.title: F13 — Cascade deletion
docs.text:
• AC-F13-1: backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py implements cascade_delete(page_id): (1) identify the target page in Postgres; (2) find all wikilinks pointing TO this page (links table, target_title match); (3) remove the target page's Postgres row (soft delete: deleted_at = now()); (4) remove the Qdrant vector for the page; (5) remove the page's entry from vault/wiki/index.md; (6) scan all pages in vault/wiki/ that contain [[<target_title>]] wikilinks and replace them with <target_title> (dead wikilink becomes plain text); (7) update the dangling=True flag on affected links table rows.
  • AC-F13-2: 3-method matching for finding references: (a) exact wikilink title match in links table; (b) slug-normalised match (lower-case, hyphenated); (c) full-text scan of wiki/ .md files as fallback. Method used is logged per deletion.
  • AC-F13-3: Shared entities are preserved: if a page being deleted has content that is also referenced by other pages (via source overlap in the edges table), the deletion logs a warning listing the sharing pages and proceeds — it does NOT block. The human operator is responsible for reviewing the warning.
  • AC-F13-4 (I1): Cascade delete only touches records related to the deleted page. It NEVER triggers a full vault rescan or graph recompute (data_version IS bumped so the graph eventually updates via the debounce, but no immediate FA2 run is forced).
  • AC-F13-5: REST endpoint: DELETE /pages/{id} triggers cascade_delete(page_id). Response: HTTP 200 with {deleted_page_id, wikilinks_cleaned int, index_entry_removed bool, shared_entity_warnings list[str]}. Documented in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate).
  • AC-F13-6: A "Delete page" action is available in the wiki page view (frontend). It shows a confirmation modal listing any shared-entity warnings before executing. On confirm, calls DELETE /pages/{id}; on success, navigates back to the page tree.
  • AC-F13-7: Unit tests: delete a page with 3 incoming wikilinks → assert all 3 cleaned; delete a page with shared source overlap → assert warning logged, deletion completes; double-delete (page already deleted) → assert HTTP 404 gracefully.

docs.title: F1-MCP-UI — MCP Configuration UI (Amendment A1, 2026-06-29)
docs.text:
Stakeholder request: Emanuele Chiummo. Surfaces the existing MCP server (v0.2/M2) in the Settings > API + MCP section. Does NOT add new MCP capability (I9 — do not reinvent).

  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-1 (backend — GET /mcp/info): A new read-only endpoint GET /mcp/info is added to FastAPI. It returns a static JSON object derived from the running FastMCP server instance (app.mcp.server.mcp): {server_name, transport, entry_point_command, tool_count, tools: [{name, description, input_schema}]}. The response is static (no DB query, no live MCP call) — it reflects the code-level tool registry at import time. The tool list and schemas must be sourced from the REAL FastMCP tool registry (not a hardcoded string array). Endpoint documented in openapi.json (D4 zero-drift gate).
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-2 (backend — no hardcoded values): The server name, entry-point command, and tool descriptions shown in the response MUST be derived from the live mcp object and settings (e.g., settings.mcp_transport or a new MCP_TRANSPORT env var, default "stdio"). No string values about the MCP server may be hardcoded in the endpoint handler.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-3 (UI — stub replaced): The SectionApiMcp function in frontend/src/components/settings/SettingsPanel.tsx no longer renders ComingSoonBadge. It fetches GET /mcp/info on mount and renders the real data. If the fetch fails, a degraded state message is shown (not a crash).
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-4 (UI — connection panel): The panel displays a clearly labelled "Connection" sub-section showing: transport type (e.g., "stdio"), entry-point command (e.g., python -m app.mcp.server), and a copy-to-clipboard button for the Claude Desktop JSON config snippet: {"mcpServers": {"synapse": {"command": "python", "args": ["-m", "app.mcp.server"]}}}. The snippet is generated from the server metadata returned by GET /mcp/info — not a static literal in the JSX.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-5 (UI — tools list): The panel renders an expandable list of all tools exposed by the server. Each row shows: tool name, description (first sentence truncated to 80 chars), and the count of input parameters. The list is sourced exclusively from the tools array returned by GET /mcp/info (the REAL server registry, currently search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages). No tool names or descriptions are hardcoded in the frontend.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-6 (i18n — EN + IT parity): All UI strings for the MCP panel are in i18n keys under settings.apiMcp.* (EN: frontend/src/i18n/locales/en.json; IT: frontend/src/i18n/locales/it.json). The settings.apiMcp.comingSoon key is replaced (or the badge removed); new keys added as needed. EN and IT translation files must have identical key sets — no missing keys in either locale.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-7 (I9 — no reinvention): The UI does NOT implement a new MCP server, does not call MCP tool endpoints directly, and does not duplicate the tool logic from backend/app/mcp/server.py. It is purely a read-only display of the existing server's metadata. Any proposal to add CRUD, tool invocation, or configuration-write capability to this panel is out of scope and must be escalated.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-8 (D4 update): GET /mcp/info is present in openapi.json after make openapi regeneration. Zero drift confirmed.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-9 (D5 screenshot): docs/screens/settings-api-mcp.png — Playwright captures the Settings > API + MCP section showing the connection panel and tools list with at least 1 tool visible. Captured as part of make screenshots.
  • AC-F1-MCP-UI-10 (tests): vitest test for the MCP panel component: mock GET /mcp/info returning a 4-tool response → assert all 4 tool names rendered; assert copy-to-clipboard button present. pytest unit test: GET /mcp/info returns HTTP 200; tool list length >= 4; server_name equals "synapse"; entry_point_command is non-empty.

docs.title: D3 (update) — Sequence diagrams: deep-research and cascade-delete
docs.text:
• AC-D3-DR-1: docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd — new Mermaid sequenceDiagram. Must show: trigger → query generation (InferenceProvider) → SearXNG multi-query (concurrency=3) → fetch+parse → assess sufficiency (InferenceProvider) → [sufficient? yes: synthesize+ingest; no: refine queries → loop, bounded by max_iter] → total_cost_usd logged. Annotate the max_iter and concurrency bounds explicitly.
  • AC-D3-CD-1: docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd — new Mermaid sequenceDiagram. Must show: DELETE /pages/{id} → find incoming wikilinks (3-method) → soft-delete Postgres row → remove Qdrant vector → clean dead wikilinks in wiki/ files → remove from index.md → bump data_version → return warnings. Annotate shared-entity warning path.
  • AC-D3-CI-1: Both new diagrams pass the CI mmdc render check (NB-6 CI step already wired from M4; both files are covered by the existing mmdc -i docs/sequences/*.mmd loop).
  • AC-D3-REV-1: Both diagrams reviewed and approved by architect and tech-writer before M5 sign-off. Consistent with actual implementation.

docs.title: D5 (update) — Screenshots refreshed for M5 UI surfaces
docs.text:
• AC-D5-M5-1: Playwright captures PNG screenshots committed to docs/screens/:
  • docs/screens/review-queue.png — F9 HITL review queue with at least 1 pending item.
  • docs/screens/deep-search-trigger.png — Deep Research section with topic input.
  • docs/screens/upload-multiformat.png — Ingest upload zone with multi-format indicator.
  • docs/screens/cascade-delete-modal.png — Delete confirmation modal with warning.
  • AC-D5-M5-2: CF-HARD-1 recapture: docs/screens/shell-collapsed-panel.png recaptured using make screenshots (root cause fixed in M4-HARD; this is the capture task only).
  • AC-D5-M5-3: make screenshots target runs all Playwright capture tests in one command and exits 0.

docs.title: 5. Explicitly Out-of-Scope Items (Anti-Scope-Creep Gate)
docs.text:
The following items are deferred to M6 or later. Any engineer proposing this work must escalate to the orchestrator before a single line of code is written. The PM will block.

Deferred to Feature IDs Reason M6 (v0.6) F2 — purpose.md injection as provider context M6 per CLAUDE.md §8 M6 (v0.6) F11 — Chrome MV3 web clipper M6 per CLAUDE.md §8 M6 (v0.6) F15-rest — PWA manifest, Tauri v2, full CI/CD M6 per CLAUDE.md §8 M6 (v0.6) K2 lint op — lint-fix loop M6 per CLAUDE.md §8 M6 (v0.6) Full MkDocs site Optional M6 deliverable M6 only (PM decision) F1-MCP-UI MCP tool invocation / CRUD / config-write Out of scope for MCP UI panel; read-only display only in M5 M6 (v0.6) D6 final completion (USER.md + DEPLOY.md v1.0) Final docs are M6 gate Never Force-directed layout on UI main thread Violates I2 Never Per-token Markdown/LaTeX parse in chat Violates I3 Never Tavily or any web-search backend other than SearXNG Violates I9 Never Hardcoded inference provider or model ID Violates I6 Never Unbounded loops (no max_iter or token_budget) Violates I7 Never Full vault rescan triggered by any operation Violates I1

docs.title: 6. Invariants in Force — I7 and I9 Are First-Class This Sprint
docs.text: All 9 invariants apply. I7 and I9 are the headline invariants for M5.

docs.title: I7 — LOOPS ARE BOUNDED (headline for M5)
docs.text:
F10 Deep Research is the most loop-intensive feature in the roadmap. Every component of I7 must be demonstrably satisfied in all M5 loops:

  1. deep_research.py: max_iter + token_budget + concurrency ≤ 3. No exceptions.
  2. review.py: pre_generated_query generation is bounded to 1 call per item with timeout.
  3. F9 Deep-Research action: delegates to deep_research.py — inherits its bounds.
  4. F17 CliAgentProvider.chat(): the delegated agent loop must have max_iter + token_budget.
  5. F12 extraction: no loop (stateless per-file extraction). Not in scope for I7.
  6. F13 cascade_delete: single pass (not a loop). data_version bump triggers the existing debounced recompute — already bounded by GraphCache (from v0.3).
  7. Every new run (deep_research_runs, any future loop log) must log total_cost_usd. Anomaly threshold per ADR-0009: flag if > $1.00 per run.

Violation of I7 in any M5 feature is a P0 blocker. QA must include a specific I7 test for F10: mock InferenceProvider always returns "insufficient" → assert loop terminates at max_iter, status = "max_iter_reached", no further calls made.

docs.title: I9 — DO NOT REINVENT (headline for M5)
docs.text:
SearXNG is already running on the developer's TrueNAS stack. F10 must reuse it.

  1. F10: SearXNG base URL from SEARXNG_URL env var. Never Tavily, DuckDuckGo, or Google.
  2. F5: Qdrant + bge-m3 already running. No new embedding service. No new vector DB.
  3. F12: pypdf / python-docx / python-pptx / openpyxl / unstructured are extractors, not inference providers. They must not make LLM calls directly — all inference goes through InferenceProvider (I6 also applies).
  4. F13: Uses existing Postgres links and pages tables. No new data structure.

docs.title: I1 — INCREMENTAL INDEX ONLY
docs.text: F13 (cascade delete) is the highest I1 risk this sprint: it modifies vault/wiki/ files. The implementation MUST: - Only touch files that reference the deleted page (not all files). - Not trigger a full watcher rescan. - Use targeted file updates (read → modify → write specific files only).

docs.title: I6 — PLUGGABLE INFERENCE
docs.text: F10 Deep Research uses InferenceProvider for (a) query generation, (b) sufficiency assessment, (c) synthesis. All three calls go through the active provider's methods. No direct Ollama/Anthropic/claude-agent-sdk calls in deep_research.py — these must route through the existing InferenceProvider abstraction. Static guard T-CQ-009 remains.

docs.title: I5 — OBSIDIAN COMPATIBILITY
docs.text: F12 and F13 both touch vault/wiki/ or vault/raw/. Verify: - F12: binary files go to vault/raw/sources/; extracted .md files also in vault/raw/; wiki/ is only written by the ingest pipeline. No broken frontmatter introduced. - F13: dead wikilinks cleaned in wiki/ files must not break YAML frontmatter. - test_obsidian_check.py (15 tests) must remain green after M5.

docs.title: I2 — GRAPH LAYOUT IS SERVER-SIDE AND CACHED
docs.text: F5 graph-expansion uses the Postgres edges table only — it does not trigger FA2. F13 data_version bump triggers the debounced recompute — already server-side (no change). No new client-side graph layout code may be introduced in any M5 PR.

docs.title: I3 — NO PER-TOKEN HEAVY WORK IN CHAT
docs.text: F5 retrieval results are assembled server-side and passed to the provider as a pre-built context string. No retrieval happens per-token during chat streaming. The Zustand store pattern from M4 is unchanged.

docs.title: 7. Dependency Graph and Phased Delivery Plan
docs.text: M5 is the largest sprint by feature count in the roadmap. Emanuele works solo, evenings only. The phased delivery plan ensures each phase passes through the full gate chain (QA + Architect + Tech-Writer + PM) independently so the sprint does not become a monolithic delivery.

docs.title: Dependency analysis
docs.text:
F5 (retrieval) ──────────────────────────────► F6 AC-F6-3 (citations populated)
    │                                            F6 AC-F6-5 (save-to-wiki enabled)
    └──────────────────────────────────────────► F17 CliAgentProvider.chat()

F10 (deep research) ─────────────────────────► F9 AC-F9-3 (Deep-Research action)
    └─ requires SearXNG env var (already set) ► D3 deep-research.mmd

F12 (multi-format) ─── independent ─────────► D5-upload screenshot
F13 (cascade delete) ── independent ─────────► D3 cascade-delete.mmd
                                               D5-cascade screenshot
F9 ─── depends on F10 for Deep-Research action; independent of F5/F12/F13

Key dependencies: 1. F5 must ship before F6 AC-F6-3/AC-F6-5 and F17 CliAgentProvider.chat() can be completed. 2. F10 must ship (at least stub-level) before F9 can wire the Deep-Research action. 3. F12 and F13 are fully independent of each other and of F5/F9/F10. 4. D3 diagrams depend on F10 (deep-research.mmd) and F13 (cascade-delete.mmd) being implemented; diagrams are written in parallel with late-phase implementation.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Retrieval foundation + carry-forward resolution
docs.text:
(F5, F6 AC-F6-3, F6 AC-F6-5, F17 CliAgentProvider.chat())

Duration: 3–4 evenings

Rationale: F5 is the dependency root. Shipping it first unblocks the 3 M4 carry-forwards which are otherwise empty stubs visible to the user (citations blank, save-to-wiki disabled, CliAgentProvider chat fails). This phase delivers immediate user-visible improvement to the chat feature.

Work: - Implement backend/app/rag/retrieval.py: 4-phase pipeline; [n] citation markers; RetrievalContext Pydantic model; GET /search endpoint. - Wire retrieval context into chat endpoint: pass RetrievalContext to provider.chat(messages, retrieval_context) for all 3 backends. - Implement CliAgentProvider.chat(): delegate to claude-agent-sdk with context injected; bounded loop; streaming interface consistent with other providers. - Enable F6 AC-F6-3: citation markers appear in assistant messages; stored with message. - Enable F6 AC-F6-5: wire save-to-wiki button to POST /ingest; show inline result. - Quick-win nits: CF-HARD-1, NB-7, NB-8, NB-9, AC-HARD-SET-5, GAP-HARD-1/GAP-HARD-5. - D4 update: GET /search endpoint in openapi.json.

Exit condition for Phase 1: F5 retrieval returns cited results in chat; CliAgentProvider chat works end-to-end; save-to-wiki button enabled; all nits disposed; AC-F5-1..8, AC-F6-3, AC-F6-5, AC-F17-CHAT-1..3, AC-BE-SEARCH-1 green; test suite green.

docs.title: Phase 2 — Deep Research (F10) + D3 deep-research diagram
docs.text:
Duration: 3–4 evenings

Rationale: F10 is the most complex and loop-intensive feature; it needs its own phase with a full gate pass before F9's Deep-Research action can be wired. Building it in isolation makes the I7 compliance review easier and prevents F9 complexity from contaminating the F10 implementation.

Work: - Implement backend/app/ops/deep_research.py with the full bounded loop: query generation → SearXNG → fetch → assess → refine (bounded) → synthesize → auto-ingest. - New Postgres tables: deep_research_runs, deep_research_sources (Alembic migration). - REST endpoints: POST /research/start, GET /research/runs, GET /research/runs/{id}. - "Deep Search" nav section activated in the UI (previously placeholder): topic input, start button, runs list with status + cost. - D3 update: docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd — diagram written in parallel with implementation; reviewed by architect + tech-writer before phase exit. - D4 update: research endpoints in openapi.json. - D2 update: make er regenerated with new tables.

Exit condition for Phase 2: F10 deep research loop runs end-to-end against live SearXNG (or mock); I7 max_iter stop verified by test; AC-F10-1..8, AC-D3-DR-1, AC-D3-CI-1 green; test suite green; D2/D4 zero drift confirmed; architect + tech-writer reviewed.

docs.title: Phase 3 — HITL Review Queue (F9) + multi-format ingest (F12)
docs.text:
Duration: 3–4 evenings

Rationale: F9 depends on F10 (Deep-Research action delegates to Phase 2's endpoint). F12 is independent and can be done in the same phase to keep the phase size manageable — both are primarily backend work with modest frontend additions. No inter-dependency between F9 and F12 within the phase.

Work: - Implement backend/app/ops/review.py: async queue with review_items table (Alembic migration); enqueue on ingest; pre-generated query generation (bounded 1 call/item). - REST endpoints: GET /review/queue, POST /review/queue/{id}/approve, /skip, /deep-research. - Review nav section activated in the UI: pending queue list, Approve / Skip / Deep-Research action buttons; TanStack Virtual if >50 items (I4). - Wire Deep-Research action → POST /research/start (Phase 2 endpoint). - Implement backend/app/ingest/extract.py (F12): dispatch function + per-format extractors; new dependencies in pyproject.toml. - Extend POST /ingest/upload file-type gate to accept PDF/DOCX/PPTX/XLSX/images. - D4 update: review endpoints + upload extension in openapi.json. - D2 update: make er regenerated with review_items table. - D5 snapshots: review-queue.png, upload-multiformat.png.

Exit condition for Phase 3: F9 review queue shows pending items and all 3 actions work; F12 can ingest a fixture PDF via upload; AC-F9-1..8, AC-F12-1..7, D5-M5-1 (partial) green; test suite green; D2/D4 zero drift; gate chain passed.

docs.title: Phase 4 — Cascade deletion (F13) + final docs gate
docs.text:
Duration: 2–3 evenings

Rationale: F13 is the most invasive operation (it modifies vault/wiki/ files directly). Placing it last means the vault is in a stable state — populated by F12 and reviewed by F9 — before deletion logic is validated. It also keeps Phase 3's gate chain clean.

Work: - Implement backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py: 3-method matching; soft-delete Postgres; Qdrant removal; wiki/ file wikilink cleanup; index.md entry removal; data_version bump. - REST endpoint: DELETE /pages/{id} with warning response. - Frontend: "Delete page" action in page view with confirmation modal. - D3 update: docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd — written in parallel with implementation. - D5 snapshots: cascade-delete-modal.png, CF-HARD-1 recapture. - D4 update: DELETE /pages/{id} in openapi.json. - Final D2 regeneration: make er after all schema changes across M5. - Full docs gate pass: tech-writer verifies D1/D2/D3/D4/D5 consistency.

Exit condition for Phase 4: F13 cascade delete cleans a page end-to-end; vault/wiki/ stays valid Obsidian vault after deletion; AC-F13-1..7, AC-D3-CD-1, AC-D5-M5-1..3, AC-D3-CI-1 all green; full test suite green including test_obsidian_check.py 15/15; all docs gates met.

docs.title: Phase 5 — MCP Configuration UI (F1-MCP-UI) [Amendment A1]
docs.text:
Duration: 1–2 evenings (lean; read-only; no new DB schema)

Rationale: F1-MCP-UI is placed as a standalone Phase 5 for three reasons: (1) it has no dependency on any Phase 1–4 feature — the MCP server has been live since M2; (2) its gate chain (analyst→architect→engineer→QA→docs→PM) must not contaminate the Phase 4 gate for F13, which is the highest vault-integrity risk of the sprint; (3) if the sprint overruns on Phase 1–4 work, Phase 5 can be deferred to M6 with a clean PM decision record rather than silently sliding into Phase 4 scope. Slotting it within Phase 3 or 4 would risk polluting those gate chains.

If time pressure in Phase 4 is severe, Phase 5 may be explicitly deferred to M6 by PM decision at Phase 4 sign-off. The deferred state is NOT a default — Phase 5 is in scope.

Work: - Add GET /mcp/info endpoint to main.py (or a new backend/app/mcp/info.py router): returns server_name, transport, entry_point_command, tools from live FastMCP registry. - Replace SectionApiMcp stub with a real component: fetch on mount, connection panel with copy-to-clipboard, tools list. - Add/update i18n keys under settings.apiMcp.* in en.json + it.json. - D4 update: make openapi regenerated. - D5 snapshot: docs/screens/settings-api-mcp.png.

Exit condition for Phase 5: Settings > API + MCP panel shows real connection command and real tool list (sourced from GET /mcp/info); copy-to-clipboard works; AC-F1-MCP-UI-1..10 all green; D4 zero drift; D5 screenshot committed; gate chain passed.

docs.title: 8. M5 Exit Criteria
docs.text:
The following is the definitive checklist for M5 sign-off. All items must be MET. Any NOT MET item holds the sign-off.

EC Feature IDs Description EC-M5-1 F5 4-phase retrieval pipeline in rag/retrieval.py; unit + integration tests green; GET /search in openapi.json. EC-M5-2 F5, F6 [n] citation markers appear in chat messages; stored with message in Postgres; render as clickable superscripts. EC-M5-3 F6 AC-F6-5 save-to-wiki button wired and functional; POST /ingest called on click; inline result shown. EC-M5-4 F17 CliAgentProvider.chat() implemented; streaming consistent with other backends; bounded by max_iter + token_budget (I7); total_cost_usd logged. EC-M5-5 F10, I7 Deep Research loop implemented with max_iter + token_budget + concurrency≤3 all enforced. I7 test: mock "always insufficient" provider → loop stops at max_iter. EC-M5-6 F10, I9 SearXNG is the only web search backend used by F10. Static import test confirms no Tavily/DuckDuckGo/Google imports anywhere in the codebase. EC-M5-7 F10 POST /research/start, GET /research/runs, GET /research/runs/{id} endpoints live and in openapi.json. EC-M5-8 F9 Review queue populated on ingest; Approve / Skip / Deep-Research actions work; pre-generated queries stored. EC-M5-9 F9, F10 F9 Deep-Research action correctly delegates to F10 POST /research/start. run_id stored on review_item. EC-M5-10 F12 PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX files successfully ingested via upload; text extracted by ingest/extract.py; binary in raw/; wiki entry produced by ingest pipeline. EC-M5-11 F12, I5 Extracted .md companion files written to vault/raw/sources/ only; vault/wiki/ written only by ingest pipeline; K1 3-layer rule intact. EC-M5-12 F13 Cascade delete removes Postgres row + Qdrant vector + index.md entry + dead wikilinks in wiki/; shared-entity warnings returned. EC-M5-13 F13, I1 Cascade delete only touches files referencing the deleted page (targeted edits); no full rescan triggered. data_version bump triggers existing debounced recompute only. EC-M5-14 I5, K7 vault/wiki/ remains a valid Obsidian vault after all M5 operations. test_obsidian_check.py 15/15 green. EC-M5-15 D3, I8 docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd and docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd present, valid Mermaid, reviewed by architect + tech-writer, pass mmdc CI check. EC-M5-16 D5, I8 Playwright captures ≥4 new M5 PNGs committed to docs/screens/; CF-HARD-1 recaptured. make screenshots exits 0. EC-M5-17 D2, D4, I8 D2 regenerated via make er (includes deep_research_runs, deep_research_sources, review_items tables). D4 regenerated via make openapi (all new endpoints). Zero drift on both. EC-M5-18 QA gate Full pytest + vitest + Playwright suite green (0 failures, 0 regressions). ruff + black + mypy + ESLint + Prettier clean. EC-M5-19 Architect gate Architect reviews: rag/retrieval.py (I1/I2/I3), deep_research.py (I7/I9), cascade_delete.py (I1/I5), review.py (I6/I7), CliAgentProvider.chat() (I6/I7). Any new ADRs for architecture decisions approved. EC-M5-20 Tech-writer gate D3 + D5 + D2/D4 verified consistent with implementation. D6 carry-forward (USER.md + DEPLOY.md) confirmed still current with M5 additions documented. EC-M5-21 PM gate This document. All EC-M5-1..22 MET. M4 carry-forward nits disposed. Velocity note filed. EC-M5-22 F1-MCP-UI (A1) Settings > API + MCP panel renders real GET /mcp/info data (not a stub). Connection command shown. Tools list shows all 4 real tools. i18n EN + IT parity. D4 zero drift (GET /mcp/info in openapi.json). D5 screenshot committed. AC-F1-MCP-UI-1..10 green. EC-M5-HCP Human checkpoint Emanuele has confirmed in browser (see conditions below). Sprint 6 blocked until this is cleared.

docs.title: 9. Human Checkpoint (EC-M5-HCP)
docs.text:
Sprint 6 must NOT begin until Emanuele confirms the following conditions live in browser. Each condition maps to a feature delivered in M5.

Condition Feature What to verify EC-M5-HCP-1 F5 Chat response contains [n] citation markers and hovering a marker shows the source page title. EC-M5-HCP-2 F5, F6 AC-F6-5 save-to-wiki button on an assistant message is enabled; clicking it creates a wiki page visible in the Pages section. EC-M5-HCP-3 F10 Deep Search section accepts a topic, triggers a run, shows progress (status=running), and completes with a generated synthesis visible in the run detail. EC-M5-HCP-4 F9 Review section shows at least one pending item after an ingest; Approve and Skip actions update the item status; Deep-Research action triggers a run visible in Deep Search. EC-M5-HCP-5 F12 Upload a PDF via the Sources section; the file is accepted (not HTTP 415); an ingest run appears; the resulting wiki page contains text from the PDF. EC-M5-HCP-6 F13 Delete a wiki page via the page view delete action; the page disappears from the tree; the index.md no longer links to it; any wiki file that referenced it now has plain text instead of a wikilink.

| EC-M5-HCP-7 | F1-MCP-UI (A1) | Settings > API + MCP section shows the real MCP server connection command (python -m app.mcp.server) and lists the exposed tools (at least search_wiki, write_page, get_page, list_pages). Copy-to-clipboard produces a valid Claude Desktop JSON snippet. |

All 7 conditions must be satisfied before M5 is fully closed and Sprint 6 (v0.6 / M6 — "Shippable") is unblocked. The conditions are recorded in the M5 closure document (docs/sprints/v0.5-m5-closure.md, to be created at sprint end).

docs.title: 10. Out-of-Scope Boundary Reminders
docs.text:
Critical boundaries that engineers must not cross during M5:

F9 is NOT the Ingest Activity View. F1-INGEST-VIEW (done in M4) is read-only history + a trigger button in the Sources nav section. F9 is a separate Review nav section with queue management and AI-driven action routing. These are different features on different nav sections. Any engineer adding Approve/Skip/Deep-Research actions to the Sources section (F1-INGEST-VIEW) during M5 is out of scope and will be blocked.

F10 SearXNG only. If during F10 implementation an engineer discovers that SearXNG is not accessible in the test environment, the correct response is to fix the SearXNG connection (check SEARXNG_URL env var, check container networking). It is NOT to substitute Tavily or any other search backend — that violates I9 and requires architect escalation.

F12 extraction only, not new inference. The multi-format extractors (pypdf, python-docx, etc.) extract text. They do NOT call any LLM for OCR, transcription, or summarization. Extracted text is passed as-is to the existing analyze()/generate() pipeline.

F13 targeted edits only. Cascade delete must use targeted file modifications (not write all wiki files). A PR that rewrites vault/wiki/index.md or vault/wiki/*.md files en masse violates I1 and will be blocked.

docs.title: 11. Velocity Note
docs.text:
M4 was the largest sprint delivered ON SCOPE with all 25+ original items delivered within the 2-week envelope (plus M4-HARD hardening). M5 is the largest sprint by feature count: 5 major features + 2 doc deliverables + 3 carry-forward ACs. The phased delivery plan (4 phases, each with independent gate chain) is designed to keep this manageable for a solo evening developer. Each phase is scoped to 3–4 evenings.

Risk assessment: - F10 (Deep Research) is the highest technical risk: most loop-complexity, I7 compliance scrutiny, live SearXNG dependency. Placed in Phase 2 with its own gate before F9 depends on it. - F13 (Cascade delete) is the highest vault-integrity risk: directly modifies wiki/ files. Placed last (Phase 4) when the vault is populated and stable. - F5 (Retrieval) is the highest architectural risk: first full RAG pipeline. Placed first (Phase 1) to unblock carry-forwards quickly and validate the architecture. - F12 (Multi-format) is low risk: extractors are well-established Python libraries. Can move to Phase 3 without concern.

If during execution any phase proves larger than estimated, the FIRST escalation action is to the orchestrator — not to silently defer ACs or reduce scope without PM approval.

Sprint 6 boundary: F2 (purpose.md injection), F11 (Chrome clipper), F15-rest (PWA/Tauri), K2 (lint loop), MkDocs are ALL M6. No M6 item may be started during M5.

PM sign-off: scope-locked 2026-06-29 | product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6) Scope amended: 2026-06-29 — Amendment A1: F1-MCP-UI added (stakeholder request: Emanuele Chiummo). Phase 5 added. EC-M5-22 added. EC-M5-HCP-7 added. §3 scope log updated. §4 F1-MCP-UI ACs (AC-F1-MCP-UI-1..10) added. §5 out-of-scope boundary updated. §7 Phase 5 added. §8 EC-M5-21 updated (now covers 1..22). §9 HCP condition count updated. Amended by: product-manager (claude-sonnet-4-6). Prerequisite confirmed: M4 CLOSED — docs/sprints/v0.4-m4-closure.md; EC-M4-HCP CLEARED; v0.4.0 released; all 4 gate sign-offs on record. Locked feature IDs: F5, F9, F10, F12, F13, F1-MCP-UI, D3-update, D5-update, F6-AC-F6-3, F6-AC-F6-5, F17-CliAgentProvider.chat(), plus M4 carry-forward nits. Handoff: acceptance criteria (§4) → functional-analyst for user story elaboration. Handoff: exit criteria verdict (§8 + §9 EC-M5-HCP) → orchestrator at M5 sign-off. Next action: Phase 1 complete; Phase 2 (F10) in progress; Phase 5 (F1-MCP-UI) begins after Phase 4 sign-off.

docs.title: Sprint 5 — v0.5 — User Stories + Acceptance Criteria
docs.text: Milestone: M5 — "Feature parity core" Authored by: functional-analyst (claude-sonnet-4-6) Date: 2026-06-29 Source of truth for ACs: docs/sprints/v0.5-scope.md §4 Exit criteria reference: docs/sprints/v0.5-scope.md §8 (EC-M5-1..21 + EC-M5-HCP) Invariants with heightened priority: I7 (bounded loops — HEADLINE), I9 (SearXNG reuse — HEADLINE) All 9 invariants apply.

docs.title: How to read this document
docs.text:
Each user story block contains: - Story ID (e.g. S-F5-1): referenced in TRACEABILITY.md. - Story header: "As a [role], I want [capability], so that [outcome]." - Numbered acceptance criteria (AC-F5-1, AC-F5-2, …): each is a concrete binary assertion. - Invariants touched: priming engineers on which non-negotiables are exercised. - D-artifacts impacted. - Planned test target(s): forward reference for qa-test-engineer.

Binary pass/fail interpretation: every AC is either fully met or not met. No partial credit.

docs.title: Roles used in stories
docs.text: Role Who vault owner Emanuele — uses Synapse to manage and query a personal knowledge base researcher A user driving Deep Research or review queue actions chat user A user engaged in a multi-turn conversation with the Synapse chat interface the Synapse service The running FastAPI process — autonomous actor in pipeline stories backend-engineer Engineer implementing Python modules frontend-engineer Engineer implementing React/TypeScript UI

docs.title: S-F5-1 — 4-phase RAG retrieval pipeline
docs.text:
As a chat user, I want my questions answered using context retrieved from the wiki via a 4-phase pipeline (tokenized keyword search → graph-expansion → token-budget allocation → context assembly), so that assistant answers are grounded in the actual vault content rather than the model's parametric knowledge alone.

Invariants touched: I1 (no vault rescan; reads from Qdrant + Postgres only), I2 (graph-expansion reads from the existing edges table — does NOT trigger FA2 recompute), I3 (retrieval assembled server-side; no per-token heavy work), I9 (reuse Qdrant + bge-m3; no new embedding service).

D-artifacts: D4 (GET /search endpoint in openapi.json).

AC-F5-1 Given backend/app/rag/retrieval.py is implemented, when a retrieval call is made, then the pipeline executes all four phases in order: (1) tokenized keyword search over Qdrant using bge-m3 vectors; (2) graph-expansion following edges from top-k results in the Postgres links and edges tables, up to depth 2; (3) token-budget allocation using 20% of the configured context window (per F14); (4) context assembly producing a RetrievalContext Pydantic object with page refs. Test target: backend/tests/test_retrieval.py — unit test with mocked Qdrant client and Postgres edges; assert all 4 phases execute and RetrievalContext is returned.

AC-F5-2 Given the pipeline has assembled a RetrievalContext, when the context is inspected, then every retrieved passage carries a 1-based citation index n; the assembled context string contains inline [n] markers; and RetrievalContext maps each n to a PageRef with fields id (uuid), title (str), and slug (url-friendly str, not null). Test target: backend/tests/test_retrieval.py — assert len(context.citations) == n_passages, each citation has id, title, slug; assert [1] appears in context.text.

AC-F5-3 Given the retrieval pipeline is executing, when it performs vector search and graph-expansion, then it calls only the existing Qdrant wrapper (bge-m3) and reads from Postgres links/edges tables — no import of a new embedding service, no new Qdrant collection, no direct Ollama embedding call. Test target: backend/tests/test_code_quality.py — static import check: no bge, sentence_transformers, or new Qdrant collection creation outside the existing Qdrant wrapper module.

AC-F5-4 Given 10 fixture passages totalling 2× the configured token budget, when the context assembly phase runs, then the assembled RetrievalContext.text length (in tokens) is less than or equal to the budget; the lowest-ranked passages are dropped first; no passage is silently truncated mid-sentence without removal. Test target: backend/tests/test_retrieval.py — seed 10 fixture passages; configure budget to half their total; assert len(context.citations) < 10 and total token length ≤ budget.

AC-F5-5 Given a retrieval call has completed, when the Postgres vault_state.data_version is read before and after the call, then the value is identical (graph-expansion reads the edges table; it does NOT bump data_version or trigger FA2). Test target: backend/tests/test_retrieval.py — assert data_version_before == data_version_after.

AC-F5-6 Given the FastAPI application is running, when GET /search?q=<query>&vault_id=<uuid> is called, then the endpoint returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing at least results (array of citation objects) and context (assembled string with [n] markers); the endpoint schema is present in docs/api/openapi.json. Test target: backend/tests/test_api.py — ASGI test client; assert HTTP 200 and required fields. backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert openapi.json contains "/search" path.

AC-F5-7 Given the retrieval unit test suite, when all unit tests in test_retrieval.py run, then the following scenarios all pass: (a) 0-result query returns empty RetrievalContext with no citations; (b) single-result query returns RetrievalContext with exactly 1 citation; (c) multi-page expansion returns linked pages in the expansion phase (seed 5 pages with known wikilinks; assert linked pages appear in results); (d) budget-overflow truncation drops lowest-ranked passages until budget is satisfied. Test target: backend/tests/test_retrieval.py — 4 parametrized test cases.

AC-F5-8 Given F5 retrieval is implemented, when the chat endpoint processes a user message, then the assembled RetrievalContext from F5 is passed to the active provider's chat(messages, retrieval_context) call for all three backends (OllamaProvider, ApiProvider, CliAgentProvider), so that [n] markers appear in assistant messages. Test target: backend/tests/test_chat_endpoint.py — mock all three providers; assert each chat() call receives a RetrievalContext argument (not None).

docs.title: S-F6-1 — Citation markers in chat messages
docs.text:
As a chat user, I want assistant messages to display [n] citation markers that are clickable and show the source page title on hover, so that I can trace assistant claims back to specific wiki pages without leaving the chat view.

Invariants touched: I3 (citations are pre-assembled server-side and stored with the message; no per-token citation resolution on the client).

D-artifacts: D2 (JSONB citations field on messages table).

AC-F6-3 (Carry-forward from M4, unblocked by F5) Given an assistant message has been generated with a non-empty RetrievalContext, when the message is stored and then rendered in the chat UI, then: (a) the messages table row has a non-null JSONB citations field mapping each n to {id, title, slug}; (b) the rendered message shows [n] as a clickable superscript element (e.g. <sup> or <span role="link">); (c) hovering the [n] element displays the source page title. Test target: backend/tests/test_chat_endpoint.py — assert citations stored in DB. frontend/src/tests/ChatMessage.test.tsx — mock message with citations; assert [1] rendered as superscript; assert hover/title attribute present.

docs.title: S-F6-2 — Save assistant message to wiki
docs.text:
As a chat user, I want to save an assistant message directly to the wiki with one click, so that insights surfaced in conversation are captured in the knowledge base without manually copying content.

Invariants touched: I1 (save-to-wiki goes through the ingest pipeline; no direct vault/wiki/ file write bypassing the orchestrator), I6 (inference for the new page goes through InferenceProvider).

D-artifacts: D4 (POST /ingest endpoint remains the intake; no new endpoint needed).

AC-F6-5 (Carry-forward from M4, unblocked by F5) Given the chat UI shows an assistant message, when the user clicks the "save to wiki" button, then: (a) the button is enabled (not disabled/grayed); (b) clicking it calls POST /ingest with the message content as source text; (c) on HTTP 202 response, an inline status indicator in the chat message shows the resulting page title and its [[wikilink]]; (d) on error, an inline error message is shown and the chat state is unchanged. Test target: frontend/src/tests/ChatMessage.test.tsx — mock POST /ingest returning 202 with {page_title, wikilink}; assert inline status rendered. frontend/src/tests/ChatMessage.test.tsx — mock POST /ingest returning 500; assert error rendered; assert chat state unchanged.

docs.title: S-F17-1 — CliAgentProvider.chat() implementation
docs.text:
As a chat user configured to use the CLI (claude-agent-sdk) provider, I want the chat endpoint to successfully stream responses from the CliAgentProvider, so that the CLI backend is fully usable for conversation (not just ingest).

Invariants touched: I6 (all inference routes through InferenceProvider — CliAgentProvider.chat() must conform to the same interface as OllamaProvider and ApiProvider), I7 (the delegated agent loop must be bounded by max_iter and token_budget; total_cost_usd logged).

D-artifacts: none (implementation only; interface unchanged).

AC-F17-CHAT-1 Given the CLI provider is configured and selected, when CliAgentProvider.chat(messages, retrieval_context) is called, then: (a) it does NOT raise NotImplementedError; (b) it delegates the chat turn to the claude-agent-sdk subprocess with retrieval_context injected as a system context message or tool input; (c) the agent loop is bounded by max_iter (configurable, default from provider_config) and token_budget (configurable); (d) the loop terminates when either a response is produced or max_iter is reached. Test target: backend/tests/test_cli_chat.py — mock claude-agent-sdk; assert no NotImplementedError; assert retrieval_context text appears in the mocked SDK input; assert loop terminates.

AC-F17-CHAT-2 Given CliAgentProvider.chat() is implemented, when a chat turn is processed by the CLI backend, then the method yields token chunks in the same format as OllamaProvider and ApiProvider (async generator of strings or chunk dicts); the chat SSE stream endpoint is unchanged; a switch from OllamaProvider to CliAgentProvider requires no change to the chat route handler. Test target: backend/tests/test_cli_chat.py — assert return type is an async generator; assert chunk format matches the shared streaming interface.

AC-F17-CHAT-3 Given CliAgentProvider.chat() has completed a turn, when the ingest_runs (or equivalent cost) row is inspected, then total_cost_usd is logged: SDK-reported cost when the API key is present, Decimal("0.00") otherwise (NB-4 convention). No exception is raised if SDK metadata is absent. Test target: backend/tests/test_cli_chat.py — mock SDK output with total_cost_usd = 0.042; assert logged value matches.
