Mock SDK output with no cost metadata; assert Decimal("0.00") logged without exception.

docs.title: S-F10-1 — Deep Research loop: bounded multi-query SearXNG loop
docs.text:
As a researcher, I want Synapse to autonomously gather web sources on a topic by running multiple SearXNG queries, assessing whether collected content is sufficient, and synthesizing a new wiki page — all within explicit iteration and cost bounds, so that I can augment the vault with current information without manual web searching.

Invariants touched: I7 (max_iter + token_budget + concurrency ≤ 3 are mandatory; any unbounded path is a P0 blocker), I9 (SearXNG is the only permitted web search backend; never Tavily, DuckDuckGo, or Google), I6 (all inference calls go through InferenceProvider — query generation, sufficiency assessment, synthesis), I1 (synthesis fed through the ingest pipeline, not written directly to vault/wiki/).

D-artifacts: D2 (deep_research_runs + deep_research_sources tables in schema.mmd), D3 (docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd), D4 (research endpoints in openapi.json).

AC-F10-1 Given backend/app/ops/deep_research.py is implemented, when run_deep_research(vault_id, topic, max_iter, token_budget) is called, then the function executes all 6 steps in order: (1) generate 2–5 SearXNG queries via InferenceProvider; (2) execute queries against SearXNG with concurrency=3 (asyncio semaphore); (3) fetch and parse result pages to markdown; (4) call InferenceProvider to assess sufficiency; if sufficient → step 5; if not and iterations < max_iter → generate follow-up queries and repeat from step 2; (5) synthesize via InferenceProvider; (6) feed synthesis into the ingest pipeline (not directly into vault/wiki/). Test target: backend/tests/test_deep_research.py — mock SearXNG and InferenceProvider; assert all 6 steps execute; assert synthesis routed to ingest pipeline.

AC-F10-2 (I7 — headline invariant for M5) Given deep_research.py is running with a mock InferenceProvider that always returns "insufficient", when the loop executes, then: (a) the loop terminates after exactly max_iter refinement rounds; (b) the run status is set to "max_iter_reached" (not still "running"); (c) no further InferenceProvider calls are made after max_iter is reached; (d) max_queries_per_iter is not exceeded per round (default 5); (e) concurrency is at most 3 (asyncio semaphore prevents more than 3 concurrent SearXNG fetches). Test target: backend/tests/test_deep_research.py — mock "always insufficient" provider; assert call_count == max_iter; assert run.status == "max_iter_reached". THIS TEST IS MANDATORY — EC-M5-5 explicitly requires it.

AC-F10-3 (I9) Given the deep research implementation and all its dependencies, when python -m pytest backend/tests/test_code_quality.py -k "no_tavily" runs, then: (a) no file in the codebase imports tavily, duckduckgo_search, googlesearch, or google-search-results; (b) SEARXNG_URL env var is the only web-search configuration entry point. Test target: backend/tests/test_code_quality.py — static import scan; assert zero matches for tavily|ddg|duckduckgo|googlesearch in all .py files.

AC-F10-4 Given the FastAPI application is running with deep research implemented, when the following REST calls are made: - POST /research/start with body {vault_id, topic, max_iter?, token_budget?}, - GET /research/runs, - GET /research/runs/{id}, then: (a) POST returns HTTP 202 with {run_id: uuid}; (b) GET /research/runs returns a list of past runs (each with status and total_cost_usd); (c) GET /research/runs/{id} returns run detail including queries_used (JSONB array), sources_fetched (int), and synthesis_text (str or null if not yet complete); (d) all three endpoints are documented in docs/api/openapi.json. Test target: backend/tests/test_research_api.py — ASGI test client; assert each response shape. backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert openapi.json contains "/research/start".

AC-F10-5 Given the F9 review queue is operational and a Deep-Research action fires on a review item, when POST /review/queue/{id}/deep-research is called, then: (a) POST /research/start is called with the review item's pre_generated_query as the topic (or the page topic if query is null); (b) the returned run_id is stored on the review_items row as a foreign key or JSONB reference; (c) the review item status is set to "deep_researched". Test target: backend/tests/test_review_integration.py — seed a review_item with pre_generated_query; call deep-research action; assert run_id stored and status updated.

AC-F10-6 Given a deep research run completes (success or max_iter_reached), when the Postgres tables are inspected, then: (a) a row exists in deep_research_runs with columns: id, vault_id, topic, status, queries_used (JSONB), sources_fetched (int), total_cost_usd, started_at, completed_at, error_message (nullable); (b) one or more rows exist in deep_research_sources with columns: id, run_id, url, title, fetched_content_md, relevance_score; (c) Alembic migration files for both tables exist in backend/alembic/versions/; (d) make er exits 0 and docs/er/schema.mmd reflects both tables. Test target: backend/tests/test_models_schema.py — static introspection of SQLAlchemy models; assert all required columns present. CI: make er exits 0.

AC-F10-7 Given the deep research unit test suite, when all tests in test_deep_research.py run, then the following scenarios all pass: (a) mock SearXNG returning 3 results → assert exactly 3 fetch calls made; (b) mock InferenceProvider returning "sufficient" on the first assessment → assert loop completes after exactly 1 round and proceeds to synthesis; (c) mock InferenceProvider returning "insufficient" for max_iter rounds → assert loop terminates at max_iter with status "max_iter_reached" and no further provider calls; (d) synthesis output is passed to the ingest pipeline, not written directly to vault/wiki/. Test target: backend/tests/test_deep_research.py — 4 parametrized test cases.

AC-F10-8 Given the frontend is running with deep research implemented, when a Playwright test navigates to the "Deep Search" nav section and enters a topic, then: (a) the nav section renders a topic input field and a Start button (not a "coming in M5" placeholder); (b) clicking Start fires POST /research/start; (c) a run row appears in the runs list with status "running" or "completed"; (d) the run detail view shows the synthesis text when status is "completed". Test target: frontend/e2e/deep-search.spec.ts — Playwright; assert topic input renders, POST fires, run appears.

docs.title: S-D3-1 — Deep Research sequence diagram
docs.text:
As the architect and tech-writer, I want a Mermaid sequence diagram documenting the Deep Research loop, so that the bounded loop structure (max_iter, concurrency, SearXNG) is auditable and onboarding engineers can understand the flow without reading source code.

Invariants touched: I8 (docs-as-DoD; diagram is a required deliverable).

D-artifacts: D3 (docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd).

AC-D3-DR-1 Given docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd exists, when the file content is inspected, then: (a) it contains sequenceDiagram keyword; (b) it shows the trigger → query generation (InferenceProvider) → SearXNG multi-query (concurrency=3) → fetch+parse → assess sufficiency (InferenceProvider) → branch [sufficient: synthesize+ingest; not sufficient: refine, bounded by max_iter] → total_cost_usd logged; (c) the diagram explicitly annotates max_iter and concurrency=3 bounds (as note or label); (d) make er or mmdc -i docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd exits 0 (valid Mermaid syntax). Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert file exists; assert contains "sequenceDiagram", "max_iter", "concurrency", "SearXNG", "InferenceProvider", "total_cost_usd". CI: mmdc render check.

docs.title: Phase 3 — HITL Review Queue + Multi-format ingest
S-F9-1 — Async HITL review queue: backend + REST API
docs.text:
As the Synapse service, I want every newly generated wiki page to be automatically enqueued in an async review queue backed by Postgres, so that human curators (K8) can approve, skip, or trigger Deep Research on any page before it is considered finalized.

Invariants touched: I7 (pre-generated query generation is bounded to 1 call per item with timeout; the queue itself is non-blocking), I6 (query generation goes through InferenceProvider), I5 (pages are written to vault/wiki/ immediately regardless of review status — the queue is advisory, not a gate).

D-artifacts: D2 (review_items table in schema.mmd), D4 (review endpoints in openapi.json).

AC-F9-1 Given backend/app/ops/review.py is implemented, when the SQLAlchemy models are introspected, then the review_items table exists with columns: id (UUID PK), vault_id (UUID FK → vaults, or vault env), page_id (UUID FK → pages, nullable), item_type (enum: new_page / update_page / deep_research_candidate), status (enum: pending / approved / skipped / deep_researched), pre_generated_query (TEXT, nullable), created_at (TIMESTAMP), reviewed_at (TIMESTAMP, nullable), reviewed_by (TEXT, nullable). Alembic migration file exists. Test target: backend/tests/test_models_schema.py — static introspection; assert all columns and types present. CI: make er reflects the table.

AC-F9-2 Given the ingest pipeline generates a new wiki page, when the page is written to vault/wiki/ (existing behavior — unchanged), then a review_items row with item_type="new_page" and status="pending" is also inserted into Postgres. This insertion is non-blocking: the page is written regardless of whether the queue insertion succeeds. Test target: backend/tests/test_ingest_review_queue.py — run ingest with mocked provider; assert review_items row created with correct page_id and status; assert vault/wiki/ page written even if queue insert fails (mock queue insert failure).

AC-F9-3 Given the REST API is running, when the following endpoints are called: - GET /review/queue?vault_id=<uuid>&status=pending (paginated), - POST /review/queue/{id}/approve, - POST /review/queue/{id}/skip, - POST /review/queue/{id}/deep-research, then: (a) GET returns a paginated list of review items with all fields; (b) approve sets status="approved" and reviewed_at=now(); (c) skip sets status="skipped"; (d) deep-research triggers F10 POST /research/start and sets status="deep_researched"; (e) all endpoints return HTTP 200 or 202 respectively and are documented in docs/api/openapi.json. Test target: backend/tests/test_review_api.py — ASGI test client; seed 2 review_items; assert each action produces correct status and DB update.

AC-F9-4 Given the ingest pipeline creates a new review item, when the pre_generated_query generation step runs, then: (a) the active InferenceProvider is called exactly once to generate 1–3 research questions for the new page; (b) the questions are stored newline-separated in review_items.pre_generated_query; (c) if the generation call fails or times out, the item is still enqueued with pre_generated_query = null and no exception propagates (I7); (d) the call uses max_iter=1 (single bounded call, not a loop). Test target: backend/tests/test_ingest_review_queue.py — mock provider.generate_queries(); assert called once; assert stored in DB. Mock provider raising timeout; assert item enqueued with null query.

AC-F9-5 (See S-F9-2 for the frontend story — AC-F9-5 is the frontend activation gate) Given the frontend Review nav section was showing a "coming in M5" placeholder in M4, when M5 Phase 3 is deployed, then the Review nav section renders an active queue list (not a placeholder); the list shows page title, item_type, and pre_generated_query (if present) for each pending item; the list is virtualized with TanStack Virtual when item count exceeds 50 (I4). Test target: frontend/src/tests/ReviewQueue.test.tsx — mount with mocked 3 pending items; assert 3 rows rendered. Mount with mocked 60 items; assert TanStack Virtual container present.

AC-F9-6 Given the review queue is operational, when the status of a page in vault/wiki/ is inspected before and after an "Approve" action, then: (a) the page file in vault/wiki/ is IDENTICAL before and after Approve (Approve does not modify the file); (b) Approve only updates the DB row status and reviewed_at; (c) Skip removes the item from the queue (status="skipped") but does not delete or modify the page file; (d) no re-ingest is triggered by Approve. Test target: backend/tests/test_review_api.py — assert file checksum unchanged after approve and skip; assert ingest pipeline not called.

AC-F9-7 Given the F1-INGEST-VIEW (Sources nav section from M4) is visible in the nav rail, when a user navigates to the Sources section, then the Sources section shows only the ingest history and Run Ingest button (unchanged from M4); the Approve/Skip/Deep-Research action buttons are NOT present in the Sources section; they appear only in the Review nav section. Test target: frontend/src/tests/IngestView.test.tsx — assert no element with text "Approve" or "Skip" or "Deep-Research" in the Sources section render.

AC-F9-8 Given the review queue has 2 seeded items, when the integration test calls approve on 1 and skip on 1, then: (a) both items have the expected updated statuses in Postgres; (b) calling the deep-research action on a third item triggers POST /research/start exactly once and stores the run_id on the review_item row. Test target: backend/tests/test_review_integration.py — end-to-end integration with seeded review_items; assert all 3 action outcomes.

docs.title: S-F9-2 — Review queue UI: Approve / Skip / Deep-Research actions
docs.text:
As a researcher, I want the Review section in the nav rail to show pending wiki pages with action buttons, so that I can fulfill the K8 "human curates, LLM maintains" principle by approving, skipping, or triggering Deep Research on each queued item.

Invariants touched: I4 (list virtualized if >50 items — no DOM thrash), I3 (no heavy work per render tick).

D-artifacts: D5 (docs/screens/review-queue.png).

AC-F9-9 (Derived from AC-F9-5 with explicit button-action coverage) Given the Review section renders a list of pending items, when the user clicks "Approve" on an item, then POST /review/queue/{id}/approve is called; the item is removed from the pending list and replaced with a success indicator; no page navigation occurs. Test target: frontend/src/tests/ReviewQueue.test.tsx — mock POST /review/queue/{id}/approve; assert API call made; assert item removed from list.

AC-F9-10 Given the Review section renders a list of pending items, when the user clicks "Skip" on an item, then POST /review/queue/{id}/skip is called; the item is removed from the list. Test target: frontend/src/tests/ReviewQueue.test.tsx — mock skip endpoint; assert call and removal.

AC-F9-11 Given the Review section renders a list of pending items with a pre_generated_query, when the user clicks "Deep-Research" on an item, then POST /review/queue/{id}/deep-research is called; the user is navigated (or a toast shown) to the Deep Search section to view the triggered run. Test target: frontend/src/tests/ReviewQueue.test.tsx — mock deep-research endpoint; assert API call; assert navigation or toast rendered.

docs.title: S-F12-1 — Multi-format document extraction and ingest
docs.text:
As a vault owner, I want to upload PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and image files to the Sources section and have them automatically ingested into the wiki, so that research materials in common office formats can be added to the knowledge base without manual conversion.

Invariants touched: I1 (uploading the same file twice does not create a duplicate — existing content-hash check applies regardless of file type), I5 (extracted text goes to vault/raw/sources/ as a companion .extracted.md; the original binary is preserved; vault/wiki/ is only written by the ingest pipeline — K1 3-layer rule intact), I6 (extractors do not call any LLM; all inference goes through InferenceProvider in the existing ingest pipeline).

D-artifacts: D4 (updated MIME type list in openapi.json).

AC-F12-1 Given backend/app/ingest/extract.py is implemented with a extract_text(file_path) -> str dispatch function, when files of each supported type are passed to extract_text, then: (a) PDF (.pdf): text from all pages extracted via pypdf; (b) DOCX (.docx): paragraph text extracted via python-docx; (c) PPTX (.pptx): slide text extracted via python-pptx; (d) XLSX (.xlsx): cell data from all sheets extracted as markdown tables via openpyxl; (e) Images (.png, .jpg, .jpeg, .gif, .webp): OCR via unstructured if installed, otherwise returns the string "image file: no text extracted" — no exception raised in either case; (f) AV files (.mp3, .mp4, .wav, .m4a): returns "AV file: transcript not available in this release". The extracted text is passed unchanged to the existing analyze()/generate() ingest loop. Test target: backend/tests/test_extract.py — fixture files for each type; assert extracted text contains expected content; assert no LLM call made during extraction.

AC-F12-2 Given POST /ingest/upload is called with a PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, or image file, when the server processes the request, then: (a) HTTP 202 Accepted is returned (not HTTP 415); (b) the MIME type acceptance list in the upload handler includes application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.*, and common image MIME types; (c) the frontend F1-UPLOAD accept attribute on the file input is updated to include these MIME types. Test target: backend/tests/test_upload.py — ASGI test with fixture PDF; assert HTTP 202. frontend/src/tests/IngestView.test.tsx — assert accept attribute includes .pdf,.docx,.pptx,.xlsx.

AC-F12-3 (I1) Given the same multi-format file has already been ingested (same mtime and content hash), when the file is uploaded again, then no duplicate Postgres row is created in the pages table and no duplicate Qdrant vector is upserted; HTTP 200 or 202 is returned with a duplicate=true indicator in the response body. Test target: backend/tests/test_upload.py — upload same fixture PDF twice; assert exactly 1 Postgres row; assert Qdrant upsert called exactly once total (not twice).

AC-F12-4 (I5 — K1 3-layer rule) Given a multi-format file has been uploaded and extracted, when the filesystem is inspected, then: (a) the original binary file is stored in vault/raw/sources/ with its original extension; (b) a companion <filename>.extracted.md is written to vault/raw/sources/ (NOT to vault/wiki/); (c) vault/wiki/ contains only files written by the ingest pipeline; (d) the .obsidian/ config remains untouched by the extraction step. Test target: backend/tests/test_extract.py — assert companion .extracted.md path; assert vault/wiki/ not written during extraction. backend/tests/test_obsidian_check.py — 15/15 green after extraction.

AC-F12-5 Given backend/pyproject.toml is inspected, when the dependencies are listed, then pypdf, python-docx, python-pptx, openpyxl, and unstructured are present with pinned versions. docker compose build exits 0 with the updated pyproject.toml. Test target: backend/tests/test_code_quality.py — assert all 5 package names appear in pyproject.toml. CI: docker build step exits 0.

AC-F12-6 Given the unit test suite for multi-format extraction, when test_extract.py runs, then the following scenarios all pass: (a) fixture PDF with 2 pages → extracted text contains content from both pages; (b) fixture DOCX → paragraph text extracted in document order; (c) fixture XLSX → sheet rows present as a markdown table (header row + data rows); (d) fixture file with unsupported extension → POST /ingest/upload returns HTTP 415. Test target: backend/tests/test_extract.py — 4 parametrized test cases with fixture files.

AC-F12-7 Given backend/app/ingest/extract.py exists as the sole dispatch module, when a static import analysis is performed, then no pypdf, docx, pptx, or openpyxl import statement appears in any Python file outside backend/app/ingest/extract.py. Test target: backend/tests/test_code_quality.py — static import scan: assert pypdf|python-docx|pptx|openpyxl imports only in ingest/extract.py.

docs.title: Phase 4 — Cascade deletion + final docs gate
S-F13-1 — Cascade deletion of wiki pages
docs.text:
As a vault owner, I want to delete a wiki page and have Synapse automatically clean up all references to it (dead wikilinks in other pages, index.md entry, Qdrant vector), so that the vault remains internally consistent without manual grep-and-edit work.

Invariants touched: I1 (cascade delete touches only files that reference the deleted page; no full vault rescan; data_version IS bumped so the debounced graph recompute fires, but no immediate FA2 run is forced), I5 (dead wikilink cleanup in vault/wiki/ must not break YAML frontmatter; vault/wiki/ must remain a valid Obsidian vault after deletion).

D-artifacts: D3 (docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd), D4 (DELETE /pages/{id} in openapi.json).

AC-F13-1 Given backend/app/ops/cascade_delete.py implements cascade_delete(page_id), when the function is called with a valid page_id, then in sequence: (1) the target page row is located in Postgres; (2) all wikilinks pointing to this page are found via the links table; (3) the page row receives a soft-delete (deleted_at = now()); (4) the Qdrant vector for the page is deleted; (5) the page's entry is removed from vault/wiki/index.md; (6) each wiki page file in vault/wiki/ that contains [[<target_title>]] has that wikilink replaced with plain text <target_title>; (7) the dangling=True flag is set on affected links table rows. Test target: backend/tests/test_cascade_delete.py — seed 1 target page + 3 pages with incoming wikilinks; call cascade_delete; assert all 7 steps executed.

AC-F13-2 Given cascade_delete is finding references to the target page, when the 3-method reference search runs, then: (a) method (a) exact wikilink title match in the links table is tried first; (b) method (b) slug-normalised match (lower-case, hyphenated) covers titles with spaces or mixed case; (c) method (c) full-text scan of vault/wiki/ .md files as fallback. The method actually used for each found reference is logged (one log entry per reference with the method label). Test target: backend/tests/test_cascade_delete.py — seed wikilinks with exact title, slug-variant, and full-text-only match; assert all 3 found; assert log entries contain method labels.

AC-F13-3 Given the target page shares source content with other pages (source_overlap in the edges table), when cascade_delete runs, then: (a) the deletion proceeds (shared entities do NOT block deletion); (b) a WARNING log entry is emitted listing the pages that share source overlap with the deleted page; (c) the response body includes shared_entity_warnings: list[str] with the warning messages. Test target: backend/tests/test_cascade_delete.py — seed target page with shared source; assert deletion completes; assert WARNING in caplog; assert warnings in response body.

AC-F13-4 (I1) Given cascade_delete is called on a page referenced by exactly 3 other pages, when the function completes, then: (a) only the 3 referencing wiki files are modified (targeted file writes); (b) no other file in vault/wiki/ is read or written; (c) vault_state.data_version is incremented exactly once (triggering the debounced graph recompute); (d) no FA2 run is triggered immediately; (e) the watcher file-event loop does NOT trigger re-ingest of the modified files (they are written by the backend directly without a vault/raw/ event). Test target: backend/tests/test_cascade_delete.py — assert file-write call count equals 3; assert data_version incremented by exactly 1; assert FA2 not called.

AC-F13-5 Given the REST API is running, when DELETE /pages/{id} is called for an existing page, then: (a) HTTP 200 is returned; (b) response body contains {deleted_page_id: uuid, wikilinks_cleaned: int, index_entry_removed: bool, shared_entity_warnings: list[str]}; (c) calling DELETE /pages/{id} again on the same (now soft-deleted) id returns HTTP 404; (d) the endpoint is documented in docs/api/openapi.json. Test target: backend/tests/test_cascade_delete_api.py — ASGI test; assert 200 response shape; assert 404 on double-delete. backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert openapi.json contains "/pages/{id}" with DELETE method.

AC-F13-6 Given the frontend page view is showing a wiki page, when the user clicks "Delete page", then: (a) a confirmation modal is shown listing any shared_entity_warnings (if present) before any action is taken; (b) if the user confirms, DELETE /pages/{id} is called; (c) on HTTP 200, the page is removed from the tree and the view navigates back to the parent; (d) if the user cancels the modal, no API call is made and the page view is unchanged. Test target: frontend/src/tests/PageView.test.tsx — mock DELETE /pages/{id}; assert modal shown with warnings; assert API called on confirm; assert no API call on cancel.

AC-F13-7 Given the unit test suite for cascade deletion, when test_cascade_delete.py runs, then the following scenarios all pass: (a) delete a page with 3 incoming wikilinks → assert all 3 wikilinks in other pages replaced with plain text; (b) delete a page with shared source overlap in edges table → assert WARNING logged and deletion completes successfully; (c) double-delete (page already deleted) → assert HTTP 404 returned gracefully with no exception in server logs. Test target: backend/tests/test_cascade_delete.py — 3 test cases.

docs.title: S-D3-2 — Cascade delete sequence diagram
docs.text:
As the architect and tech-writer, I want a Mermaid sequence diagram documenting the cascade delete flow, so that the 3-method matching, soft-delete steps, and Obsidian-compatibility constraints are auditable.

Invariants touched: I8 (docs-as-DoD).

D-artifacts: D3 (docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd).

AC-D3-CD-1 Given docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd exists, when the file content is inspected, then: (a) it contains sequenceDiagram keyword; (b) it shows: DELETE /pages/{id} → find incoming wikilinks (3-method) → soft-delete Postgres row → remove Qdrant vector → clean dead wikilinks in wiki/ files → remove from index.md → bump data_version → return warnings; (c) the shared-entity warning path is explicitly shown as a branch or note; (d) mmdc -i docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd exits 0 (valid Mermaid syntax). Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert file exists; assert contains "sequenceDiagram", "3-method" or "wikilink", "soft-delete" or "deleted_at", "data_version", "warning". CI: mmdc render check.

AC-D3-CI-1 Given the CI mmdc render step is wired from M4 (NB-6), when CI runs, then both docs/sequences/deep-research.mmd and docs/sequences/cascade-delete.mmd are included in the mmdc -i docs/sequences/*.mmd render loop; both render without error; CI step exits 0. Test target: CI: mmdc render step; backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert both files exist and contain "sequenceDiagram".

docs.title: S-D5-1 — Playwright screenshots for M5 UI surfaces
docs.text:
As the tech-writer, I want Playwright to capture screenshots of the 4 new M5 UI surfaces and commit them to docs/screens/, so that the D5 artifact (CLAUDE.md §9) is current with M5 and the docs gate can be signed off.

Invariants touched: I8 (docs-as-DoD; screenshots are a required DoD artifact).

D-artifacts: D5 (new PNGs in docs/screens/).

AC-D5-M5-1 Given the M5 UI surfaces are implemented, when make screenshots is run, then Playwright captures and commits the following PNG files to docs/screens/: - docs/screens/review-queue.png (F9 HITL review queue with at least 1 pending item); - docs/screens/deep-search-trigger.png (Deep Search section with topic input visible); - docs/screens/upload-multiformat.png (Ingest upload zone with multi-format file types indicated); - docs/screens/cascade-delete-modal.png (Delete confirmation modal with a shared-entity warning). Each file must be non-empty (> 0 bytes) and a valid PNG. Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert all 4 files exist and are non-empty. Playwright: frontend/e2e/m5-screenshots.spec.ts captures each surface.

AC-D5-M5-2 (CF-HARD-1 recapture) Given the root cause of the missing shell-collapsed-panel.png was fixed in M4-HARD, when make screenshots runs, then docs/screens/shell-collapsed-panel.png is captured (or re-captured if stale); the file is non-empty and committed. Test target: backend/tests/test_docs.py — assert file exists and is non-empty.

AC-D5-M5-3 Given make screenshots is the canonical target for all Playwright captures, when make screenshots is run, then the command exits 0 with no Playwright test failures; all capture tests pass in one invocation; no manual step is required to populate docs/screens/. Test target: CI: make screenshots exits 0.

docs.title: Ambiguities flagged to architect before Phase 1 engineering begins
docs.text: AQ ID Blocks ACs Question Recommended resolution AQ-v0.5-1 AC-F5-1 BM25 hybrid search in Qdrant: is it available on the current Qdrant 1.9+ instance, or should Phase 1 start with dense-only and add BM25 in a follow-up? Recommend: start with dense-only (bge-m3 vectors); add BM25 sparse hybrid if Qdrant sparse index is enabled. Document in ADR if hybrid is chosen. AQ-v0.5-2 AC-F5-4 Token budget calculation: count tokens by character/4 approximation or tiktoken? Recommend: tiktoken (cl100k_base) for Anthropic/OpenAI paths; fallback to char/4 for Ollama. Consistent with F14. Architect to confirm and document. AQ-v0.5-3 AC-F10-1 Synthesis step: should the synthesis go through the full analyze→generate→validate ingest loop, or just the generate() call with the synthesis as pre-analyzed input? Recommend: generate() only (synthesis is already structured text; no re-analysis needed). Document in ADR-0020 if a new decision. AQ-v0.5-4 AC-F10-2 max_iter default value: should it be 3 (current spec) or configurable via provider_config? Recommend: configurable with default=3; stored in provider_config or a new deep_research_config table. Architect to decide. AQ-v0.5-5 AC-F13-1 Soft delete vs. hard delete: the spec says deleted_at = now(). Does the watcher ignore soft-deleted pages on startup? Confirm no resurrection on restart. Recommend: watcher ignores pages with deleted_at IS NOT NULL; confirm in ADR or watcher docstring. AQ-v0.5-6 AC-F9-1 review_items.vault_id: FK to a vaults table (does one exist?) or FK to vault_state.vault_id (existing)? Confirm: use the same vault_id UUID as in vault_state and provider_config; no separate vaults table needed unless architect decides otherwise. AQ-v0.5-7 AC-F17-CHAT-1 CliAgentProvider.chat() bounded loop: should max_iter be the same field as the ingest max_iter from provider_config, or a separate chat_max_iter parameter? Recommend: separate chat_max_iter in provider_config to allow different caps for chat vs. ingest. Architect to confirm.

docs.title: M6 / v0.6 → v1.0 — Build Status & Exit-Criteria Tracker

Now: backend 968 / frontend 711, all green.

This file tracks the M6 "Shippable" milestone toward the v1.0.0 tag. It is the single source of truth for what is done, what remains, and the gate that separates "code-complete" from "v1.0 released".

docs.text:
Feature Deliverable Commit Verified K2 Lint-fix loop (3rd core op) ops/lint.py bounded HITL loop + 6 /lint/* endpoints + migration 0014 + ADR-0037 + lint-fix.mmd 745600f pytest 946; I6/I7/I1 static tests K2 Lint UI LintView + lintStore + lintClient + nav slot + i18n EN/IT ac90d35 vitest 680; tsc+eslint clean F11 Web clipper + secure ingress MV3 extension/ (Readability+Turndown) + hardened POST /clip + ADR-0038 + web-clip.mmd 7c91354 pytest 968; 22 security tests (401/403/413/safe-join) F15 CI gate tests run in CI, sprint/** trigger, pinned ruff/black, mmdc check 8bbd2a9 ruff/black clean tree-wide; pins 0.15.20 / 24.1.0 F15 PWA manifest + offline app-shell SW (API NetworkOnly) cc365f6 vite build emits sw.js; vitest 711 F15 Tauri v2 shell src-tauri/ scaffold + tag-only multi-OS CI build + ADR-0039 f68b2ad cargo resolves; config valid; native build CI-only F2 purpose.md context already wired (ingest orchestrator.py:961, chat context.py:86) (pre-existing) test_chat::test_includes_purpose_and_overview audit reference docs/reference/llm_wiki-audit/* (parity input) c1cdc29 —

Invariants: I1, I2, I3, I4, I5, I6, I7, I8, I9 all held across every commit (no "never"-list violation introduced). F11's secure ingress is the deliberate inverse of the reference app's unauthenticated clip server (audit S-1…S-6).

• D2 (ER) zero-drift — maintained each migration (0014 added; make er run by the lint agent). ✓
  • D4 (OpenAPI) — regenerated for /lint/* and /clip. ✓
  • D3 (sequences) — lint-fix.mmd, web-clip.mmd added. ✓ (NB-7/NB-8 cosmetic open)

docs.title: 5. EC-M6-HCP — what the human checkpoint requires (blocks the v1.0.0 tag)
docs.text:
The v1.0.0 tag is intentionally not applied autonomously. Per the project DoD and because these need a real environment (TrueNAS stack + browser + OS runners), the following must be done by Emanuele (or on live CI) before tagging:

  1. Live stack smoke — docker compose up, point at Ollama/Qdrant/bge-m3, run a real ingest → verify wiki pages, graph, chat with citations, lint scan, and a real web-clip → auto-ingest.
  2. PWA — install the built frontend as an app; confirm offline app-shell loads and API calls are never served stale.
  3. Tauri desktop — let the tag-only CI matrix build the macOS/Windows/Linux binaries (or build locally with WebKit libs); launch and smoke-test the desktop window.
  4. D5 screenshots — capture the M5 + M6 pending PNGs into docs/screens/.
  5. 3-provider matrix — smoke ingest+chat on Ollama / API / CLI (I6).
  6. Sign off the four gate chains (QA / architect / tech-writer / PM) per the established process.

On HCP pass → roll up DOCS_STATUS/BACKLOG/TRACEABILITY, bump versions to 1.0.0 (frontend/package.json, src-tauri/tauri.conf.json, backend/pyproject.toml), and tag v1.0.0.

docs.title: 6. Remaining autonomous work (no human needed) before HCP
docs.text:
Small, safe, can be done next without a live environment: - FU-P4-3 ADR-0026 atomicity note; FU-P4-4 AC alignment; NB-7/NB-8 .mmd cosmetics. - DEPLOY.md env table: add LINT_* + CLIP_*; FU-P3-2 USER.md note. - DOCS_STATUS.md + BACKLOG.md + TRACEABILITY.md M6 roll-up. - (optional) MkDocs site.

After those, the only thing between this branch and v1.0.0 is EC-M6-HCP (§5).

docs.title: Sprint 6 — v0.6 → v1.0 — PM Scope Lock
docs.text: Milestone: M6 — "Shippable" (final milestone; tag target v1.0.0) Author: orchestrator (acting PM) — autonomous build Date: 2026-06-30 Gate: Sprint start — formal scope confirmation Branch: sprint/v0.6 (cut from sprint/v0.5; carries the nashsu/llm_wiki reference audit under docs/reference/llm_wiki-audit/) Prerequisite: M5 MET-PENDING-EC-M5-HCP (docs/sprints/v0.5-m5-exit-criteria.md). Baseline on cut: backend 926 pytest / frontend 621 vitest green. Inputs: CLAUDE.md §3/§4/§7/§8/§9, BACKLOG.md §"Sprint 6 — v0.6 — M6", docs/reference/llm_wiki-audit/* (parity reference).

docs.title: 1. Sprint Objective
docs.text:
Take Synapse from "feature parity core" (M5) to shippable v1.0: a self-hosted product a new user can install and use day-one, at functional and graphical parity with the reference app nashsu/llm_wiki. The headline theme is closing the loop: the third Karpathy operation (Lint) lands; the ingress surface (web clipper) is added securely; the app is packaged (PWA + Tauri v2) with a CI/CD that actually runs the test suite; and the full D1–D7 documentation set is completed and consistent.

Headline invariants for M6: - I7 (bounded loops) — the new Lint-fix loop has max_iter + token_budget + cost log. - I6 (pluggable inference) — Lint's semantic pass routes through InferenceProvider; no hardcoded backend. - I8 (docs-as-DoD) — M6 is the final docs gate: every D-artifact complete and consistent, ER matches schema. - I9 (do not reinvent) — no Tavily/alt-search; SearXNG only; no local embedding reimplementation.

Parity-reference rule: the llm_wiki audit is advisory input, not a spec to copy. Where the reference app made a security/architecture mistake, Synapse must NOT repeat it. The audit's standout finding — the reference's clip server on :19827 is unauthenticated, accepts drive-by cross-origin writes, and writes to an attacker-controlled base path — is an explicit anti-pattern: Synapse's F11 ingress MUST be authenticated, origin-checked, body-capped, and path-safe (see F11 ACs).

docs.title: 2. Locked Feature IDs for v0.6
docs.text:
Exactly the following. Anything else is out of scope and requires explicit re-scope.

ID Feature Status at cut K2 Lint-fix loop (3rd core operation) backend DONE on this branch; frontend in progress F11 Chrome MV3 web clipper + secure local clip ingress + auto-ingest not started F15 (rest) PWA (manifest + service worker) + Tauri v2 shell + CI/CD multi-OS not started F2 purpose.md injected as provider context (verify/complete) partial — verify in ingest + chat MkDocs Optional docs site optional — only if it does not jeopardize the gate D1–D7 All docs artifacts complete and consistent (final gate) continuous

docs.title: K2 — Lint-fix loop
docs.text:
• AC-K2-1 ops/lint.py runs a bounded loop (LINT_MAX_ITER, LINT_TOKEN_BUDGET, LINT_MAX_FINDINGS); total_cost_usd logged 4dp; terminal status always written (I7). ✅ (test_lint.py)
  • AC-K2-2 All LLM calls via resolve_provider_config — no hardcoded backend, no class-name branching (I6). ✅ static test.
  • AC-K2-3 Scan reads only pages/links (no vault walk); apply touches only referencing pages; data_version bumped ≤1 per fix (I1). ✅
  • AC-K2-4 Human gate: scan never applies a fix; apply only on explicit call. Flag-only categories (orphan/contradiction/stale) acknowledge without editing human-curated content (K8). ✅
  • AC-K2-5 Frontend Lint section: Run-Lint action, findings grouped by category with Apply/Dismiss, cost line, empty state; nav slot activated; i18n EN+IT parity; vitest green; I3/I4 respected. ⏳ (in progress)
  • AC-K2-6 ADR-0037 + docs/sequences/lint-fix.mmd present and valid; openapi.json + ER regenerated. ✅

docs.title: F11 — Web clipper + secure clip ingress
docs.text:
• AC-F11-1 Chrome MV3 extension under extension/: Readability + Turndown (HTML→Markdown, tables), popup with project/vault picker, posts to the local ingress.
  • AC-F11-2 (SECURITY — non-negotiable) The local clip ingress requires a token (rejects unauthenticated → 401); validates Origin/Host against an allowlist (chrome-extension://<id> + loopback); caps the body size; and writes only inside the vault via a safe-join path check (no attacker-controlled base path). A regression test proves each: unauth→401, bad origin→reject, oversized→413, traversal→reject. (Directly answers llm_wiki audit S-1…S-6.)
  • AC-F11-3 A clipped page lands in vault/raw/sources/ and is auto-ingested through the existing pipeline (I1/I5/K1) — no new ingest path.
  • AC-F11-4 Clip content is sanitized before render; no unbounded polling race (use the existing watcher/queue, not a naive 3 s poll that double-ingests).
  • AC-F11-5 ADR for the ingress security model; D3 sequence web-clip.mmd; openapi.json updated if an HTTP endpoint is added.

docs.title: F15 (rest) — PWA + Tauri v2 + CI/CD
docs.text:
• AC-F15-1 PWA: web manifest + service worker; the frontend is installable; offline shell loads. No violation of I2/I3/I4.
  • AC-F15-2 Tauri v2 shell wraps the frontend as a desktop app (macOS/Windows/Linux targets configured). Close-behavior is a user setting (not hardcoded).
  • AC-F15-3 (CI HARDENING) CI runs on sprint/** + main (fix NB-9 branch filter) and executes the test suites (pytest + vitest) and the linters with pinned versions (resolve the ruff/black drift on v0.5 files), plus the mmdc Mermaid render check (GAP-v0.5-3). Multi-OS build matrix produces installable artifacts.
  • AC-F15-4 Linter drift cleared: ruff check + black --check green tree-wide with pinned versions recorded in pyproject.toml/CI.

docs.title: F2 — purpose.md as provider context
docs.text: • AC-F2-1 Confirm (and complete if missing) that purpose.md is injected into the ingest analysis/generation prompts and the chat/query context; add a test asserting its presence in the assembled provider input.

docs.title: D1–D7 final gate
docs.text: • AC-D-1 All D-artifacts present and consistent; ER matches live schema (make er zero-drift); openapi.json current; D5 screenshots captured; ADR index complete; USER.md + DEPLOY.md cover all M6 additions (incl. LINT_*, clipper token/env, PWA/Tauri).

docs.title: 4. Out of scope (M6) / never (invariants)
docs.text:
• MinerU cloud PDF parsing, multimodal image captioning, Louvain communities + graph-insights, browser-extension for non-Chrome — not in M6 (post-v1.0 backlog; not part of the locked parity set).
  • The "never" list stands (BACKLOG §"Out of scope — never"): full-rescan (I1), main-thread force layout (I2), per-token parse (I3), WYSIWYG editor (I4), hardcoded provider (I6), unbounded loops (I7), skipping D-artifacts (I8), Tavily/alt-search or local embedding reimpl (I9).

docs.title: 5. Deferred follow-up disposition (carried into M6)
docs.text: FU Item M6 disposition GAP-v0.5-3 mmdc Mermaid CI render check DO — folds into AC-F15-3 NB-9 CI branch filter (add sprint/v0.3+) DO — AC-F15-3 NB-7 / NB-8 cosmetic .mmd label fixes DO — docs gate FU-P3-2 USER.md note: CLI path does not enqueue reviews DO — docs gate FU-P4-3 ADR-0026: cascade_delete non-atomic/idempotent-on-retry note (or single-session wrap) DO — pick one; document FU-P4-4 F13 files_written equality contract (== vs <=) CONFIRM — align code/AC EC-M5-HCP / D5 live-stack screenshots Fold into the M6 human checkpoint

docs.title: 6. Exit criteria (EC-M6) → v1.0.0
docs.text:
• EC-M6-1 K2 complete (backend + frontend), all ACs green.
  • EC-M6-2 F11 clipper + secure ingress, all security ACs proven by tests.
  • EC-M6-3 F15: PWA + Tauri shell build; CI runs tests + pinned linters + mmdc on the right branches; multi-OS artifacts.
  • EC-M6-4 F2 verified; deferred FUs disposed.
  • EC-M6-5 Full suite green (pytest + vitest + Playwright); linters clean tree-wide.
  • EC-M6-6 D1–D7 complete and consistent; ER zero-drift; ADR index complete.
  • EC-M6-7 wiki/ remains a valid Obsidian vault after all M6 ops (I5/K7).
  • EC-M6-HCP Human checkpoint (Emanuele) in a live browser/desktop build → then tag v1.0.0.

docs.title: 7. Execution model
docs.text: Autonomous, one vertical at a time, each landed as a verified green commit before the next: K2-frontend → F11 → F15 → F2 + deferred FUs → docs gate → v1.0.0 tag. Subagent team per CLAUDE.md §13: ai-agent-engineer (provider-touching loops), backend-engineer (endpoints/data), frontend-engineer (UI), qa-test-engineer (gates), solution-architect (ADRs/invariants), devops-engineer (CI/CD/packaging), tech-writer (docs gate). No conflicting agents on the same files concurrently.

docs.title: Synapse v0.6 — Runbook di test (per Emanuele)
docs.text: Obiettivo: allineare il tuo Mac a GitHub e testare TUTTO ciò che è entrato in M6/v0.6. Branch di riferimento: sprint/v0.6 — HEAD cb55457, 14 commit sopra sprint/v0.5. Nota: questo runbook gira sul TUO Mac (io sono in un container remoto e non vedo /Users/...).

docs.title: 0. Allinea il locale a GitHub
docs.text:
Il tuo checkout è in /Users/emanuelechiummo/Documents/00_Personal/00_Claude/LLM Wiki Project (path con spazi → usa gli apici).

cd "/Users/emanuelechiummo/Documents/00_Personal/00_Claude/LLM Wiki Project"

git fetch origin --prune
git status                      # committa/stasha eventuali modifiche locali PRIMA
git checkout sprint/v0.6
git pull --ff-only origin sprint/v0.6

Verifica di essere allineato (deve dire 0 / 0): 

git rev-list --left-right --count origin/sprint/v0.6...HEAD   # atteso: 0   0
git log --oneline -1                                          # atteso: cb55457 feat(settings): Web Clipper + Web Search ...

Se hai lavoro locale che vuoi buttare per allinearti esattamente a GitHub: 

git reset --hard origin/sprint/v0.6      # ⚠️ distrugge le modifiche locali non committate

Migrations attese nel repo (fino alla 0016): 0014_lint_tables · 0015_vault_state_clip_config · 0016_vault_state_web_search_config.

docs.title: 1. Prerequisiti
docs.text:
• Docker Desktop (per Postgres + backend)
  • Node.js 20+ e npm (frontend)
  • Python 3.11 (solo se vuoi girare i test backend fuori da Docker)
  • Rust (rustup) — solo per il build desktop Tauri (su macOS il WebKit è di sistema, non servono librerie extra)
  • Servizi già sul tuo TrueNAS/Tailscale: Ollama (+ bge-m3), Qdrant, SearXNG

docs.title: 2. Configura l'ambiente
docs.text:
cp .env.example backend/.env      # oppure ./.env, a seconda di come monti il compose
Nel .env punta ai TUOI servizi (localhost / Tailscale / host.docker.internal): 
DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://synapse:synapse@postgres:5432/synapse
QDRANT_URL=http://host.docker.internal:6333
EMBEDDING_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434/api/embeddings
EMBEDDING_MODEL=bge-m3
EMBEDDING_DIM=1024
OLLAMA_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11434
# opzionale ora: SEARXNG e CLIP si possono configurare anche dalle Impostazioni (UI)
# SEARXNG_URL=http://host.docker.internal:8888
VAULT_ID=default
VAULT_PATH=/vault

CLIP_* e LINT_* NON sono obbligatori nel .env: il clipper è disattivo di default e si configura da Impostazioni → Web Clipper; SearXNG si può impostare da Impostazioni → Web Search.

docs.title: 3. Avvia lo stack (un solo comando) — consigliato per il Mac
docs.text:
Synapse è client/server: l'UI (web/PWA/desktop) è un client sottile, il motore è il backend + Postgres + Qdrant + Ollama. Quindi "aprire l'app" da solo non basta: serve lo stack su. Sul tuo Mac usa lo stack completo in un comando (frontend + backend + postgres + qdrant):

make dev            # = docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
Requisito unico: Ollama attivo a http://localhost:11434 (Ollama.app). Il container esegue da solo alembic upgrade head (quindi applica anche 0014/0015/0016).

Poi apri http://localhost:5173 (UI). API su http://localhost:8000. 

curl -s localhost:8000/status | jq          # health check backend
Stop: make dev-down.

Alternativa "produzione TrueNAS" (backend + postgres in container; Qdrant/Ollama/SearXNG esterni; niente frontend containerizzato → il frontend lo servi a parte): make up + make logs + make migrate, e per l'UI cd frontend && npm ci && npm run dev (proxy → :8000, override con BACKEND_PROXY_TARGET).

Verifica migration applicate (deve arrivare a 0016): 

docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml exec synapse-backend alembic current

Infine in UI: Impostazioni → LLM Models → aggiungi un provider (Local Ollama / API / CLI) — senza, ingest e chat non partono.

docs.title: 4. Usare il tuo abbonamento Claude (provider CLI, costo $0) — ADR-0042
docs.text:
Il provider CLI (claude-agent-sdk) può girare sul tuo abbonamento Pro/Max invece che a consumo con una API key. Funziona anche dentro il container, via un token OAuth a lunga durata.

Passo 1 — genera il token (una volta, sul Mac loggato con l'account Pro/Max): 

claude setup-token          # stampa un token OAuth (sk-ant-oat01-...), valido ~1 anno — copialo

Passo 2 — mettilo nel .env e NON impostare la API key: 

CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN=sk-ant-oat01-...     # il token del passo 1
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY  ← lascialo NON impostato (nemmeno vuoto)!

⚠️ Trappola di precedenza: se ANTHROPIC_API_KEY è impostata (anche =""), vince lei e ti fa pagare a token. Per l'abbonamento deve essere completamente assente.

Passo 3 — riavvia lo stack (make dev): il container legge CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, il claude CLI incluso nell'SDK autentica via abbonamento. Nell'ingest/chat il total_cost_usd sarà $0.00.

Passo 4 — in UI: Impostazioni → LLM Models → aggiungi un provider CLI e scegli il modello (es. claude-opus-4-8). Fai un ingest o una chat: costo $0 nell'Activity panel.

I tre modi di autenticazione (ne scegli uno), documentati anche in .env.example:

Variabile Provider Costo ANTHROPIC_API_KEY API / CLI a token CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN CLI (abbonamento, in container) ✅ $0 CLAUDE_CODE_USE_SUBSCRIPTION=true CLI (login host/montato) $0

Nota Tauri: il guscio desktop resta un client HTTP verso localhost:8000 — non cambia nulla per l'auth. L'abbonamento è deciso dal backend, non da Tauri.

docs.title: 5. Test automatici (attesi verdi)
docs.text:
Backend (venv locale): 

cd backend
python -m venv .venv && . .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"
python -m pytest -q          # atteso: 1018 passed, 4 skipped
ruff check app tests && black --check app tests && mypy app   # tutto clean
Frontend: 
cd frontend
npm run test                 # atteso: 742 passed
npx tsc --noEmit && npm run lint    # clean

docs.title: 6. Smoke manuale (EC‑M6‑HCP) — checklist
docs.text:
Spunta ognuna nel browser/app:

Core (Ingest · Query · Lint) - [ ] Crea progetto → Sources: importa un documento → parte l'ingest (Activity panel). - [ ] Compaiono pagine wiki, entità/concetti; Graph mostra i nodi/archi. - [ ] Chat: fai una domanda → risposta con citazioni [n]. - [ ] Lint (nuovo): sezione Lint → Run Lint → compaiono findings (orphan/missing‑xref/contradiction/stale/missing‑page) → prova Fix (missing‑xref/missing‑page) e Acknowledge (gli altri) e Dismiss.

Web Search (nuovo pannello) - [ ] Impostazioni → Web Search: imposta l'URL SearXNG (es. http://host.docker.internal:8888), categorie, max query → salva; il badge "source" passa a db. - [ ] Deep Research su un gap → esegue query SearXNG → sintetizza → auto‑ingest.

Web Clipper (nuovo pannello + estensione) - [ ] Impostazioni → Web Clipper: Enable ON → Genera token (copia il valore mostrato una‑tantum). - [ ] Carica l'estensione: Chrome → chrome://extensions → Modalità sviluppatore → Carica non pacchettizzata → seleziona la cartella extension/ → copia l'ID dell'estensione. - [ ] In Impostazioni → Web Clipper, aggiungi chrome-extension://<ID> in Allowed origins → salva. - [ ] Nell'estensione (Options): base URL http://localhost:8000, incolla il token. - [ ] Vai su una pagina web → clicca l'estensione → Clip → il markdown finisce in vault/raw/sources/ e viene auto‑ingerito (lo vedi nell'Activity panel). - [ ] Prova gli errori attesi: token errato → 401; se disabiliti l'ingress → 503.

Altro - [ ] Review queue: un ingest genera proposte → Approve / Deep‑Research / Skip. - [ ] Cascade delete: elimina una source → la sua pagina wiki e i wikilink morti spariscono; entità condivise restano.

PWA - [ ] cd frontend && npm run build && npm run preview → apri l'URL → in Chrome Installa app → verifica shell offline; le chiamate API NON sono cachate (nessun dato stantìo).

Desktop (Tauri) - [ ] cd frontend && npm run tauri:build (serve Rust) → installer in src-tauri/target/release/bundle/… → lancia l'app. - [ ] In alternativa: npm run tauri:dev per la finestra desktop in sviluppo.

Matrice 3 provider (I6) - [ ] Ripeti un ingest+chat con provider Local (Ollama), API, CLI.

docs.title: 7. Se qualcosa non va — check rapidi
docs.text: Sintomo Verifica /status non risponde make logs; Postgres su? docker compose ps Ingest non parte provider configurato in Impostazioni → LLM Models? EMBEDDING_URL/OLLAMA_URL raggiungibili? Deep Research 503 SearXNG impostato (Impostazioni → Web Search o SEARXNG_URL)? Clip 401 token corretto nelle Options dell'estensione? Enable ON? Clip 403 chrome-extension://<ID> negli Allowed origins? Migrazioni mancanti docker compose exec synapse-backend alembic upgrade head Frontend non chiama il backend proxy: backend su :8000? BACKEND_PROXY_TARGET giusto?

docs.title: 8. Quando lo smoke è OK → verso v1.0.0
docs.text:
Superata la checklist §6 (questa È l'EC‑M6‑HCP), il tag è meccanico: 1. bump versione a 1.0.0 in frontend/package.json, src-tauri/tauri.conf.json, backend/pyproject.toml; 2. cattura gli screenshot D5 in docs/screens/; 3. git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0 (fa scattare il job CI build multi‑OS Tauri).

Dimmi "vai" e faccio io i bump + il tag quando avrai dato l'OK allo smoke.
